### 1. INTERACTION DESIGN SYSTEM

#### 1.1 Micro-Interaction Vocabulary

Every interactive element in Skye follows a strict interaction vocabulary. No interaction may exist that is not catalogued below.

##### 1.1.1 Tap Interactions

| Element | Tap Behavior | Visual Feedback | Duration |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|----------|
| Primary Button | Scale to 0.97, opacity to 0.9, execute action on release | Background darkens 10% | Press: 60ms, Release: 120ms |
| Secondary Button | Scale to 0.98, execute action on release | Border color intensifies 20% | Press: 40ms, Release: 100ms |
| Ghost/Text Button | Opacity to 0.6, execute action on release | None (opacity only) | Press: 0ms (instant), Release: 150ms |
| List Row | Background highlight (systemGray5), execute on release | Row background shifts to highlight color | Press: 0ms (instant), Release: 200ms fade |
| Tab Bar Icon | Scale to 0.90 then spring to 1.0, switch tab | Icon fills/activates with spring animation | 300ms total spring |
| Avatar/Profile Image | Scale to 0.95, navigate to profile | Subtle shadow increase | Press: 60ms, Release: 100ms |
| FAB (Floating Action) | Scale to 0.92, shadow elevation increase, execute | Shadow radius 4 -> 8, color brightens 5% | Press: 80ms, Release: 200ms spring |
| Card Element | Scale to 0.98, slight shadow lift, navigate | Shadow offset Y: 2 -> 6, opacity 0.98 | Press: 100ms, Release: 150ms |
| Chip/Tag | Scale to 0.95, toggle state | Background color crossfade | 200ms |
| Icon Button (toolbar) | Opacity to 0.4, execute on release | None (opacity only) | Press: 0ms, Release: 150ms |
| Toggle/Switch | Track slides with gesture, snaps to state | Track color crossfade, thumb slides | 250ms spring |
| Segmented Control | Selected segment slides with spring | Background indicator translates | 350ms spring |

##### 1.1.2 Long-Press Interactions

| Element | Trigger Threshold | Behavior | Visual Feedback |
|---------|-------------------|----------|-----------------|
| Contact List Row | 500ms | Context menu appears (Call, Message, Email, Edit, Delete) | Row scales to 0.97, blurs background at 200ms mark |
| Property Card | 500ms | Context menu (Share, Edit, Archive, View on Map) | Card lifts with shadow, background blurs |
| Chat Message (own) | 500ms | Context menu (Copy, Edit, Delete, Reply) | Message bubble scales to 0.97, haptic at trigger |
| Chat Message (AI) | 500ms | Context menu (Copy, Regenerate, Share, Save to Notes) | Message bubble scales to 0.97, haptic at trigger |
| Studio Content Item | 500ms | Context menu (Share, Download, Edit, Delete, Duplicate) | Item lifts, background dims to 0.6 opacity |
| Image/Photo | 500ms | Preview popover (ala Peek), options on further press | Image zooms to 1.1 scale in context |
| Phone Number | 300ms | Copy to clipboard (shorter threshold for utility) | Flash highlight, toast confirmation |
| Email Address | 300ms | Copy to clipboard | Flash highlight, toast confirmation |
| URL/Link | 300ms | Preview + Copy option | Link text highlights in brand color |

All long-press interactions MUST include haptic feedback at the trigger threshold (see Section 1.2).

##### 1.1.3 Swipe Interactions

| Element | Swipe Direction | Distance Threshold | Behavior |
|---------|----------------|-------------------|----------|
| Contact List Row | Left (partial, 80pt) | 80pt to reveal | Reveal action buttons: Call (green), Delete (red) |
| Contact List Row | Left (full, >180pt) | 180pt to trigger | Execute primary trailing action (Call) |
| Contact List Row | Right (partial, 80pt) | 80pt to reveal | Reveal action button: Message (blue) |
| Contact List Row | Right (full, >180pt) | 180pt to trigger | Execute primary leading action (Message) |
| Property List Row | Left (partial) | 80pt to reveal | Archive (yellow), Share (blue) |
| Chat Message | Right (partial, 60pt) | 60pt to trigger | Reply to message (spring snap back) |
| Screen (from left edge) | Right (from 0-20pt edge) | 50% screen width or velocity > 500pt/s | Navigate back (interactive pop) |
| Modal/Bottom Sheet | Down | 100pt or velocity > 800pt/s | Dismiss modal (interactive) |
| Image Gallery | Left/Right | 50% width or velocity > 300pt/s | Navigate to next/previous image |
| Notification Banner | Up | 40pt | Dismiss notification |
| Notification Banner | Down | 60pt | Expand notification details |
| Settings Toggle Row | N/A (not swipeable) | N/A | Settings rows are tap-only, no swipe actions |

**Swipe Mechanics:**
- All swipe actions use SwiftUI `.swipeActions()` modifier for standard list row actions, or a custom `DragGesture` for advanced interactive swipe behaviors
- Swipe reveal actions have a rubberband effect beyond their snap point: clamp translation so the revealed area does not overshoot
- Full-swipe actions have a commit threshold at which point the row auto-completes the action with a spring animation
- Velocity-based detection: if horizontal velocity > 500pt/s at release, the swipe completes regardless of distance
- Opposite-direction interruption: starting a swipe in the opposite direction of an open action immediately closes the open action

##### 1.1.4 Pull-to-Refresh

| Screen | Pull Distance | Trigger Threshold | Animation |
|--------|--------------|-------------------|-----------|
| People (Contact List) | 0-120pt elastic | 80pt to trigger | Skye logo mark rotates 360 degrees, scales from 0.5 to 1.0 |
| Properties List | 0-120pt elastic | 80pt to trigger | Same branded animation |
| Chat History | 0-120pt elastic (inverted) | 80pt to trigger | Loading older messages, skeleton rows appear at top |
| Studio Gallery | 0-120pt elastic | 80pt to trigger | Same branded animation |
| Settings | N/A | N/A | No pull-to-refresh (static content) |

**Branded Pull-to-Refresh Animation Specification:**
- Implementation: Custom `DragGesture` + SwiftUI `@State` / `@GestureState` animated properties (NOT default SwiftUI `.refreshable()`)
- Phase 1 (0-40pt pull): Skye icon fades in from 0 to 1 opacity, positioned at pull distance Y
- Phase 2 (40-80pt pull): Skye icon rotates proportionally to pull distance (0 to 180 degrees), scales from 0.6 to 1.0
- Phase 3 (80pt+ trigger): Icon snaps to center, begins continuous rotation at 1 revolution per 800ms
- Phase 4 (refresh complete): Icon scales to 1.2, haptic success, fades out over 200ms, content springs into place
- Rubberband physics: `translationY * 0.5` beyond trigger threshold (content resists further pull)
- Color: Brand primary color icon on semi-transparent (`Color.black.opacity(0.03)`) background circle
- Implementation: SwiftUI animated state properties + `lottie-ios` (SPM) for the icon animation sequence

##### 1.1.5 Shake Gesture

| Context | Behavior | Confirmation |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Any screen | Presents "Report a Problem" / "Undo" action sheet | System alert with options: "Report a Problem", "Undo Last Action" (if applicable), "Cancel" |
| Chat (after send) | Shake within 3 seconds of sending -> Undo Send option | Undo confirmation with 2-second auto-dismiss countdown |

Detection: Override `motionEnded(_:with:)` in a UIKit hosting controller, or use a SwiftUI wrapper that listens for `UIEvent.EventSubtype.motionShake`. Threshold: 2.5g over 500ms window (system default handles this).

##### 1.1.6 Pinch-to-Zoom

| Element | Behavior | Bounds |
|---------|----------|--------|
| Property Street View | Zoom 1.0x to 4.0x with pan | Clamp at bounds, rubberband at edges, double-tap to toggle 1x/2x |
| Property Photos | Zoom 1.0x to 5.0x with pan | Full-screen takeover at >1.5x, dismiss on pinch back to <1.0x |
| Studio Generated Images | Zoom 1.0x to 3.0x with pan | Same as Property Photos |
| Profile Photos | Zoom 1.0x to 3.0x | Same as Property Photos |
| Chat Images | Zoom 1.0x to 4.0x with pan | Full-screen with dark background, dismiss on pinch to <1.0x |

**Zoom Implementation:**
- Use `MagnifyGesture` composed with `DragGesture` via `.simultaneously(with:)`
- Scale and offset are `@State` / `@GestureState` animated properties driving `.scaleEffect()` and `.offset()` modifiers
- When zoom > 1.0x, disable parent `ScrollView` scrolling (use `ScrollViewReader` or a custom `UIScrollView` wrapper to toggle `isScrollEnabled`)
- Double-tap: animated zoom to 2.0x centered on tap point, or back to 1.0x if already zoomed
- Double-tap animation: `withAnimation(.spring(response: 0.35, dampingFraction: 0.7))` to target scale

---

#### 1.2 Haptic Feedback Map

Skye uses iOS haptic feedback through `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator`, `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator`, and `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator`. Every haptic is mapped to a specific semantic action category. Haptics MUST be triggered synchronously on the main thread where possible.

**Pre-warming:** Call `.prepare()` on the appropriate feedback generator when a view appears (`.onAppear`) that will use haptics, to eliminate the ~50ms cold-start latency of the Taptic Engine.

##### 1.2.1 Impact Feedback (Physical Metaphor)

| Haptic Type | Trigger | Semantic Meaning |
|-------------|---------|-----------------|
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)` | Tab bar icon tap | Lightweight UI element selection |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)` | Chip/tag selection | Small element state change |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)` | Toggle switch flip | Lightweight binary state change |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)` | Segmented control change | Selection within a group |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)` | Pull-to-refresh crosses trigger threshold | Threshold acknowledgment |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium)` | Button tap (Primary, Secondary) | Standard interactive element activation |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium)` | Card tap/press | Navigational element activation |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium)` | Swipe action reveal (actions become visible) | Physical "landing" of revealed actions |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium)` | Bottom sheet snap to point | Sheet settling at a snap position |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .heavy)` | Long-press trigger (context menu appears) | Significant UI state change |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .heavy)` | Full-swipe action commit | Destructive or significant action commitment |
| `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .heavy)` | FAB tap | High-importance action initiation |

##### 1.2.2 Selection Feedback

| Haptic Type | Trigger | Semantic Meaning |
|-------------|---------|-----------------|
| `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator` | Scrolling through a picker wheel | Detent-by-detent selection change |
| `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator` | Dragging a slider to discrete values | Value change at each discrete step |
| `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator` | Reordering list items (as item crosses boundary) | Item has crossed a positional threshold |
| `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator` | Scrubbing through dates in a calendar | Each date boundary crossing |

##### 1.2.3 Notification Feedback

| Haptic Type | Trigger | Semantic Meaning |
|-------------|---------|-----------------|
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success)` | Message sent successfully | Positive completion |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success)` | Contact saved | Data persistence confirmed |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success)` | Content generated in Studio | AI task completed |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success)` | Pull-to-refresh complete | Refresh completed with new data |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success)` | Property saved/favorited | Positive action committed |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.warning)` | Form validation error (inline) | Non-blocking issue requires attention |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.warning)` | Network degraded (offline banner appears) | System state change requiring awareness |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.warning)` | Approaching character limit in chat input | Threshold warning |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.error)` | Action failed (API error) | Operation could not complete |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.error)` | Delete confirmation (at the moment of deletion) | Destructive action executed |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.error)` | Authentication failure | Security-related failure |
| `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.error)` | Network request timeout | Connection failure |

##### 1.2.4 Haptic Rules

1. **Never double-fire:** If a single user action produces both a visual change and a navigation, fire haptic once (on the action, not the navigation).
2. **No haptics on passive events:** Content loading, background sync, time-based updates produce zero haptics.
3. **No haptics on scroll:** Scrolling through content must never produce haptic feedback (except picker wheels).
4. **Respect system settings:** Check `CHHapticEngine.capabilitiesForHardware()` and gracefully degrade on devices without Taptic Engine. If the device is in Low Power Mode, skip haptics.
5. **Timing:** Haptics must fire within 10ms of the triggering gesture. Any delay >50ms creates a perceptible disconnect.
6. **iPad:** iPads do not support haptic feedback. All haptic calls must be wrapped in a platform/device check that gracefully no-ops on iPad.

---

#### 1.3 Gesture Vocabulary

All gesture recognizers use SwiftUI's declarative `.gesture()` API. Gestures are composed using `.simultaneously(with:)`, `.exclusively(before:)`, and `.sequenced(before:)` as appropriate.

##### 1.3.1 Standardized Gestures Across All Screens

| Gesture | Implementation | Behavior | Scope |
|---------|---------------|----------|-------|
| Edge Swipe Back | Built-in `NavigationStack` interactive pop (default UIKit back gesture) | Interactive pop with parallax (current screen slides right, previous screen slides from -33% to 0%) | All stack-pushed screens |
| Swipe-to-Dismiss Modal | `DragGesture` on modal container or `.interactiveDismissDisabled(false)` | Modal translates down with finger, opacity fades, dismiss on release past threshold | All modals and bottom sheets |
| Tap-Outside-to-Dismiss | `.onTapGesture` on overlay/backdrop `Color.black.opacity(...)` | Single tap on dimmed backdrop dismisses modal/sheet | All modals, bottom sheets, dropdowns |
| Pull-to-Refresh | Custom `DragGesture` on scrollable container | Branded refresh animation (see 1.1.4) | Scrollable list screens |
| Pinch-to-Zoom | `MagnifyGesture` + `DragGesture` via `.simultaneously(with:)` | Scale + translate image content | Image viewers only |
| Double-Tap-to-Zoom | `TapGesture(count: 2)` | Toggle between 1x and 2x zoom | Image viewers only |
| Long-Press | `LongPressGesture(minimumDuration: 0.5)` | Context menu / preview | List items, messages, images |

##### 1.3.2 Gesture Conflict Resolution

When multiple gesture recognizers exist on the same view hierarchy, conflicts are resolved as follows:

1. **Scroll vs. Swipe-to-Action:** Horizontal swipe on list rows is detected first. If the initial gesture direction is >30 degrees from horizontal, it falls through to the vertical scroll. Implementation: `DragGesture(minimumDistance: 10)` with direction detection in `.onChanged` -- if `abs(translation.width) > abs(translation.height)`, handle as swipe; otherwise, pass to scroll.

2. **Scroll vs. Edge Swipe Back:** The navigation edge swipe recognizer is limited to the leftmost 20pt of the screen. Any touch originating >20pt from the left edge is not captured by the back gesture. Implementation: UIKit's default `interactivePopGestureRecognizer` with its built-in edge detection.

3. **Pinch-to-Zoom vs. Scroll:** When a pinch gesture is detected (two fingers), scroll is disabled. When zoom > 1.0x, panning within the zoomed content takes priority over parent scroll. When zoom returns to 1.0x, parent scroll is re-enabled.

4. **Swipe-to-Dismiss vs. Scroll (Bottom Sheets):** The sheet's internal `ScrollView` handles this: when scrolled to top and dragging down, the sheet drag takes over from the scroll. Implementation handled by `UISheetPresentationController` internally, or by custom `DragGesture` coordination with the `ScrollView`.

5. **Long-Press vs. Swipe:** Long-press and swipe are mutually exclusive. If horizontal movement >10pt occurs during the long-press delay, the long-press is cancelled and swipe takes over. Implementation: compose with `.exclusively(before:)` where the swipe gesture has priority when movement exceeds 10pt.

---

#### 1.4 Touch Target Sizes

Per [Apple HIG](https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/), the absolute minimum touch target is 44x44pt. Skye enforces this strictly and exceeds it where possible.

| Element Type | Minimum Touch Target | Content Size | Padding Strategy |
|-------------|---------------------|-------------|-----------------|
| Primary Button | 44pt height, full-width or min 120pt wide | 17pt text, 16pt horizontal padding | `.padding(.vertical, 12)` `.padding(.horizontal, 24)` |
| Secondary Button | 44pt height, min 100pt wide | 15pt text | `.padding(.vertical, 10)` `.padding(.horizontal, 20)` |
| Icon Button (Toolbar) | 44x44pt | 22x22pt icon | Invisible padding extends hit area to 44x44 |
| Tab Bar Item | 49pt height (standard), width = screen / tabCount | 25x25pt icon + 10pt label | Centered in tab width |
| List Row | 56pt minimum height | Content dependent | `.padding(.vertical, 8)` `.padding(.horizontal, 16)` |
| Text Link (inline) | 44pt height (with invisible padding) | Text natural height | `.padding(.vertical, 10)` invisible extension |
| Checkbox / Radio | 44x44pt | 22x22pt visible element | 11pt invisible padding per side |
| Close / Dismiss Button | 44x44pt | 15x15pt "X" icon | Center icon in 44x44 hit area |
| Back Button | 44x44pt | 12x20pt chevron + label | Standard UINavigationBar sizing |
| Search Bar | 44pt height | 34pt visible bar height | 5pt vertical padding |
| Chat Send Button | 44x44pt | 28x28pt icon | 8pt padding |
| Swipe Action Button | 74pt width x row height | 22pt icon + 12pt label | Centered in 74pt width |
| Avatar (tappable) | 44x44pt minimum | 36x36pt image | 4pt invisible padding |
| FAB | 56x56pt | 24x24pt icon | 16pt padding |
| Segmented Control | 44pt height | Per Apple HIG standard | Segments divide evenly |

**Implementation Rule:** Every interactive SwiftUI `Button`, `View` with `.onTapGesture`, or custom tappable element MUST use `.contentShape(Rectangle())` and appropriate `.frame(minWidth:minHeight:)` to ensure the touch target is at least 44x44pt:

```swift
/// Ensures a minimum 44x44pt touch target for any content.
struct MinTouchTarget: ViewModifier {
    let contentWidth: CGFloat
    let contentHeight: CGFloat

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .frame(
                minWidth: max(contentWidth, 44),
                minHeight: max(contentHeight, 44)
            )
            .contentShape(Rectangle())
    }
}

extension View {
    func ensureMinTouchTarget(contentWidth: CGFloat, contentHeight: CGFloat) -> some View {
        modifier(MinTouchTarget(contentWidth: contentWidth, contentHeight: contentHeight))
    }
}
```

---

#### 1.5 Spring Animation Curves

All spring animations in Skye use SwiftUI's `Animation.spring(response:dampingFraction:blendDuration:)` or `withAnimation(.spring(...))`. Below are the exact configurations for every animation type. Engineers MUST use these values verbatim. No ad-hoc spring configurations are permitted.

> **Spring Parameter Mapping Note (Reanimated -> SwiftUI):**
>
> React Native Reanimated uses physics-based springs with `{ damping, stiffness, mass }`.
> SwiftUI uses `Animation.spring(response:dampingFraction:blendDuration:)` where:
>
> - `response` = 2 * pi * sqrt(mass / stiffness)  (the natural period of oscillation)
> - `dampingFraction` = damping / (2 * sqrt(stiffness * mass))  (1.0 = critically damped)
>
> All values below have been converted using these formulas. The original Reanimated
> parameters are preserved in comments for cross-reference.

##### 1.5.1 Named Spring Presets

```swift
// Springs.swift -- Canonical spring configurations
// Import and use ONLY these presets. Do not create ad-hoc springs.

import SwiftUI

enum Springs {
    // Snappy -- Used for small, immediate UI responses (button presses, toggles)
    // Original: { damping: 20, stiffness: 300, mass: 0.8 }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(0.8/300) = 0.324s
    // dampingFraction = 20 / (2 * sqrt(300*0.8)) = 0.645
    static let snappy = Animation.spring(response: 0.324, dampingFraction: 0.645, blendDuration: 0)

    // Gentle -- Used for content transitions (page pushes, card reveals)
    // Original: { damping: 20, stiffness: 120, mass: 1 }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(1/120) = 0.574s
    // dampingFraction = 20 / (2 * sqrt(120*1)) = 0.913
    static let gentle = Animation.spring(response: 0.574, dampingFraction: 0.913, blendDuration: 0)

    // Bouncy -- Used for playful elements (FAB press, tab icon, empty state illustrations)
    // Original: { damping: 12, stiffness: 150, mass: 0.9 }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(0.9/150) = 0.487s
    // dampingFraction = 12 / (2 * sqrt(150*0.9)) = 0.516
    static let bouncy = Animation.spring(response: 0.487, dampingFraction: 0.516, blendDuration: 0)

    // Heavy -- Used for large elements (bottom sheet, modal, full-screen transitions)
    // Original: { damping: 28, stiffness: 200, mass: 1.2 }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(1.2/200) = 0.487s
    // dampingFraction = 28 / (2 * sqrt(200*1.2)) = 0.904
    static let heavy = Animation.spring(response: 0.487, dampingFraction: 0.904, blendDuration: 0)

    // Sheet -- Used specifically for bottom sheet snap animations
    // Original: { damping: 30, stiffness: 250, mass: 0.9, overshootClamping: true }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(0.9/250) = 0.377s
    // dampingFraction = 30 / (2 * sqrt(250*0.9)) = 1.0 (clamped, no overshoot)
    static let sheet = Animation.spring(response: 0.377, dampingFraction: 1.0, blendDuration: 0)

    // Navigation -- Used for screen push/pop transitions
    // Original: { damping: 25, stiffness: 180, mass: 1, overshootClamping: true }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(1/180) = 0.468s
    // dampingFraction = 25 / (2 * sqrt(180*1)) = 0.931 -> clamped to 1.0 (overshootClamping)
    static let navigation = Animation.spring(response: 0.468, dampingFraction: 1.0, blendDuration: 0)

    // Keyboard -- Used for keyboard avoidance translations
    // Original: { damping: 30, stiffness: 250, mass: 0.8, overshootClamping: true }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(0.8/250) = 0.355s
    // dampingFraction = 30 / (2 * sqrt(250*0.8)) = 1.061 -> clamped to 1.0 (overshootClamping)
    static let keyboard = Animation.spring(response: 0.355, dampingFraction: 1.0, blendDuration: 0)

    // Micro -- Used for very small, subtle animations (icon state changes, indicator dots)
    // Original: { damping: 15, stiffness: 400, mass: 0.5 }
    // response = 2pi * sqrt(0.5/400) = 0.222s
    // dampingFraction = 15 / (2 * sqrt(400*0.5)) = 0.530
    static let micro = Animation.spring(response: 0.222, dampingFraction: 0.530, blendDuration: 0)
}
```

##### 1.5.2 Spring Usage Map

| Animation | Spring Preset | Animated Property | From | To |
|-----------|--------------|-------------------|------|-----|
| Button press down | `snappy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 1.0 | 0.97 |
| Button release | `bouncy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 0.97 | 1.0 |
| Tab bar icon activate | `bouncy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 0.90 | 1.0 |
| Tab bar indicator slide | `snappy` | `.offset(x:)` | Previous position | New position |
| Screen push (forward) | `navigation` | `.offset(x:)` | Screen width | 0 |
| Screen pop (back) | `navigation` | `.offset(x:)` | 0 | Screen width |
| Screen push (previous screen) | `navigation` | `.offset(x:)` | 0 | -screenWidth * 0.33 |
| Modal present (bottom sheet rise) | `heavy` | `.offset(y:)` | Screen height | Snap point |
| Modal dismiss | `sheet` | `.offset(y:)` | Current position | Screen height |
| Bottom sheet snap | `sheet` | `.offset(y:)` | Current | Target snap point |
| Card press | `snappy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 1.0 | 0.98 |
| Card release | `gentle` | `.scaleEffect()` | 0.98 | 1.0 |
| FAB press | `bouncy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 1.0 | 0.92 |
| FAB release | `bouncy` | `.scaleEffect()` | 0.92 | 1.0 |
| Toast enter | `bouncy` | `.offset(y:)` | -80 | 0 |
| Toast exit | `gentle` | `.opacity` + `.offset(y:)` | 1.0, 0 | 0, -40 |
| Skeleton shimmer | N/A (timing) | `.opacity` | 0.3 | 0.7 (loop, 1000ms) |
| Content fade-in | N/A (timing) | `.opacity` | 0 | 1 (200ms ease-out) |
| List item enter (staggered) | `gentle` | `.opacity` + `.offset(y:)` | 0, 20 | 1, 0 (stagger: 50ms) |
| Swipe action reveal | `snappy` | `.offset(x:)` | 0 | -80 (action width) |
| Swipe action snap back | `bouncy` | `.offset(x:)` | Current | 0 |
| Keyboard avoidance | `keyboard` | `.offset(y:)` | 0 | -keyboardHeight |
| Segmented control indicator | `snappy` | `.offset(x:)` + `frame(width:)` | Previous segment | Selected segment |
| Toggle switch thumb | `snappy` | `.offset(x:)` | 0 or 20 | 20 or 0 |
| Pull-to-refresh icon rotation | N/A (timing) | `.rotationEffect()` | 0deg | 360deg (800ms linear loop) |
| Context menu scale-in | `micro` | `.scaleEffect()` + `.opacity` | 0.8, 0 | 1.0, 1.0 |
| Empty state illustration | `bouncy` | `.scaleEffect()` + `.opacity` | 0.9, 0 | 1.0, 1.0 |
| Header collapse (scroll-linked) | N/A (interpolation) | `frame(height:)` + `.opacity` | Expanded | Collapsed (scroll-driven) |

##### 1.5.3 Timing Animation Exceptions

Some animations use explicit timing rather than springs, for predictability:

```swift
// Timings.swift -- Non-spring animation presets

import SwiftUI

enum Timings {
    // Opacity transitions -- springs overshoot on opacity looks wrong
    static let fadeIn = Animation.easeOut(duration: 0.2)
    static let fadeOut = Animation.easeIn(duration: 0.15)

    // Skeleton shimmer loop
    static let shimmer = Animation.easeInOut(duration: 1.0).repeatForever(autoreverses: true)

    // Color transitions (background, text color changes)
    static let colorChange = Animation.easeOut(duration: 0.25)

    // Scroll-linked interpolations use GeometryReader / ScrollViewReader
    // with computed values -- not withAnimation; they are driven directly
    // by scroll position via PreferenceKey or .scrollPosition() modifier.
}
```

##### 1.5.4 Reduce Motion Overrides

When `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)` is `true`:

| Standard Behavior | Reduced Motion Replacement |
|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Spring scale animations | Instant state change (no animation) |
| Slide transitions (push/pop) | Cross-dissolve: `withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.25))` on opacity |
| Bottom sheet spring to snap | `withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3))` -- no spring, no overshoot |
| Bouncy elements (FAB, tab icon) | Simple opacity change, no scale |
| Pull-to-refresh rotation | Static icon (no rotation), progress indicated by opacity |
| Staggered list entry | Instant render (no stagger, no translate) |
| Skeleton shimmer | Solid placeholder color (no shimmer animation) |
| Auto-scrolling content | Static display |
| Parallax effects | No parallax (static positioning) |

Implementation: Wrap all animations in a utility function:

```swift
import SwiftUI

struct AdaptiveAnimation: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) private var reduceMotion

    let spring: Animation
    let reducedMotionDuration: Double

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .transaction { transaction in
                if reduceMotion {
                    transaction.animation = .easeInOut(duration: reducedMotionDuration)
                } else {
                    transaction.animation = spring
                }
            }
    }
}

extension View {
    func adaptiveAnimation(
        _ spring: Animation,
        reducedMotionDuration: Double = 0.25
    ) -> some View {
        modifier(AdaptiveAnimation(
            spring: spring,
            reducedMotionDuration: reducedMotionDuration
        ))
    }
}

/// Utility function for imperative animation contexts
func adaptiveAnimate(
    reduceMotion: Bool,
    spring: Animation,
    reducedMotionDuration: Double = 0.25,
    body: @escaping () -> Void
) {
    if reduceMotion {
        withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: reducedMotionDuration)) { body() }
    } else {
        withAnimation(spring) { body() }
    }
}
```

---

### 2. NAVIGATION FLOW DIAGRAMS

#### 2.1 Complete Navigation Graph

##### 2.1.1 Root Architecture

```
AppRoot (WindowGroup / @main App)
+-- AuthGate (conditional, using @AppStorage or @Observable auth state)
|   +-- [NOT_AUTHENTICATED] -> AuthStack (fullScreenCover presentation)
|   |   +-- Welcome Screen
|   |   +-- Sign In (Email + Password)
|   |   +-- Sign In (Apple) -- via AuthenticationServices
|   |   +-- Sign Up
|   |   +-- Forgot Password
|   |   +-- Email Verification
|   |
|   +-- [AUTHENTICATED] -> MainTabView (TabView)
|       +-- Tab 1: Chat (AI Assistant)
|       |   +-- ChatStack (NavigationStack)
|       |       +-- ChatList (conversation history)
|       |       +-- ChatConversation (streaming AI chat)
|       |       +-- -> ContactDetail (push, from mentioned contact)
|       |       +-- -> PropertyDetail (push, from mentioned property)
|       |       +-- -> DocumentPreview (fullScreenCover, from shared document)
|       |
|       +-- Tab 2: People (CRM)
|       |   +-- PeopleStack (NavigationStack)
|       |       +-- PeopleList (searchable contact list)
|       |       +-- ContactDetail (full contact profile)
|       |       |   +-- -> EditContact (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)])
|       |       |   +-- -> AddNote (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.4), .fraction(0.7)])
|       |       |   +-- -> ActivityLog (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> PropertyDetail (push, from linked properties)
|       |       |   +-- -> ChatConversation (push, "Ask AI about this contact")
|       |       |   +-- -> Phone/SMS/Email (system action, external)
|       |       +-- AddContact (fullScreenCover)
|       |       +-- ImportContacts (fullScreenCover)
|       |       +-- FilterContacts (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.5), .fraction(0.85)])
|       |
|       +-- Tab 3: Properties
|       |   +-- PropertiesStack (NavigationStack)
|       |       +-- PropertiesList (searchable property list)
|       |       +-- PropertyDetail
|       |       |   +-- -> StreetView (push, full screen with zoom)
|       |       |   +-- -> PhotoGallery (fullScreenCover with zoom/swipe)
|       |       |   +-- -> EditProperty (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)])
|       |       |   +-- -> LinkedContacts (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> ShareProperty (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.4)])
|       |       |   +-- -> MapView (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> ChatConversation (push, "Ask AI about this property")
|       |       +-- AddProperty (fullScreenCover)
|       |       +-- FilterProperties (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.5), .fraction(0.85)])
|       |
|       +-- Tab 4: Studio (Content Generation)
|       |   +-- StudioStack (NavigationStack)
|       |       +-- StudioHome (gallery of generated content)
|       |       +-- CreateContent (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> SelectTemplate (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> SelectProperty (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)])
|       |       |   +-- -> SelectContact (sheet with presentationDetents: [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)])
|       |       |   +-- -> PreviewContent (push)
|       |       +-- ContentDetail (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> ShareContent (system share sheet via ShareLink or UIActivityViewController)
|       |       |   +-- -> EditContent (push)
|       |       |   +-- -> FullScreenPreview (fullScreenCover)
|       |       +-- ContentHistory (push)
|       |
|       +-- Tab 5: Settings
|           +-- SettingsStack (NavigationStack)
|               +-- SettingsHome
|               +-- ProfileSettings (push)
|               |   +-- -> EditProfile (push)
|               +-- NotificationSettings (push)
|               +-- SubscriptionSettings (push)
|               |   +-- -> PaywallModal (fullScreenCover)
|               +-- IntegrationSettings (push)
|               |   +-- -> IntegrationDetail (push)
|               +-- AppearanceSettings (push)
|               +-- PrivacySettings (push)
|               +-- DataExport (push)
|               +-- HelpAndSupport (push)
|               |   +-- -> WebView (push, using WKWebView via UIViewRepresentable)
|               +-- About (push)
|               +-- DeleteAccount (fullScreenCover with confirmation)
|
+-- Global Overlays (presented over any tab via .overlay() at root level)
|   +-- IncomingCallBanner (custom overlay, not a screen)
|   +-- NetworkOfflineBanner (custom overlay, not a screen)
|   +-- ToastNotification (custom overlay, not a screen)
|   +-- ForceUpdateModal (fullScreenCover, non-dismissible with interactiveDismissDisabled(true))
|
+-- Deep Link Handler (see 2.2)
```

##### 2.1.2 Back-Navigation Path Rules

| Screen | Back Button Target | Gesture Back Target | Tab Memory |
|--------|-------------------|-------------------|------------|
| ChatConversation | ChatList | ChatList | Preserves conversation scroll position |
| ContactDetail | PeopleList | PeopleList | Preserves list scroll position + search state |
| ContactDetail (from Chat link) | ChatConversation | ChatConversation | Cross-tab: returns to Chat tab |
| PropertyDetail | PropertiesList | PropertiesList | Preserves list scroll position |
| PropertyDetail (from Contact link) | ContactDetail | ContactDetail | Returns to referring contact |
| PropertyDetail (from Chat link) | ChatConversation | ChatConversation | Cross-tab: returns to Chat tab |
| EditContact (sheet) | Dismiss sheet | Swipe down dismisses | No navigation impact |
| AddContact (fullScreenCover) | Dismiss modal | Swipe down dismisses | If saved: PeopleList with new contact at top |
| Any Settings sub-screen | Parent Settings screen | Parent Settings screen | Settings stack unaffected by tab switching |

**Rule:** When navigating cross-tab (e.g., from Chat to a Contact Detail), the target screen is pushed onto the originating tab's stack. It does NOT switch tabs. This preserves the user's mental model of "where they are."

---

#### 2.2 Deep Link Resolution Map

##### 2.2.1 URL Scheme

```
skye://                         -> App root (current tab)
skye://chat                     -> Chat tab, ChatList
skye://chat/{conversationId}    -> Chat tab, ChatConversation
skye://people                   -> People tab, PeopleList
skye://people/{contactId}       -> People tab, ContactDetail
skye://properties               -> Properties tab, PropertiesList
skye://properties/{propertyId}  -> Properties tab, PropertyDetail
skye://studio                   -> Studio tab, StudioHome
skye://studio/{contentId}       -> Studio tab, ContentDetail
skye://settings                 -> Settings tab, SettingsHome
skye://settings/profile         -> Settings tab, ProfileSettings
skye://settings/subscription    -> Settings tab, SubscriptionSettings
```

##### 2.2.2 Push Notification Deep Link Resolution

| Notification Type | Payload Key | Target Screen | Fallback Screen | Auth Gate |
|-------------------|-------------|--------------|----------------|-----------|
| `new_message` | `conversationId` | ChatConversation | ChatList (if conversationId invalid) | YES -- queue deep link, present auth, navigate after success |
| `contact_reminder` | `contactId` | ContactDetail | PeopleList (if contactId not found) | YES |
| `property_update` | `propertyId` | PropertyDetail | PropertiesList (if propertyId not found) | YES |
| `studio_complete` | `contentId` | ContentDetail | StudioHome (if contentId not found) | YES |
| `subscription_expiring` | none | SubscriptionSettings | SettingsHome | YES |
| `system_announcement` | `url` (optional) | WebView or SettingsHome | SettingsHome | YES (but show announcement regardless) |
| `referral_invite` | `referralCode` | Sign Up (with pre-filled code) | Welcome Screen | NO -- auth gate not required for sign up flow |

##### 2.2.3 Deep Link Resolution Logic

```
1. App receives deep link (URL or notification tap)
2. Parse link into { screen, params } using URLComponents
3. Check authentication state:
   a. IF authenticated:
      - Check if target screen exists in navigation state (NavigationPath)
      - IF resource ID provided: validate resource exists (API call with 3s timeout)
        - IF valid: navigate to target screen with params (append to NavigationPath)
        - IF invalid (404): navigate to fallback screen, show toast "Item not found"
        - IF timeout: navigate to target screen (optimistic), let screen handle error state
      - IF no resource ID: navigate to target screen
   b. IF NOT authenticated:
      - Store deep link in UserDefaults: { pendingDeepLink: { screen, params, timestamp } }
      - Present AuthStack (fullScreenCover)
      - After successful authentication:
        - Read pendingDeepLink from UserDefaults
        - IF timestamp < 15 minutes old: execute navigation
        - IF timestamp >= 15 minutes old: discard, navigate to default tab
        - Clear pendingDeepLink from UserDefaults
4. App state handling:
   a. App killed (cold start): .onOpenURL { } at WindowGroup level
   b. App backgrounded: .onOpenURL { } continues to work
   c. App foregrounded: @Environment(\.scenePhase) == .active with pending URL check
```

---

#### 2.3 Tab-Switching State Preservation

| Tab | Preserved State on Tab Switch Away | Restored State on Tab Return |
|-----|-----------------------------------|------------------------------|
| Chat | Full stack (all pushed screens remain), scroll positions, draft message text in input, conversation scroll position | Full stack restored, scroll positions restored, draft message persists, keyboard state: dismissed (never auto-show keyboard on tab return) |
| People | Full stack, list scroll position, active search query text, active filter state, sort order | Full stack restored, scroll position restored. Search bar state: if was active, remains active with query text but keyboard dismissed. Filter badges persist. |
| Properties | Full stack, list scroll position, active search query, filter state, map vs list view toggle state | Full stack restored, scroll position restored. Map/List toggle state preserved. |
| Studio | Full stack, gallery scroll position, in-progress content generation (continues in background) | Full stack restored. If generation completed while away, shows completion badge on tab icon. |
| Settings | Full stack, scroll positions | Full stack restored. No special persistence needed. |

**Implementation:** SwiftUI `TabView` preserves each tab's `NavigationStack` by default. Enhancement:
- Each tab's content view is eagerly initialized (not lazy) to preserve state across tab switches
- Scroll positions stored in `@State` properties (SwiftUI preserves view state when the view is not destroyed)
- Draft text stored in an `@Observable` model object keyed by screen identifier, injected via `.environment()`
- Keyboard dismissal on tab switch: use `@FocusState` and set it to `nil` in the tab selection `onChange` handler

---

#### 2.4 Modal vs. Push Navigation Decisions

##### 2.4.1 Decision Framework

```
Should this screen be MODAL or PUSH?

1. Is this a self-contained task with a clear Save/Cancel outcome?
   -> YES -> MODAL (sheet or fullScreenCover)

2. Is the user drilling deeper into a content hierarchy?
   -> YES -> PUSH (NavigationStack with back button)

3. Does the user need to reference the screen behind this one?
   -> YES -> SHEET (partial modal with .presentationDetents())
   -> NO  -> Full-screen push or fullScreenCover

4. Can the task be completed in <3 taps?
   -> YES -> SHEET (detent at .fraction(0.4) - .fraction(0.6))

5. Does the task require significant scrollable content or a form with many fields?
   -> YES -> FULL-SCREEN MODAL (fullScreenCover with close button and save/done)

6. Is this a destructive or irreversible action confirmation?
   -> YES -> ALERT DIALOG (.alert() modifier, not a screen)

7. Is this selecting from a list to populate a field on the previous screen?
   -> YES -> SHEET (detent at .fraction(0.6) - .fraction(0.9)) with search
```

##### 2.4.2 Complete Modal/Push Classification

| Screen Transition | Presentation Type | Justification |
|-------------------|-------------------|---------------|
| PeopleList -> ContactDetail | **Push** | Drilling into hierarchy, needs back navigation context |
| ContactDetail -> EditContact | **Sheet** (.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)) | Self-contained edit task, user may reference profile behind |
| ContactDetail -> AddNote | **Sheet** (.fraction(0.4), .fraction(0.7)) | Quick task, benefits from seeing contact context |
| PeopleList -> AddContact | **fullScreenCover** | Multi-field form requiring focus, Save/Cancel outcome |
| PeopleList -> ImportContacts | **fullScreenCover** | Complex multi-step flow, needs full screen |
| PeopleList -> FilterContacts | **Sheet** (.fraction(0.5), .fraction(0.85)) | Quick selection task, user references list behind |
| PropertiesList -> PropertyDetail | **Push** | Drilling into hierarchy |
| PropertyDetail -> PhotoGallery | **fullScreenCover** | Immersive media experience requiring full screen |
| PropertyDetail -> EditProperty | **Sheet** (.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)) | Self-contained edit, reference property behind |
| PropertyDetail -> ShareProperty | **Sheet** (.fraction(0.4)) | Quick action with few options |
| PropertyDetail -> StreetView | **Push** | Content exploration, needs back navigation |
| ChatList -> ChatConversation | **Push** | Drilling into hierarchy, frequent back-and-forth |
| ChatConversation -> DocumentPreview | **fullScreenCover** | Immersive document view |
| StudioHome -> CreateContent | **Push** | Multi-step wizard, needs back navigation through steps |
| StudioHome -> ContentDetail | **Push** | Drilling into hierarchy |
| ContentDetail -> FullScreenPreview | **fullScreenCover** | Immersive preview |
| SettingsHome -> Any sub-screen | **Push** | Standard drill-down hierarchy |
| Settings -> DeleteAccount | **fullScreenCover** | Destructive action requiring focused confirmation |
| Any -> System Share Sheet | **System Modal** | iOS system-provided (ShareLink or UIActivityViewController) |
| Any -> Phone Call | **System Action** | iOS system-provided |

##### 2.4.3 Bottom Sheet Specifications

All bottom sheets use SwiftUI `.sheet()` with `.presentationDetents()` and `.presentationDragIndicator(.visible)`. For advanced snapping and interactive behaviors beyond what SwiftUI provides, use a `UISheetPresentationController` wrapper via `UIViewControllerRepresentable`.

| Bottom Sheet Context | Snap Points | Initial Snap | Enable Pan-to-Close | Handle Style |
|---------------------|-------------|-------------|---------------------|-------------|
| EditContact | [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)] | .fraction(0.6) | Yes | Visible grab handle, 36x5pt, 4pt border-radius |
| AddNote | [.fraction(0.4), .fraction(0.7)] | .fraction(0.4) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| FilterContacts | [.fraction(0.5), .fraction(0.85)] | .fraction(0.5) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| FilterProperties | [.fraction(0.5), .fraction(0.85)] | .fraction(0.5) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| ShareProperty | [.fraction(0.4)] | .fraction(0.4) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| SelectProperty (Studio) | [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)] | .fraction(0.6) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| SelectContact (Studio) | [.fraction(0.6), .fraction(0.9)] | .fraction(0.6) | Yes | Visible grab handle |
| Quick Actions (from FAB) | [.height(contentHeight)] | .height(contentHeight) | Yes | Visible grab handle |

**Bottom Sheet Shared Configuration:**

```swift
extension View {
    func skyeSheet<Content: View>(
        isPresented: Binding<Bool>,
        detents: Set<PresentationDetent>,
        selectedDetent: Binding<PresentationDetent>? = nil,
        @ViewBuilder content: @escaping () -> Content
    ) -> some View {
        self.sheet(isPresented: isPresented) {
            content()
                .presentationDetents(detents, selection: selectedDetent ?? .constant(detents.first ?? .medium))
                .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
                .presentationCornerRadius(16)
                .presentationBackgroundInteraction(.enabled(upThrough: detents.min() ?? .medium))
                .interactiveDismissDisabled(false)
        }
    }
}

// Handle indicator style: system default grab handle (36x5pt, rounded)
// is provided by .presentationDragIndicator(.visible)
// Background: uses the default system surface color with 16pt top corner radius
// Backdrop: system-provided dimming (approximately rgba(0,0,0,0.4))
```

---

#### 2.5 Gesture-Based Navigation

##### 2.5.1 Interactive Screen Pop (Edge Swipe Back)

```swift
// NavigationStack provides built-in interactive pop gesture.
// The system UINavigationController's interactivePopGestureRecognizer
// handles edge swipe back with these characteristics:
//
// - Gesture response distance: ~20pt from left edge (system default)
// - The current screen slides right, the previous screen slides from -33% to 0% (parallax)
// - Shadow on left edge of current screen during transition
//
// Custom transition configuration (via UINavigationControllerDelegate if needed):

struct SkyeNavigationTransition {
    // The system interactive pop gesture handles:
    // - Shadow: black, offset width -3, opacity 0.15 at full presentation
    // - Overlay on previous screen: black at 0.07 opacity when fully covered
    //
    // Spring configuration for programmatic transitions:
    // Uses Springs.navigation (response: 0.468, dampingFraction: 1.0)
    //
    // For custom matched geometry or hero transitions, use
    // .navigationTransition(.zoom(sourceID:in:)) or
    // .matchedGeometryEffect(id:in:) with NavigationStack.
}
```

**Interactive pop behavior:**
- Gesture is velocity-sensitive: if finger velocity > 500pt/s at release, pop completes regardless of distance
- If finger has traveled > 50% of screen width at release, pop completes
- If finger has traveled < 50% and velocity < 500pt/s, pop cancels (screen springs back)
- During gesture: previous screen is visible with parallax offset, dimming overlay on previous screen decreases proportionally

##### 2.5.2 Interactive Modal Dismissal

```swift
// Modal dismiss configuration for fullScreenCover presentations
struct ModalDismissConfig {
    static let velocityThreshold: CGFloat = 800     // Downward velocity > 800pt/s dismisses regardless
    static let distanceThreshold: CGFloat = 0.35    // Translated > 35% of modal height dismisses
    static let overDragResistanceFactor: CGFloat = 6 // Rubberband resistance when dragging beyond top
}

// For sheet() presentations, the system handles interactive dismiss automatically.
// For fullScreenCover with custom dismiss gesture:
struct DismissableFullScreenModal<Content: View>: View {
    @Environment(\.dismiss) private var dismiss
    @GestureState private var dragOffset: CGFloat = 0
    let content: () -> Content

    var body: some View {
        content()
            .offset(y: max(0, dragOffset))
            .opacity(1.0 - Double(dragOffset / UIScreen.main.bounds.height) * 0.5)
            .gesture(
                DragGesture()
                    .updating($dragOffset) { value, state, _ in
                        if value.translation.height > 0 {
                            state = value.translation.height
                        }
                    }
                    .onEnded { value in
                        let screenHeight = UIScreen.main.bounds.height
                        let shouldDismiss =
                            value.translation.height > screenHeight * ModalDismissConfig.distanceThreshold ||
                            value.predictedEndTranslation.height > screenHeight * 0.5
                        if shouldDismiss {
                            dismiss()
                        }
                    }
            )
            .animation(Springs.heavy, value: dragOffset)
    }
}
```

---

### 3. SCREEN STATE MACHINES

#### 3.1 Universal State Machine

Every data-driven screen in Skye implements the following state machine. No screen may deviate from this pattern without documented justification.

```
                        +-------------+
                        |    IDLE     |  (Screen mounted, no data request yet)
                        +------+------+
                               | .onAppear / .task (if stale)
                               v
                        +-------------+
                   +--->|  LOADING    |  (First load, show skeleton)
                   |    +------+------+
                   |           |
                   |    +------+------------------+
                   |    |                         |
                   |    v                         v
                   | +-------------+     +-------------+
                   | |   LOADED   |     |    EMPTY    |  (0 items)
                   | | (has data) |     +------+------+
                   | +------+------+           |
                   |        |                  | user action (add item)
                   |        |                  +-------> LOADING
                   |        |
                   |   +----+----------------+
                   |   |                     |
                   |   v                     v
                   | +-------------+  +-------------+
                   | | REFRESHING |  |   STALE     |
                   | | (pull-to-  |  +------+------+
                   | |  refresh)  |         |
                   | +------+------+        +-------> REFRESHING
                   |        |
                   |   +----+----------+
                   |   |               |
                   |   v               v
                   | LOADED     +-------------+
                   |            |   ERROR     |
                   |            +------+------+
                   |                   | retry
                   |                   +-------> LOADING (no cache) / REFRESHING (has cache)
                   |
                   |    +-------------+
                   +----+  OFFLINE    |
                        +------+------+
                               | network restored
                               +-------> REFRESHING (has cache) / LOADING (no cache)
```

##### 3.1.1 State Definitions

| State | Definition | UI Behavior |
|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `IDLE` | Screen is mounted but has not initiated a data fetch | Instantaneous, transitions to LOADING on `.task`. Users should never perceive this state. |
| `LOADING` | First data fetch in progress, no cached data available | Show skeleton screen (see 3.3). No spinner. No blank screen. |
| `LOADED` | Data successfully fetched and rendered | Full content visible. Pull-to-refresh enabled. |
| `EMPTY` | Data fetch succeeded but returned zero items | Empty state illustration + message + primary CTA (e.g., "Add your first contact"). |
| `REFRESHING` | Data refresh in progress, stale/cached data still visible | Current data remains visible. If pull-to-refresh: branded animation runs. If background refresh: subtle inline indicator (not full skeleton). |
| `STALE` | Cached data is older than staleness threshold, refresh needed | Identical to LOADED visually, but auto-triggers REFRESHING on next `.onAppear` or `scenePhase == .active`. |
| `ERROR` | Data fetch failed | If has cached data: show cached data + inline error banner at top ("Couldn't refresh. Tap to retry."). If no cached data: full-screen error state with illustration + message + retry button. |
| `OFFLINE` | Device has no network connectivity | If has cached data: show cached data + persistent offline banner at top. If no cached data: full-screen offline state with illustration + "You're offline" message. |

##### 3.1.2 Staleness Thresholds

| Screen | Staleness Threshold | Auto-Refresh Trigger |
|--------|--------------------|--------------------|
| ChatConversation | 30 seconds | On focus (tab switch or `scenePhase == .active`) |
| ChatList | 60 seconds | On focus |
| PeopleList | 5 minutes | On focus |
| ContactDetail | 2 minutes | On focus |
| PropertiesList | 5 minutes | On focus |
| PropertyDetail | 2 minutes | On focus |
| StudioHome | 5 minutes | On focus |
| Settings screens | Never stale | Manual refresh only (where applicable) |

##### 3.1.3 Error Retry Strategy

```
Attempt 1: Immediate retry
Attempt 2: After 2 second delay
Attempt 3: After 5 second delay
After 3 failures: Stop auto-retry. Show persistent error state with manual "Retry" button.
```

Each retry uses exponential backoff. During retry delay, show a subtle progress indicator (not full skeleton) and text "Retrying..." beneath the error message.

---

#### 3.2 Per-Screen State Machines

##### 3.2.1 Chat Conversation Screen

```
States: IDLE -> LOADING -> LOADED -> STREAMING -> ERROR -> OFFLINE

Additional states specific to Chat:
- STREAMING: AI is generating a response (tokens arriving via SSE/URLSession streaming)
  UI: Message bubble appears immediately with typing indicator,
      then text streams in token-by-token. User can scroll up during stream.
      "Stop Generating" button appears below streaming message.
- WAITING_FOR_AI: User message sent, waiting for AI response to begin
  UI: User message appears immediately (optimistic), typing indicator
      pulses in AI message area. Duration: typically 500ms-3s.

State transitions:
- User sends message -> LOADED (optimistic user message) -> WAITING_FOR_AI -> STREAMING -> LOADED
- Network loss during STREAMING -> Show partial response + "Response interrupted" label + Retry button
- Error during WAITING_FOR_AI -> Show failed message with retry icon on user's message bubble
```

##### 3.2.2 People List Screen

```
States: IDLE -> LOADING -> LOADED/EMPTY -> REFRESHING -> ERROR -> OFFLINE

Additional states:
- SEARCHING: User has activated search, keyboard is visible
  UI: List filters in real-time (debounced 300ms). Empty search state:
      "No contacts match [query]" with suggestion to broaden search.
- FILTERING: User has active filters applied
  UI: Filter badge count on filter button. Inline chip bar showing active filters.
      "Clear all" option. Empty filtered state: "No contacts match these filters."
```

##### 3.2.3 Contact Detail Screen

```
Sub-sections load independently:
1. Header + Contact Info (from cache: instant; from API: skeleton for 200-500ms)
2. Activity Timeline (skeleton, loads within 500ms-1s)
3. Linked Properties (skeleton, loads within 500ms-1.5s)
4. Notes (skeleton, loads within 500ms-1.5s)

Each section independently transitions through LOADING -> LOADED/EMPTY/ERROR.
The screen as a whole is LOADED when section 1 (header) is loaded.
```

##### 3.2.4 Property Detail Screen

```
Sub-sections load independently:
1. Header (address, price, hero image): Loads first (from list cache if available)
2. Property details (beds, baths, sqft, description): Loads with header
3. Street View thumbnail: Loads second (separate image load)
4. Photo gallery thumbnails: Loads third (lazy, loads as user scrolls to section)
5. Linked contacts: Loads fourth (separate API)
6. Market data: Loads fifth (separate API, may be slowest)
```

##### 3.2.5 Studio Home Screen

```
Additional states:
- GENERATING: Content generation is in progress
  UI: Generating item appears at top of gallery with indeterminate progress bar
      + "Generating your listing description..." text.
      Persists if user navigates away and returns.
- GENERATED: Content just completed generation
  UI: Item at top of gallery pulses with brand highlight ring for 3 seconds,
      success haptic fires if user is on this screen when generation completes.
```

---

#### 3.3 Skeleton Screen Specifications

Every skeleton screen MUST match the exact layout of its loaded state. Skeleton placeholders must be the same size and position as the content they replace.

##### 3.3.1 Skeleton Visual Style

```swift
struct SkeletonStyle {
    // Light mode
    static let backgroundColor = Color.black.opacity(0.06)
    // Dark mode
    // static let backgroundColor = Color.white.opacity(0.08)

    static let cornerRadius: CGFloat = 4
    static let shimmerHighlight = Color.black.opacity(0.02)
    static let shimmerDuration: Double = 1.0 // seconds per cycle
}

// Shimmer modifier
struct ShimmerModifier: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) private var reduceMotion
    @State private var phase: CGFloat = 0

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        if reduceMotion {
            content
                .foregroundStyle(SkeletonStyle.backgroundColor)
        } else {
            content
                .foregroundStyle(SkeletonStyle.backgroundColor)
                .overlay(
                    LinearGradient(
                        colors: [.clear, SkeletonStyle.shimmerHighlight, .clear],
                        startPoint: .leading,
                        endPoint: .trailing
                    )
                    .rotationEffect(.degrees(45))
                    .offset(x: phase)
                    .animation(
                        .linear(duration: SkeletonStyle.shimmerDuration)
                        .repeatForever(autoreverses: false),
                        value: phase
                    )
                )
                .onAppear { phase = 200 }
                .clipped()
        }
    }
}
```

Shimmer animation: A linear gradient sweeps left-to-right across the skeleton at 45 degrees. When Reduce Motion is enabled, skeleton is solid gray with no shimmer.

##### 3.3.2 Per-Screen Skeleton Layouts

**People List Skeleton:**
```
+--------------------------------+
| [Search Bar Skeleton]          |  <- 36pt height, full width - 32pt margin
|                                |
| [O] ===============           |  <- 56pt row: 40pt circle avatar + name line
|     ============               |     Name: 140pt width, 14pt height
|     =========                  |     Subtitle: 100pt width, 12pt height
|--------------------------------|
| [O] ===============           |  <- Repeat 8 rows (fills typical viewport)
|     ============               |
|     =========                  |
|--------------------------------|
|        (repeat x6 more)       |
+--------------------------------+
```

**Contact Detail Skeleton:**
```
+--------------------------------+
|          [  O  ]               |  <- 80pt circle avatar, centered
|       ================         |  <- Name: 160pt centered, 18pt height
|         ============           |  <- Role: 120pt centered, 14pt height
|          ==========            |  <- Company: 100pt centered, 14pt height
|                                |
| [O]      [O]      [O]         |  <- Action buttons: 3x 44pt circles
|                                |
| -- SECTION DIVIDER --          |
| ========                       |  <- Section header: 80pt, 12pt height
| =======================        |  <- Detail rows
| ===================            |
+--------------------------------+
```

**Properties List Skeleton:**
```
+--------------------------------+
| [Search Bar Skeleton]          |
|                                |
| +----------------------------+ |  <- Property card: 200pt height
| | ========================== | |     Hero image placeholder: 140pt
| |                            | |
| | ================           | |     Address: 180pt
| | ============               | |     Price: 120pt
| | ===  ===  ====             | |     Beds/Baths/Sqft
| +----------------------------+ |
|                                |
| +----------------------------+ |  <- Repeat 3 cards
| | ========================== | |
| | ================           | |
| | ============               | |
| +----------------------------+ |
+--------------------------------+
```

**Chat Conversation Skeleton:**
```
+--------------------------------+
|                                |
|          ================      |  <- AI message bubble (left-aligned)
|          ============          |
|                                |
|    ================            |  <- User message bubble (right-aligned)
|                                |
|          ====================  |  <- AI message (left)
|          ==================    |
|          ============          |
|                                |
+--------------------------------+
| [Message Input Bar]            |  <- Input bar is NOT skeleton; real and interactive immediately
+--------------------------------+
```

##### 3.3.3 Progressive Loading Transitions

Content replaces skeleton with a crossfade:
- Skeleton element fades out: `withAnimation(.easeIn(duration: 0.15))` opacity to 0
- Real content fades in: `withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.2).delay(0.05))` opacity to 1
- The 50ms overlap creates a seamless crossfade, not a flash
- If a section loads in <100ms, skip the skeleton entirely (show content directly) to avoid flicker
- Staggered sections: each section's skeleton crossfades independently as its data arrives

---

### 4. KEYBOARD AND INPUT BEHAVIOR

#### 4.1 Keyboard Avoidance Strategy

Skye uses SwiftUI's built-in keyboard avoidance, enhanced with `@FocusState` for field management and `ScrollViewReader` for scrolling to focused fields. SwiftUI on iOS 17+ automatically adjusts `ScrollView` and `List` content when the keyboard appears.

##### 4.1.1 Global Setup

```swift
// In the root App struct
import SwiftUI

@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView()
                // SwiftUI handles keyboard avoidance automatically.
                // No wrapping provider needed -- the system manages
                // keyboard offset for ScrollView, List, and Form.
        }
    }
}
```

##### 4.1.2 Per-Screen Keyboard Behavior

| Screen | Input Elements | Avoidance Strategy | Keyboard Type |
|--------|---------------|-------------------|---------------|
| Chat Conversation | Message TextField (expandable) | `padding` mode: content pushes up, input bar stays above keyboard. Use `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` for input bar. | `.default` |
| AddContact | Name, Phone, Email, Address, Notes | `ScrollView` + `ScrollViewReader`: focused field scrolls into view via `.scrollTo()` | `.default`, `.phonePad`, `.emailAddress` per field |
| EditContact | Same as AddContact | Same as AddContact | Same as AddContact |
| AddProperty | Address, Price, Description, etc. | `ScrollView` + `ScrollViewReader` | `.default`, `.decimalPad` for price |
| Search (People) | Search TextField | No avoidance needed (search bar is at top, list shrinks) | `.default` |
| Search (Properties) | Search TextField | No avoidance needed | `.default` |
| Sign In | Email, Password | `ScrollView`: form scrolls up to keep active field + submit button visible | `.emailAddress`, `.default` |
| Sign Up | Name, Email, Password, Confirm | `ScrollView` with scroll | `.default`, `.emailAddress`, `.default`, `.default` |
| AddNote | Note TextField | `padding` mode (sheet context: sheet pushes up above keyboard automatically) | `.default` |
| Studio CreateContent | Prompt TextField | `ScrollView`: prompt field + generate button remain visible | `.default` |
| Settings (Profile Edit) | Name, Bio, etc. | `ScrollView` + `ScrollViewReader` | `.default` |

##### 4.1.3 Keyboard Dismiss Behavior

| Trigger | Behavior |
|---------|----------|
| Tap outside text input | Dismiss keyboard (`.onTapGesture { focusedField = nil }` on background, or `.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)`) |
| Scroll list | Dismiss keyboard on scroll begin (`.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)` on `ScrollView` / `List`) |
| Tab switch | Dismiss keyboard (set `@FocusState` to `nil` in `.onChange(of: selectedTab)`) |
| Back navigation | Dismiss keyboard (handled by navigation transition automatically) |
| Sheet drag | Dismiss keyboard (handled by system sheet presentation) |
| Swipe-to-dismiss modal | Dismiss keyboard at start of gesture |

---

#### 4.2 Chat Input: Expanding Text Input Behavior

The chat message input is the most complex input in the app. It must match the behavior of Apple Messages.

```swift
struct ChatInputConfig {
    static let multiline = true
    static let minHeight: CGFloat = 36              // Single line height
    static let maxHeight: CGFloat = 120             // Maximum expansion (approximately 5 lines of text)
    static let lineHeight: CGFloat = 22             // Per-line height at default font size
    static let fontSize: CGFloat = 16               // Body text size
    static let verticalPadding: CGFloat = 8
    static let horizontalPadding: CGFloat = 12
    static let cornerRadius: CGFloat = 20           // Pill shape when single-line
    // cornerRadius adjusts: 20 at 1 line, 16 at 2 lines, 12 at 3+ lines

    // Expansion behavior:
    // - Use a TextEditor or UITextView via UIViewRepresentable
    // - Track intrinsic content height via GeometryReader or sizeThatFits
    // - Height is animated with Springs.keyboard
    // - When content exceeds maxHeight, internal scroll is enabled
    // - scrollEnabled transitions from false -> true when content > maxHeight

    // Send button behavior:
    // - Send button appears when text is non-empty (trimming whitespace)
    // - Send button replaces voice/attachment button with crossfade (200ms)
    // - Send button is 30x30pt, positioned vertically centered to last line of text
    // - Send button has UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium) on tap
}
```

**Chat Input Layout (single line):**
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
| [+]  | Message...                        |  [>]  |
+--------------------------------------------------+
  |      |                                    |
  |      TextField (expands upward)           Send (appears when text non-empty)
  Attachment button                           OR microphone (when text empty)
```

**When expanded (multi-line):**
```
+--------------------------------------------------+
| [+]  | This is a longer message that     |       |
|      | wraps to multiple lines. The      | [>]   |
|      | input grows upward.               |       |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

When scrollable (content exceeds maxHeight):
- A subtle top border (1px, Color(uiColor: .systemGray5)) appears at the top of the TextEditor to indicate scrollable content above
- Scroll indicator is visible inside the TextEditor
- The input container stops growing; internal scroll takes over

---

#### 4.3 Search Input Behavior

| Property | Value | Rationale |
|----------|-------|-----------|
| Debounce timing | 300ms | Balances responsiveness with API/filter performance |
| Minimum query length | 1 character | Start filtering immediately; real-time feel |
| Cancel button | Appears when search bar is focused (animated slide-in from right, 200ms) | Apple HIG standard pattern |
| Cancel button tap | Clears text, dismisses keyboard, hides cancel button | Full reset |
| Clear button ("X") | Appears when text is non-empty, inside search bar at trailing edge | Clears text only, keyboard stays, search remains focused |
| Keyboard dismiss on scroll | Yes (`.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)`) | Standard iOS behavior |
| Search bar style | 36pt height visible, system gray background, 8pt border-radius, magnifying glass icon leading | Matches UISearchBar (use `.searchable()` modifier) |
| Placeholder text | People: "Search contacts...", Properties: "Search properties...", Studio: "Search content..." | Context-specific |
| Voice search | Not supported in V2 | Planned for V3 |
| Search results | Local-first (filter cached list), then API (if query length >= 3 and local results < 5) | Instant local results + server augmentation |
| Empty search results | Illustration + "No results for '[query]'" + suggestion text | Never show a blank list |
| Recent searches | Show 5 most recent searches when search bar is focused and empty | Stored in UserDefaults / SwiftData, clearable |

---

#### 4.4 Form Input Behavior

##### 4.4.1 Focus Order (Tab Order)

Forms follow a strict top-to-bottom, left-to-right focus order. The Return key on the keyboard advances to the next field.

**AddContact Form:**
```
1. First Name       -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Last Name
2. Last Name        -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Phone
3. Phone            -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Email
4. Email            -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Company
5. Company          -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Role/Title
6. Role/Title       -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Address
7. Address          -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Notes
8. Notes (multiline)-> Return key: "Return" (new line) -> Submit via toolbar "Done" button
```

**Sign In Form:**
```
1. Email            -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Password
2. Password         -> Return key: "Go"   -> submits form
```

**Sign Up Form:**
```
1. Full Name        -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Email
2. Email            -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Password
3. Password         -> Return key: "Next" -> focuses Confirm Password
4. Confirm Password -> Return key: "Join" -> submits form
```

Implementation: Use a `@FocusState` enum to represent each field. On `.onSubmit` of field N, set `focusedField = .fieldN+1`. The last field either submits the form or (for multiline) inserts a newline.

```swift
enum AddContactField: Hashable {
    case firstName, lastName, phone, email, company, role, address, notes
}

@FocusState private var focusedField: AddContactField?

// Usage on each TextField:
TextField("First Name", text: $firstName)
    .focused($focusedField, equals: .firstName)
    .submitLabel(.next)
    .onSubmit { focusedField = .lastName }
```

##### 4.4.2 Return Key Configuration

| Field Type | `.submitLabel()` | Behavior |
|-----------|-----------------|----------|
| Any field with a next field | `.next` | Focus next field |
| Last single-line field in form | `.go` or `.done` or `.join` | Submit form |
| Multiline field (Notes, Description) | `.return` | Insert newline |
| Search field | `.search` | Execute search (secondary, since real-time filtering is primary) |
| Chat input | `.return` | Return inserts newline; send is button-based |

##### 4.4.3 Inline Validation Timing

| Validation Type | Trigger Timing | Visual Feedback |
|----------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Required field empty | On blur (field loses focus) only if user interacted with field | Red border, error text below: "This field is required" |
| Email format invalid | On blur + after 500ms of no typing (live) | Red border, error text: "Enter a valid email address" |
| Phone format invalid | On blur | Red border, error text: "Enter a valid phone number" |
| Password too short | Live (as user types) once >= 1 character typed | Strength indicator: red (<8), yellow (8-11), green (12+) |
| Password mismatch | On blur of confirm password field | Red border on confirm field: "Passwords don't match" |
| Duplicate contact | On blur of email or phone (async check) | Warning (yellow) border: "A contact with this email already exists" |

**Validation visual pattern:**
- Error state: border color transitions to `.red` (250ms), error text fades in below field (150ms), field label turns red
- Recovery: when error is corrected, border transitions back to default (250ms), error text fades out (150ms)
- Haptic: `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.warning)` on first error appearance per field (not on every keystroke)

---

### 5. SCROLL BEHAVIOR AND PERFORMANCE

#### 5.1 List Optimization Strategy (500+ Items)

Skye uses SwiftUI `List` with `LazyVStack` as the primary approach for moderate lists, and `UICollectionView` with `UICollectionViewCompositionalLayout` (via `UIViewControllerRepresentable`) for high-performance lists requiring 500+ items, variable cell sizes, or advanced prefetching.

##### 5.1.1 List Configuration by Screen

| Screen | List Component | Estimated Item Size | Draw Distance | Item Type |
|--------|---------------|--------------------|----|-----------|
| PeopleList | `List` with `LazyVStack` (or `UICollectionView` for 500+ contacts) | 64pt | 250pt prefetch window | Uniform height contact rows |
| PropertiesList | `List` with `LazyVStack` | 220pt | 300pt prefetch window | Uniform height property cards |
| ChatConversation | `UICollectionView` (inverted via `.transform` rotation) | 80pt | 500pt prefetch window | Variable height message bubbles |
| StudioHome | `LazyVGrid` (2 or 3 columns) | 180pt | 300pt prefetch window | Uniform height content cards |
| ChatList | `List` with `LazyVStack` | 72pt | 250pt prefetch window | Uniform height rows |
| ActivityLog | `List` with `LazyVStack` | 56pt | 200pt prefetch window | Uniform height rows |
| Search Results | `List` with `LazyVStack` | 64pt | 200pt prefetch window | Uniform height rows |

##### 5.1.2 List Performance Configuration

```swift
// Image optimization:
// - All list images use AsyncImage with disk + memory cache, or a third-party
//   caching library (e.g., Kingfisher or Nuke via SPM)
// - Avatar images: 40x40pt rendered size, source images max 80x80px (2x Retina)
// - Property thumbnails: exact rendered size, not full-resolution
// - Image placeholder: brand-colored background while loading

// Render optimization (UICollectionView path):
// - Use UICollectionViewDiffableDataSource for efficient updates
// - Key extractors use stable IDs (never array index)
// - Cells use prepareForReuse() to reset state
// - Prefetching enabled via UICollectionViewDataSourcePrefetching

// Render optimization (SwiftUI path):
// - All list item views use Equatable conformance for efficient diffing
// - Use @Observable models so SwiftUI only re-renders on actual changes
// - No inline closures in ForEach that capture changing state

struct ContactRow: View, Equatable {
    let contact: Contact

    static func == (lhs: ContactRow, rhs: ContactRow) -> Bool {
        lhs.contact.id == rhs.contact.id &&
        lhs.contact.updatedAt == rhs.contact.updatedAt
    }

    var body: some View {
        // render
    }
}
```

##### 5.1.3 Section List Pattern (Alphabetical Contacts)

```
List with section headers:
- Use SwiftUI List with ForEach over sections, each with a Section header
- Section headers: 16pt height, system gray background, bold 13pt label
- Sticky headers provided automatically by List
- Section index (A-Z sidebar): use .listStyle(.sidebar) or a custom
  UITableView sectionIndexTitles equivalent via UIViewRepresentable
  - Touch + drag scrolls to section (with UISelectionFeedbackGenerator at each letter)
  - Sidebar letters: 10pt font, 16pt touch target height
  - Large letter overlay appears center-screen during sidebar scrub
```

---

#### 5.2 Scroll-Linked Animations

##### 5.2.1 Header Collapse (Large Title to Small Title)

Applicable screens: PeopleList, PropertiesList, StudioHome, ChatList, Settings

```swift
// Use SwiftUI's built-in .navigationTitle() with .navigationBarTitleDisplayMode(.large)
// which provides automatic large-to-inline title collapse on scroll.
//
// If custom behavior is needed beyond what the system provides:
struct HeaderCollapseConfig {
    // Large title height: 52pt (text) + 16pt padding = 68pt collapsible area
    // Navigation bar height: 44pt (standard) + status bar
    // Total header: 44 + 68 = 112pt expanded, 44pt collapsed

    // Scroll range: 0 -> 68pt of scroll triggers full collapse
    // Animation: scroll-linked (direct 1:1 mapping, no springs)

    static let expandedTitleFontSize: CGFloat = 34   // SF Pro Display Bold (system .largeTitle)
    static let collapsedTitleFontSize: CGFloat = 17  // SF Pro Text Semibold (system .headline)

    // Large title translateY: lerp(scrollY, in: 0...68, out: 0...(-68), clamped)
    // Large title opacity:    lerp(scrollY, in: 0...40...68, out: 1...1...0, clamped)
    // Small title opacity:    lerp(scrollY, in: 50...68, out: 0...1, clamped)
    // Search bar: scrolls with content, sticks when reaching nav bar bottom

    // Snap behavior: if scroll position between 20-48pt on release, snap to 0 or 68
    // Snap threshold: 34pt (midpoint). Below -> snap expanded. Above -> snap collapsed.
    // Snap animation: Springs.snappy

    // Use .coordinateSpace(name: "scroll") + GeometryReader to read scroll offset,
    // or a PreferenceKey to propagate scroll position to parent views.
}
```

##### 5.2.2 Tab Bar Auto-Hide

Applicable: ChatConversation only (tab bar hides to maximize chat space)

```swift
struct TabBarAutoHideConfig {
    // Trigger: scrolling DOWN by >20pt
    // Animation: tab bar translates down by 83pt (including safe area on notched iPhones)
    //   withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.25))

    // Trigger show: scrolling UP by >10pt OR reaching bottom of chat
    //   withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.25))

    // Hides ONLY in ChatConversation
    // On tab switch: tab bar always resets to visible

    // When tab bar is hidden, chat input extends to safe area bottom
    // Input bar animates bottom padding from tabBarHeight to safeAreaBottom

    // Implementation: use .toolbar(.hidden, for: .tabBar) conditionally,
    // or manage tab bar visibility via a shared @Observable environment object.
}
```

##### 5.2.3 FAB Behavior

Applicable: PeopleList, PropertiesList, StudioHome

```swift
struct FABScrollBehavior {
    // Default: visible at bottom-right, 16pt from right, 16pt above tab bar
    // Scroll DOWN >30pt: FAB shrinks to mini (scale 0.7, opacity 0.8) via Springs.snappy
    // Scroll UP >10pt: FAB returns to full (scale 1.0, opacity 1.0)
    // At scroll top (offset < 50): always fully visible
    // At scroll bottom: always fully visible

    // FAB does NOT fully hide -- reduces to mini but remains tappable
    // Mini: icon only, reduced shadow
    // Full: icon + label (if applicable), full shadow

    // Implementation: .overlay(alignment: .bottomTrailing) on the list container,
    // with .scaleEffect() and .opacity() driven by scroll offset state.
}
```

---

#### 5.3 Momentum Scrolling

All scrollable containers use the default iOS deceleration rate (UIScrollView.DecelerationRate.normal, 0.998).

```swift
// ScrollView and List use the system default deceleration rate.
// For UIScrollView-backed views:
let scrollConfig: [String: Any] = [
    // decelerationRate: .normal (default, matches UIScrollView)
    // showsVerticalScrollIndicator: true (default for ScrollView)
    // showsHorizontalScrollIndicator: false

    // Horizontal (photo gallery):
    // Use TabView with .tabViewStyle(.page) for paged horizontal scrolling,
    // or ScrollView(.horizontal) with .scrollTargetBehavior(.paging)

    // bounces: true (default)
    // alwaysBounceVertical: true (default for ScrollView)
]
```

---

#### 5.4 Pull-to-Refresh Implementation

See Section 1.1.4 for animation specification. Technical details:

```swift
struct PullToRefreshConfig {
    // Custom DragGesture (NOT .refreshable() modifier for branded animation)
    // Threshold: 80pt to trigger
    // Elastic resistance: beyond 80pt, 0.5x factor
    // Max pull: 160pt
    // Trigger: instant UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light)
    // Minimum display time: 600ms (even if API returns faster)
    // Maximum display time: 15s (timeout, show error toast)
    // Complete: UINotificationFeedbackGenerator(.success), content springs to top
```
