                DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
                    withAnimation(SkyeSpring.bouncy) {
                        scale = 1.0
                    }
                }
            }
            UINotificationFeedbackGenerator().notificationOccurred(.success)
        }
    }
}
```

**Error shake:**
- Horizontal shake: translateX oscillation: 0 -> 8 -> -8 -> 6 -> -6 -> 4 -> -4 -> 2 -> -2 -> 0
- Duration: `duration.normal` (0.3s)
- Easing: `easing.standard`
- Applied to: the form field or element with the error
- Haptic: `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator()` with `.error` type
- Visual: border color transitions to `border.error` simultaneously

```swift
struct ShakeModifier: ViewModifier {
    @Binding var trigger: Bool
    @State private var offset: CGFloat = 0

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .offset(x: offset)
            .onChange(of: trigger) { _, newValue in
                guard newValue else { return }
                let values: [CGFloat] = [0, 8, -8, 6, -6, 4, -4, 2, -2, 0]
                let stepDuration = SkyeDuration.normal / Double(values.count)
                for (index, value) in values.enumerated() {
                    DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + stepDuration * Double(index)) {
                        withAnimation(SkyeEasing.standard(duration: stepDuration)) {
                            offset = value
                        }
                    }
                }
                UINotificationFeedbackGenerator().notificationOccurred(.error)
                DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + SkyeDuration.normal) {
                    trigger = false
                }
            }
    }
}
```

#### 2.6.9 Shared Element Transitions

**Contact avatar (list to detail):**
- `matchedGeometryEffect` id: `"contact-avatar-{id}"`
- Properties animated: `width`, `height`, position, `borderRadius`
- List avatar: 44pt x 44pt, `borderRadius.full`
- Detail avatar: 80pt x 80pt, `borderRadius.full`
- Spring: `spring.smooth` (mass:1, damping:22, stiffness:150)

**Property card (list to detail):**
- `matchedGeometryEffect` id: `"property-image-{id}"`
- Properties animated: `width`, `height`, position, `borderRadius`
- List thumbnail: 100% width x 180pt height, `borderRadius.lg` (14pt)
- Detail hero: 100% width x 280pt height, `borderRadius.none` (0pt)
- Spring: `spring.smooth`

**SwiftUI `NavigationTransition` (iOS 18+):**

```swift
// Using the new NavigationTransition API for hero transitions:
NavigationStack {
    ContactListView()
        .navigationDestination(for: Contact.self) { contact in
            ContactDetailView(contact: contact)
                .navigationTransition(.zoom(sourceID: "contact-avatar-\(contact.id)", in: namespace))
        }
}
```

#### 2.6.10 Gesture-Driven Animations

**Interactive modal dismissal:**
- Trigger: drag gesture downward on modal content
- Progress mapping: finger Y translation / 300pt = progress (0.0 to 1.0)
- Modal translation: follows finger Y directly (no spring, direct manipulation)
- Modal scale: interpolate from 1.0 to 0.9 over progress 0.0 to 1.0
- Background scrim opacity: interpolate from `opacity.overlay` to 0.0
- Dismiss threshold: progress > 0.25 (75pt of drag)
- If released above threshold: snap back with `spring.gentle`
- If released below threshold: animate to dismiss with `spring.gentle`
- Rubber-band resistance: when dragging up (above starting position), apply 0.3x dampening factor

**SwiftUI interactive dismiss:** SwiftUI sheets support interactive dismissal natively. For custom implementations:

```swift
struct InteractiveDismissModifier: ViewModifier {
    @Binding var isPresented: Bool
    @GestureState private var dragOffset: CGFloat = 0

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .offset(y: max(0, dragOffset))
            .scaleEffect(1.0 - (dragOffset / 3000))
            .gesture(
                DragGesture()
                    .updating($dragOffset) { value, state, _ in
                        let translation = value.translation.height
                        state = translation > 0
                            ? translation
                            : translation * 0.3 // rubber-band resistance
                    }
                    .onEnded { value in
                        if value.translation.height > 75 { // 25% of 300pt
                            withAnimation(SkyeSpring.gentle) {
                                isPresented = false
                            }
                        }
                        // else snaps back via @GestureState reset
                    }
            )
            .animation(SkyeSpring.gentle, value: dragOffset)
    }
}
```

**Rubber-band overscroll:**
- Applied automatically by iOS `ScrollView`
- Custom implementation for non-scroll containers: `translation * (1 - translation / (translation + 300))`
- Maximum rubber-band distance: 80pt
- Release: spring back with `spring.stiff`

**Swipe-to-navigate-back (iOS):**
- Uses `NavigationStack` with the system edge-swipe gesture recognizer
- Shared element transitions (via `matchedGeometryEffect` or `.navigationTransition(.zoom(...))`) are progress-based (track finger position)
- Cancel threshold: less than 50% progress when released
- Complete threshold: greater than 50% progress or velocity > 500pt/s

#### 2.6.11 Reduce Motion Alternatives

When the user has enabled "Reduce Motion" in iOS Settings, every animation is replaced. In SwiftUI, access this via `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)`:

| Standard Animation | Reduce Motion Alternative |
|---|---|
| Push/Pop slide transitions | Cross-dissolve fade, `duration.normal` (0.3s) |
| Modal slide-up | Cross-dissolve fade, `duration.normal` |
| Spring bounces | Timing-based with `easing.standard`, no overshoot |
| Shared element transitions | Cross-dissolve fade |
| Skeleton shimmer sweep | Static skeleton with pulsing opacity (0.4 to 0.7) |
| AI shimmer gradient | Static border in `brand.secondary` with opacity pulse |
| Success checkmark draw | Instant full checkmark, no animation |
| Error shake | Red border flash (opacity 0 to 1 to 0), no position change |
| Pull-to-refresh rotation | Static spinner icon with opacity pulse |
| Engagement dot pulse (hot) | No pulse, static dot |
| Button scale press | Opacity change (1.0 to 0.7) instead of scale |

**Implementation:** Create an `adaptiveAnimation()` ViewModifier that reads `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)` and returns the appropriate animation configuration. All animated components must respect this flag.

```swift
struct AdaptiveAnimation: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) var reduceMotion
    let standard: Animation
    let reduced: Animation
    let value: some Equatable

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .animation(reduceMotion ? reduced : standard, value: value)
    }
}

extension View {
    func adaptiveAnimation<V: Equatable>(
        _ standard: Animation,
        reduced: Animation = .easeInOut(duration: SkyeDuration.normal),
        value: V
    ) -> some View {
        modifier(AdaptiveAnimation(standard: standard, reduced: reduced, value: value))
    }
}

// Convenience for checking reduce motion in imperative code:
struct MotionPreference {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) static var reduceMotion

    static func animation(_ standard: Animation) -> Animation {
        // Access reduce motion from view context instead
        standard // See below for view-based approach
    }
}

// View-based approach (preferred):
struct AnimatedView: View {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) var reduceMotion
    @State private var isActive = false

    var body: some View {
        Circle()
            .scaleEffect(isActive ? 1.15 : 1.0)
            .animation(
                reduceMotion
                    ? nil // no animation
                    : SkyeSpring.bouncy.repeatForever(autoreverses: true),
                value: isActive
            )
    }
}
```

---

### 2.7 Dark Mode Design Philosophy

#### 2.7.1 Core Principles

Skye's dark mode is not a color-inverted light mode. It is a purpose-built visual system designed for:

1. **OLED power efficiency** -- True black (`#000000`) canvas background saves significant battery on OLED displays (iPhone X and later).
2. **Reduced eye strain** -- Dark surfaces with carefully calibrated contrast reduce eye fatigue during evening and nighttime use (common for agents reviewing contacts after showings).
3. **Maintained hierarchy** -- The four-tier background system (canvas, surface, elevated, overlay) uses luminance steps that preserve the same depth hierarchy as light mode.
4. **Content prominence** -- Property photos, agent headshots, and AI-generated content are the stars. Dark chrome recedes, making content pop.

#### 2.7.2 Material Hierarchy (Dark Mode)

Skye maps to Apple's material system for blur/vibrancy effects:

| Material | Blur Radius | Background Alpha (Dark) | SwiftUI Equivalent | Usage in Skye |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Ultra Thin Material | 5pt | 0.40 | `.ultraThinMaterial` | Floating labels over property images |
| Thin Material | 10pt | 0.60 | `.thinMaterial` | Tab bar, navigation bar |
| Regular Material | 20pt | 0.72 | `.regularMaterial` | Bottom sheets, action sheets |
| Thick Material | 30pt | 0.85 | `.thickMaterial` | Full-screen modals, alerts |
| Chrome Material | 15pt | 0.80 | `.bar` | Toolbar backgrounds, segmented controls on bar |

In dark mode, the material tint color shifts from white-based to a warm gray (`#1E1E1E`) to avoid a cold, clinical feel. This matches Apple's own dark mode materials.

**iOS 26+ Liquid Glass:** On iOS 26+, `.thinMaterial` and `.regularMaterial` on navigation bars and tab bars should be replaced with `.glassBackgroundEffect()` to adopt the system Liquid Glass appearance. SwiftUI views that use the standard `NavigationStack` and `TabView` will automatically receive Liquid Glass treatment on iOS 26+ when the deployment target supports it.

#### 2.7.3 Image Treatment in Dark Mode

Property photos, contact avatars, and any user-uploaded images receive special treatment in dark mode to prevent them from "blowing out" against the dark UI:

| Image Type | Treatment |
|---|---|
| Property photos (full width) | 15% opacity black overlay on top (`rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.15)`), rounded corners maintained |
| Property photos (thumbnail in list) | No overlay (too small to cause blow-out) |
| Contact avatars | No overlay (circular crop + small size limits contrast shock) |
| AI-generated content images (Studio) | 10% opacity black overlay (`rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.10)`) |
| Onboarding/marketing images | Dedicated dark mode image variants (pre-authored for dark backgrounds) |
| Map views | Dark map style (Apple Maps dark or `MKStandardMapConfiguration` with `.dark` color scheme) |
| Empty state illustrations | Separate dark mode illustration set with muted colors and dark-friendly palette |

**Implementation:** Use a SwiftUI overlay modifier that conditionally applies a dimming overlay based on the current color scheme:

```swift
struct DarkModeImageDimmer: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.colorScheme) var colorScheme
    let overlayOpacity: Double

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .overlay(
                colorScheme == .dark
                    ? Color.black.opacity(overlayOpacity)
                    : Color.clear
            )
    }
}

extension View {
    func darkModeDimmed(opacity: Double = SkyeOpacity.imageOverlay) -> some View {
        modifier(DarkModeImageDimmer(overlayOpacity: opacity))
    }
}

// Usage:
AsyncImage(url: property.imageURL) { image in
    image
        .resizable()
        .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fill)
        .darkModeDimmed()
        .clipShape(.rect(cornerRadius: SkyeBorderRadius.lg, style: .continuous))
} placeholder: {
    Rectangle()
        .fill(Color.fill.quaternary)
        .shimmer()
}
```

#### 2.7.4 Shadow Behavior in Dark Mode

As specified in Section 2.5.2, shadows are replaced in dark mode:

- **Elevation via luminance:** Higher-elevation surfaces are literally lighter (higher luminance). `bg.canvas` (#000000) -> `bg.surface` (#1C1C1E) -> `bg.elevated` (#2C2C2E).
- **Luminance edge borders:** A subtle top border (`rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04)` to `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.10)`) simulates the light catching the top edge of an elevated element.
- **No drop shadows:** In dark mode, shadow modifiers should output `.shadow(color: .clear, radius: 0, x: 0, y: 0)`. Shadows on black are invisible and waste GPU resources.

#### 2.7.5 Color Adjustments for Dark Mode

Not all light mode colors can simply be brightened for dark mode. Specific adjustments:

| Element | Light Mode | Dark Mode | Why Different |
|---|---|---|---|
| `brand.primary` | `#0066FF` | `#3D8AFF` | Lighter blue reduces vibration against dark backgrounds |
| `semantic.error` | `#DC3545` | `#FF6B6B` | Desaturated red prevents harsh vibration on black |
| `semantic.success` | `#28A745` | `#34D058` | Brighter green maintains visibility without neon appearance |
| `semantic.warning` | `#E8910D` | `#FFB340` | Brighter amber ensures visibility against dark surfaces |
| Engagement dot (hot) | `#E53935` | `#FF5252` | Slightly brightened for visibility, reduced saturation |
| Card borders | `rgba(60,60,67,0.12)` | `rgba(84,84,88,0.65)` | Higher opacity in dark mode to be visible against dark bg |
| Separator | `rgba(60,60,67,0.29)` | `rgba(84,84,88,0.60)` | Higher opacity for visibility |

**Desaturation rule:** Any fully-saturated color (S > 85% in HSL) used in light mode must be desaturated by 10-15% in dark mode to prevent visual vibration against dark backgrounds. This is why `#DC3545` becomes `#FF6B6B` (softer, warmer red) rather than simply brightness-boosted.

#### 2.7.6 Status Bar Handling

| Mode | Status Bar Style | Background Treatment |
|---|---|---|
| Light mode | `.darkContent` (dark text/icons) | Transparent, content scrolls under |
| Dark mode | `.lightContent` (light text/icons) | Transparent, content scrolls under |
| Over hero image (light mode) | `.lightContent` | Gradient overlay on image: transparent to `rgba(0,0,0,0.30)` top 80pt |
| Over hero image (dark mode) | `.lightContent` | Gradient overlay on image: transparent to `rgba(0,0,0,0.50)` top 80pt |
| During modal presentation | Matches modal appearance | Automatic with SwiftUI sheet presentation |

**Implementation:** SwiftUI manages status bar appearance automatically based on the color scheme. For screens with hero images, use `.toolbarBackground(.hidden, for: .navigationBar)` and overlay a gradient on the image for status bar legibility:

```swift
struct HeroImageView: View {
    @Environment(\.colorScheme) var colorScheme

    var body: some View {
        ZStack(alignment: .top) {
            Image("propertyHero")
                .resizable()
                .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fill)

            // Status bar gradient overlay
            LinearGradient(
                colors: [
                    Color.black.opacity(colorScheme == .dark ? 0.50 : 0.30),
                    Color.clear
                ],
                startPoint: .top,
                endPoint: .bottom
            )
            .frame(height: 80)
        }
        .toolbarBackground(.hidden, for: .navigationBar)
        .preferredColorScheme(nil) // follow system
        .statusBarHidden(false)
    }
}
```

For explicit status bar style control, use `.preferredColorScheme()` or the `Info.plist` key `UIStatusBarStyle`.

#### 2.7.7 Dark Mode Transition

When the user switches between light and dark mode (either via system settings or in-app toggle):

- Transition duration: `duration.normal` (0.3s)
- All color properties cross-fade simultaneously
- No flicker: SwiftUI's `@Environment(\.colorScheme)` handles this automatically with smooth transitions
- Persistent preference: store user preference in `UserDefaults` or `@AppStorage`. Default to `.system` (follow device setting).
- Three modes supported: `.light`, `.dark`, `.system`

```swift
enum AppearanceMode: String, CaseIterable {
    case system
    case light
    case dark

    var colorScheme: ColorScheme? {
        switch self {
        case .system: return nil
        case .light: return .light
        case .dark: return .dark
        }
    }
}

struct SkyeApp: App {
    @AppStorage("appearanceMode") var appearanceMode: String = AppearanceMode.system.rawValue

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView()
                .preferredColorScheme(
                    AppearanceMode(rawValue: appearanceMode)?.colorScheme
                )
                .animation(.easeInOut(duration: SkyeDuration.normal), value: appearanceMode)
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 2.8 Brand Identity Integration

#### 2.8.1 Skye Logo

**Logo concept:** The Skye wordmark uses SF Pro Rounded Bold with custom letter-spacing (+1.2pt tracking). The "S" in Skye features a subtle gradient treatment (from `brand.primary` to `brand.secondary`) that evokes the sky/horizon metaphor.

**Logo mark:** A simplified abstract symbol representing a horizon line with a rising arc -- suggesting skyline, aspiration, and vision. Clean geometric construction with 2pt stroke weight at 24pt size.

**Logo usage specifications:**

| Variant | Usage | Minimum Size |
|---|---|---|
| Logo mark + wordmark (horizontal) | Splash screen, onboarding, marketing materials | 120pt wide |
| Logo mark only | App icon, chat AI avatar, compact header | 24pt |
| Wordmark only | Navigation bar title (Settings > About), legal | 60pt wide |

**Color variations:**

| Context | Logo Colors |
|---|---|
| Light background | `brand.primary` mark + `text.primary` wordmark |
| Dark background | `#FFFFFF` mark + `#FFFFFF` wordmark |
| On brand.primary background | `#FFFFFF` mark + `#FFFFFF` wordmark |
| Monochrome (legal/print) | Single color -- `text.primary` for both |

**Clear space:** Minimum clear space around the logo equals the height of the "k" in "Skye" (cap height) on all four sides.

**SwiftUI logo wordmark implementation:**

```swift
Text("Skye")
    .font(.system(size: 28, weight: .bold, design: .rounded))
    .tracking(1.2)
```

#### 2.8.2 Splash Screen Design

**Static splash (Launch Screen storyboard or `Info.plist` configuration):**
- Background: `bg.canvas` (white in light mode, black in dark mode)
- Center: Skye logo mark at 80pt, `brand.primary` color
- No text on static splash (text renders inconsistently across devices during cold start)

**Animated transition (after app load, before first screen):**
1. **Frame 0-300ms:** Logo mark holds at center, 80pt.
2. **Frame 300-600ms:** Logo mark scales down from 80pt to 28pt using `spring.smooth`, translates to final position (top-left of first screen header or center of tab bar chat icon). Simultaneously, the first screen content fades in from `opacity: 0` to `opacity: 1` using `easing.enter`.
3. **Frame 600-800ms:** First screen fully visible. Logo mark settles into its final resting position (AI chat avatar or app header).

**Reduce Motion alternative:** Logo holds for 500ms, then cross-dissolve to first screen over 300ms. No position animation.

**Implementation:** Use the system Launch Screen storyboard (or `Info.plist` launch screen configuration) for the native static splash. The animated transition is a custom SwiftUI overlay view that renders over the first screen, animates using `matchedGeometryEffect` or explicit state transitions, then removes itself:

```swift
struct SplashTransitionView: View {
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) var reduceMotion
    @State private var isAnimationComplete = false
    @State private var logoScale: CGFloat = 1.0
    @State private var logoOffset: CGSize = .zero
    @State private var contentOpacity: Double = 0
    @Namespace private var splashNamespace

    var body: some View {
        ZStack {
            // Main content underneath
            MainTabView()
                .opacity(contentOpacity)

            // Splash overlay
            if !isAnimationComplete {
                Color.bg.canvas
                    .overlay(
                        Image("skye-logo-mark")
                            .resizable()
                            .frame(width: 80 * logoScale, height: 80 * logoScale)
                            .offset(logoOffset)
                    )
                    .ignoresSafeArea()
            }
        }
        .onAppear {
            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.3) {
                if reduceMotion {
                    withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: SkyeDuration.normal)) {
                        contentOpacity = 1.0
                        isAnimationComplete = true
                    }
                } else {
                    withAnimation(SkyeSpring.smooth) {
                        logoScale = 28.0 / 80.0
                        // logoOffset calculated to final position
                    }
                    withAnimation(SkyeEasing.enter(duration: SkyeDuration.normal)) {
                        contentOpacity = 1.0
                    }
                    DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.5) {
                        isAnimationComplete = true
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 2.8.3 App Icon Specifications

**Source artwork:** 1024x1024px PNG, no transparency, no rounded corners (iOS applies the mask automatically).

**Design description:**
- Background: Linear gradient from `#0052CC` (top-left) to `#6E42D2` (bottom-right)
- Foreground: White Skye logo mark, centered, sized at approximately 60% of the icon area
- No text in the icon (illegible at small sizes)
- Subtle inner glow effect at the top to add dimension (10% white overlay, feathered 200px)

**Required sizes (iOS):**

| Size (px) | Scale | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| 1024x1024 | 1x | App Store listing |
| 180x180 | 3x | iPhone home screen (60pt @3x) |
| 120x120 | 2x | iPhone home screen (60pt @2x) |
| 167x167 | 2x | iPad Pro home screen (83.5pt @2x) |
| 152x152 | 2x | iPad home screen (76pt @2x) |
| 120x120 | 3x | iPhone Spotlight (40pt @3x) |
| 80x80 | 2x | iPhone Spotlight (40pt @2x) |
| 87x87 | 3x | iPhone Settings (29pt @3x) |
| 58x58 | 2x | iPhone Settings (29pt @2x) |
| 60x60 | 3x | iPhone Notification (20pt @3x) |
| 40x40 | 2x | iPhone Notification (20pt @2x) |

**Note:** As of Xcode 15+, a single 1024x1024 image in the asset catalog is sufficient; Xcode will automatically generate all required sizes.

**Testing:** The icon must be visually tested at all sizes, especially 58x58 (Settings) and 40x40 (Notification) to ensure the logo mark remains legible.

#### 2.8.4 Empty State Illustration Style Guide

Empty states occur on: empty contact list, empty property list, empty chat (first launch), no search results, offline state, error state.

**Illustration style:**
- **Technique:** Flat vector illustrations with limited color palette
- **Color palette:** Maximum 4 colors per illustration: `brand.primary`, `brand.secondary`, `fill.secondary` (for gray elements), and one semantic color if contextually appropriate
- **Line weight:** 2pt strokes, matching the SF Symbols regular weight visual density
- **Dimensions:** 200pt x 160pt maximum bounding box (centered in available space)
- **Character style:** No human figures (avoids cultural/demographic assumptions). Use abstract shapes, buildings, chat bubbles, stars, and property-related objects.
- **Dark mode variants:** Each illustration has a dedicated dark mode version with colors adjusted per Section 2.7.3 -- muted palette, reduced brightness, warm undertones.

**Empty state layout:**
1. Illustration (centered, 200x160pt max)
2. Gap: `spacing.xl` (24pt)
3. Title: `type.title3` (20pt, Semibold), `text.primary`, centered
4. Gap: `spacing.sm` (8pt)
5. Description: `type.body` (17pt, Regular), `text.secondary`, centered, max 2 lines
6. Gap: `spacing.lg` (16pt)
7. CTA button (if applicable): primary button style, centered

**SwiftUI `ContentUnavailableView` (iOS 17+):**

```swift
// Leverage the system ContentUnavailableView for semantic empty states:
ContentUnavailableView {
    Label("No Contacts", systemImage: "person.2")
} description: {
    Text("Add your first contact to get started with Skye.")
} actions: {
    Button("Add Contact") {
        // action
    }
    .buttonStyle(.borderedProminent)
    .tint(Color.brand.primary)
}

// For custom styled empty states matching the spec:
struct SkyeEmptyStateView: View {
    let illustration: Image
    let title: String
    let description: String
    let ctaTitle: String?
    let ctaAction: (() -> Void)?

    var body: some View {
        VStack(spacing: 0) {
            illustration
                .resizable()
                .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fit)
                .frame(maxWidth: 200, maxHeight: 160)

            Spacer().frame(height: SkyeSpacing.xl)

            Text(title)
                .font(.system(.title3, weight: .semibold))
                .foregroundStyle(Color.text.primary)
                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)

            Spacer().frame(height: SkyeSpacing.sm)

            Text(description)
                .font(.body)
                .foregroundStyle(Color.text.secondary)
                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)
                .lineLimit(2)

            if let ctaTitle, let ctaAction {
                Spacer().frame(height: SkyeSpacing.lg)

                Button(ctaTitle, action: ctaAction)
                    .buttonStyle(.borderedProminent)
                    .tint(Color.brand.primary)
                    .controlSize(.large)
            }
        }
        .padding(.horizontal, SkyeSpacing.lg)
    }
}
```

#### 2.8.5 Onboarding Visual Style

**Screen count:** 3 onboarding screens maximum (research shows completion rate drops after 3).

**Visual specifications per screen:**
- Hero illustration: 280pt x 220pt, centered in top 60% of screen
- Illustration style: matches empty state style guide (Section 2.8.4) but larger and more detailed
- Background: `bg.canvas` with optional subtle gradient overlay (`gradient.premium` at 5% opacity)
- Title: `type.title1` (28pt, Bold), `text.primary`, center-aligned
- Subtitle: `type.body` (17pt, Regular), `text.secondary`, center-aligned, max 3 lines
- Page indicator: 3 dots, 8pt diameter, `spacing.sm` (8pt) gap between dots
  - Active: `brand.primary`, scale 1.0
  - Inactive: `fill.secondary`, scale 1.0
  - Transition: dot color cross-fades over `duration.fast` (0.2s)
- CTA button: full-width, 50pt height, `brand.primary` background, `text.onPrimary` label, `borderRadius.md` (10pt)
- Skip button: `type.body` (17pt, Regular), `text.brand` color, top-right corner

**Transition between onboarding screens:**
- Swipe gesture-driven, follows finger position
- Page indicator updates in sync with gesture progress
- Spring: `spring.responsive` on release

**SwiftUI implementation:**

```swift
struct OnboardingView: View {
    @State private var currentPage = 0

    var body: some View {
        ZStack(alignment: .topTrailing) {
            TabView(selection: $currentPage) {
                ForEach(0..<3) { index in
                    OnboardingPageView(page: onboardingPages[index])
                        .tag(index)
                }
            }
            .tabViewStyle(.page(indexDisplayMode: .never))
            .animation(SkyeSpring.responsive, value: currentPage)

            // Skip button
            Button("Skip") {
                // dismiss onboarding
            }
            .font(.body)
            .foregroundStyle(Color.text.brand)
            .padding(.trailing, SkyeSpacing.lg)
            .padding(.top, SkyeSpacing.lg)

            // Custom page indicator + CTA at bottom
            VStack(spacing: SkyeSpacing.lg) {
                HStack(spacing: SkyeSpacing.sm) {
                    ForEach(0..<3) { index in
                        Circle()
                            .fill(index == currentPage ? Color.brand.primary : Color.fill.secondary)
                            .frame(width: 8, height: 8)
                            .animation(.easeInOut(duration: SkyeDuration.fast), value: currentPage)
                    }
                }

                Button("Get Started") {
                    // complete onboarding
                }
                .font(.body.weight(.semibold))
                .foregroundStyle(Color.text.onPrimary)
                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
                .frame(height: 50)
                .background(Color.brand.primary, in: .rect(cornerRadius: SkyeBorderRadius.md, style: .continuous))
                .padding(.horizontal, SkyeSpacing.lg)
            }
            .frame(maxHeight: .infinity, alignment: .bottom)
            .padding(.bottom, SkyeSpacing.xxl)
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 2.9 Design Token Implementation Guide

#### 2.9.1 Token File Structure

```
Sources/
  SkyeUI/
    Theme/
      Tokens/
        SkyeColors.swift          # All color primitives and semantic tokens as Color extensions
        SkyeTypography.swift      # Type scale, font weights, font families
        SkyeSpacing.swift         # Spacing tokens and layout constants
        SkyeBorderRadius.swift    # Corner radius scale
        SkyeShadows.swift         # Shadow/elevation definitions (light + dark)
        SkyeAnimation.swift       # Duration, easing, spring configurations
        SkyeBlur.swift            # Material/blur specifications
        SkyeIcons.swift           # Icon size and weight mappings
      SkyeTheme.swift             # Unified theme export
      SkyeThemeEnvironment.swift  # EnvironmentKey + EnvironmentValues for theme
      ReducedMotionModifier.swift # Accessibility motion preference utilities
```

#### 2.9.2 Color Token Resolution

```swift
// Color extensions with automatic light/dark resolution
extension Color {
    enum Brand {
        static let primary = Color("brand.primary") // Asset catalog with light/dark variants
        static let primaryHover = Color("brand.primaryHover")
        static let primaryMuted = Color("brand.primaryMuted")
        static let secondary = Color("brand.secondary")
        static let secondaryMuted = Color("brand.secondaryMuted")
    }

    enum Background {
        static let canvas = Color("bg.canvas")
        static let surface = Color("bg.surface")
        static let elevated = Color("bg.elevated")
        static let overlay = Color("bg.overlay")
        static let sunken = Color("bg.sunken")
    }

    enum TextColor {
        static let primary = Color("text.primary")
        static let secondary = Color("text.secondary")
        static let tertiary = Color("text.tertiary")
        static let quaternary = Color("text.quaternary")
        static let inverse = Color("text.inverse")
        static let brand = Color("text.brand")
        static let onPrimary = Color("text.onPrimary")
    }
}

// Alternative: programmatic color resolution without asset catalog
extension Color {
    enum Brand {
        static var primary: Color {
            Color(light: Color(hex: "#0066FF"), dark: Color(hex: "#3D8AFF"))
        }
        static var secondary: Color {
            Color(light: Color(hex: "#6E42D2"), dark: Color(hex: "#9B7AE8"))
        }
    }

    enum Background {
        static var canvas: Color {
            Color(light: Color(hex: "#F2F2F7"), dark: Color(hex: "#000000"))
        }
        static var surface: Color {
            Color(light: Color(hex: "#FFFFFF"), dark: Color(hex: "#1C1C1E"))
        }
        static var elevated: Color {
            Color(light: Color(hex: "#FFFFFF"), dark: Color(hex: "#2C2C2E"))
        }
    }
}

// Helper initializer for light/dark adaptive colors
extension Color {
    init(light: Color, dark: Color) {
        self.init(uiColor: UIColor { traitCollection in
            traitCollection.userInterfaceStyle == .dark
                ? UIColor(dark)
                : UIColor(light)
        })
    }
}

// Hex initializer convenience
extension Color {
    init(hex: String) {
        let hex = hex.trimmingCharacters(in: CharacterSet.alphanumerics.inverted)
        var int: UInt64 = 0
        Scanner(string: hex).scanHexInt64(&int)
        let a, r, g, b: UInt64
        switch hex.count {
        case 6:
            (a, r, g, b) = (255, int >> 16, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
        case 8:
            (a, r, g, b) = (int >> 24, int >> 16 & 0xFF, int >> 8 & 0xFF, int & 0xFF)
        default:
            (a, r, g, b) = (255, 0, 0, 0)
        }
        self.init(
            .sRGB,
            red: Double(r) / 255,
            green: Double(g) / 255,
            blue: Double(b) / 255,
            opacity: Double(a) / 255
        )
    }
}

// Usage in views:
// Text("Hello")
//     .foregroundStyle(Color.TextColor.primary)
// Rectangle()
//     .fill(Color.Background.surface)
```

#### 2.9.3 Accessibility Audit Checklist

Every new screen and component must pass these checks before merge:

- All `text.primary` on `bg.surface` combinations meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA)
- All `text.secondary` on `bg.surface` combinations meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- All `text.primary` on `bg.canvas` combinations meet 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- All large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold) on all backgrounds meets 3:1 contrast ratio
- All interactive icon colors on their backgrounds meet 3:1 contrast ratio
- All engagement health dot colors are paired with text labels for VoiceOver
- All animations have Reduce Motion alternatives implemented (using `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)`)
- All text uses Dynamic Type text styles (`.font(.body)`, `.font(.headline)`, etc. -- not hardcoded sizes)
- All interactive elements have minimum 44x44pt touch targets (`.frame(minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44)` + `.contentShape(Rectangle())`)
- Dark mode has been tested on actual OLED device (not just simulator)
- Images in dark mode have appropriate overlay treatment
- Status bar style is correct in both light and dark modes
- VoiceOver audit: all interactive elements have `.accessibilityLabel()` and appropriate `.accessibilityTraits()`

---

### 2.10 Quick Reference: Token Summary

**Color Tokens (35 tokens):**
`brand.primary`, `brand.primaryHover`, `brand.primaryMuted`, `brand.secondary`, `brand.secondaryMuted`, `semantic.success`, `semantic.warning`, `semantic.error`, `semantic.info`, `bg.canvas`, `bg.surface`, `bg.elevated`, `bg.overlay`, `bg.sunken`, `text.primary`, `text.secondary`, `text.tertiary`, `text.quaternary`, `text.inverse`, `text.brand`, `text.onPrimary`, `engagement.hot`, `engagement.warm`, `engagement.cool`, `engagement.cold`, `engagement.inactive`, `fill.primary`, `fill.secondary`, `fill.tertiary`, `fill.quaternary`, `border.default`, `border.focused`, `border.error`, `separator.default`, `separator.opaque`

`type.largeTitle`, `type.title1`, `type.title2`, `type.title3`, `type.headline`, `type.body`, `type.bodyEmphasized`, `type.callout`, `type.subheadline`, `type.subheadlineEmphasized`, `type.footnote`, `type.footnoteEmphasized`, `type.caption1`, `type.caption1Emphasized`, `type.caption2`, `type.code`, `type.codeBlock`

**Spacing Tokens (9 tokens):**
`spacing.xxs` (2), `spacing.xs` (4), `spacing.sm` (8), `spacing.md` (12), `spacing.lg` (16), `spacing.xl` (24), `spacing.xxl` (32), `spacing.xxxl` (48), `spacing.xxxxl` (64)

`borderRadius.none` (0), `borderRadius.xs` (4), `borderRadius.sm` (6), `borderRadius.md` (10), `borderRadius.lg` (14), `borderRadius.xl` (20), `borderRadius.xxl` (24), `borderRadius.full` (9999)

**Shadow Tokens (6 tokens):**
`shadow.none`, `shadow.xs`, `shadow.sm`, `shadow.md`, `shadow.lg`, `shadow.xl`

**Animation Tokens (18 tokens):**
`duration.instant` (0.1s), `duration.fast` (0.2s), `duration.normal` (0.3s), `duration.slow` (0.4s), `duration.dramatic` (0.5s), `duration.skeleton` (1.5s), `duration.aiShimmer` (2.0s), `easing.standard`, `easing.enter`, `easing.exit`, `easing.emphasized`, `easing.linear`, `spring.snappy`, `spring.responsive`, `spring.smooth`, `spring.gentle`, `spring.bouncy`, `spring.stiff`

When in doubt, refer to this document. When this document is silent, refer to the Apple Human Interface Guidelines.*

# 3. Chat Engine — Swift / SwiftUI

---

## 2.1 Streaming Engine Architecture

### 2.1.1 SSE Connection State Machine

The streaming engine operates as a deterministic finite state machine with six states. Every SSE interaction follows this lifecycle exactly.

```
States:
  IDLE          -> No active connection. Default state on appear and after graceful close.
  CONNECTING    -> POST request initiated to /api/chat. Awaiting first byte.
  STREAMING     -> First SSE event received. Tokens flowing and rendering.
  COMPLETING    -> Final SSE event received ([DONE] sentinel). Flushing remaining buffer.
  ERROR         -> Connection failed or stream interrupted. Triggers reconnection protocol.
  CLOSED        -> Connection explicitly torn down. Terminal state before returning to IDLE.

Transitions:
  IDLE -> CONNECTING          User taps send or retry.
  CONNECTING -> STREAMING     First SSE data event arrives (within 10s timeout).
  CONNECTING -> ERROR         Timeout (10s), network failure, or HTTP error (4xx/5xx).
  STREAMING -> COMPLETING     Server sends [DONE] event or SSE stream closes cleanly.
  STREAMING -> ERROR          Connection drops, malformed event, heartbeat timeout (15s).
  COMPLETING -> CLOSED        Final buffer flush complete, response persisted to local store.
  CLOSED -> IDLE              Cleanup complete, ready for next request.
  ERROR -> CONNECTING         Auto-reconnect triggered (within retry budget).
  ERROR -> IDLE               Retry budget exhausted. Manual retry required.
```

**Implementation:** Store the state in an `@Observable` class (not a `@State` on the view) to avoid unnecessary view re-renders on high-frequency transitions. Expose a derived `streamingStatus` as a `@Published` property throttled via `MainActor` to update at most once per display frame using a `CADisplayLink`-driven approach.

```swift
@Observable
final class StreamEngine {
    enum State: Sendable {
        case idle, connecting, streaming, completing, error, closed
    }

    /// Internal state — mutated at network frequency, not directly observed by views.
    private(set) var internalState: State = .idle

    /// Derived status for UI consumption — throttled to once per frame.
    var streamingStatus: State { internalState }
}
```

### 2.1.2 SSE Connection Protocol

```
Request:
  POST /api/chat
  Headers:
    Content-Type: application/json
    Accept: text/event-stream
    Authorization: Bearer {accessToken}
    X-Chat-Session-Id: {sessionId}
    X-Client-Message-Id: {uuidv4}  // Idempotency key for deduplication
  Body:
    {
      "message": string,
      "sessionId": string,
      "attachments": Attachment[] | null
    }

Response Stream (SSE format):
  event: token
  data: {"content": "Hello", "index": 0}

  event: token
  data: {"content": " there", "index": 1}

  event: tool_call_start
  data: {"toolCallId": "tc_001", "toolName": "lookupContact", "args": {"name": "Marcus Johnson"}}

  event: tool_call_result
  data: {"toolCallId": "tc_001", "result": {ContactObject}, "status": "success"}

  event: token
  data: {"content": "I found Marcus...", "index": 47}

  event: done
  data: {"messageId": "msg_xyz", "usage": {"promptTokens": 340, "completionTokens": 289}}
```

### 2.1.3 Chunked Response Parsing and Token Buffering

Tokens arrive as individual SSE `data:` lines. The parser operates as follows:

1. **Line accumulator:** An internal `String` buffer accumulates bytes from `URLSession.AsyncBytes` until a double newline (`\n\n`) is detected, signaling a complete SSE event.
2. **Event parser:** Each complete event is split into `event:` and `data:` fields. The `data:` field is parsed as JSON via `JSONDecoder`. If JSON parsing fails, the raw string is treated as a text token (defensive fallback).
3. **Token buffer:** Parsed tokens are appended to a `tokenBuffer` array (a class property on the stream engine actor). A `CADisplayLink`-driven loop drains the buffer and appends tokens to the displayed message string. This decouples network I/O frequency from render frequency.
4. **Render batching:** The display-link loop concatenates all buffered tokens into a single string append per frame. At 60fps (or 120fps on ProMotion), this means the UI updates at most every 16.67ms (or 8.33ms), regardless of how rapidly tokens arrive. Typical LLM output (40-80 tokens/second) results in 1-2 tokens per frame.
5. **Flush on complete:** When the `done` event arrives, the buffer is immediately flushed (bypassing display-link scheduling) to ensure the complete response is visible without a frame delay.

```swift
/// Complete SSE parser using URLSession AsyncBytes
final class SSEParser {
    private var lineBuffer = ""

    /// Yields parsed SSE events from a URLSession byte stream.
    func events(from bytes: URLSession.AsyncBytes) -> AsyncThrowingStream<SSEEvent, Error> {
        AsyncThrowingStream { continuation in
            let task = Task {
                var currentEvent = ""
                var currentData = ""

                for try await line in bytes.lines {
                    if Task.isCancelled { break }

                    if line.isEmpty {
                        // Double newline: end of SSE event
                        if !currentData.isEmpty {
                            let event = SSEEvent(
                                type: currentEvent.isEmpty ? "message" : currentEvent,
                                data: currentData
                            )
                            continuation.yield(event)
                        }
                        currentEvent = ""
                        currentData = ""
                        continue
                    }

                    if line.hasPrefix("event:") {
                        currentEvent = String(line.dropFirst(6)).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
                    } else if line.hasPrefix("data:") {
                        let value = String(line.dropFirst(5)).trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespaces)
                        currentData += currentData.isEmpty ? value : "\n" + value
                    }
                    // Ignore "id:", "retry:", and comment lines (starting with ":")
                }
                continuation.finish()
            }
            continuation.onTermination = { _ in task.cancel() }
        }
    }
}

struct SSEEvent: Sendable {
    let type: String
    let data: String
}
```

```swift
/// Token buffer with CADisplayLink-driven drain
@Observable
@MainActor
final class StreamingMessageBuffer {
    private var tokenBuffer: [String] = []
    private(set) var displayText: String = ""
    private var displayLink: CADisplayLink?

    func appendToken(_ token: String) {
        tokenBuffer.append(token)
    }

    func startDraining() {
        guard displayLink == nil else { return }
        let link = CADisplayLink(target: self, selector: #selector(drainBuffer))
        link.add(to: .main, forMode: .common)
        displayLink = link
    }

    func stopDraining() {
        displayLink?.invalidate()
        displayLink = nil
    }

    func flushImmediately() {
        stopDraining()
        if !tokenBuffer.isEmpty {
            let batch = tokenBuffer
            tokenBuffer.removeAll()
            displayText += batch.joined()
        }
    }

    @objc private func drainBuffer() {
        guard !tokenBuffer.isEmpty else { return }
        let batch = tokenBuffer
        tokenBuffer.removeAll()
        displayText += batch.joined()
    }
}
```

### 2.1.4 Reconnection Protocol

When a connection drops during streaming, the following protocol activates:

| Parameter | Value |
|---|---|
| Detection method | Heartbeat timeout: server sends `:heartbeat\n\n` every 10s. If no event (data or heartbeat) received for 15s, connection is considered dead. |
| Base delay | 1,000ms |
| Backoff formula | `min(baseDelay * 2^attempt + jitter, maxDelay)` |
| Jitter | Random 0-500ms added to each delay to prevent thundering herd |
| Max delay | 30,000ms |
| Max automatic retries | 3 |
| After max retries | Transition to IDLE. Display inline error: "Connection lost. Tap to retry." with manual retry button. |
| Reconnection request | Includes `X-Resume-After-Token: {lastReceivedTokenIndex}` header so the server can resume from the correct position. If server does not support resumption, full response is re-fetched and diffed against local buffer. |

**Backoff sequence:** 1s -> 2s -> 4s (plus jitter), then manual retry.

```swift
struct ReconnectionPolicy {
    let baseDelay: TimeInterval = 1.0
    let maxDelay: TimeInterval = 30.0
    let maxRetries = 3

    func delay(forAttempt attempt: Int) -> TimeInterval {
        let exponential = baseDelay * pow(2.0, Double(attempt))
        let jitter = Double.random(in: 0...0.5)
        return min(exponential + jitter, maxDelay)
    }
}
```

### 2.1.5 Partial Response Preservation

If the stream drops after receiving N tokens, those N tokens remain permanently visible in the chat. The message is marked with a `partial: true` flag in local state.

- **Visual treatment:** The partial message renders normally with a subtle footer indicator: "Response interrupted" in muted text with a "Retry" link.
- **On retry:** A new SSE connection is opened. If the server supports resumption (via `X-Resume-After-Token`), new tokens are appended. If not, the AI regenerates the full response -- the previous partial is replaced with the new complete response using a crossfade animation (200ms).
- **Persistence:** Partial messages are saved to local storage (SwiftData or UserDefaults) with `status: .partial`. On app relaunch, they display with the interrupted indicator.

### 2.1.6 Concurrent Request Handling

**Rule: Only one active SSE stream per chat session at any time.**

If the user sends a new message while a previous response is still streaming:

1. The current SSE streaming `Task` is cancelled via structured concurrency (`task.cancel()`).
2. The partial AI response is preserved in the message list with `status: .partial` and no interrupted indicator (it was intentionally cut short).
3. The new user message is appended to the message list.
4. A new SSE connection is established for the new message.
5. The send button is disabled (grayed, non-interactive) while the AI is actively streaming. The input field remains editable so the user can compose their next message, but they cannot send until streaming completes or they explicitly cancel the current stream via a "Stop generating" button that replaces the send button during streaming.

```swift
@Observable
final class ChatViewModel {
    private var activeStreamTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    func sendMessage(_ text: String) {
        // Cancel any existing stream
        activeStreamTask?.cancel()

        // Preserve partial response if needed
        if let lastAI = messages.last(where: { $0.type == .aiText && $0.isStreaming }) {
            lastAI.status = .partial
            lastAI.isStreaming = false
        }

        // Start new stream
        activeStreamTask = Task {
            await startSSEStream(for: text)
        }
    }

    func stopGenerating() {
        activeStreamTask?.cancel()
        activeStreamTask = nil
    }
}
```

### 2.1.7 Background Behavior (iOS)

iOS grants approximately 30 seconds of background execution time after the app moves to the background.

| App State | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Active -> Background** | Stream continues for the iOS background execution window (~30s). Register a background task via `UIApplication.shared.beginBackgroundTask(expirationHandler:)`. |
| **Background, stream completes within 30s** | Response is persisted to local storage. A local notification is posted: "Skye has a response ready." |
| **Background, 30s elapses, stream still active** | Gracefully close the SSE connection. Persist the partial response with `status: .partial`. When app returns to foreground, display partial + "Response interrupted" indicator with retry. |
| **Background -> Foreground** | Check `streamEngine.state`. If `.idle` with a pending partial, show retry UI. If `.idle` with completed response, no action needed -- the response is already in the list. If somehow still `.streaming` (within 30s window), continue rendering as normal. |

Listen for scene phase changes via SwiftUI's `@Environment(\.scenePhase)`:

```swift
struct ChatView: View {
    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase
    @State private var viewModel: ChatViewModel

    var body: some View {
        MessageListView(messages: viewModel.messages)
            .onChange(of: scenePhase) { oldPhase, newPhase in
                switch newPhase {
                case .active:
                    if viewModel.streamEngine.internalState == .error {
                        viewModel.attemptReconnection()
                    }
                case .background:
                    viewModel.registerBackgroundTask()
                default:
                    break
                }
            }
    }
}
```

```swift
extension ChatViewModel {
    func registerBackgroundTask() {
        var backgroundTaskID: UIBackgroundTaskIdentifier = .invalid
        backgroundTaskID = UIApplication.shared.beginBackgroundTask(
            withName: "SSEStreamCompletion"
        ) {
            // Expiration handler: time is up
            self.activeStreamTask?.cancel()
            self.persistPartialResponse()
            UIApplication.shared.endBackgroundTask(backgroundTaskID)
            backgroundTaskID = .invalid
        }

        // When stream completes naturally in background
        Task {
            await waitForStreamCompletion()
            if backgroundTaskID != .invalid {
                scheduleLocalNotification(body: "Skye has a response ready.")
                UIApplication.shared.endBackgroundTask(backgroundTaskID)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.1.8 Network Transition Handling

| Transition | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **WiFi -> Cellular (mid-stream)** | The underlying TCP connection will break. The `URLSession` stream errors out. The reconnection protocol (Section 2.1.4) activates immediately. Because cellular is now available, the first reconnection attempt (1s delay) typically succeeds. Partial tokens are preserved. |
| **Cellular -> WiFi (mid-stream)** | Same behavior -- TCP connection breaks, reconnection protocol activates. WiFi is typically faster, so reconnection succeeds on first attempt. |
| **Any -> Offline** | `NWPathMonitor` listener detects offline state. Reconnection attempts are paused (no point retrying with no network). A banner appears at the top of the chat: "No internet connection." When connectivity is restored (`NWPathMonitor` fires), reconnection resumes automatically. |
| **Implementation** | Use `NWPathMonitor` from the `Network` framework to subscribe to connectivity changes. Combine with the SSE state machine: if `path.status == .unsatisfied`, transition to a `WAITING_FOR_NETWORK` sub-state that bypasses the retry counter. |

```swift
import Network

@Observable
final class NetworkMonitor {
    private let monitor = NWPathMonitor()
    private(set) var isConnected = true

    init() {
        monitor.pathUpdateHandler = { [weak self] path in
            Task { @MainActor in
                self?.isConnected = (path.status == .satisfied)
            }
        }
        monitor.start(queue: DispatchQueue(label: "NetworkMonitor"))
    }

    deinit {
        monitor.cancel()
    }
}
```

### 2.1.9 Token-by-Token Rendering Performance

**Target: 60fps (16.67ms per frame) during rapid token delivery.**

Strategies:

1. **Isolate re-renders:** Only the actively-streaming message view re-renders on each token batch. All other messages in the list use `EquatableView` wrappers or rely on SwiftUI's identity-based diffing with stable `id` values so they do not re-render.
2. **DisplayLink-batched updates:** As described in Section 2.1.3, tokens are batched per display frame. The string concatenation happens outside SwiftUI's render cycle (in the buffer class), and a single property update per frame triggers the re-render.
3. **Avoid layout thrashing:** The streaming message uses a fixed-width container. As text grows, only the height changes. Use `withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.1))` on height changes or rely on SwiftUI's built-in layout animation -- no layout recalculation cascade.
4. **Markdown parsing deferral:** During active streaming, raw text is rendered (no markdown parsing). Markdown is parsed and rendered only after: (a) a 300ms pause in token delivery, or (b) the stream completes. This eliminates the cost of re-parsing growing markdown on every token. During streaming, basic formatting (bold with `**`, inline code with backticks) is applied via regex-based lightweight styling, not full AST parsing.
5. **Off-main-thread markdown parsing:** When full markdown parsing triggers, it runs on a background thread via `Task(priority: .userInitiated)` using `Task.detached` or a dedicated actor -- parse only the delta since the last parse, not the entire string.

### 2.1.10 Memory Management for Long Sessions

**Target: <150MB for 500 messages. Graceful handling of 1000+ messages.**

| Strategy | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Virtualized list** | SwiftUI `ScrollView` with `LazyVStack` for cell-level lazy loading. Only messages in the viewport plus a system-managed buffer above and below are materialized. All other messages exist only as data in the messages array. |
| **Message data pagination** | On initial load, fetch the most recent 50 messages from `/api/chat/history`. When the user scrolls to the top, fetch the next 50 (paginated via cursor). Maximum 200 messages in memory at any time. When a new page is loaded at the top, the oldest page at the bottom is evicted from the rendered array (but the full message IDs are retained for potential re-fetch). |
| **Image memory management** | Tool result card images (property thumbnails, avatars) use `AsyncImage` with a custom caching `URLSession` configuration (aggressive disk caching via `URLCache` with 200MB disk limit, 50MB memory limit). Images not in the viewport are evicted from memory cache. Blurhash placeholders (stored as 20-30 byte strings) are used when images are recycled out and back in (via a lightweight `BlurHashDecode` utility). |
| **Markdown AST caching** | Parsed markdown ASTs are memoized per `messageId` in an `NSCache`-backed store. When a message scrolls out of the lazy stack and back in, the cached AST is reused -- no re-parsing. `NSCache` is configured with a 100-entry `countLimit`. |
| **String interning** | Repeated strings in tool results (status labels, field names) use a shared string pool to reduce memory duplication. |
| **Memory pressure response** | Subscribe to `UIApplication.didReceiveMemoryWarningNotification`. On memory warning, aggressively evict: image caches, markdown AST cache, and reduce prefetch buffers. Log the event for analytics. |

```swift
@Observable
final class MarkdownCache {
    private let cache = NSCache<NSString, MarkdownAST>()

    init() {
        cache.countLimit = 100
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            forName: UIApplication.didReceiveMemoryWarningNotification,
            object: nil,
            queue: .main
        ) { [weak self] _ in
            self?.cache.removeAllObjects()
        }
    }

    func ast(for messageId: String, content: String) -> MarkdownAST {
        let key = messageId as NSString
        if let cached = cache.object(forKey: key) {
            return cached
        }
        let parsed = MarkdownAST(parsing: content)
        cache.setObject(parsed, forKey: key)
        return parsed
    }
}
```

---

## 2.2 Message Type System

Every message conforms to a base type and is rendered by a dedicated view. The message list is a heterogeneous list -- `LazyVStack` with `ForEach` uses each message's `type` property for view identity and conditional rendering, enabling optimal view reuse.

### 2.2.1 Base Message Schema

```swift
enum MessageType: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case userText = "user_text"
    case aiText = "ai_text"
    case aiToolCall = "ai_tool_call"
    case aiToolResult = "ai_tool_result"
    case aiActionConfirmation = "ai_action_confirmation"
    case system = "system"
}

enum MessageStatus: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case sending, sent, partial, failed, delivered, queued
}

protocol BaseMessage: Identifiable, Codable, Sendable {
    var id: String { get }
    var sessionId: String { get }
    var type: MessageType { get }
    var timestamp: Date { get }
    var status: MessageStatus { get set }
}
```

### 2.2.2 User Text Message

```swift
struct UserTextMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .userText
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var content: String
    var attachments: [Attachment]?
}

struct Attachment: Codable, Identifiable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let uri: URL
    let mimeType: AttachmentMIME
    let thumbnailUri: URL
    let width: CGFloat
    let height: CGFloat
    var ocrText: String?

    enum AttachmentMIME: String, Codable, Sendable {
        case jpeg = "image/jpeg"
        case png = "image/png"
        case heic = "image/heic"
    }
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Alignment | Right-aligned (trailing edge) |
| Max width | 78% of screen width |
| Bubble background | `#007AFF` (Skye brand blue) |
| Bubble corner radius | 18pt. Bottom-right corner: 4pt (tail effect for last message in group) |
| Text color | `#FFFFFF` |
| Text font | SF Pro, 16pt, regular weight |
| Horizontal padding (inside bubble) | 14pt left, 14pt right |
| Vertical padding (inside bubble) | 10pt top, 10pt bottom |
| Bubble margin from screen edge | 16pt right |
| Bubble margin from opposite edge | Minimum 54pt left (ensures asymmetry) |
| Avatar | None displayed for user messages |
| Attachment rendering | Images render as rounded rectangles (12pt radius) above the text, max 240pt wide, aspect-ratio preserved, tap to view full-screen. Multiple images arranged in a 2-column grid. |

**Sending states:**

| State | Visual Treatment |
|---|---|
| `sending` | Bubble at 60% opacity. No timestamp yet. Subtle upward slide-in animation (offset y: 20 -> 0, 200ms ease-out via `withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.2))`). |
| `sent` | Full opacity. Timestamp available on tap. |
| `failed` | Bubble background shifts to `#FF3B30` (system red) at 80% opacity. Below the bubble: "Not sent. Tap to retry." in 13pt, `#FF3B30`. Tap retries the send. |

```swift
struct UserBubbleView: View {
    let message: UserTextMessage
    let isLastInGroup: Bool

    var body: some View {
        HStack {
            Spacer(minLength: 54)
            VStack(alignment: .trailing, spacing: 4) {
                if let attachments = message.attachments, !attachments.isEmpty {
                    AttachmentGridView(attachments: attachments)
                }
                Text(message.content)
                    .font(.system(size: 16))
                    .foregroundStyle(.white)
                    .padding(.horizontal, 14)
                    .padding(.vertical, 10)
                    .background(
                        message.status == .failed
                            ? Color(hex: 0xFF3B30).opacity(0.8)
                            : Color(hex: 0x007AFF)
                    )
                    .clipShape(
                        .rect(
                            topLeadingRadius: 18,
                            bottomLeadingRadius: 18,
                            bottomTrailingRadius: isLastInGroup ? 4 : 18,
                            topTrailingRadius: 18
                        )
                    )
                    .opacity(message.status == .sending ? 0.6 : 1.0)

                if message.status == .failed {
                    Text("Not sent. Tap to retry.")
                        .font(.system(size: 13))
                        .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0xFF3B30))
                }
            }
            .padding(.trailing, 16)
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.2.3 AI Text Message

```swift
struct AITextMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .aiText
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var content: String         // Markdown string, grows during streaming
    var isStreaming: Bool        // True while tokens are still arriving
    var modelId: String?        // For display if needed in future
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Alignment | Left-aligned (leading edge) |
| Max width | 85% of screen width |
| Bubble background | `Color(.systemGray6)` (adapts light/dark automatically) |
| Bubble corner radius | 18pt. Bottom-left corner: 4pt (tail effect for last message in group) |
| Text color | `Color(.label)` (adapts light/dark) |
| Text font | SF Pro, 16pt, regular weight. Markdown-derived styles override as needed. |
| Horizontal padding (inside bubble) | 14pt left, 14pt right |
| Vertical padding (inside bubble) | 10pt top, 10pt bottom |
| Bubble margin from screen edge | 16pt left |
| Bubble margin from opposite edge | Minimum 40pt right |
| Avatar | 28pt diameter Skye logo, displayed at bottom-left of the bubble, only for the last message in a consecutive AI group. Vertically aligned with the bottom of the bubble, 8pt to the left of the bubble. |
| Streaming cursor | A blinking vertical bar (`|`) appended to the last character during streaming. Blink cycle: 530ms on, 530ms off. Disappears when streaming completes. |

```swift
struct AIBubbleView: View {
    let message: AITextMessage
    let isLastInGroup: Bool
    let showAvatar: Bool
    @State private var cursorVisible = true

    var body: some View {
        HStack(alignment: .bottom, spacing: 8) {
            if showAvatar {
                Image("skye-logo")
                    .resizable()
                    .frame(width: 28, height: 28)
                    .clipShape(Circle())
            } else {
                Spacer().frame(width: showAvatar ? 36 : 0)
            }

            VStack(alignment: .leading) {
                Group {
                    if message.isStreaming {
                        Text(message.content + (cursorVisible ? "|" : ""))
                            .font(.system(size: 16))
                    } else {
                        // Full markdown via MarkdownUI
                        Markdown(message.content)
                            .markdownTextStyle { FontSize(16) }
                    }
                }
                .foregroundStyle(Color(.label))
                .padding(.horizontal, 14)
                .padding(.vertical, 10)
                .background(Color(.systemGray6))
                .clipShape(
                    .rect(
                        topLeadingRadius: 18,
                        bottomLeadingRadius: isLastInGroup ? 4 : 18,
                        bottomTrailingRadius: 18,
                        topTrailingRadius: 18
                    )
                )
            }
            .frame(maxWidth: UIScreen.main.bounds.width * 0.85, alignment: .leading)

            Spacer(minLength: 40)
        }
        .padding(.leading, 16)
        .onAppear {
            if message.isStreaming {
                withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.53).repeatForever()) {
                    cursorVisible.toggle()
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.2.4 AI Tool Call Message

Displayed when the AI invokes a tool. Appears inline in the message stream as a compact status indicator.

```swift
struct AIToolCallMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .aiToolCall
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var toolCallId: String
    var toolName: String
    var displayLabel: String     // Human-readable: "Looking up contact..."
    var args: [String: AnyCodable]
    var toolStatus: ToolCallStatus

    enum ToolCallStatus: String, Codable, Sendable {
        case running, completed, failed
    }
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Alignment | Left-aligned, same indent as AI text messages |
| Max width | 85% of screen width |
| Background | None (no bubble). Rendered as a single line with icon. |
| Icon | 16pt animated icon left of text. Running: spinning circle (use `.rotationEffect` with `.repeatForever()`). Completed: checkmark, green `#34C759`. Failed: exclamation triangle, red `#FF3B30`. |
| Text | `displayLabel` in 14pt, SF Pro, medium weight, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| Vertical spacing | 4pt above, 4pt below. Compact -- these should feel like status annotations, not full messages. |
| Animation | Slide-in from left (offset x: -10 -> 0, 150ms ease-out) on appearance. Icon cross-fades between states (running -> completed/failed) over 200ms via `.contentTransition(.symbolEffect(.replace))`. |

**Tool name to display label mapping (examples):**

| `toolName` | `displayLabel` |
|---|---|
| `lookupContact` | "Looking up contact..." |
| `searchProperties` | "Searching properties..." |
| `createContact` | "Creating contact..." |
| `generateContent` | "Generating content..." |
| `getMarketAnalysis` | "Analyzing market data..." |
| `scheduleFollowUp` | "Scheduling follow-up..." |

```swift
struct ToolCallStatusView: View {
    let toolCall: AIToolCallMessage
    @State private var appeared = false
    @State private var spinAngle: Double = 0

    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 6) {
            Group {
                switch toolCall.toolStatus {
                case .running:
                    Image(systemName: "circle.dotted")
                        .rotationEffect(.degrees(spinAngle))
                        .onAppear {
                            withAnimation(.linear(duration: 1).repeatForever(autoreverses: false)) {
                                spinAngle = 360
                            }
                        }
                case .completed:
                    Image(systemName: "checkmark.circle.fill")
                        .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0x34C759))
                case .failed:
                    Image(systemName: "exclamationmark.triangle.fill")
                        .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0xFF3B30))
                }
            }
            .font(.system(size: 16))
            .contentTransition(.symbolEffect(.replace))

            Text(toolCall.displayLabel)
                .font(.system(size: 14, weight: .medium))
                .foregroundStyle(Color(.secondaryLabel))
        }
        .padding(.leading, 52) // Align with AI message content
        .padding(.vertical, 4)
        .offset(x: appeared ? 0 : -10)
        .opacity(appeared ? 1 : 0)
        .onAppear {
            withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.15)) {
                appeared = true
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.2.5 AI Tool Result Message

Displayed after a tool call completes. Renders as a rich, interactive card. The specific card type depends on the tool that was called. See Section 2.4 for detailed card specifications.

```swift
enum ToolResultDisplayType: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case contactCard = "contact_card"
    case propertyCard = "property_card"
    case contentPreview = "content_preview"
    case marketAnalysis = "market_analysis"
    case taskCard = "task_card"
    case generic
}

struct AIToolResultMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .aiToolResult
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var toolCallId: String       // Links to the corresponding tool call
    var toolName: String
    var result: ToolResultPayload
    var displayType: ToolResultDisplayType
    var resultCount: Int         // If >1, renders as carousel
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Alignment | Left-aligned, full width minus 32pt margins (16pt each side) |
| Max width | `screenWidth - 32pt` |
| Card styling | See Section 2.4 for per-card-type specs. All cards share: 12pt corner radius, 1pt border (`Color(.separator)` adapts light/dark), subtle shadow (`.shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.08), radius: 8, x: 0, y: 2)`). |
| Carousel (multiple results) | Horizontal `ScrollView(.horizontal)` with paging via `.scrollTargetBehavior(.paging)`, 8pt gap between cards. Page indicator dots below carousel: 6pt diameter, `#C7C7CC` inactive, `#007AFF` active (custom `PageIndicatorView`). Cards are `screenWidth - 64pt` wide each (peek next card by 32pt). |
| Loading state | Skeleton card: same dimensions as final card, 3 animated shimmer bars (gray gradient sweep, 1.5s loop). Appears immediately when tool call starts. |
| Error state | Card with light red background (`#FFF2F2`), exclamation icon, "Couldn't complete this action" text, and "Retry" button. |

### 2.2.6 AI Action Confirmation Message

Used for destructive or significant actions that require user approval before execution.

```swift
enum ActionStatus: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case pending, confirmed, cancelled, executed
}

struct AIActionConfirmationMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .aiActionConfirmation
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var prompt: String           // "I'll create this contact with the following details:"
    var previewData: [String: AnyCodable]
    var confirmLabel: String     // "Create Contact"
    var cancelLabel: String      // "Cancel"
    var actionStatus: ActionStatus
    var toolName: String
    var toolArgs: [String: AnyCodable]
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Alignment | Left-aligned |
| Max width | 85% of screen width |
| Background | White bubble with 1pt `Color(.separator)` border (light) / `Color(.secondarySystemBackground)` with 1pt `Color(.separator)` border (dark) |
| Corner radius | 16pt |
| Prompt text | 15pt, SF Pro, regular, `Color(.label)`. Padding: 14pt all sides. |
| Preview section | Below prompt text. Key-value pairs rendered as: key in 13pt medium `Color(.secondaryLabel)`, value in 15pt regular `Color(.label)`. 8pt vertical gap between pairs. 12pt top padding, separated from prompt by a 1pt `Color(.separator)` divider. |
| Button row | Below preview, separated by 1pt divider. Two buttons side by side. |
| Confirm button | Left button: `confirmLabel` text in 15pt semibold `#007AFF`, centered, 44pt tap target height. Haptic: `.impact(.medium)` on tap. |
| Cancel button | Right button: `cancelLabel` text in 15pt regular `#FF3B30`, centered, 44pt tap target height. |
| Button divider | 1pt vertical `Color(.separator)` line between buttons. |
| Post-action state | After confirm: buttons replaced with "Confirmed" text + checkmark in green. After cancel: buttons replaced with "Cancelled" text in muted gray. Both states are non-interactive and final. |

### 2.2.7 System Message

Used for non-conversational information: date separators, connection status changes, errors.

```swift
enum SystemSubtype: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case dateSeparator = "date_separator"
    case connectionStatus = "connection_status"
    case error
    case sessionStart = "session_start"
}

struct SystemMessage: BaseMessage, Codable, Sendable {
    let id: String
    let sessionId: String
    let type: MessageType = .system
    let timestamp: Date
    var status: MessageStatus
    var subtype: SystemSubtype
    var content: String
}
```

**Layout specification:**

| Subtype | Rendering |
|---|---|
| `dateSeparator` | Centered text: "Today", "Yesterday", or "Mon, Jan 15". Font: 12pt, SF Pro, semibold, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. 20pt vertical margin above and below. No bubble. Optional: subtle horizontal lines extending from text to edges (hairline, `Color(.separator)`). |
| `connectionStatus` | Centered text with icon. "Reconnecting..." with spinning indicator. "Connected" with green dot (auto-dismisses after 2s fade-out). Font: 12pt, SF Pro, medium, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| `error` | Centered text, red-tinted. "Something went wrong. Tap to retry." Font: 12pt, SF Pro, medium, `#FF3B30`. Tap handler for retry. |
| `sessionStart` | Centered: "New conversation" with a subtle horizontal rule. Font: 12pt, SF Pro, medium, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. 24pt vertical margin. |

### 2.2.8 Message Grouping

Consecutive messages from the same sender are visually grouped:

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Avatar display | Only the last message in a consecutive group shows the avatar (AI messages only). |
| Bubble tail | Only the last message in a group has the small corner radius (tail). All other messages in the group have uniform 18pt radius on all corners. |
| Intra-group spacing | 3pt between consecutive messages from the same sender. |
| Inter-group spacing | 12pt between message groups from different senders. |
| Timestamp grouping | If all messages in a group are within 60 seconds of each other, a single timestamp applies to the group (shown on the last message on tap). If messages span >60 seconds, a subtle inline timestamp is shown at the breakpoint. |
| Tool call/result grouping | Tool call status lines and their result cards are grouped with surrounding AI text messages into a single visual unit, with no extra spacing. The entire AI "turn" (text + tool calls + results + more text) is one group. |

---

## 2.3 Markdown Rendering

### 2.3.1 Supported Syntax (Full CommonMark + Extensions)

Use the `MarkdownUI` SPM package (gonzalezreal/swift-markdown-ui) for full CommonMark rendering.

| Element | Syntax | Rendering |
|---|---|---|
| Bold | `**text**` | SF Pro, 16pt, **semibold** weight |
| Italic | `*text*` | SF Pro, 16pt, *italic* style |
| Bold Italic | `***text***` | SF Pro, 16pt, ***semibold italic*** |
| Strikethrough | `~~text~~` | SF Pro, 16pt, ~~line-through~~ decoration |
| Inline code | `` `code` `` | SF Mono, 14pt, background `Color(.systemGray5)`, 3pt horizontal padding, 2pt corner radius |
| Code block | ` ```lang ` | See Section 2.3.2 |
| Links | `[text](url)` | Text in `#007AFF`, underlined on long-press only. Tap opens `SFSafariViewController` (via `BrowserService`). |
| Headers | `# H1` through `### H3` | H1: 22pt bold. H2: 19pt bold. H3: 17pt semibold. H4-H6: 16pt semibold. All have 8pt top margin, 4pt bottom margin. |
| Blockquote | `> text` | 3pt left border in `#C7C7CC`, 12pt left padding, text in `#6C6C70` italic. Nested blockquotes increase left padding by 12pt each level. |
| Unordered list | `- item` | 6pt bullet (`Color(.secondaryLabel)`), 8pt left indent per nesting level, 4pt vertical gap between items. |
| Ordered list | `1. item` | Number in `Color(.secondaryLabel)` followed by period, same spacing as unordered. |
| Horizontal rule | `---` | 1pt line, `Color(.separator)`, full bubble width minus 28pt (14pt margin each side), 12pt vertical margin. |
| Tables | `\| col \| col \|` | See Section 2.3.4 |

### 2.3.2 Code Block Rendering

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| javascript                            [  Copy  ]  |  <- Header bar
+---------------------------------------------------+
| function greetAgent(name) {                        |
|   return `Welcome, ${name}!`;                      |
| }                                                  |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Container background | `#1E1E1E` (always dark, regardless of app theme -- convention from all major chat apps) |
| Container corner radius | 10pt |
| Container margin | 0pt (fills full bubble width minus bubble padding) |
| Header bar | 32pt height, `#2D2D2D` background, contains language label (left) and copy button (right). |
| Language label | 12pt SF Mono, `#A0A0A0`, lowercase. Detected from the code fence language tag. If unspecified, label reads "code". |
| Copy button | "Copy" text in 12pt SF Pro medium `#A0A0A0`. On tap: text changes to "Copied!" with checkmark for 2s, haptic `.notification(.success)`. Copies code content (not the fence markers or language label) to clipboard via `UIPasteboard.general`. |
| Code text | 13pt SF Mono, syntax-highlighted. Colors follow a VS Code Dark+ inspired palette. |
| Horizontal overflow | Code block scrolls horizontally via `ScrollView(.horizontal)`. No wrapping. Scroll indicator visible during scroll. |
| Vertical overflow | No max height -- code blocks expand to show all lines. Very long blocks (>30 lines) show a "Show more" / "Show less" toggle at 15 lines, with smooth expand animation. |
| Syntax highlighting | Use `Splash` SPM package (JohnSundell/Splash) or `HighlightSwift` for native syntax highlighting to `AttributedString`. Support languages: javascript, typescript, python, json, html, css, sql, bash, swift, markdown. Unrecognized languages render without highlighting. |

```swift
struct CodeBlockView: View {
    let language: String
    let code: String
    @State private var copied = false
    @State private var expanded = true

    private var lineCount: Int { code.components(separatedBy: "\n").count }
    private var shouldCollapse: Bool { lineCount > 30 }
    private var displayCode: String {
        if shouldCollapse && !expanded {
            return code.components(separatedBy: "\n").prefix(15).joined(separator: "\n")
        }
        return code
    }

    var body: some View {
        VStack(spacing: 0) {
            // Header bar
            HStack {
                Text(language.isEmpty ? "code" : language.lowercased())
                    .font(.system(size: 12, design: .monospaced))
                    .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0xA0A0A0))
                Spacer()
                Button {
                    UIPasteboard.general.string = code
                    UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light).impactOccurred()
                    copied = true
                    Task {
                        try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(2))
                        copied = false
                    }
                } label: {
                    Text(copied ? "Copied!" : "Copy")
                        .font(.system(size: 12, weight: .medium))
                        .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0xA0A0A0))
                }
            }
            .padding(.horizontal, 12)
            .frame(height: 32)
            .background(Color(hex: 0x2D2D2D))

            // Code content
            ScrollView(.horizontal, showsIndicators: true) {
                Text(highlightedCode)
                    .font(.system(size: 13, design: .monospaced))
                    .padding(12)
            }

            if shouldCollapse {
                Button(expanded ? "Show less" : "Show more") {
                    withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.25)) {
                        expanded.toggle()
                    }
                }
                .font(.system(size: 12, weight: .medium))
                .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0x007AFF))
                .padding(.bottom, 8)
            }
        }
        .background(Color(hex: 0x1E1E1E))
        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 10))
    }

    private var highlightedCode: AttributedString {
        // Use Splash or HighlightSwift for syntax highlighting
