```swift
        // Fallback to plain white text if language not supported
        var attributed = AttributedString(displayCode)
        attributed.foregroundColor = .white
        return attributed
    }
}
```

### 2.3.3 Link Handling

| Interaction | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Tap | Opens URL in `SFSafariViewController` (in-app browser). The chat remains in the background -- user can swipe down to return. If URL is a deep link to an in-app screen (e.g., a contact or property URL matching Skye's scheme), navigate to that screen instead. Use `@Environment(\.openURL)` for scheme detection, `SFSafariViewController` presented via a `UIViewControllerRepresentable` wrapper for web URLs. |
| Long-press (500ms) | Show a native context menu via `.contextMenu { }`: (1) "Open" -- same as tap, (2) "Copy Link" -- copies URL to clipboard with `.notification(.success)` haptic, (3) "Share" -- opens iOS share sheet via `ShareLink` or `UIActivityViewController`. A link preview appears above the menu (iOS provides this natively with `UIContextMenuInteraction` preview providers). |
| URL display | Inline in text, styled as `#007AFF`. No underline by default (matches iOS convention). Underline appears on long-press to indicate the link is being activated. |

### 2.3.4 Table Rendering

When the AI returns tabular data in markdown:

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Container | Horizontally scrollable if table exceeds bubble width via `ScrollView(.horizontal)`. Rounded corners (8pt), 1pt border `Color(.separator)`. |
| Header row | Background `Color(.systemGray6)`. Text: 13pt SF Pro semibold. |
| Body rows | Alternating: `Color(.systemBackground)` / `Color(.secondarySystemBackground)`. Text: 13pt SF Pro regular. |
| Cell padding | 8pt horizontal, 6pt vertical. |
| Column alignment | Auto-detect from markdown alignment syntax (`:---`, `:---:`, `---:`). Default: left-aligned. |
| Max visible rows | 10 rows visible before vertical scroll activates within the table container. |

### 2.3.5 Extensibility Architecture

The markdown renderer is designed as a pipeline with pluggable stages:

```
Raw Markdown String
  -> Parser (swift-markdown by Apple or cmark)
  -> AST (Abstract Syntax Tree / Markup Document)
  -> Transform plugins (optional: LaTeX, custom card embeds, future extensions)
  -> Renderer (AST -> SwiftUI Views via MarkdownUI themes)
  -> Cached view tree
```

Each stage is independently replaceable. To add LaTeX support in the future, add a transform plugin that recognizes `$...$` and `$$...$$` delimiters and produces custom AST nodes, then add a `MarkdownUI` theme block handler for those nodes (likely using a `MathJax` WebView or custom `CoreText` renderer). No existing code changes required.

### 2.3.6 Performance Requirements

| Requirement | Target |
|---|---|
| Parse time for a 500-word message | <10ms on iPhone 12 and newer |
| Incremental parse during streaming | Parse only the new delta (appended tokens), not the full string. Maintain a running AST that is extended, not rebuilt. |
| Thread | Full markdown parsing (for completed messages) runs off the main thread via `Task.detached(priority: .userInitiated)`. During streaming, the lightweight regex-based formatter runs on the main actor (it is <1ms per invocation). |
| Memoization | Each completed message's rendered component tree is memoized by `messageId + content hash` using `NSCache`. Re-renders of the message list do not re-parse markdown for unchanged messages. |
| Crash resilience | If the markdown parser throws on malformed input, catch the error and fall back to rendering the raw string as plain `Text`. Never crash the app due to markdown parsing. |

---

## 2.4 Tool Result Card System

All cards share a common elevated surface style and are rendered as tappable views that navigate to the relevant detail screen on tap.

### 2.4.0 Common Card Properties

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Background | `Color(.systemBackground)` (adapts light/dark) |
| Corner radius | 12pt |
| Border | 1pt, `Color(.separator)` |
| Shadow | `.shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.08), radius: 8, x: 0, y: 2)` |
| Padding | 14pt all sides |
| Tap feedback | Slight scale reduction (0.98) over 100ms on press-in, return to 1.0 on press-out via `ButtonStyle`. Haptic: `.selection`. |
| Tap action | Navigate to the relevant detail screen via `NavigationLink` or programmatic `NavigationPath.append()`. |
| Type icon | 20pt icon in top-left corner of card, color-coded by type (see per-card specs). 6pt right margin from card title. |

```swift
struct CardPressStyle: ButtonStyle {
    func makeBody(configuration: Configuration) -> some View {
        configuration.label
            .scaleEffect(configuration.isPressed ? 0.98 : 1.0)
            .animation(.easeOut(duration: 0.1), value: configuration.isPressed)
            .onChange(of: configuration.isPressed) { _, isPressed in
                if isPressed {
                    UISelectionFeedbackGenerator().selectionChanged()
                }
            }
    }
}
```

### 2.4.1 Contact Card

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| [person] CONTACT                                   |
|                                                    |
|  +----+  Marcus Johnson                            |
|  | MJ |  phone (555) 123-4567                      |
|  +----+  mail  marcus@email.com                    |
|          green-dot Last engaged 2 days ago         |
|                                                    |
|  [Call]  [Text]  [Email]                   >       |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Type badge | "CONTACT" in 10pt SF Pro bold, `#007AFF`, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.5pt. Person icon (`person.fill` SF Symbol) in `#007AFF`. |
| Avatar | 44pt diameter circle. If contact has a photo, show photo. If not, show initials on a deterministic color background (hash of name -> color from a predefined palette of 12 colors). |
| Name | 17pt SF Pro semibold, `Color(.label)`. Single line, truncate with ellipsis via `.lineLimit(1)`. |
| Phone | 14pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Phone icon 14pt, `Color(.tertiaryLabel)`. Tap the phone number directly to initiate a call (via `UIApplication.shared.open(URL(string: "tel:...")!)`). |
| Email | 14pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Mail icon 14pt, `Color(.tertiaryLabel)`. Tap to compose email via `UIApplication.shared.open(URL(string: "mailto:...")!)`. |
| Engagement dot | Green (`#34C759`) if engaged within 7 days. Yellow (`#FF9500`) if 7-30 days. Red (`#FF3B30`) if >30 days. Dot is 8pt diameter, inline with engagement text. Text: "Last engaged X days ago" in 13pt, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| Quick action buttons | Horizontal row. Each button: 32pt height, rounded rect (8pt radius), `Color(.secondarySystemBackground)` background, 13pt SF Pro medium text. Tap triggers the action directly (call, text, email) without leaving chat. |
| Chevron | Right-pointing chevron (`chevron.right` SF Symbol), `Color(.tertiaryLabel)`, 14pt, right-aligned vertically centered. Indicates tappability for full detail navigation. |

```swift
struct ContactCardView: View {
    let contact: ContactResult
    @Environment(\.openURL) private var openURL

    var body: some View {
        Button {
            // Navigate to contact detail
        } label: {
            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 10) {
                // Type badge
                HStack(spacing: 4) {
                    Image(systemName: "person.fill")
                        .font(.system(size: 12))
                    Text("CONTACT")
                        .font(.system(size: 10, weight: .bold))
                        .tracking(0.5)
                }
                .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0x007AFF))

                // Contact info row
                HStack(spacing: 12) {
                    AvatarView(name: contact.name, photo: contact.photoURL, size: 44)

                    VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 4) {
                        Text(contact.name)
                            .font(.system(size: 17, weight: .semibold))
                            .foregroundStyle(Color(.label))
                            .lineLimit(1)

                        if let phone = contact.phone {
                            Button { openURL(URL(string: "tel:\(phone)")!) } label: {
                                Label(phone, systemImage: "phone.fill")
                                    .font(.system(size: 14))
                                    .foregroundStyle(Color(.secondaryLabel))
                            }
                        }

                        if let email = contact.email {
                            Button { openURL(URL(string: "mailto:\(email)")!) } label: {
                                Label(email, systemImage: "envelope.fill")
                                    .font(.system(size: 14))
                                    .foregroundStyle(Color(.secondaryLabel))
                            }
                        }

                        EngagementIndicator(daysSince: contact.daysSinceEngagement)
                    }

                    Spacer()
                    Image(systemName: "chevron.right")
                        .font(.system(size: 14))
                        .foregroundStyle(Color(.tertiaryLabel))
                }

                // Quick actions
                HStack(spacing: 8) {
                    QuickActionButton(title: "Call", icon: "phone.fill") {
                        openURL(URL(string: "tel:\(contact.phone ?? "")")!)
                    }
                    QuickActionButton(title: "Text", icon: "message.fill") {
                        openURL(URL(string: "sms:\(contact.phone ?? "")")!)
                    }
                    QuickActionButton(title: "Email", icon: "envelope.fill") {
                        openURL(URL(string: "mailto:\(contact.email ?? "")")!)
                    }
                }
            }
            .padding(14)
            .background(Color(.systemBackground))
            .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
            .overlay(
                RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12)
                    .stroke(Color(.separator), lineWidth: 1)
            )
            .shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.08), radius: 8, x: 0, y: 2)
        }
        .buttonStyle(CardPressStyle())
    }
}
```

### 2.4.2 Property Card

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| +-----------------------------------------------+ |
| |                                                | |
| |        [Street View / Property Image]          | |
| |                                                | |
| +-----------------------------------------------+ |
| house 1234 Oak Avenue, Austin, TX 78701           |
|                                                    |
| $875,000           3 bd  |  2 ba  |  2,100 sqft   |
|                                                    |
| Listed 5 days ago                     Active  [g]  |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Image | Full card width minus 14pt padding each side. 160pt height. Corner radius 8pt. `contentMode: .fill` with `.clipped()`. Source: Google Street View API thumbnail or MLS photo. Loading: blurhash placeholder (computed server-side, sent with property data as a ~30-character string). Progressive JPEG rendering via `URLSession` with `.progressive` content policy. If no image available: gradient placeholder `Color(.systemGray5)` -> `Color(.systemGray6)` with a house icon centered. |
| Address | 16pt SF Pro semibold, `Color(.label)`. Max 2 lines, truncate with ellipsis. House icon (`house.fill` SF Symbol) 16pt, `#007AFF`. |
| Price | 20pt SF Pro bold, `Color(.label)`. Formatted with locale-aware currency (`$875,000`) via `Text(price, format: .currency(code: "USD"))`. |
| Metrics row | Three items separated by 1pt vertical dividers. Each: value in 15pt SF Pro semibold + label in 13pt SF Pro regular `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. E.g., "3 bd", "2 ba", "2,100 sqft". |
| Status | "Listed X days ago" in 13pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Status badge: "Active" in 11pt SF Pro bold, `#34C759`, with green dot. Or "Pending" in `#FF9500`, "Sold" in `#FF3B30`. Badge has a subtle background tint matching the color at 10% opacity, 4pt horizontal padding, 4pt corner radius. |

### 2.4.3 Content Preview Card

For AI-generated marketing content (Instagram captions, email drafts, listing descriptions).

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| pencil GENERATED CONTENT                           |
|                                                    |
| Instagram Caption                                  |
| -----------------------------------------------   |
| "Just listed! This stunning 3-bed                  |
| craftsman in East Austin features..."              |
|                                                    |
|                                        [Copy]      |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Type badge | "GENERATED CONTENT" in 10pt SF Pro bold, `#AF52DE` (purple), uppercase. `pencil` SF Symbol icon. |
| Content type label | "Instagram Caption" or "Email Draft" or "Listing Description" in 15pt SF Pro semibold. |
| Divider | 1pt `Color(.separator)`, full width minus 28pt, 8pt vertical margin (use `Divider()` with padding). |
| Content preview | 14pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Max 6 lines visible via `.lineLimit(6)`. If content exceeds 6 lines, truncate with "... Show more" in `#007AFF`. Tap "Show more" expands inline with `withAnimation`. |
| Copy button | Bottom-right corner. "Copy" + clipboard icon. 13pt SF Pro medium, `#007AFF`. On tap: copies full content to clipboard via `UIPasteboard.general.string`, button text changes to "Copied!" with checkmark for 2s, haptic `.notification(.success)`. |
| Content is markdown-capable | The generated content may include emoji, hashtags, line breaks. Render faithfully. |

### 2.4.4 Market Analysis Card

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| chart MARKET ANALYSIS -- 78701                     |
|                                                    |
|  Median Price     Avg DOM      Inventory           |
|  $542,000         28 days      156 listings        |
|  ^ 4.2%           v 3 days     ^ 12%              |
|                                                    |
| +-----------------------------------------------+ |
| |  /\     Price Trend (6mo)                      | |
| | /  \  /\                                       | |
| |/    \/  \/                                     | |
| +-----------------------------------------------+ |
|                                                    |
| Seller's Market - 4.2 months supply                |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Type badge | "MARKET ANALYSIS" in 10pt SF Pro bold, `#FF9500` (orange), uppercase + zip code or area name. `chart.line.uptrend.xyaxis` SF Symbol icon. |
| Key metrics | 3-column layout (via `HStack` with equal-width `VStack`s). Each column: value in 18pt SF Pro bold, label in 12pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`, trend in 13pt. Trend colors: up = `#34C759` with arrow.up, down = `#FF3B30` with arrow.down, flat = `Color(.secondaryLabel)` with arrow.right. |
| Mini chart | 120pt height. Rendered using SwiftUI `Charts` framework (iOS 16+). Simple `LineMark` with gradient fill below the line (`AreaMark` with brand blue to transparent gradient). 6 data points (months). No axis labels (too small) -- the chart is illustrative, not analytical. Tap the chart to navigate to a full market detail screen. |
| Summary line | Bottom of card. "Seller's Market" / "Buyer's Market" / "Balanced" in 14pt SF Pro medium, with supply months. |

```swift
import Charts

struct MiniMarketChart: View {
    let dataPoints: [MarketDataPoint]

    var body: some View {
        Chart(dataPoints) { point in
            LineMark(
                x: .value("Month", point.month),
                y: .value("Price", point.medianPrice)
            )
            .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0x007AFF))
            .interpolationMethod(.catmullRom)

            AreaMark(
                x: .value("Month", point.month),
                y: .value("Price", point.medianPrice)
            )
            .foregroundStyle(
                LinearGradient(
                    colors: [Color(hex: 0x007AFF).opacity(0.3), .clear],
                    startPoint: .top,
                    endPoint: .bottom
                )
            )
            .interpolationMethod(.catmullRom)
        }
        .chartXAxis(.hidden)
        .chartYAxis(.hidden)
        .frame(height: 120)
    }
}
```

### 2.4.5 Task / Follow-up Card

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| checkmark FOLLOW-UP                                |
|                                                    |
| Call Marcus Johnson re: 1234 Oak Ave showing       |
| calendar Tomorrow at 2:00 PM                       |
|                                                    |
| [Mark Complete]                                    |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Type badge | "FOLLOW-UP" or "TASK" in 10pt SF Pro bold, `#34C759` (green), uppercase. Checkmark icon (`checkmark.square.fill` SF Symbol). |
| Task description | 15pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.label)`. Max 3 lines via `.lineLimit(3)`. |
| Due date | 14pt SF Pro medium, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Calendar icon (`calendar` SF Symbol) 14pt. If overdue: text in `#FF3B30`, icon in `#FF3B30`. If today: text in `#FF9500`. If future: default `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| Contact reference | If the task references a contact, the contact name is rendered as a tappable link (`#007AFF`). Tap navigates to Contact Detail. |
| Mark Complete button | Full width, 40pt height, `#34C759` background, white text "Mark Complete" in 14pt SF Pro semibold, 10pt corner radius. On tap: haptic `.notification(.success)`, button collapses to a centered checkmark with "Done" text over 300ms via `withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3))`, card background gets a subtle green tint (`Color(hex: 0xF0FFF4)`), button becomes non-interactive. The completion is sent to the API: `POST /api/tasks/{taskId}/complete`. |

### 2.4.6 Card Loading State (Skeleton)

While a tool call is executing and the result has not yet arrived:

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| [====shimmer====]                                  |  <- shimmer bar 1 (type badge)
|                                                    |
| [===========shimmer=============]                  |  <- shimmer bar 2 (title)
| [========shimmer========]                          |  <- shimmer bar 3 (subtitle)
|                                                    |
| [===============shimmer=================]          |  <- shimmer bar 4 (content)
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Dimensions | Match the expected card type. If the card type is unknown (generic tool call), use a default 200pt height. |
| Shimmer bars | Rounded rectangles (4pt radius), `Color(.systemGray5)` base color with a gradient highlight (`Color(.systemGray4)`) sweeping left-to-right at 1.5s interval. Use `TimelineView(.animation)` or a `withAnimation(.linear(duration: 1.5).repeatForever(autoreverses: false))` phase-based shimmer that runs on the render thread. |
| Shimmer count | 3-4 bars of varying widths (70%, 50%, 85%, 40% of card width) to suggest the structure of the eventual content. |
| Transition | When the result arrives, the skeleton cross-fades to the real card over 250ms (`.transition(.opacity)` with `withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.25))` simultaneous). |

```swift
struct ShimmerView: View {
    @State private var phase: CGFloat = 0

    var body: some View {
        RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 4)
            .fill(Color(.systemGray5))
            .overlay(
                LinearGradient(
                    colors: [.clear, Color(.systemGray4), .clear],
                    startPoint: .leading,
                    endPoint: .trailing
                )
                .offset(x: phase)
            )
            .clipped()
            .onAppear {
                withAnimation(.linear(duration: 1.5).repeatForever(autoreverses: false)) {
                    phase = 300
                }
            }
    }
}

struct SkeletonCardView: View {
    var body: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 12) {
            ShimmerView().frame(width: 100, height: 12)  // type badge
            ShimmerView().frame(width: 220, height: 14)  // title
            ShimmerView().frame(width: 160, height: 12)  // subtitle
            ShimmerView().frame(height: 14)               // content (full width)
                .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
        }
        .padding(14)
        .frame(height: 200)
        .background(Color(.systemBackground))
        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
        .overlay(
            RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12)
                .stroke(Color(.separator), lineWidth: 1)
        )
        .shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.08), radius: 8, x: 0, y: 2)
    }
}
```

### 2.4.7 Card Error State

```
+---------------------------------------------------+
| warning-icon                                       |
| Couldn't complete this action                      |
| There was a problem looking up this contact.       |
|                                                    |
|                    [Retry]                          |
+---------------------------------------------------+
```

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Background | `Color(hex: 0xFFF2F2)` (light) / `Color(hex: 0x3A2020)` (dark) |
| Icon | Warning triangle (`exclamationmark.triangle.fill` SF Symbol), 32pt, `#FF3B30`, centered at top. |
| Title | "Couldn't complete this action" in 15pt SF Pro semibold, `Color(.label)`. |
| Detail | Contextual error message in 13pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. E.g., "There was a problem looking up this contact." |
| Retry button | Centered, 36pt height, `#007AFF` text "Retry" in 14pt SF Pro medium. On tap: re-dispatches the original tool call. Skeleton loading state replaces the error card. |

---

## 2.5 Input Bar Design

The input bar is a persistent component anchored to the bottom of the chat screen. It must handle keyboard interaction flawlessly -- this is the most interaction-dense area of the entire app.

### 2.5.1 Layout Structure

```
+----------------------------------------------------------+
| [Attachment preview chips -- if any attachments selected] |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|  [+]  | Message Skye...                    | [mic] [send] |
|       |                                    |              |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|                    Safe area padding                      |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

**Component hierarchy:** The input bar is placed within the `safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` modifier or managed by SwiftUI's built-in keyboard avoidance (`.ignoresSafeArea(.keyboard)` on the scroll content with manual `.safeAreaInset` for the input bar), ensuring it stays pinned above the keyboard at all times with smooth animated transitions.

### 2.5.2 Text Input Field

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Component | `TextEditor` (multiline) or custom `UITextView` wrapper via `UIViewRepresentable` for finer control |
| Placeholder | "Message Skye..." in 16pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.placeholderText)` |
| Font | 16pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.label)` |
| Background | `Color(.secondarySystemBackground)` |
| Corner radius | 20pt (pill shape when single line) |
| Horizontal padding | 16pt left, 16pt right (additional right padding when send button is visible) |
| Vertical padding | 10pt top, 10pt bottom |
| Min height | 40pt (single line) |
| Growth behavior | Starts at 1 line (40pt). Grows line-by-line as text wraps. Maximum visible height: 6 lines (~132pt). After 6 lines, internal scroll activates. Growth animation: `withAnimation(.spring(response: 0.25, dampingFraction: 0.7))` on height change. |
| Max characters | 4,000 (API limit). No visible counter until 3,800 characters, then a subtle "200 remaining" label appears above the input bar in 11pt `#FF9500`. At 4,000: input stops accepting characters, label turns red "Character limit reached". |
| Return key | Default return key inserts a newline (multiline input). Send is always via the send button. |
| Autocorrect | Enabled. `.autocorrectionDisabled(false)`, `.textInputAutocapitalization(.sentences)`. |
| Content type | `.textContentType(nil)` to prevent password autofill suggestions. |

```swift
struct GrowingTextEditor: UIViewRepresentable {
    @Binding var text: String
    @Binding var height: CGFloat
    let maxHeight: CGFloat = 132
    let minHeight: CGFloat = 40

    func makeUIView(context: Context) -> UITextView {
        let textView = UITextView()
        textView.font = .systemFont(ofSize: 16)
        textView.backgroundColor = .clear
        textView.textContainerInset = UIEdgeInsets(top: 10, left: 12, bottom: 10, right: 12)
        textView.isScrollEnabled = false
        textView.delegate = context.coordinator
        textView.textContentType = nil
        textView.autocorrectionType = .yes
        textView.spellCheckingType = .yes
        return textView
    }

    func updateUIView(_ textView: UITextView, context: Context) {
        if textView.text != text { textView.text = text }
        recalculateHeight(textView)
    }

    private func recalculateHeight(_ textView: UITextView) {
        let size = textView.sizeThatFits(
            CGSize(width: textView.frame.width, height: .greatestFiniteMagnitude)
        )
        let newHeight = min(max(size.height, minHeight), maxHeight)
        textView.isScrollEnabled = size.height > maxHeight
        if height != newHeight {
            DispatchQueue.main.async { height = newHeight }
        }
    }

    func makeCoordinator() -> Coordinator { Coordinator(self) }

    final class Coordinator: NSObject, UITextViewDelegate {
        let parent: GrowingTextEditor
        init(_ parent: GrowingTextEditor) { self.parent = parent }

        func textViewDidChange(_ textView: UITextView) {
            parent.text = textView.text
            parent.recalculateHeight(textView)
        }

        func textView(_ textView: UITextView, shouldChangeTextIn range: NSRange,
                       replacementText text: String) -> Bool {
            let current = textView.text ?? ""
            let newLength = current.count - range.length + text.count
            return newLength <= 4000
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.5.3 Send Button

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Visibility | Hidden when text input is empty AND no attachments are present. Appears when `text.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).count > 0 OR attachments.count > 0`. |
| Appearance animation | Scale from 0 -> 1 over 150ms (`.spring(response: 0.15, dampingFraction: 0.6)` on `.scaleEffect` via `.transition(.scale)`). Position: right side of the input field, vertically centered with the last line of text. |
| Icon | Up-arrow in filled circle (SF Symbol: `arrow.up.circle.fill`). 32pt diameter. Color: `#007AFF`. |
| Tap behavior | (1) Haptic `.impact(.light)`. (2) Dispatch message to send queue. (3) Clear input field. (4) Input field height animates back to single line. (5) Scroll to bottom of message list. |
| Disabled state | During active AI streaming: send button is replaced with a "Stop" button (`stop.circle.fill` SF Symbol, `#FF3B30`). Tapping stop cancels the current SSE stream `Task`. After cancel, send button reappears if text is present. |
| Tap target | Minimum 44x44pt via `.frame(minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44)`, even though the visible icon is 32pt. |

### 2.5.4 Attachment Button

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Position | Left side of the input field, vertically centered. |
| Icon | Plus icon in circle (SF Symbol: `plus.circle.fill`), 28pt, `#007AFF`. |
| Tap behavior | Haptic `.selection`. Opens a `.confirmationDialog` with options: "Take Photo" (camera), "Choose from Library" (photo picker), "Cancel". |
| Camera | Opens the device camera via a `UIImagePickerController` wrapper (`UIViewControllerRepresentable`) with `sourceType: .camera`. Max resolution: 2048px on longest edge (downscaled before upload to control payload size). |
| Photo Library | Opens the iOS photo picker (`PHPickerViewController` via `PhotosUI`'s `PhotosPicker`) with `maxSelectionCount: 3`. |
| Attachment preview | Selected images appear as chips above the input field: 56pt tall, 56pt wide, 8pt corner radius, with a small "X" button (20pt circle, top-right corner) to remove. Multiple chips scroll horizontally via `ScrollView(.horizontal)`. |
| Upload behavior | Attachments are uploaded immediately on selection to a presigned S3 URL (via `POST /api/upload`). A circular `ProgressView` overlays each chip during upload. If upload fails, chip shows a red exclamation badge -- tap to retry upload. |

### 2.5.5 Voice Input Button

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Position | To the left of the send button (when send button is hidden, the microphone occupies the right position). |
| Icon | Microphone (SF Symbol: `mic.fill`), 24pt, `#007AFF`. |
| Tap behavior | Activates iOS system dictation. Use `UIPasteboard` observation or simply rely on the `TextEditor`/`UITextView`'s built-in dictation support via the keyboard's microphone button. Alternatively, trigger dictation programmatically if the keyboard is active. |
| Visibility | Hidden when the send button is visible (text is present). Appears only when the input field is empty. This avoids cluttering the input bar. |
| Fallback | If dictation is disabled in iOS settings, the button is hidden entirely. Check availability and simply always render the button; iOS handles the unavailable state gracefully by showing a system alert. |

### 2.5.6 Safe Area and Keyboard Handling

| Behavior | Implementation |
|---|---|
| Safe area (no keyboard) | Input bar bottom padding includes the device safe area inset (home indicator area on Face ID devices, ~34pt). SwiftUI handles this automatically with `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` or when using `GeometryReader` with `safeAreaInsets.bottom`. |
| Keyboard open | SwiftUI's built-in keyboard avoidance (default behavior) or explicit management with `ScrollViewReader` + `.onReceive(NotificationCenter.default.publisher(for: UIResponder.keyboardWillShowNotification))`. The input bar animates upward in sync with the keyboard animation (matched duration and curve). No jumping, no gap. |
| Keyboard dismiss (tap) | Tapping the message list area above the keyboard dismisses the keyboard. Implemented via `.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)` on the `ScrollView`. |
| Keyboard dismiss (drag) | Interactive dismissal: `.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)` provides this behavior natively -- dragging down on the message list pulls the keyboard down. The input bar tracks the keyboard position frame-by-frame. If the user drags <50% of keyboard height and releases, keyboard snaps back open. If >50%, keyboard dismisses fully. |
| Keyboard type switch | When switching between text and emoji keyboards, the input bar smoothly tracks the height change (no jump). SwiftUI handles this via its automatic keyboard avoidance animation. |
| Rotation | On device rotation (if supported), the input bar width adjusts. Text input reflows. Keyboard re-renders at new width. Safe area insets update. All transitions animated automatically by SwiftUI. |

---

## 2.6 Chat UX Behaviors

### 2.6.1 Auto-Scroll Logic

The auto-scroll behavior must match user expectations set by iMessage and WhatsApp: new messages scroll into view automatically, unless the user has deliberately scrolled up to read earlier messages.

**Algorithm:**

```swift
let nearBottomThreshold: CGFloat = 100   // pt from bottom
let showPillThreshold: CGFloat = 200     // pt from bottom

func onNewMessage(_ message: any BaseMessage) {
    let distanceFromBottom = scrollContentHeight - scrollOffset - viewportHeight

    if distanceFromBottom <= nearBottomThreshold {
        // User is at/near bottom -> auto-scroll to new message
        withAnimation(.easeOut(duration: 0.25)) {
            scrollProxy.scrollTo(message.id, anchor: .bottom)
        }
    } else {
        // User has scrolled up -> don't scroll, show pill
        showNewMessagePill(for: message)
    }
}
```

For iOS 17+, use `.defaultScrollAnchor(.bottom)` on the `ScrollView` to anchor at bottom by default, combined with `ScrollPosition` for programmatic control:

```swift
@State private var scrollPosition = ScrollPosition(edge: .bottom)

ScrollView {
    LazyVStack(spacing: 0) {
        ForEach(messages, id: \.id) { message in
            MessageRowView(message: message)
        }
    }
}
.defaultScrollAnchor(.bottom)
.scrollPosition($scrollPosition)
```

| Component | Specification |
|---|---|
| Auto-scroll animation | Programmatic scroll with duration 250ms, ease-out curve. Smooth, not jarring. |
| "New message" pill | Floating pill at bottom-center of message list, 24pt above the input bar. Design: `#007AFF` background, white text "New message (down-arrow)" in 13pt SF Pro semibold, 20pt height, 16pt horizontal padding, fully rounded corners (`.capsule`). Shadow: `.shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.15), radius: 4, x: 0, y: 2)`. |
| Pill behavior | Tap scrolls to bottom (animated, 300ms). Pill dismisses when the user scrolls to within `nearBottomThreshold` of the bottom (regardless of how they get there). Pill auto-dismisses after 5s if not tapped (fade-out 200ms). |
| Scroll-to-bottom button | A separate button from the pill. Appears when the user is >200pt from the bottom AND no new-message pill is active. Circular, 36pt diameter, `Color(.systemBackground)` background, subtle shadow, down-arrow icon `#007AFF`. Positioned bottom-right of the message list, 16pt from right edge, 8pt above input bar. Tap scrolls to bottom. |
| During streaming | If user is near bottom, each token batch triggers a micro-scroll to keep the streaming content in view. Use instant scroll (no animation) to avoid jarring repeated animations. |

### 2.6.2 Typing Indicator

Shown after the user sends a message and before the first SSE token arrives.

```
+--------------+
|  *  *  *     |   <- animated dots inside an AI-style bubble
+--------------+
```

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Trigger | Appears when a user message is sent and the SSE state transitions to `CONNECTING`. Removed when the state transitions to `STREAMING` (first token received). |
| Position | Rendered as the last item in the message list (below the user's message), left-aligned like an AI message. |
| Bubble | Same styling as AI text message bubble, but smaller: 52pt wide, 36pt tall. |
| Dots | Three circles, 8pt diameter each, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`, spaced 6pt apart, centered vertically and horizontally in the bubble. |
| Animation | Sequential bounce: each dot translates Y from 0 -> -6pt -> 0 over 400ms, staggered 150ms apart. Loop continuously. Use `withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.4).repeatForever())` with staggered delays. |
| Timeout | If typing indicator is visible for >15s (no first token), append a subtitle below the bubble: "This is taking longer than usual..." in 12pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| Removal | On first token, the typing indicator cross-fades out (opacity 1 -> 0, 150ms via `.transition(.opacity)`) while the AI message bubble fades in at the same position. No layout jump. |

```swift
struct TypingIndicator: View {
    @State private var animatingDot1 = false
    @State private var animatingDot2 = false
    @State private var animatingDot3 = false
    @State private var showSlowWarning = false
    let appearedAt: Date

    var body: some View {
        VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 4) {
            HStack(spacing: 6) {
                DotView(animating: $animatingDot1)
                DotView(animating: $animatingDot2)
                DotView(animating: $animatingDot3)
            }
            .padding(.horizontal, 14)
            .padding(.vertical, 10)
            .frame(width: 52, height: 36)
            .background(Color(.systemGray6))
            .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 18))

            if showSlowWarning {
                Text("This is taking longer than usual...")
                    .font(.system(size: 12))
                    .foregroundStyle(Color(.secondaryLabel))
                    .transition(.opacity)
            }
        }
        .padding(.leading, 52)
        .onAppear {
            withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.4).repeatForever().delay(0)) {
                animatingDot1 = true
            }
            withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.4).repeatForever().delay(0.15)) {
                animatingDot2 = true
            }
            withAnimation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.4).repeatForever().delay(0.3)) {
                animatingDot3 = true
            }

            // 15s slow warning
            Task {
                try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(15))
                withAnimation { showSlowWarning = true }
            }
        }
    }
}

struct DotView: View {
    @Binding var animating: Bool
    var body: some View {
        Circle()
            .fill(Color(.secondaryLabel))
            .frame(width: 8, height: 8)
            .offset(y: animating ? -6 : 0)
    }
}
```

### 2.6.3 Timestamp Display

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Inline timestamps | Automatically inserted between messages when the time gap exceeds **5 minutes**. Rendered as `system` messages with subtype `dateSeparator`. Format: "2:34 PM" (same day), "Yesterday 2:34 PM", or "Mon, Jan 15, 2:34 PM" (older than 2 days). |
| Per-message timestamp | Tap any message bubble to reveal its exact timestamp. Timestamp appears above the bubble in 11pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`, with a slide-down animation (offset y: -8 -> 0, opacity: 0 -> 1, 200ms). Tapping again or tapping elsewhere hides it. |
| Date separators | Full date separators ("Today", "Yesterday", "January 15, 2026") are inserted between messages from different calendar days. Rendered per Section 2.2.7. |

### 2.6.4 Message Sending States

| State | Visual | Transition |
|---|---|---|
| **Composing** | Text in input field. Not yet in message list. | User taps send -> `sending` |
| **Sending** | Message appears in list immediately (optimistic). Bubble at 60% opacity. No timestamp. Subtle upward slide-in animation. | API acknowledges -> `sent`. API fails -> `failed`. |
| **Sent** | Full opacity. Timestamp available on tap. | -- |
| **Failed** | Bubble turns `#FF3B30` tinted. Below bubble: "Not sent. Tap to retry." Tap the error text or the bubble to retry. | Retry -> `sending`. User deletes -> removed from list. |

**Optimistic insertion timing:** The user message appears in the list within the same frame as the send button tap (before the network request is even initiated). This creates the perception of instant responsiveness. Achieved by appending to the `@Observable` messages array synchronously before the `Task` for the network request is created.

### 2.6.5 Long-Press Message Actions

Long-pressing (500ms) on a message triggers a context menu with haptic feedback.

| Message Type | Available Actions |
|---|---|
| User text message | **Copy Text** -- Copies message content to clipboard. **Delete** -- "Delete Message" with confirmation alert ("Are you sure? This cannot be undone."). On confirm, message is removed from local list and `DELETE /api/chat/messages/{id}` is called. **Share** -- Opens iOS share sheet via `ShareLink` or `UIActivityViewController`. |
| AI text message | **Copy Text** -- Copies the full markdown source (not rendered text) to clipboard. **Share** -- Opens iOS share sheet. **Regenerate** -- Re-sends the previous user message to get a new AI response. The current AI response is replaced. |
| Tool result card | **Copy** -- Copies the primary content (contact name, address, generated text) depending on card type. Other actions per card type (e.g., "Share Property" for property cards). |
| System message | No long-press action. |

**Implementation:** Use the native SwiftUI `.contextMenu { }` modifier for native iOS context menu presentation (`UIContextMenuInteraction`). The menu appears with the standard iOS blur background and haptic feedback (handled natively by UIKit). For preview providers, use `.contextMenu(menuItems:preview:)` to show a message preview above the menu.

```swift
struct MessageBubble: View {
    let message: any BaseMessage

    var body: some View {
        bubbleContent
            .contextMenu {
                if message.type == .userText {
                    Button { UIPasteboard.general.string = (message as? UserTextMessage)?.content } label: {
                        Label("Copy Text", systemImage: "doc.on.doc")
                    }
                    Button(role: .destructive) { deleteMessage(message) } label: {
                        Label("Delete", systemImage: "trash")
                    }
                    ShareLink(item: (message as? UserTextMessage)?.content ?? "")
                } else if message.type == .aiText {
                    Button { UIPasteboard.general.string = (message as? AITextMessage)?.content } label: {
                        Label("Copy Text", systemImage: "doc.on.doc")
                    }
                    ShareLink(item: (message as? AITextMessage)?.content ?? "")
                    Button { regenerateResponse() } label: {
                        Label("Regenerate", systemImage: "arrow.clockwise")
                    }
                }
            }
    }
}
```

### 2.6.6 Pull-to-Load Earlier History

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Trigger | User scrolls to the top of the message list. Detected via `ScrollView` geometry or `onAppear` of a sentinel view at index 0. |
| Loading indicator | A `ProgressView()` spinner appears at the top of the list while loading. Alternative: 3 skeleton message bubbles at the top (left-aligned for AI, right-aligned for user, in a plausible pattern). |
| API call | `GET /api/chat/history?sessionId={id}&cursor={oldestMessageId}&limit=50` |
| Scroll position | After new messages are prepended, the scroll position is maintained so the user continues reading from where they were. Use `ScrollViewReader` to preserve position by scrolling to the previously-first-visible message ID after prepend, or use iOS 18+ `.scrollPosition(id:)` with `.scrollTargetLayout()`. |
| End of history | When the API returns fewer than 50 messages (or an empty array), stop requesting. Show a subtle "Beginning of conversation" label at the top. |
| Performance | Prepending 50 messages should not cause a visible stutter. `LazyVStack` ensures only visible cells are rendered. No full re-render of existing messages. |

### 2.6.7 Empty Chat State

When the chat has no messages (new session or first launch):

```
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                          |
|                                                          |
|                     [Skye Logo]                          |
|                     64pt, brand blue                     |
|                                                          |
|              Hi! I'm Skye, your AI                       |
|              real estate assistant.                       |
|                                                          |
|              How can I help you today?                    |
|                                                          |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    | magnify  "Who should I follow up with today?" |      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    | house  "Find listings under $500K in 78704"   |      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    | pencil  "Draft an open house Instagram post"  |      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|    | chart  "What's the market like in 78701?"     |      |
|    +----------------------------------------------+      |
|                                                          |
|                                                          |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Logo | Skye app icon or wordmark, 64pt, centered. |
| Greeting | "Hi! I'm Skye, your AI real estate assistant." in 20pt SF Pro semibold, centered. "How can I help you today?" in 16pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`, centered. 8pt gap between lines. |
| Suggested prompts | 4 tappable cards, vertically stacked, 12pt gap between cards. Each card: full width minus 48pt margins, 48pt height, `Color(.secondarySystemBackground)` background, 12pt corner radius. Left icon (relevant SF Symbol), text in 15pt SF Pro regular, left-aligned with 12pt padding. |
| Prompt tap behavior | Tap inserts the prompt text into the input field AND immediately sends it (single tap = full send, not just insertion). The empty state disappears as the first message appears. |
| Animation | Prompts stagger-fade-in on mount: each card fades and slides up (offset y: 10 -> 0, opacity: 0 -> 1, 200ms) with 50ms stagger between cards via `.animation(.easeOut(duration: 0.2).delay(Double(index) * 0.05))`. |
| Visibility | The empty state disappears permanently once the first message exists in the session. It does not reappear if all messages are deleted. |

```swift
struct EmptyChatView: View {
    let onPromptTapped: (String) -> Void

    private let prompts = [
        ("magnifyingglass", "Who should I follow up with today?"),
        ("house.fill", "Find listings under $500K in 78704"),
        ("pencil", "Draft an open house Instagram post"),
        ("chart.line.uptrend.xyaxis", "What's the market like in 78701?"),
    ]

    var body: some View {
        VStack(spacing: 8) {
            Spacer()
            Image("skye-logo")
                .resizable()
                .frame(width: 64, height: 64)

            Text("Hi! I'm Skye, your AI\nreal estate assistant.")
                .font(.system(size: 20, weight: .semibold))
                .multilineTextAlignment(.center)

            Text("How can I help you today?")
                .font(.system(size: 16))
                .foregroundStyle(Color(.secondaryLabel))

            VStack(spacing: 12) {
                ForEach(Array(prompts.enumerated()), id: \.offset) { index, prompt in
                    Button {
                        UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .light).impactOccurred()
                        onPromptTapped(prompt.1)
                    } label: {
                        HStack(spacing: 12) {
                            Image(systemName: prompt.0)
                                .font(.system(size: 16))
                                .foregroundStyle(Color(hex: 0x007AFF))
                            Text("\"\(prompt.1)\"")
                                .font(.system(size: 15))
                                .foregroundStyle(Color(.label))
                            Spacer()
                        }
                        .padding(.horizontal, 12)
                        .frame(height: 48)
                        .background(Color(.secondarySystemBackground))
                        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 12))
                    }
                    .opacity(1)
                    .offset(y: 0)
                    .animation(
                        .easeOut(duration: 0.2).delay(Double(index) * 0.05),
                        value: true
                    )
                }
            }
            .padding(.horizontal, 24)
            .padding(.top, 16)

            Spacer()
        }
    }
}
```

### 2.6.8 Chat Session Management

| Concept | Specification |
|---|---|
| Session | A chat session is a single continuous conversation with the AI. It has a unique `sessionId` (UUID). All messages within a session share this ID. |
| Session persistence | Sessions are persisted server-side. The client stores the `currentSessionId` in `UserDefaults` or `SwiftData`. On app launch, the most recent session is loaded. |
| New Chat | The user can start a new chat session from the navigation header (icon: `square.and.pencil` SF Symbol, top-right via `.toolbar`). Tap creates a new `sessionId`, clears the message list, and shows the empty state. The previous session remains accessible from a session list/drawer. |
| Session list | Accessible from the navigation header (icon: `list.bullet` SF Symbol). Shows previous sessions with: first user message as title (truncated to 40 chars), last message timestamp, AI's first response as subtitle (truncated to 60 chars). Tap loads that session. Presented as a `.sheet()` or side panel via `NavigationSplitView` on iPad. |
| Session title | Auto-generated by the AI after the first exchange. The server returns a `sessionTitle` field on the first AI response. E.g., "Follow-up with Marcus Johnson". Updated if the conversation topic shifts significantly (server-driven). |
| Session deletion | Swipe-to-delete on session list items via `.onDelete` or `.swipeActions`. Confirmation alert via `.alert()`. Deletes all messages via `DELETE /api/chat/sessions/{sessionId}`. |

---

## 2.7 Performance Specifications

These are hard targets. The app must meet these benchmarks on an iPhone 12 (A14 Bionic) running iOS 16 or later. If benchmarks are met on iPhone 12, they will be exceeded on newer hardware.

| Metric | Target | Measurement Method |
|---|---|---|
| **First token to screen** | <100ms from the moment the first SSE `data:` event is received by the client to the moment the token is rendered in the message list. | Instrument with `CFAbsoluteTimeGetCurrent()` or `os_signpost` timestamps at SSE parse and SwiftUI view `onAppear`. |
| **Time to first byte (TTFB)** | <500ms from send button tap to first SSE event. This is primarily server-side latency (AI model inference startup). | Timestamp at send dispatch vs. first parsed SSE event. Monitored via analytics. |
| **Message list scroll FPS** | 60fps sustained with 200+ messages rendered (via `LazyVStack`). 0 blank cells during normal-speed scrolling. | Measure with Xcode Instruments (Core Animation FPS / SwiftUI performance instrument). |
| **Input keystroke latency** | <16ms (1 frame) from physical key press to character rendered. | Profile with Instruments. Ensure no main-actor blocking during typing. |
| **Memory -- 50 messages** | <80MB total app memory. | Xcode Memory Graph. |
| **Memory -- 200 messages** | <120MB total app memory. | Xcode Memory Graph. |
| **Memory -- 500 messages** | <150MB total app memory. This requires aggressive lazy loading and image cache management. | Xcode Memory Graph. |
| **App launch to chat ready** | <1.5s from app icon tap to chat screen rendered with the most recent 20 messages visible and input bar interactive. | Profile with Instruments or `MetricKit` launch time metrics. |
| **Image load (card thumbnails)** | Blurhash placeholder visible <50ms. Full image loaded <500ms on WiFi, <1500ms on cellular. | Instrument `AsyncImage` phase changes or custom `URLSession` task metrics. |
| **Markdown parse (completed message)** | <10ms for a 500-word message. <50ms for a 2,000-word message. | Benchmark in isolation with `ContinuousClock` / `clock.measure { }`. |

### 2.7.1 Virtualization Configuration

```swift
struct ChatMessageList: View {
    let messages: [any BaseMessage]
    @State private var scrollPosition = ScrollPosition(edge: .bottom)

    var body: some View {
        ScrollView {
            LazyVStack(spacing: 0) {
                ForEach(messages, id: \.id) { message in
                    MessageRowView(
                        message: message,
                        isLastInGroup: isLastInGroup(message),
                        showAvatar: shouldShowAvatar(message)
                    )
                    .id(message.id)
                }
            }
            .padding(.top, 16)
            .padding(.bottom, 8)
        }
        .defaultScrollAnchor(.bottom)            // iOS 17+: anchor at bottom
        .scrollPosition($scrollPosition)
        .scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)  // Interactive keyboard dismiss
    }

    /// Determine spacing based on message grouping
    private func spacing(for message: any BaseMessage, previousMessage: (any BaseMessage)?) -> CGFloat {
        guard let prev = previousMessage else { return 0 }
        if prev.type.isAISender == message.type.isAISender {
            return 3   // Intra-group: 3pt
        }
        return 12      // Inter-group: 12pt
    }
}
```

### 2.7.2 Image Loading Strategy

| Stage | Implementation |
|---|---|
| **Placeholder** | Blurhash string (sent from server with every image URL). Decoded to a low-res `UIImage` (~4x3 pixels) via a lightweight blurhash decoder and displayed as the blurred placeholder. Decode time: <5ms. |
| **Thumbnail** | Request a 200px-wide thumbnail via CDN image transformation URL parameter (e.g., `?w=200&q=70`). Uses `URLSession` with `URLCache` (200MB disk, 50MB memory). This loads quickly on any connection. |
| **Full image** | On tap (full-screen view), load the full-resolution image. Progressive JPEG -- image renders at increasing quality as bytes arrive (handled by `URLSession` data tasks with delegate for incremental image decoding). |
| **Cache** | Custom `URLSession` with a dedicated `URLCache` instance (disk cache: LRU, 200MB limit; memory cache: 50MB limit, auto-evicted under pressure). Cache key is the URL without query parameters (so different size transformations of the same image share a cache group). Alternatively, use a dedicated image caching library like `Kingfisher` or `Nuke` via SPM. |
| **Prefetch** | When a tool result card with an image enters the `LazyVStack` buffer (before becoming visible), prefetch the thumbnail URL via `URLSession.shared.dataTask(with:)` started in `onAppear` of a sentinel view. This ensures images are loaded by the time the user scrolls to them. |

---

## 2.8 Error States and Edge Cases

### 2.8.1 Network Offline During Send

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Detection | `NWPathMonitor` reports `path.status == .unsatisfied` at the time the user taps send. |
| User experience | Message is inserted into the list with `status: .queued`. Bubble at 50% opacity with a clock icon (SF Symbol `clock`) to the right. Below the bubble: "Will send when connected" in 12pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. |
| Queue | Messages are queued in an ordered array persisted to `UserDefaults` (via `Codable` serialization) or `SwiftData`. Maximum queue depth: 5 messages. If the user tries to send a 6th, show an alert via `.alert()`: "You have unsent messages waiting for a connection." |
| Reconnection | When `NWPathMonitor` reports `path.status == .satisfied`, the queue is drained sequentially (one at a time, awaiting each response before sending the next). Each message transitions from `.queued` -> `.sending` -> `.sent` as it processes. |
| Queue persistence | The queue survives app restart. On relaunch, check connectivity and drain if online. |

### 2.8.2 API Returns 401 During Chat

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Detection | SSE connection or any API call returns HTTP 401 or 403. |
| Handling | **Silently** attempt token refresh: `POST /api/auth/refresh` with the stored refresh token. This is invisible to the user. |
| Success | New access token is stored (in Keychain). The failed request is retried automatically with the new token. The user never sees an error. |
| Failure | If refresh fails (refresh token also expired), redirect to the login screen with a message: "Your session has expired. Please sign in again." Preserve the current chat state so it's available after re-authentication. Navigate via `NavigationPath` root replacement or an `@Environment` auth state flag. |
| Rate limit | Maximum 2 silent refresh attempts per minute to prevent infinite loops. |

### 2.8.3 API Returns 500 During Chat

| Behavior | Specification |
|---|---|
| Display | An inline error message appears below the user's message (where the AI response would be). Styled as a system message with red tint: "Something went wrong on our end." with a "Retry" button. |
| Retry | Tap "Retry" re-sends the last user message to the API. Maximum 2 automatic retries with 2s delay between them. After 2 failures, show: "We're having trouble right now. Please try again later." with no automatic retry -- only a manual "Try again" button. |
| Logging | All 500 errors are logged to the analytics service with: endpoint, timestamp, request payload hash (no PII), response body if available, device info. Use `os_log` / `Logger` for local logs plus analytics SDK for remote. |

### 2.8.4 SSE Stream Malformed

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| Invalid JSON in `data:` field | Skip the malformed event. Log the raw event to analytics via `Logger`. Continue processing subsequent events. The token buffer is unaffected -- only valid tokens are appended. |
| Incomplete SSE event (no `\n\n` terminator) | The line accumulator holds the partial data until the next chunk arrives and completes it. If the connection closes with an unterminated event, discard the partial event. |
| Unexpected event type | Ignore unknown `event:` types. Forward-compatible -- new event types can be added server-side without breaking the client. |
| Empty `data:` field | Skip. No token appended. |
| Stream delivers valid JSON but unexpected schema | Validate each event against expected `Codable` shape. If decoding fails, skip and log. Do not crash. |

### 2.8.5 Tool Call Takes >15 Seconds

| Timing | User Experience |
|---|---|
| 0-5s | Skeleton card with standard shimmer animation. Tool call status line shows "Looking up contact..." (spinner). |
| 5-15s | No change. This is within normal range for some operations (e.g., market analysis with heavy computation). |
| 15s+ | Text appears below the skeleton card: "This is taking longer than usual..." in 13pt `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. A "Cancel" link appears next to it. |
| 30s+ | Text updates to: "Still working on it..." with the "Cancel" link still available. |
| Cancel behavior | Tapping "Cancel" cancels the SSE streaming `Task` via structured concurrency. The tool call message transitions to `toolStatus: .failed` with an error card: "Action was cancelled." No retry button (user initiated the cancellation). The AI may send a follow-up text acknowledging the cancellation. |
| Server timeout (60s) | If the server itself times out, it sends an error event via SSE. Client renders the error card (Section 2.4.7). |

### 2.8.6 Empty Response from AI

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| SSE stream opens, sends `done` event with 0 tokens | Display a system-style message in the AI position: "I didn't have a response for that. Try rephrasing your question." in 14pt SF Pro regular, `Color(.secondaryLabel)`. Italic styling via `.italic()`. No bubble -- renders as inline text, left-aligned. |
| SSE stream delivers only whitespace tokens | Same as empty response -- trim and check. If the trimmed concatenation is empty, treat as empty response. |
| AI response is only a tool call with no text | This is valid -- the tool result card is the response. No empty message indicator needed. |

### 2.8.7 Extremely Long AI Response (10,000+ Tokens)

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Memory | The response string grows in the `StreamingMessageBuffer` class property, not in repeated `@State` updates. Only one `@Observable` property update per frame (DisplayLink-batched). The string itself at 10,000 tokens is ~40-60KB -- trivial for memory. |
| Markdown parsing | After streaming completes, the full markdown parse of a 10,000-token response may take 50-100ms. Run this parse on a background thread via `Task.detached(priority: .userInitiated)`. While parsing, the raw text (with lightweight regex formatting) remains visible -- no blank flash. Once parsing completes, the rendered markdown replaces the raw text with a crossfade. |
| Scroll performance | The message bubble will be very tall. `LazyVStack` handles this via lazy rendering. The bubble is a single list item -- it may be 2000+ pt tall. SwiftUI's lazy stack ensures off-screen portions don't consume render resources. |
| Display | No "show more" / truncation on AI responses. The user should see the full response. The lazy stack ensures off-screen portions don't consume render resources. |
| Scroll-to-bottom during streaming | If the user is near the bottom, the auto-scroll keeps pace with token delivery. If the response is generating faster than the user can read, the scroll remains at the bottom (most recent content visible). The user can scroll up at any time to read earlier parts -- auto-scroll pauses per Section 2.6.1. |

### 2.8.8 Rapid-Fire User Messages

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| User sends while AI is streaming | The send button is replaced with a "Stop" button during streaming (Section 2.5.3). The user must either wait for streaming to complete or tap "Stop" first. After stopping, the send button reappears, and they can send their next message. |
| User sends multiple messages quickly (no streaming active) | Messages are queued and sent sequentially. Each message is dispatched only after the previous SSE stream completes. The queue is processed FIFO. All queued messages appear in the chat list immediately (optimistic, `status: .sending`). |
| User pastes and sends very rapidly | Debounce is NOT applied to sends -- every tap of the send button dispatches. But the sequential queue prevents concurrent SSE streams. |
| Typing while AI streams | Allowed and encouraged. The input field is always editable. Keystrokes must not be affected by streaming render activity (isolated view update scopes via `@Observable` ensure this). |

### 2.8.9 App Kill During Active Stream

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| User force-quits the app while AI is streaming | The SSE connection is severed. Partial response is NOT saved (no opportunity to persist). On next launch, the message list loads from the server-side history (`/api/chat/history`). The server should have persisted whatever was generated before the disconnect. If the server persisted a complete response, it appears in full. If the server only persisted a partial, it appears with `status: .partial`. |
| iOS terminates the app for memory pressure | Same as force-quit. No cleanup opportunity. Rely on server-side state as source of truth. |
| Crash during streaming | Same handling. The crash reporter logs the crash. On relaunch, the chat loads from server history. |

---

## 2.9 Technology Stack Summary

| Concern | Library / Approach |
|---|---|
| SSE Client | `URLSession` with `AsyncBytes` (`url.bytes.lines`) for line-by-line SSE parsing with custom exponential backoff wrapper |
| Virtualized List | SwiftUI `ScrollView` + `LazyVStack` with `ForEach` (stable `id` per message) |
| Keyboard Handling | SwiftUI built-in keyboard avoidance + `.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)` + `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` |
| Animations | SwiftUI `withAnimation()`, `.animation()` modifiers, `Phase​Animator` for multi-step sequences -- all GPU-accelerated via Core Animation |
| Markdown Rendering | `MarkdownUI` SPM package (gonzalezreal/swift-markdown-ui) with custom `Theme` for Skye styling |
| Syntax Highlighting | `Splash` SPM (JohnSundell/Splash) or `HighlightSwift` for native `AttributedString` syntax coloring |
| Image Loading | `AsyncImage` for simple cases; `Kingfisher` or `Nuke` SPM for advanced caching, blurhash placeholders, and prefetching |
| Haptic Feedback | `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator`, `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator`, `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator` (UIKit, available from SwiftUI via `.sensoryFeedback()` on iOS 17+) |
| Context Menus | SwiftUI `.contextMenu { }` modifier (native `UIContextMenuInteraction`) |
| Network State | `NWPathMonitor` from the `Network` framework |
| In-App Browser | `SFSafariViewController` via `UIViewControllerRepresentable` wrapper, or `@Environment(\.openURL)` for deep links |
| Charts (Market Analysis) | SwiftUI `Charts` framework (iOS 16+) with `LineMark`, `AreaMark` |
| Safe Area | SwiftUI automatic safe area handling + `GeometryReader` for manual inset reads |
| Navigation | `NavigationStack` with `NavigationPath` for programmatic navigation |
| Local Storage | `SwiftData` for structured persistence (messages, sessions); `UserDefaults` for lightweight KV (session IDs, queue); `Keychain` (via Security framework) for tokens |
| Image Picker | `PhotosUI` `PhotosPicker` for library access; `UIImagePickerController` wrapper for camera |
| Gesture Handling | SwiftUI `.gesture()` modifiers, `DragGesture`, `LongPressGesture`, `TapGesture` |
| Structured Concurrency | Swift `async/await`, `Task`, `TaskGroup`, `AsyncStream`, `AsyncThrowingStream` |
| Background Tasks | `UIApplication.shared.beginBackgroundTask(expirationHandler:)` |
| Accessibility | SwiftUI `.accessibilityLabel()`, `.accessibilityHint()`, `.accessibilityElement()`, `AccessibilityNotification.Announcement` |

---

## 2.10 Accessibility Requirements

The chat screen must be fully usable with VoiceOver enabled.

| Element | Accessibility Specification |
|---|---|
| Message bubbles | `.accessibilityElement(children: .combine)` + `.accessibilityLabel("[Sender] said: [message content]")`. `.accessibilityHint("Double tap to reveal timestamp. Long press for options.")` |
| Tool result cards | `.accessibilityElement(children: .combine)` + `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)` + `.accessibilityLabel("[Card type]: [summary]")`. E.g., "Contact card: Marcus Johnson, phone 555-123-4567." `.accessibilityHint("Double tap to view details.")` |
| Action confirmation buttons | `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)`. Labels: `accessibilityLabel("Confirm: [action]")` and `accessibilityLabel("Cancel")`. |
| Send button | `.accessibilityLabel("Send message")`. `.accessibilityAddTraits(hasText ? [] : .isStaticText)` + disabled trait when empty. |
| Attachment button | `.accessibilityLabel("Add attachment")`. `.accessibilityHint("Opens camera and photo options.")` |
| Typing indicator | `.accessibilityElement(children: .ignore)` + `.accessibilityLabel("Skye is thinking")`. Post announcement via `AccessibilityNotification.Announcement("Skye is thinking").post()`. |
| New message pill | `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)` + `.accessibilityLabel("New message. Tap to scroll to bottom.")` |
| Suggested prompts (empty state) | `.accessibilityAddTraits(.isButton)` + `.accessibilityLabel("Suggested prompt: [prompt text]")`. |
| Dynamic content | Streaming messages post an `AccessibilityNotification.Announcement` when the response is complete (not every token -- that would be unusable). Announce once when streaming ends: "Skye responded: [first 100 characters]." |
| Reduce Motion | Check `UIAccessibility.isReduceMotionEnabled` or use `@Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion)`. If true, disable: typing indicator bounce animation (show static dots), message slide-in animations (instant appear), pill animations. Functional behavior is unchanged. |

```swift
struct AccessibleMessageView: View {
    let message: AITextMessage
    @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) private var reduceMotion

    var body: some View {
        AIBubbleView(message: message, isLastInGroup: true, showAvatar: true)
            .accessibilityElement(children: .combine)
            .accessibilityLabel("Skye said: \(message.content)")
            .accessibilityHint("Double tap to reveal timestamp. Long press for options.")
            .onChange(of: message.isStreaming) { wasStreaming, isStreaming in
                if wasStreaming && !isStreaming {
                    let preview = String(message.content.prefix(100))
                    AccessibilityNotification.Announcement("Skye responded: \(preview)")
                        .post()
                }
            }
    }
}
```

---

This specification defines every aspect of the Skye chat system with implementation-ready precision for Swift/SwiftUI. Every visual measurement, timing value, state transition, and error handling path is explicitly defined using native iOS frameworks and modern Swift concurrency. A development team should be able to implement this screen from this document alone, without design ambiguity or architectural questions.

---

## V2: CRM Screens — People, Contacts, Properties (Swift/SwiftUI)

---

### 1. People Tab — Contact List

#### 1.1 List Virtualization Strategy

The People tab renders the full contact list using SwiftUI's `List` (backed by `UICollectionView` under the hood, fully virtualized by the system). With a target of 500+ contacts at 60fps, SwiftUI `List` with `LazyVStack` achieves native 60fps trivially without the manual tuning that React Native's FlashList or FlatList requires. The OS handles cell reuse, measurement, and off-screen view recycling automatically.

| Consideration | SwiftUI Approach | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Virtualization | `List` or `LazyVStack` inside `ScrollView` | Cells are lazily instantiated and reused by the system. No manual `windowSize` or `maxToRenderPerBatch` tuning is necessary. |
| Fixed-height rows | `.frame(height: 76)` on each row | When row heights are uniform, the system can compute scroll offsets without measurement passes. |
| Cell identity | `ForEach(contacts, id: \.id)` | Stable `Identifiable` conformance using the API `id`. Never use array indices. |
| Section headers | `ForEach(sectionedContacts)` with `Section(header:)` | When sort mode is `alphabetical`, the list uses `Section` headers. The system pins them automatically. |
| Off-screen recycling | Automatic | Unlike React Native, there is no need for `removeClippedSubviews` — SwiftUI `List` and `LazyVStack` manage the view lifecycle natively. |

**Section Header Consideration:** When sort mode is `alphabetical`, the list groups contacts into sections. Section headers are 32pt tall and sticky by default in `List`. A precomputed section structure is built once on data change:

```swift
struct SectionedContact: Identifiable {
    let id: String // The section letter
    let letter: String
    let contacts: [Contact]
}

func buildSections(from contacts: [Contact]) -> [SectionedContact] {
    let grouped = Dictionary(grouping: contacts) { contact in
        String(contact.lastName.prefix(1)).uppercased()
    }
    return grouped.map { SectionedContact(id: $0.key, letter: $0.key, contacts: $0.value) }
        .sorted { $0.letter < $1.letter }
}
```

#### 1.2 Contact List Item Layout

Each contact row is exactly **76pt tall** with the following internal layout:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 16pt padding-left                                                   │
│  ┌──────┐  12pt gap  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   Quick Icons │
│  │Avatar│             │ Name              ●(health) │   phone  msg  │
│  │ 44pt │             │ Company · Last Activity     │              │
│  └──────┘             └─────────────────────────────┘   16pt pad-R │
│ 16pt padding-left                                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Component breakdown:**

| Element | Spec |
|---|---|
| **Row container** | Height: 76pt. Padding: 16pt horizontal, 16pt vertical. `HStack` with `.alignment: .center`. Background: `Color(.systemBackground)`. Bottom border: 0.5pt `Color(.separator)` via `.overlay(Divider(), alignment: .bottom)`. |
| **Avatar** | 44pt x 44pt circle. If `contact.avatarURL` exists: `AsyncImage(url:)` with `.clipShape(Circle())`. If nil: initials fallback — background color derived from `contact.id.hashValue % 8` mapping to palette `[Color(hex: "#FF3B30"), Color(hex: "#FF9500"), Color(hex: "#FFCC00"), Color(hex: "#34C759"), Color(hex: "#007AFF"), Color(hex: "#5856D6"), Color(hex: "#AF52DE"), Color(hex: "#FF2D55")]`. Initials: first letter of first name + first letter of last name, uppercase, `.font(.system(size: 17, weight: .semibold))`, color `.white`. |
| **Name line** | `.font(.system(size: 17, weight: .semibold))`, `.foregroundStyle(.primary)`. Max 1 line, `.lineLimit(1)`, `.truncationMode(.tail)`. Engagement health dot appears 6pt to the right of the name text, vertically centered with the name baseline. |
| **Subtitle line** | `.font(.system(size: 15, weight: .regular))`, `.foregroundStyle(.secondary)`. Format: `"{company} · {lastActivityRelative}"`. If no company: `"{contactType} · {lastActivityRelative}"`. `lastActivityRelative` uses: "Just now", "2h ago", "Yesterday", "3d ago", "2w ago", "Mar 15". Max 1 line, truncated. |
| **Quick action icons** | Two icons in a horizontal row, spaced 12pt apart, positioned at the trailing edge. Phone icon: `Image(systemName: "phone.fill")`, tint `.green` (`#34C759`), 20pt. Message icon: `Image(systemName: "message.fill")`, tint `.blue` (`#007AFF`), 20pt. Each wrapped in a `Button` with a 36pt x 36pt `.frame` hit target. `onTap` triggers the respective action directly (calls `openURL(URL(string: "tel:")!)` or `openURL(URL(string: "sms:")!)`). Icons hidden if contact lacks phone/email respectively. |

#### 1.3 Engagement Health Dot System

The engagement health dot is a critical at-a-glance indicator. It encodes the recency of last meaningful interaction (call, email, meeting, note — not automated system events).

| Status | Color | Hex | Condition | Animation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Hot** | Red | `#FF3B30` | Last contact within 7 calendar days | Pulsing glow animation (see below) |
| **Warm** | Amber | `#FF9500` | Last contact 8-30 calendar days ago | Static |
| **Cold** | Blue | `#007AFF` | Last contact 31-90 calendar days ago | Static |
| **Inactive** | Gray | `#8E8E93` | Last contact 91+ days ago, or never | Static |

**Dot specifications:**

- **Size:** 8pt diameter circle (`.frame(width: 8, height: 8)`, `.clipShape(Circle())`).
- **Position on list row:** 6pt to the right of the last character of the contact name, vertically centered with the name text baseline. If name is truncated, dot appears 6pt after the ellipsis.
- **Position on detail header:** 8pt to the right of the name, vertically centered.
- **Shadow (all states):** `.shadow(color: dotColor.opacity(0.4), radius: 2, x: 0, y: 0)`.

**Hot pulsing animation:**

```swift
struct PulsingDotView: View {
    let color: Color
    @State private var isPulsing = false

    var body: some View {
        ZStack {
            // Pulse ring
            Circle()
                .fill(color)
                .frame(width: 8, height: 8)
                .scaleEffect(isPulsing ? 1.8 : 1.0)
                .opacity(isPulsing ? 0.0 : 0.4)

            // Core dot
            Circle()
                .fill(color)
                .frame(width: 8, height: 8)
                .shadow(color: color.opacity(0.4), radius: 2)
        }
        .onAppear {
            withAnimation(
                .easeOut(duration: 1.0)
                .repeatForever(autoreverses: false)
            ) {
                isPulsing = true
            }
        }
    }
}
```

The animation creates a ring that expands from 8pt to ~14.4pt (1.8x) while fading from 40% to 0% opacity over 1000ms, then instantly resets. The core dot remains static at 8pt with full opacity. The pulse ring is rendered as a separate `Circle` view behind the dot with the same fill color.

**Computation:** The engagement status is computed client-side from `contact.lastActivityDate` (ISO 8601 timestamp from the API). The computation runs once when data is loaded/updated and the result is cached per contact. Never recomputed during scroll.

```swift
enum EngagementStatus: String, Codable {
    case hot, warm, cold, inactive
}

func computeEngagement(lastActivityDate: Date?) -> EngagementStatus {
    guard let lastActivityDate else { return .inactive }
    let daysSince = Calendar.current.dateComponents([.day], from: lastActivityDate, to: .now).day ?? Int.max
    if daysSince <= 7 { return .hot }
    if daysSince <= 30 { return .warm }
    if daysSince <= 90 { return .cold }
    return .inactive
}
```

#### 1.4 Search

**Architecture:** The search bar is rendered using SwiftUI's `.searchable(text:placement:)` modifier with `.placement(.navigationBarDrawer(displayMode: .always))`, ensuring it remains persistently visible above the list and does not scroll away.

| Aspect | Specification |
|---|---|
| **Search bar component** | Native `.searchable()` modifier. The system provides the standard 36pt input with magnifying glass icon, clear button, and cancel behavior. Custom styling: placeholder "Search contacts...". The `.searchable` modifier handles keyboard presentation and dismissal automatically. |
| **Debounce** | 300ms debounce on text input. Uses a `Task` with `try await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(300))` that is cancelled on each keystroke via structured concurrency. |
| **Search pipeline** | 1. On text change (after debounce): search local SQLite cache first using FTS5 `MATCH` on `first_name`, `last_name`, `company`, `email`, `phone`. 2. Display local results immediately. 3. Simultaneously fire `GET /api/contacts/search?q={query}` to the server. 4. When server results arrive, merge with local results (deduplicate by `contact.id`), preferring server data for any conflicts. 5. Update display. |
| **Result highlighting** | Matching substrings in name, company, email, and phone are rendered using `AttributedString` with `.backgroundColor` set to `Color.yellow.opacity(0.25)` (light mode) or `Color.yellow.opacity(0.15)` (dark mode) and `.font(.body.weight(.bold))`. |
| **Empty search state** | If query returns 0 results: `ContentUnavailableView` (iOS 17+) with image `Image(systemName: "magnifyingglass.circle")`, text "No contacts found", description "Try a different search or add a new contact", and an "Add Contact" button via `.actions`. |
| **Cancel behavior** | Handled natively by `.searchable()`. Tapping Cancel clears the query, dismisses the keyboard, and restores the full contact list. The `List` scroll position is preserved across search/cancel cycles via `ScrollViewReader`. |
| **Keyboard handling** | SwiftUI `List` automatically dismisses the keyboard on scroll (equivalent to `keyboardDismissMode: 'on-drag'`). Use `.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)` for interactive dismiss behavior. |

#### 1.5 Filter Chips

**Layout:** A horizontally scrollable `ScrollView(.horizontal, showsIndicators: false)` rendered between the search bar and the `List`. Height: 48pt (including 8pt top + 8pt bottom padding). Content inset: 16pt on both sides via `.contentMargins(.horizontal, 16)`.

**Chip design:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Height | 32pt |
| Padding | 8pt horizontal, 0pt vertical |
| Corner radius | 16pt (fully rounded pill via `Capsule()` shape) |
| Background (unselected) | `Color(.systemGray6)` |
| Background (selected) | `Color.accentColor` (`#007AFF`) |
| Text color (unselected) | `Color(.label)` |
| Text color (selected) | `Color.white` |
| Font | `.font(.system(size: 14, weight: .medium))` |
| Spacing between chips | 8pt |
