```
      |                  |                    |  table             |                |
      |                  |                    |                    |--------------->|
      |                  |                    |                    |                |
      |                  |                    |                    |<---------------|
      |                  |                    |                    |                |
      |                  |                    |  11. Generate      |                |
      |                  |                    |  Skye session:     |                |
      |                  |                    |  - access_token    |                |
      |                  |                    |    (JWT, 1hr)      |                |
      |                  |                    |  - refresh_token   |                |
      |                  |                    |    (opaque, 30d)   |                |
      |                  |                    |  - Sign Supabase   |                |
      |                  |                    |    JWT with        |                |
      |                  |                    |    SUPABASE_JWT_   |                |
      |                  |                    |    SECRET          |                |
      |                  |                    |                    |                |
      |  12. Return { accessToken, refreshToken, expiresAt,       |                |
      |               supabaseAccessToken, user }                 |                |
      |<-------------------------------------------------------|                |
      |                  |                    |                    |                |
      |  13. Store in    |                    |                    |                |
      |  Keychain via    |                    |                    |                |
      |  KeychainManager:|                    |                    |                |
      |  - accessToken   |                    |                    |                |
      |  - refreshToken  |                    |                    |                |
      |  - supabaseToken |                    |                    |                |
      |  - expiresAt     |                    |                    |                |
      |                  |                    |                    |                |
      |  14. Navigate    |                    |                    |                |
      |  to Home screen  |                    |                    |                |
      v                  v                    v                    v                v
```

#### 1.3 Redirect URI Configuration

**Decision: Neither custom scheme nor Universal Links is required for the primary flow.**

The `GoogleSignIn-iOS` SDK uses the native Google Sign-In flow, which handles the OAuth flow entirely within the native layer using `ASWebAuthenticationSession` internally. No redirect URI is configured in the app itself; the Google SDK manages the callback internally using the reversed iOS client ID as a URL scheme (e.g., `com.googleusercontent.apps.XXXX`).

However, a custom URL scheme is still registered for other deep linking purposes:

| Purpose | URI | Registration |
|---|---|---|
| Google SDK internal callback | `com.googleusercontent.apps.{IOS_CLIENT_ID}` | Automatic via `GoogleService-Info.plist` + `Info.plist` URL Types |
| App deep links (notifications, etc.) | `com.skyecrm://` | `Info.plist` > `CFBundleURLTypes` |
| Universal Links (future web-to-app) | `https://app.skyecrm.com/.well-known/apple-app-site-association` | AASA file on server + Associated Domains entitlement |

The deep link scheme `com.skyecrm://` will be validated as described in Section 6.6 (Deep Link Injection).

#### 1.4 Google Cloud Console Setup

Create a **separate iOS OAuth Client ID** in the Google Cloud Console. Do NOT reuse the web client ID.

**Configuration:**

| Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| **Project** | `skye-crm` (same GCP project as web) |
| **Application type** | iOS |
| **Bundle ID** | `com.skyecrm.app` |
| **App Store ID** | (add after first App Store submission) |
| **Team ID** | (Apple Developer Team ID) |

**Required OAuth Scopes:**

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `openid` | Required for ID token |
| `email` | User email for account lookup |
| `profile` | User name and avatar |

No additional Google API scopes are required. Skye does not access Google Calendar, Gmail, or Drive. Requesting minimal scopes keeps the consent screen simple and approval fast.

**Consent Screen Configuration:**

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| App name | Skye CRM |
| User support email | support@skyecrm.com |
| App logo | 120x120 Skye logo |
| Application home page | https://skyecrm.com |
| Application privacy policy | https://skyecrm.com/privacy |
| Application terms of service | https://skyecrm.com/terms |
| Authorized domains | skyecrm.com |
| Publishing status | Production (after verification) |

**Credential Files:**

- Download `GoogleService-Info.plist` for iOS and place it in the Xcode project root (ensure it is added to the app target).
- Add the reversed client ID as a URL scheme in `Info.plist` under `CFBundleURLTypes`.
- The **web client ID** (from the existing web OAuth credential) is required as the `serverClientID` parameter in the `GIDConfiguration` to enable `serverAuthCode` generation for backend exchange.

**Xcode Project Configuration (`Info.plist`):**

```xml
<!-- Info.plist -->
<key>CFBundleURLTypes</key>
<array>
  <dict>
    <key>CFBundleTypeRole</key>
    <string>Editor</string>
    <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
    <array>
      <!-- Reversed iOS client ID from GoogleService-Info.plist -->
      <string>com.googleusercontent.apps.IOS_CLIENT_ID_HERE</string>
    </array>
  </dict>
</array>

<key>GIDClientID</key>
<string>IOS_CLIENT_ID_HERE.apps.googleusercontent.com</string>

<key>GIDServerClientID</key>
<string>WEB_CLIENT_ID_HERE.apps.googleusercontent.com</string>
```

**App entry point configuration (`SkyeApp.swift`):**

```swift
import SwiftUI
import GoogleSignIn

@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView()
                .onOpenURL { url in
                    GIDSignIn.sharedInstance.handle(url)
                }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 1.5 Web App Compatibility

The native auth flow and the existing web NextAuth flow MUST coexist without interference. This is achieved through architectural separation at the OAuth layer and convergence at the user and session data layer.

**Architecture:**

```
                    +--------------------------------+
                    |       Google Cloud Console      |
                    |       (Single GCP Project)      |
                    +----------------+---------------+
                    |  Web Client ID | iOS Client ID  |
                    |  (type: web)   | (type: iOS)    |
                    +-------+--------+-------+--------+
                            |                |
                  +---------v------+  +------v---------+
                  |  NextAuth      |  |  GoogleSignIn   |
                  |  (Web App)     |  |  iOS SDK (SPM)  |
                  |  Cookie-based  |  |  serverAuthCode |
                  |  sessions      |  |  to backend     |
                  +-------+--------+  +------+---------+
                          |                  |
                          v                  v
                  +----------------------------------+
                  |     Next.js API Routes           |
                  +--------------+-------------------+
                  | /api/auth/*  | /api/auth/native   |
                  | (NextAuth)   | (Native endpoint)  |
                  +------+-------+----------+--------+
                         |                  |
                         v                  v
                  +----------------------------------+
                  |     Shared Supabase Database      |
                  |     +---------------------+      |
                  |     |   users table        |      |
                  |     |   (single source     |      |
                  |     |    of truth)          |      |
                  |     +---------------------+      |
                  |     +---------------------+      |
                  |     |   sessions table     |      |
                  |     |   (web + native)     |      |
                  |     +---------------------+      |
                  |     Row Level Security (RLS)      |
                  +----------------------------------+
```

**Key compatibility guarantees:**

1. **Separate Client IDs:** The web app uses a `Web Application` type OAuth client ID. The iOS app uses an `iOS` type OAuth client ID. Both are in the same GCP project and share the same consent screen.

2. **Shared User Table:** Both flows resolve to the same `users` row. The `/api/auth/native` endpoint looks up users by `google_sub` (Google's stable user identifier) or `email`. If a user signed up via web, their first native sign-in finds the existing record. If a user signs up via native first, their first web sign-in finds the existing record.

3. **Compatible Session Format:** The backend mints a Supabase-compatible JWT for both flows. The JWT payload structure:

```json
{
  "aud": "authenticated",
  "exp": 1709423400,
  "iat": 1709419800,
  "iss": "https://your-project.supabase.co/auth/v1",
  "sub": "user-uuid-from-users-table",
  "email": "agent@example.com",
  "role": "authenticated",
  "app_metadata": {
    "provider": "google",
    "client": "native"
  },
  "user_metadata": {
    "full_name": "Jane Agent",
    "avatar_url": "https://..."
  }
}
```

This JWT is signed with the `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` and is accepted by Supabase RLS policies. The `sub` claim matches the user's UUID in the `users` table, so `auth.uid()` in RLS policies resolves correctly regardless of whether the session originated from web or native.

4. **Independent Sessions:** Web sessions are cookie-based (managed by NextAuth). Native sessions are token-based (managed by the native app via Keychain). A user can be logged into both simultaneously. Revoking one does not revoke the other unless "Log out all devices" is invoked (see Section 7.2).

5. **No NextAuth modification required:** The `/api/auth/native` endpoint is a standalone Next.js API route. It does not import or modify NextAuth configuration. NextAuth continues to operate via `/api/auth/[...nextauth]` exactly as before.

**Backend endpoint specification: `POST /api/auth/native`**

```
Request:
  POST /api/auth/native
  Content-Type: application/json
  {
    "serverAuthCode": "4/0AX4X...",
    "platform": "ios",
    "deviceId": "device-fingerprint-uuid",
    "deviceName": "iPhone 15 Pro"
  }

Response (success):
  200 OK
  {
    "accessToken": "eyJhbG...",          // JWT, 1hr expiry
    "refreshToken": "rt_a8f3c...",       // Opaque, 30-day expiry
    "expiresAt": 1709423400,             // Unix timestamp
    "supabaseAccessToken": "eyJhbG...",  // Supabase-compatible JWT
    "user": {
      "id": "uuid",
      "email": "agent@example.com",
      "name": "Jane Agent",
      "avatarUrl": "https://...",
      "createdAt": "2024-01-15T00:00:00Z"
    }
  }

Response (error):
  401 Unauthorized
  { "error": "invalid_auth_code", "message": "..." }
```

#### 1.6 Error Handling Matrix

Every OAuth failure mode must be handled gracefully. The native app must NEVER show a raw error or crash.

| Error Scenario | Detection Method | User-Facing Behavior | Technical Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| **User cancellation** | `GIDSignInError.canceled` from `GIDSignIn.sharedInstance.signIn()` | Silently return to sign-in screen. No error message. | No-op. Log analytics event `auth_cancelled`. |
| **Network error (no internet)** | `URLError.notConnectedToInternet` or `NWPathMonitor` check | Show inline banner: "No internet connection. Please check your connection and try again." | Check `NWPathMonitor` before initiating. Retry button. |
| **Google server error** | `GIDSignInError` with non-cancel code or HTTP 5xx from Google | Show modal: "Google sign-in is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes." | Exponential backoff retry (max 3 attempts). Log to Sentry. |
| **EMM error** | `GIDSignInError.hasNoAuthInKeychain` or EMM-related errors | iOS: Log as anomaly. | Log to Sentry with device info. |
| **Invalid server auth code** | Backend `/api/auth/native` returns 401 `invalid_auth_code` | Show modal: "Authentication failed. Please try again." | Clear any partial state, redirect to sign-in. Log to Sentry. |
| **Account not authorized** | Backend returns 403 `account_not_authorized` | Show modal: "This Google account is not authorized to use Skye. Contact your administrator." | Do not retry. Show "Use a different account" link. |
| **Backend unreachable** | `URLSession` timeout (15s) or network error on `/api/auth/native` | Show inline banner: "Unable to connect to Skye. Please try again." | Retry with exponential backoff. Check server status endpoint first. |
| **Duplicate account (email conflict)** | Backend returns 409 `email_conflict` | Show modal: "An account with this email already exists. Please sign in with your existing account." | Offer "Contact support" link. |
| **Rate limited** | Backend returns 429 | Show modal: "Too many sign-in attempts. Please wait a moment." | Respect `Retry-After` header. Disable sign-in button with countdown. |
| **Token storage failure** | Keychain `SecItemAdd` returns non-`errSecSuccess` | Show modal: "Unable to save your session securely. Please restart the app." | Log to Sentry with error details. Attempt cleanup and retry once. |

**Implementation pattern:**

```swift
import GoogleSignIn
import UIKit

enum AuthError: Error {
    case noServerAuthCode
    case cancelled
    case networkUnavailable
    case apiError(statusCode: Int, message: String)
    case tokenStorageFailed
    case unknown(Error)
}

@MainActor
final class GoogleAuthService {
    private let apiClient: APIClient
    private let keychainManager: KeychainManager
    private let analytics: AnalyticsService

    init(apiClient: APIClient, keychainManager: KeychainManager, analytics: AnalyticsService) {
        self.apiClient = apiClient
        self.keychainManager = keychainManager
        self.analytics = analytics
    }

    func handleGoogleSignIn(presenting viewController: UIViewController) async throws {
        do {
            let result = try await GIDSignIn.sharedInstance.signIn(
                withPresenting: viewController,
                hint: nil,
                additionalScopes: []
            )

            guard let serverAuthCode = result.serverAuthCode else {
                throw AuthError.noServerAuthCode
            }

            let deviceId = await DeviceIdentifier.getOrCreate()
            let deviceName = UIDevice.current.name

            let response: AuthResponse = try await apiClient.post(
                "/api/auth/native",
                body: NativeAuthRequest(
                    serverAuthCode: serverAuthCode,
                    platform: "ios",
                    deviceId: deviceId,
                    deviceName: deviceName
                )
            )

            try keychainManager.saveTokens(
                TokenSet(
                    accessToken: response.accessToken,
                    refreshToken: response.refreshToken,
                    supabaseAccessToken: response.supabaseAccessToken,
                    expiresAt: response.expiresAt
                )
            )

            // Navigation handled by @Observable AuthState change

        } catch let error as GIDSignInError {
            switch error.code {
            case .canceled:
                analytics.track("auth_cancelled")
                return // Silent return
            case .hasNoAuthInKeychain:
                SentrySDK.capture(error: error)
                return
            default:
                throw AuthError.unknown(error)
            }
        } catch let error as AuthError {
            throw error
        } catch {
            SentrySDK.capture(error: error)
            throw AuthError.unknown(error)
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 2. Token Management

#### 2.1 Token Types

Skye uses a dual-token architecture with an additional Supabase-specific token for direct database access.

| Token | Format | Lifetime | Purpose | Storage |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Access Token** | JWT (signed with app secret) | 1 hour | Authenticates API requests to Skye backend | Keychain key: `skye_access_token` |
| **Refresh Token** | Opaque string (UUID-based, `rt_` prefix) | 30 days | Obtains new access tokens without re-authentication | Keychain key: `skye_refresh_token` |
| **Supabase Access Token** | JWT (signed with `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET`) | 1 hour (rotated alongside access token) | Direct Supabase queries with RLS enforcement | Keychain key: `skye_supabase_token` |
| **Token Expiry** | Unix timestamp (number) | N/A | Client-side expiry check | Keychain key: `skye_token_expires_at` |

**Access Token JWT Claims:**

```json
{
  "sub": "user-uuid",
  "email": "agent@example.com",
  "iat": 1709419800,
  "exp": 1709423400,
  "iss": "skye-api",
  "aud": "skye-native",
  "jti": "unique-token-id",
  "session_id": "session-uuid",
  "client": "native-ios"
}
```

**Refresh Token Server-Side Record:**

```sql
CREATE TABLE refresh_tokens (
  id          UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  token_hash  TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- SHA-256 hash, never store plaintext
  user_id     UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  session_id  UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  device_id   TEXT NOT NULL,
  expires_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL,
  created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  revoked_at  TIMESTAMPTZ,          -- Non-null = revoked
  replaced_by UUID REFERENCES refresh_tokens(id) -- Token rotation chain
);
```

#### 2.2 Secure Storage

**Mandatory: iOS Keychain via `KeychainManager` wrapper (direct `Security.framework` calls). NEVER use `UserDefaults`, files, or in-memory storage for tokens.**

**Configuration:**

```swift
import Foundation
import Security

/// Thread-safe Keychain wrapper for Skye token storage.
/// Uses `kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly` for all items.
final class KeychainManager: Sendable {

    enum Key: String, CaseIterable, Sendable {
        case accessToken = "skye_access_token"
        case refreshToken = "skye_refresh_token"
        case supabaseToken = "skye_supabase_token"
        case tokenExpiresAt = "skye_token_expires_at"
        case biometricEnabled = "skye_biometric_enabled"
    }

    private let service = "com.skyecrm.app"

    // MARK: - Core Operations

    func set(_ value: String, for key: Key) throws {
        let data = Data(value.utf8)

        // Delete existing item first (SecItemUpdate is unreliable across OS versions)
        delete(key)

        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrService as String: service,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key.rawValue,
            kSecValueData as String: data,
            kSecAttrAccessible as String: kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly
            // AFTER_FIRST_UNLOCK_THIS_DEVICE_ONLY:
            //   - Accessible after first unlock (supports background refresh)
            //   - THIS_DEVICE_ONLY prevents iCloud Keychain sync (tokens stay on-device)
            //   - NOT available before first unlock (acceptable trade-off)
            //
            // We do NOT use kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlocked because:
            //   - It prevents background token refresh (e.g., push notification handling)
            //
            // We do NOT use kSecAttrAccessibleAlways because:
            //   - It is deprecated by Apple and is the least secure option
        ]

        let status = SecItemAdd(query as CFDictionary, nil)
        guard status == errSecSuccess else {
            throw KeychainError.unableToStore(status: status)
        }
    }

    func get(_ key: Key) -> String? {
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrService as String: service,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key.rawValue,
            kSecReturnData as String: true,
            kSecMatchLimit as String: kSecMatchLimitOne
        ]

        var result: AnyObject?
        let status = SecItemCopyMatching(query as CFDictionary, &result)

        guard status == errSecSuccess, let data = result as? Data else {
            return nil
        }

        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
    }

    @discardableResult
    func delete(_ key: Key) -> Bool {
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrService as String: service,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key.rawValue
        ]

        let status = SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary)
        return status == errSecSuccess || status == errSecItemNotFound
    }

    // MARK: - Token Operations

    func saveTokens(_ tokens: TokenSet) throws {
        try set(tokens.accessToken, for: .accessToken)
        try set(tokens.refreshToken, for: .refreshToken)
        try set(tokens.supabaseAccessToken, for: .supabaseToken)
        try set(String(tokens.expiresAt), for: .tokenExpiresAt)
    }

    func getAccessToken() -> String? {
        get(.accessToken)
    }

    func getRefreshToken() -> String? {
        get(.refreshToken)
    }

    func isTokenExpired() -> Bool {
        guard let expiresAtString = get(.tokenExpiresAt),
              let expiresAt = TimeInterval(expiresAtString) else {
            return true
        }
        // Add 60-second buffer to avoid edge-case expiry during request
        return Date().timeIntervalSince1970 >= expiresAt - 60
    }

    func clearAll() {
        Key.allCases.forEach { delete($0) }
    }
}

enum KeychainError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case unableToStore(status: OSStatus)
    case unableToRetrieve(status: OSStatus)

    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .unableToStore(let status):
            return "Keychain store failed with status: \(status)"
        case .unableToRetrieve(let status):
            return "Keychain retrieve failed with status: \(status)"
        }
    }
}
```

**Size constraint:** Keychain items have no practical size limit on iOS, but JWTs should be kept compact for performance. If the access token exceeds 1500 bytes, audit the claims for unnecessary data.

**Persistence behavior:** iOS Keychain data may persist after app uninstall if the app is reinstalled with the same bundle ID. On reinstall, check for orphaned tokens and validate them against the server before use. If the server rejects them, clear them silently.

#### 2.3 Token Refresh Flow

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                          TOKEN REFRESH STATE MACHINE                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|   +----------+     token valid      +--------------+                         |
|   |  IDLE    | ------------------> |  MAKE API     |                         |
|   |          |                      |  REQUEST      |                         |
|   +----+-----+                      +------+--------+                         |
|        |                                   |                                  |
|        | token expired                     | 200 OK                           |
|        | (checked before                   v                                  |
|        |  every request)          +--------------+                            |
|        |                          |  RETURN       |                            |
|        |                          |  RESPONSE     |                            |
|        v                          +--------------+                            |
|   +----------+                                                                |
|   | ACQUIRE  |                                                                |
|   | LOCK     | <---- Other requests wait here                                 |
|   +----+-----+                                                                |
|        |                                                                      |
|        | lock acquired                                                        |
|        v                                                                      |
|   +--------------+     +--------------+                                      |
|   | CHECK AGAIN  |---->| TOKEN NOW    | (another request refreshed it)        |
|   | (double-     |     | VALID        |---------> MAKE API REQUEST            |
|   |  check)      |     +--------------+                                      |
|   +----+---------+                                                            |
|        |                                                                      |
|        | still expired                                                        |
|        v                                                                      |
|   +--------------+                                                            |
|   | POST         |                                                            |
|   | /api/auth/   |                                                            |
|   | refresh      |                                                            |
|   +----+----+----+                                                            |
|        |    |                                                                 |
|   200 OK    | 401 (refresh token                                              |
|        |    |  revoked/expired)                                                |
|        v    |                                                                 |
|   +---------+  |   +-------------+    +--------------+                       |
|   | SAVE    |  +-->| CLEAR       |--->| NAVIGATE TO  |                       |
|   | NEW     |      | ALL TOKENS  |    | AUTH SCREEN  |                       |
|   | TOKENS  |      +-------------+    +--------------+                       |
|   +----+----+                                                                 |
|        |                                                                      |
|        | release lock                                                         |
|        v                                                                      |
|   +--------------+                                                            |
|   | RETRY        |                                                            |
|   | ORIGINAL     |                                                            |
|   | REQUEST      |                                                            |
|   +--------------+                                                            |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

**Refresh endpoint specification:**

```
Request:
  POST /api/auth/refresh
  Content-Type: application/json
  {
    "refreshToken": "rt_a8f3c..."
  }

Response (success):
  200 OK
  {
    "accessToken": "eyJhbG...",           // New JWT
    "refreshToken": "rt_b9d4e...",        // NEW refresh token (old one invalidated)
    "supabaseAccessToken": "eyJhbG...",   // New Supabase JWT
    "expiresAt": 1709427000
  }

Response (failure):
  401 Unauthorized
  { "error": "refresh_token_revoked" }
```

#### 2.4 Race Condition Handling: Token Refresh via Actor

When multiple API calls fire simultaneously and the token is expired, only ONE refresh request must execute. All others must wait for its result. Swift actors provide built-in serialisation, eliminating the need for manual mutexes.

```swift
import Foundation

/// Actor-based token manager ensuring thread-safe refresh.
/// Only one refresh can execute at a time; concurrent callers await the same result.
actor TokenManager {
    private let keychainManager: KeychainManager
    private let apiBaseURL: URL
    private var activeRefreshTask: Task<String, Error>?

    init(keychainManager: KeychainManager, apiBaseURL: URL) {
        self.keychainManager = keychainManager
        self.apiBaseURL = apiBaseURL
    }

    func getValidAccessToken() async throws -> String {
        // Fast path: token is still valid
        if !keychainManager.isTokenExpired(),
           let token = keychainManager.getAccessToken() {
            return token
        }

        // If a refresh is already in progress, wait for it
        if let existingTask = activeRefreshTask {
            return try await existingTask.value
        }

        // Start a new refresh
        let task = Task<String, Error> {
            defer { activeRefreshTask = nil }

            // Double-check after acquiring the actor (another caller may have refreshed)
            if !keychainManager.isTokenExpired(),
               let token = keychainManager.getAccessToken() {
                return token
            }

            guard let refreshToken = keychainManager.getRefreshToken() else {
                throw AuthExpiredError.noRefreshToken
            }

            // Perform the refresh
            let url = apiBaseURL.appendingPathComponent("/api/auth/refresh")
            var request = URLRequest(url: url)
            request.httpMethod = "POST"
            request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
            request.httpBody = try JSONEncoder().encode(
                RefreshRequest(refreshToken: refreshToken)
            )

            let (data, response) = try await URLSession.shared.data(for: request)

            guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
                throw NetworkError.invalidResponse
            }

            if httpResponse.statusCode == 401 {
                keychainManager.clearAll()
                throw AuthExpiredError.refreshTokenRevoked
            }

            guard httpResponse.statusCode == 200 else {
                throw NetworkError.httpError(statusCode: httpResponse.statusCode)
            }

            let tokens = try JSONDecoder().decode(TokenRefreshResponse.self, from: data)
            try keychainManager.saveTokens(
                TokenSet(
                    accessToken: tokens.accessToken,
                    refreshToken: tokens.refreshToken,
                    supabaseAccessToken: tokens.supabaseAccessToken,
                    expiresAt: tokens.expiresAt
                )
            )

            return tokens.accessToken
        }

        activeRefreshTask = task
        return try await task.value
    }
}

// MARK: - Supporting Types

enum AuthExpiredError: Error {
    case noRefreshToken
    case refreshTokenRevoked
}

enum NetworkError: Error {
    case invalidResponse
    case httpError(statusCode: Int)
}

struct RefreshRequest: Codable {
    let refreshToken: String
}

struct TokenRefreshResponse: Codable {
    let accessToken: String
    let refreshToken: String
    let supabaseAccessToken: String
    let expiresAt: TimeInterval
}

struct TokenSet {
    let accessToken: String
    let refreshToken: String
    let supabaseAccessToken: String
    let expiresAt: TimeInterval
}
```

**No external dependencies required.** Swift actors provide serialisation guarantees natively, replacing `async-mutex`.

#### 2.5 Token Rotation

On every refresh, the server performs the following atomically (within a database transaction):

1. Look up the refresh token by its SHA-256 hash.
2. Verify it is not revoked (`revoked_at IS NULL`) and not expired (`expires_at > now()`).
3. Generate a new refresh token.
4. Mark the old refresh token as revoked (`revoked_at = now()`) and set `replaced_by` to the new token's ID.
5. Insert the new refresh token record.
6. Generate new access token and Supabase access token.
7. Return all new tokens to the client.

**Reuse detection:** If a revoked refresh token is presented, it indicates potential token theft. The server must:

1. Walk the `replaced_by` chain to find the most recent token in the family.
2. Revoke the ENTIRE token family (all tokens in the chain).
3. Return 401 with `error: "token_family_revoked"`.
4. Send a security alert email to the user.
5. Log the incident to the security audit log.

This prevents stolen refresh tokens from being used even once after the legitimate client has already refreshed.

#### 2.6 Logout Flow

Logout must be thorough. Partial logout is a security vulnerability.

```swift
import GoogleSignIn

@MainActor
final class LogoutService {
    private let keychainManager: KeychainManager
    private let apiClient: APIClient
    private let offlineDatabase: OfflineDatabase
    private let imageCache: ImageCacheManager
    private let supabaseClient: SupabaseClient
    private let appState: AppState

    init(
        keychainManager: KeychainManager,
        apiClient: APIClient,
        offlineDatabase: OfflineDatabase,
        imageCache: ImageCacheManager,
        supabaseClient: SupabaseClient,
        appState: AppState
    ) {
        self.keychainManager = keychainManager
        self.apiClient = apiClient
        self.offlineDatabase = offlineDatabase
        self.imageCache = imageCache
        self.supabaseClient = supabaseClient
        self.appState = appState
    }

    func logout() async {
        do {
            // 1. Revoke refresh token on server (best-effort)
            if let refreshToken = keychainManager.getRefreshToken(),
               let accessToken = keychainManager.getAccessToken() {
                try? await apiClient.post(
                    "/api/auth/logout",
                    body: LogoutRequest(refreshToken: refreshToken),
                    bearerToken: accessToken
                )
                // Server revocation failed (offline, etc.)
                // Continue with local cleanup -- token will expire naturally
                // Server should also clean expired tokens via cron
            }

            // 2. Clear Keychain (all tokens)
            keychainManager.clearAll()

            // 3. Clear offline database
            try? await offlineDatabase.deleteAllData()

            // 4. Clear image cache
            try? await imageCache.clearAll()

            // 5. Clear any in-memory caches
            appState.reset()

            // 6. Clear Supabase client session
            try? await supabaseClient.auth.signOut()

            // 7. Sign out from Google (clears cached Google credential)
            GIDSignIn.sharedInstance.signOut()

            // 8. Navigate to auth screen (reset navigation via @Observable state)
            appState.authStatus = .unauthenticated

        } catch {
            // Even if steps fail, ensure local state is cleared
            SentrySDK.capture(error: error)
            keychainManager.clearAll()
            appState.authStatus = .unauthenticated
        }
    }
}
```

#### 2.7 Biometric Re-Authentication

Optional Face ID / Touch ID to unlock the app after it has been in the background. This is a user-configurable setting (disabled by default).

**When triggered:**

- App returns from background after more than 5 minutes (configurable).
- User opens the Settings > Security screen.
- Before executing high-sensitivity actions (optional, e.g., deleting a deal).

**Implementation:**

```swift
import LocalAuthentication

actor BiometricGate {
    private let keychainManager: KeychainManager

    init(keychainManager: KeychainManager) {
        self.keychainManager = keychainManager
    }

    func authenticate() async -> Bool {
        let biometricEnabled = keychainManager.get(.biometricEnabled)
        guard biometricEnabled == "true" else {
            return true // Not enabled, pass through
        }

        let context = LAContext()
        var error: NSError?

        let canEvaluate = context.canEvaluatePolicy(
            .deviceOwnerAuthenticationWithBiometrics,
            error: &error
        )

        guard canEvaluate else {
            // Hardware unavailable or no biometrics enrolled
            // Fall back to allowing access (don't lock the user out)
            return true
        }

        do {
            let success = try await context.evaluatePolicy(
                .deviceOwnerAuthentication, // Allows device passcode as fallback
                localizedReason: "Unlock Skye"
            )
            return success
        } catch {
            // Authentication failed or cancelled
            // User stays on the lock screen
            return false
        }
    }
}
```

**Required `Info.plist` configuration for Face ID:**

```xml
<!-- Info.plist -->
<key>NSFaceIDUsageDescription</key>
<string>Skye uses Face ID to secure your account and protect sensitive client data.</string>
```

**App lifecycle integration (SwiftUI `@Environment(\.scenePhase)`):**

```swift
import SwiftUI

@Observable
final class AppLockManager {
    var isLocked = false
    private var backgroundTimestamp: Date?
    private let lockTimeout: TimeInterval = 5 * 60 // 5 minutes
    private let biometricGate: BiometricGate

    init(biometricGate: BiometricGate) {
        self.biometricGate = biometricGate
    }

    func handleScenePhaseChange(_ phase: ScenePhase) {
        switch phase {
        case .background:
            backgroundTimestamp = Date()
        case .active:
            if let timestamp = backgroundTimestamp {
                let elapsed = Date().timeIntervalSince(timestamp)
                backgroundTimestamp = nil
                if elapsed > lockTimeout {
                    isLocked = true
                    Task {
                        let authenticated = await biometricGate.authenticate()
                        await MainActor.run {
                            isLocked = !authenticated
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        case .inactive:
            break
        @unknown default:
            break
        }
    }
}

// Usage in root view:
struct ContentView: View {
    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase
    @State private var appLockManager: AppLockManager

    var body: some View {
        ZStack {
            MainTabView()

            if appLockManager.isLocked {
                BiometricLockScreen()
            }
        }
        .onChange(of: scenePhase) { _, newPhase in
            appLockManager.handleScenePhaseChange(newPhase)
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 3. API Client Security

#### 3.1 Base API Client Architecture

All HTTP communication flows through a single, centralized API client. No view or service may make direct `URLSession` calls to the backend.

```swift
import Foundation

/// Centralized API client. All backend communication flows through this single point.
actor APIClient {
    private let baseURL: URL
    private let session: URLSession
    private let tokenManager: TokenManager
    private let appVersion: String

    #if DEBUG
    static let apiBaseURL = URL(string: "https://dev-api.skyecrm.com")!
    #else
    static let apiBaseURL = URL(string: "https://api.skyecrm.com")!
    #endif

    init(
        baseURL: URL = APIClient.apiBaseURL,
        tokenManager: TokenManager,
        appVersion: String
    ) {
        self.baseURL = baseURL
        self.tokenManager = tokenManager
        self.appVersion = appVersion

        let config = URLSessionConfiguration.default
        config.timeoutIntervalForRequest = 15 // 15s default
        config.httpAdditionalHeaders = [
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "X-Client-Platform": "ios",
            "X-Client-Version": appVersion
        ]
        self.session = URLSession(configuration: config)
    }

    func request<T: Decodable>(
        _ method: String,
        path: String,
        body: (any Encodable)? = nil,
        timeout: TimeInterval? = nil,
        requiresAuth: Bool = true
    ) async throws -> T {
        var url = baseURL.appendingPathComponent(path)
        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
        request.httpMethod = method

        if let timeout {
            request.timeoutInterval = timeout
        }

        if let body {
            request.httpBody = try JSONEncoder().encode(body)
        }

        // Auth header injection -- the ONLY place where the access token is read and attached
        if requiresAuth && !path.hasPrefix("/api/auth/") {
            let accessToken = try await tokenManager.getValidAccessToken()
            request.setValue("Bearer \(accessToken)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        }

        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)

        guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
            throw NetworkError.invalidResponse
        }

        // Handle 401 with automatic retry after token refresh
        if httpResponse.statusCode == 401 && requiresAuth {
            let newToken = try await tokenManager.getValidAccessToken()
            request.setValue("Bearer \(newToken)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
            let (retryData, retryResponse) = try await session.data(for: request)

            guard let retryHTTP = retryResponse as? HTTPURLResponse else {
                throw NetworkError.invalidResponse
            }

            if retryHTTP.statusCode == 401 {
                NotificationCenter.default.post(name: .sessionExpired, object: nil)
                throw AuthExpiredError.refreshTokenRevoked
            }

            return try JSONDecoder().decode(T.self, from: retryData)
        }

        // Handle rate limiting
        if httpResponse.statusCode == 429 {
            let retryAfter = httpResponse.value(forHTTPHeaderField: "Retry-After")
            let waitSeconds = retryAfter.flatMap(Double.init) ?? 5.0
            throw RateLimitError(retryAfterSeconds: waitSeconds)
        }

        guard (200...299).contains(httpResponse.statusCode) else {
            throw NetworkError.httpError(statusCode: httpResponse.statusCode)
        }

        return try JSONDecoder().decode(T.self, from: data)
    }

    // MARK: - Convenience Methods

    func get<T: Decodable>(_ path: String, timeout: TimeInterval? = nil) async throws -> T {
        try await request("GET", path: path, timeout: timeout)
    }

    func post<T: Decodable>(_ path: String, body: any Encodable, timeout: TimeInterval? = nil) async throws -> T {
        try await request("POST", path: path, body: body, timeout: timeout)
    }

    func post(_ path: String, body: any Encodable, bearerToken: String) async throws {
        var request = URLRequest(url: baseURL.appendingPathComponent(path))
        request.httpMethod = "POST"
        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
        request.setValue("Bearer \(bearerToken)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        request.httpBody = try JSONEncoder().encode(body)
        let (_, _) = try await session.data(for: request)
    }
}

// MARK: - Notification Names

extension Notification.Name {
    static let sessionExpired = Notification.Name("com.skyecrm.sessionExpired")
}

struct RateLimitError: Error {
    let retryAfterSeconds: Double
}
```

#### 3.2 Auth Header Injection

Auth header injection is handled within the `APIClient` actor's `request` method (shown above in Section 3.1). This is the ONLY place where the access token is read and attached. The actor isolation guarantees thread safety without explicit locks.

**Session expiration handling:** When a 401 is received after retry, a `Notification.Name.sessionExpired` notification is posted. The root `@Observable` `AppState` observes this notification and transitions the UI to the authentication screen:

```swift
import SwiftUI

@Observable
final class AppState {
    var authStatus: AuthStatus = .unknown

    init() {
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            forName: .sessionExpired,
            object: nil,
            queue: .main
        ) { [weak self] _ in
            self?.authStatus = .unauthenticated
        }
    }

    func reset() {
        authStatus = .unauthenticated
    }
}

enum AuthStatus {
    case unknown
    case authenticated
    case unauthenticated
}
```

#### 3.3 Certificate Pinning

**Decision: Use `TrustKit` (SPM: `datatheorem/TrustKit`) for native SSL public key pinning, configured at app startup.**

TrustKit hooks into the `URLSession` delegate chain at the native level, so all `URLSession` requests are automatically covered without changing any networking code after initialization.

**Justification:** Public key pinning (rather than certificate pinning) is preferred because public keys survive certificate renewal. When the server certificate is rotated, the new certificate typically reuses the same public key, avoiding app breakage.

**Setup:**

```swift
import TrustKit

// Call ONCE at app startup in SkyeApp.init(), before any API calls
func configureCertificatePinning() {
    #if !DEBUG
    let trustKitConfig: [String: Any] = [
        kTSKSwizzleNetworkDelegates: true,
        kTSKPinnedDomains: [
            "api.skyecrm.com": [
                kTSKIncludeSubdomains: true,
                kTSKEnforcePinning: true,
                kTSKPublicKeyHashes: [
                    "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=", // Current leaf cert SHA-256
                    "BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB=", // Backup intermediate cert SHA-256
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
    TrustKit.initSharedInstance(withConfiguration: trustKitConfig)
    #endif
}
```

**Key rotation procedure:**

1. Before the current certificate expires, generate the new certificate with a new key pair.
2. Extract the new public key hash using OpenSSL:
   ```bash
   openssl x509 -in new_cert.pem -pubkey -noout | \
   openssl pkey -pubin -outform DER | \
   openssl dgst -sha256 -binary | base64
   ```
3. Add the new hash as a third pin in the next app release (keep both old pins).
4. After the app release has been adopted by >95% of users, rotate the server certificate.
5. In the following release, remove the old leaf cert hash (keep the intermediate as backup).

**Development builds:** Pinning MUST be disabled in `DEBUG` builds to allow proxy tools (Charles, Proxyman) for debugging.

#### 3.4 Request Signing (HMAC) for Sensitive Endpoints

For high-sensitivity mutations (contact creation, deal modification, financial data updates), requests are signed with HMAC-SHA256 to prevent tampering even if the TLS layer is compromised.

**Implementation:**

```swift
import Foundation
import CryptoKit

/// Signs requests with HMAC-SHA256 for sensitive endpoints.
struct RequestSigner {

    static let signedEndpoints = [
        "/api/contacts",
        "/api/deals",
        "/api/properties",
        "/api/user/delete",
    ]

    static func shouldSign(method: String, path: String) -> Bool {
        guard method.uppercased() != "GET" else { return false }
        return signedEndpoints.contains { path.hasPrefix($0) }
    }

    /// Signs a request using HMAC-SHA256.
    /// The signing key is derived from the access token (not stored separately),
    /// meaning the signature is only valid for the current session.
    static func sign(
        method: String,
        path: String,
        body: Data,
        timestamp: Int,
        accessToken: String
    ) -> String {
        // Derive signing key from first 32 characters of access token
        let signingKey = String(accessToken.prefix(32))
        let bodyString = String(data: body, encoding: .utf8) ?? ""
        let payload = "\(method)\n\(path)\n\(timestamp)\n\(bodyString)"

        let key = SymmetricKey(data: Data(signingKey.utf8))
        let signature = HMAC<SHA256>.authenticationCode(
            for: Data(payload.utf8),
            using: key
        )

        return Data(signature).map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
    }
}
```

**Integration in `APIClient`:** The signing logic is applied within the `request` method for matching endpoints:

```swift
// Inside APIClient.request(), before sending:
if RequestSigner.shouldSign(method: method, path: path),
   let bodyData = request.httpBody {
    let timestamp = Int(Date().timeIntervalSince1970)
    let accessToken = try await tokenManager.getValidAccessToken()
    let signature = RequestSigner.sign(
        method: method,
        path: path,
        body: bodyData,
        timestamp: timestamp,
        accessToken: accessToken
    )
    request.setValue("\(timestamp)", forHTTPHeaderField: "X-Request-Timestamp")
    request.setValue(signature, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-Request-Signature")
}
```

**Server-side validation:** The backend recomputes the HMAC and compares. Requests with a timestamp older than 5 minutes are rejected (replay protection).

#### 3.5 Rate Limiting Awareness

```swift
// Rate limit handling is built into the APIClient (Section 3.1).
// When a 429 is received, a RateLimitError is thrown with the retry-after interval.
//
// For UI integration:
@Observable
final class RateLimitState {
    var isRateLimited = false
    var retryAfterSeconds: Double = 0

    func handleRateLimit(_ error: RateLimitError) {
        isRateLimited = true
        retryAfterSeconds = error.retryAfterSeconds

        Task {
            try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(error.retryAfterSeconds))
            await MainActor.run {
                self.isRateLimited = false
                self.retryAfterSeconds = 0
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 3.6 Request Timeouts

| Request Category | Timeout | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| Standard API calls | 15 seconds | Adequate for typical REST operations over cellular |
| AI chat streaming | 30 seconds (initial response) | LLM inference can take time; streaming begins within 30s |
| OCR image upload | 60 seconds | Image upload + server-side processing |
| File downloads (documents) | 60 seconds | Large PDF/document downloads |
| Token refresh | 10 seconds | Should be fast; fail quickly to surface issues |
| Health check / ping | 5 seconds | Lightweight; long timeout suggests server issues |

**Implementation:**

```swift
// Per-request timeout overrides via APIClient convenience methods
enum ChatAPI {
    static func sendMessage(_ payload: ChatPayload, using client: APIClient) async throws -> ChatResponse {
        try await client.post("/api/chat", body: payload, timeout: 30)
    }
}

enum OCRAPI {
    static func uploadImage(_ imageData: Data, using client: APIClient) async throws -> OCRResult {
        // Multipart upload with extended timeout
        try await client.uploadMultipart(
            "/api/ocr/analyze",
            fileData: imageData,
            fileName: "image.jpg",
            mimeType: "image/jpeg",
            timeout: 60
        )
    }
}
```

#### 3.7 No Sensitive Data in URLs

**Rule: ALL sensitive parameters MUST be in the request body, NEVER in query strings.**

Query strings appear in:
- Server access logs
- CDN logs
- WebView history (if WebView is used)
- iOS `URLSession` logs
- Proxy tool logs
- Crash reports

**Prohibited patterns:**

```
BAD:  GET /api/contacts?phone=+15551234567
BAD:  GET /api/search?email=jane@example.com
BAD:  GET /api/deals?ssn=123-45-6789
```

**Required patterns:**

```
GOOD: POST /api/contacts/search   { "phone": "+15551234567" }
GOOD: POST /api/search            { "email": "jane@example.com" }
```

The only query parameters permitted are:
- Pagination: `?page=2&limit=20`
- Sorting: `?sort=created_at&order=desc`
- Non-sensitive filters: `?status=active&type=buyer`
- Resource IDs in path segments: `/api/contacts/uuid-here`

---

### 4. Data Protection

#### 4.1 Sensitive Data Classification

| Tier | Classification | Examples | Storage | Encryption |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Tier 1: Secrets** | Authentication credentials, API keys | Access tokens, refresh tokens, Supabase JWT, encryption keys | iOS Keychain only (via `KeychainManager` / `Security.framework`) | Hardware-backed (Secure Enclave) |
| **Tier 2: PII** | Personally identifiable information of contacts and deals | Contact names, phone numbers, emails, addresses, deal amounts, property details, notes | Encrypted SQLite (SQLCipher via `GRDB` + `SQLCipher`) | AES-256 at rest |
| **Tier 3: App State** | Non-sensitive application state | UI preferences, theme, last viewed screen, feature flags, cached non-PII | `UserDefaults` or `SwiftData` | None required |

**Data flow principle:** Tier 1 data NEVER touches Tier 2 or Tier 3 storage. Tier 2 data NEVER downgrades to Tier 3 storage. Violations of this principle are treated as P0 security bugs.

#### 4.2 SQLite Encryption (SQLCipher)

The offline cache stores contact PII, deal details, and property information for offline access. This data MUST be encrypted at rest.

**SPM Dependencies:**

- `groue/GRDB.swift` -- SQLite wrapper with native Swift support
- Built with `SQLCipher` for transparent encryption (via `GRDB` SQLCipher-enabled variant)

**Database initialization:**

```swift
import Foundation
import Security
import GRDB

final class EncryptedDatabaseManager {
    private let keychainManager: KeychainManager
    private var dbPool: DatabasePool?

    private static let dbKeyKeychainKey = "skye_db_encryption_key"

    init(keychainManager: KeychainManager) {
        self.keychainManager = keychainManager
    }

    private func getOrCreateDbKey() throws -> String {
        // Check Keychain for existing key
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrService as String: "com.skyecrm.app",
            kSecAttrAccount as String: Self.dbKeyKeychainKey,
            kSecReturnData as String: true,
            kSecMatchLimit as String: kSecMatchLimitOne
        ]

        var result: AnyObject?
        let status = SecItemCopyMatching(query as CFDictionary, &result)

        if status == errSecSuccess, let data = result as? Data,
           let key = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) {
            return key
        }

        // Generate a 256-bit random key, encoded as hex
        var randomBytes = [UInt8](repeating: 0, count: 32)
        let randomStatus = SecRandomCopyBytes(kSecRandomDefault, 32, &randomBytes)
        guard randomStatus == errSecSuccess else {
            throw KeychainError.unableToStore(status: randomStatus)
        }

        let key = randomBytes.map { String(format: "%02x", $0) }.joined()
        let keyData = Data(key.utf8)

        let addQuery: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrService as String: "com.skyecrm.app",
            kSecAttrAccount as String: Self.dbKeyKeychainKey,
            kSecValueData as String: keyData,
            kSecAttrAccessible as String: kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly
        ]

        let addStatus = SecItemAdd(addQuery as CFDictionary, nil)
        guard addStatus == errSecSuccess else {
            throw KeychainError.unableToStore(status: addStatus)
        }

        return key
    }

    func openDatabase() throws -> DatabasePool {
        if let existing = dbPool { return existing }

        let dbKey = try getOrCreateDbKey()
        let dbPath = try FileManager.default
            .url(for: .applicationSupportDirectory, in: .userDomainMask, appropriateFor: nil, create: true)
            .appendingPathComponent("skye_offline.db")
            .path

        var configuration = Configuration()
        configuration.prepareDatabase { db in
            // Set SQLCipher encryption key immediately after opening
            try db.usePassphrase(dbKey)
        }

        let pool = try DatabasePool(path: dbPath, configuration: configuration)

        // Performance tuning for encrypted database
        try pool.write { db in
            try db.execute(sql: "PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
            try db.execute(sql: "PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL")
        }

        self.dbPool = pool
        return pool
    }
}
```

**The database encryption key is stored in Tier 1 storage (Keychain) and is NEVER logged, transmitted, or exposed beyond the initialization call.**

#### 4.3 No Secrets in Bundle

**Rule: Zero API keys, zero client secrets, zero server URLs with credentials in the compiled binary.**

What goes in the bundle (compile-time constants):
- API base URL (https://api.skyecrm.com) -- this is public.
- Google OAuth iOS client ID -- this is public (embedded in `GoogleService-Info.plist` by design).
- Sentry DSN -- this is public by design.
- App version and build number.

What NEVER goes in the bundle:
- `SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET` -- server-side only.
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` -- server-side only.
- Google OAuth client secret -- server-side only (the iOS client type does not have a client secret; the server uses the web client secret).
- Any database connection strings.
- Any third-party API keys (OpenAI, Twilio, etc.) -- server-side only.
- HMAC signing secrets -- derived from session tokens, not stored.

**Enforcement:**

1. Add a pre-commit hook that scans for common secret patterns (`sk_live_`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE`, `-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----`, etc.) using `detect-secrets` or `gitleaks`.
2. Add an Xcode Build Phase "Run Script" that scans the compiled binary and embedded resources for known secret patterns.
3. Compile-time constants are defined in a non-secret `Configuration.swift` file (or `.xcconfig` files) and must only contain non-secret values. Secrets are NEVER embedded via `#if`, `Info.plist` expansion, or `.xcconfig`.

#### 4.4 Screenshot Protection

When the app enters the background, iOS captures a snapshot for the app switcher. This snapshot can expose sensitive data (contact details, deal amounts).

**Decision: Use native `UIWindow` overlay for app switcher protection, plus `UIScreen.isCaptured` observation for screen recording detection.**

```swift
import SwiftUI
import UIKit

/// Manages screenshot and screen recording protection at the UIWindow level.
final class ScreenPrivacyManager {
    private var blurView: UIVisualEffectView?

    func install() {
        #if !DEBUG
        // Observe app lifecycle for app-switcher blur
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            self,
            selector: #selector(willResignActive),
            name: UIApplication.willResignActiveNotification,
            object: nil
        )

        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            self,
            selector: #selector(didBecomeActive),
            name: UIApplication.didBecomeActiveNotification,
            object: nil
        )

        // Observe screen recording
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            self,
            selector: #selector(screenCapturedChanged),
            name: UIScreen.capturedDidChangeNotification,
            object: nil
        )
        #endif
    }

    @objc private func willResignActive() {
        guard let window = UIApplication.shared.connectedScenes
            .compactMap({ $0 as? UIWindowScene })
            .flatMap(\.windows)
            .first(where: \.isKeyWindow) else { return }

        let blur = UIVisualEffectView(effect: UIBlurEffect(style: .light))
        blur.frame = window.bounds
        blur.tag = 9999
        window.addSubview(blur)
        self.blurView = blur
    }

    @objc private func didBecomeActive() {
        blurView?.removeFromSuperview()
        blurView = nil
    }

    @objc private func screenCapturedChanged() {
        if UIScreen.main.isCaptured {
            // Optional: log that screen recording is active
            AnalyticsService.shared.track("screen_recording_detected")
        }
    }
}
```

#### 4.5 Clipboard Security

When a user copies sensitive data (phone numbers, email addresses), the clipboard should be cleared after 60 seconds to prevent accidental paste into other apps.

```swift
import UIKit

actor ClipboardManager {
    private let clearDelay: TimeInterval = 60 // 60 seconds
    private var clearTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    func copyWithAutoExpiry(_ text: String, label: String? = nil) async {
        UIPasteboard.general.string = text

        // Show brief toast: "Copied! Clipboard will clear in 60 seconds."
        await MainActor.run {
            ToastManager.shared.show("\(label ?? "Text") copied. Clipboard clears in 60s.")
        }

        // Cancel any existing clear timer
        clearTask?.cancel()

        // Schedule clipboard clear
        clearTask = Task {
            try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(clearDelay))

            guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }

            // Only clear if clipboard still contains our text
            await MainActor.run {
                if UIPasteboard.general.string == text {
                    UIPasteboard.general.string = ""
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**Usage:** All "copy" actions for contact phone numbers, emails, addresses, and deal IDs must use `ClipboardManager.copyWithAutoExpiry` instead of direct `UIPasteboard.general.string =` assignment.

#### 4.6 Debug / Release Build Differences

| Feature | Debug (`DEBUG`) | Release |
|---|---|---|
| `os_log` / `print` logging | Enabled | **Stripped** (via `DEBUG` compilation condition guards) |
| Instruments profiling | Enabled | Available (standard Xcode behavior) |
| SwiftUI Previews | Enabled | **Not included** |
| Network inspector | Enabled | **Disabled** (no proxy-friendly config) |
| Certificate pinning | **Disabled** (allow proxy tools) | **Enabled** |
| Screenshot protection | **Disabled** (for testing screenshots) | **Enabled** |
| dSYM / debug symbols | Available locally | **Not in App Store binary** (uploaded to Sentry via sentry-cli) |
| Performance monitors | Enabled | **Disabled** |
| Error boundaries | Show error details | Show generic "Something went wrong" |
| ATS exceptions | localhost allowed (via `NSAllowsLocalNetworking`) | **No exceptions** |

**Logging configuration:**

```swift
import os

/// Logger that automatically strips messages in release builds.
enum Log {
    private static let subsystem = "com.skyecrm.app"

    static let auth = Logger(subsystem: subsystem, category: "auth")
    static let network = Logger(subsystem: subsystem, category: "network")
    static let database = Logger(subsystem: subsystem, category: "database")

    // All log calls use os_log privacy redaction:
    // Log.auth.info("User signed in: \(email, privacy: .private)")
    // In release builds, .private values are redacted automatically by os_log.
}
```

---

### 5. Apple Privacy Compliance

#### 5.1 Privacy Manifest (`PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy`)

The Privacy Manifest is required for App Store submission. It declares the Required Reason APIs used by the app and all third-party SDKs.

**Exact file contents for Skye:**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"
  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>

  <!-- SECTION 1: Required Reason APIs -->
  <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypes</key>
  <array>

    <!-- UserDefaults: Used by app for preferences and feature flags -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryUserDefaults</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypeReasons</key>
      <array>
        <string>CA92.1</string>
        <!-- CA92.1: Access UserDefaults to read/write app-specific
             configuration and state (e.g., onboarding completion,
             theme preference, feature flags) -->
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- File Timestamp: Used by SQLite WAL and cache management
         for file metadata -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryFileTimestamp</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypeReasons</key>
      <array>
        <string>C617.1</string>
        <!-- C617.1: Access file timestamps of files within the
             app container for cache management and data retention -->
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- System Boot Time: Used for performance timing
         and scheduling -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategorySystemBootTime</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypeReasons</key>
      <array>
        <string>35F9.1</string>
        <!-- 35F9.1: Access system boot time for measuring elapsed time
             intervals within the app (performance monitoring) -->
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- Disk Space: Used for cache management
         and image download pre-checks -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPIType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryDiskSpace</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyAccessedAPITypeReasons</key>
      <array>
        <string>E174.1</string>
        <!-- E174.1: Access disk space to check available capacity
             before downloading large files (property images, documents) -->
      </array>
    </dict>

  </array>

  <!-- SECTION 2: Tracking Domains -->
  <key>NSPrivacyTrackingDomains</key>
  <array>
    <!-- Skye does NOT use any tracking domains.
         No advertising SDKs, no cross-app tracking.
         This array is intentionally empty. -->
  </array>

  <!-- SECTION 3: Tracking Declaration -->
  <key>NSPrivacyTracking</key>
  <false/>
  <!-- Skye does NOT track users across apps or websites.
       No IDFA access, no fingerprinting, no ad networks. -->

  <!-- SECTION 4: Collected Data Types -->
  <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypes</key>
  <array>

    <!-- Email Address -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeEmailAddress</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <true/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAppFunctionality</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- Name -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeName</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <true/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAppFunctionality</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- User ID (Google sub, internal UUID) -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeUserID</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <true/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAppFunctionality</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- Photos (property images uploaded by user) -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePhotos</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <true/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAppFunctionality</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- Crash Data (via Sentry) -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeCrashData</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAnalytics</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

    <!-- Performance Data (via Sentry) -->
    <dict>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataType</key>
      <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePerformanceData</string>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeLinked</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypeTracking</key>
      <false/>
      <key>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposes</key>
      <array>
        <string>NSPrivacyCollectedDataTypePurposeAnalytics</string>
      </array>
    </dict>

  </array>

</dict>
</plist>
```

**This file is placed at `Skye/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` in the Xcode project and added to the app target's "Copy Bundle Resources" build phase.**

#### 5.2 App Tracking Transparency (ATT) Analysis

**Decision: ATT prompt is NOT required for Skye. Do NOT include the ATT framework.**

**Detailed analysis of every SDK in the Skye stack:**

| SDK / Library | Accesses IDFA? | Cross-app tracking? | Tracking domain? | ATT trigger? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `GoogleSignIn-iOS` (SPM) | No | No | No | No |
| `Security.framework` (Keychain) | No | No | No | No |
| `GRDB.swift` + SQLCipher | No | No | No | No |
| `LocalAuthentication.framework` | No | No | No | No |
| `UIKit` (screen capture protection) | No | No | No | No |
| `UserNotifications.framework` | No | No | No | No |
| `Sentry` (SPM: `sentry-cocoa`) | No | No (crash/perf only) | No (`sentry.io` is not a tracking domain) | No |
| `TrustKit` (SPM) | No | No | No | No |
| `URLSession` | No | No | No | No |
| `Supabase` (SPM: `supabase-swift`) | No | No | No | No |
| `SwiftUI` / `Observation` framework | No | No | No | No |

**Conclusion:** Skye does not access the IDFA, does not perform cross-app or cross-site tracking, does not use advertising SDKs, and does not share data with data brokers. The ATT prompt is not required and MUST NOT be included (showing it unnecessarily degrades user experience and may raise App Review questions).

**`NSUserTrackingUsageDescription` is NOT included in `Info.plist`.** Including it without a valid reason may trigger App Review flags.

#### 5.3 Privacy Nutrition Labels (App Store Connect)

When submitting to the App Store, the following entries must be declared in App Store Connect under "App Privacy":

**Data Linked to You:**

| Data Type | Category | Purpose | Linked to Identity | Used for Tracking |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Name | Contact Info | App Functionality | Yes | No |
| Email Address | Contact Info | App Functionality | Yes | No |
| User ID | Identifiers | App Functionality | Yes | No |
| Photos or Videos | User Content | App Functionality | Yes | No |

**Data Not Linked to You:**

| Data Type | Category | Purpose | Linked to Identity | Used for Tracking |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Crash Data | Diagnostics | Analytics | No | No |
| Performance Data | Diagnostics | Analytics | No | No |

**Data NOT Collected (explicit declarations):**

- Location: NOT collected (no geolocation features in v2).
- Contacts: NOT collected (Skye has its own contact database; it does NOT access the iOS Contacts app).
- Browsing History: NOT collected.
- Search History: NOT collected (search queries are processed but not logged for analytics).
- Advertising Data: NOT collected.
- Financial Information: NOT collected (deal amounts are user-entered CRM data, not financial instruments).
- Health & Fitness: NOT collected.
- Sensitive Information: NOT collected.

#### 5.4 Data Retention Policy

| Data Category | Local Retention | Server Retention | Purge Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Auth tokens** (Tier 1) | Until logout or expiry | Access: 1hr. Refresh: 30 days. | Logout, token rotation, or expiry |
| **Contact/Deal cache** (Tier 2) | 30 days since last sync | Per account terms (indefinite while active) | Logout, account deletion, or 30-day offline expiry |
| **Property images** (Tier 2) | 7 days (LRU cache, max 500MB) | Indefinite (cloud storage) | Cache eviction, logout, or manual clear |
| **AI chat history** (Tier 2) | 7 days (last 50 conversations) | 90 days | Logout or manual clear |
| **App preferences** (Tier 3) | Indefinite | Not stored on server | App uninstall |
| **Crash/perf data** | Not stored locally | 90 days (Sentry retention) | Automatic Sentry retention policy |

**Automatic purge:** A background task runs on app launch to check offline cache age. Data older than the retention period is deleted without user intervention.

```swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

func purgeStaleOfflineData(db: DatabasePool, imageCache: ImageCacheManager) async throws {
    let thirtyDaysAgo = Date().addingTimeInterval(-(30 * 24 * 60 * 60))
    let sevenDaysAgo = Date().addingTimeInterval(-(7 * 24 * 60 * 60))

    try await db.write { db in
        try db.execute(
            sql: "DELETE FROM contacts_cache WHERE last_synced_at < ?",
            arguments: [thirtyDaysAgo.timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000]
        )
        try db.execute(
            sql: "DELETE FROM deals_cache WHERE last_synced_at < ?",
            arguments: [thirtyDaysAgo.timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000]
        )
        try db.execute(
            sql: "DELETE FROM chat_history WHERE created_at < ?",
            arguments: [sevenDaysAgo.timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000]
        )
    }

    // Purge image cache exceeding 500MB
    try await imageCache.evictToSize(500 * 1024 * 1024)
}
```

#### 5.5 Account Deletion Flow

Apple requires that any app offering account creation must provide in-app account deletion (App Store Review Guideline 5.1.1(v)). The deletion option must be easy to find.

**User flow:**

```
Settings > Account > Delete My Account
       |
       v
+------------------------------------------+
|  DELETE YOUR ACCOUNT                     |
|                                          |
|  This action is permanent and cannot     |
|  be undone. All your data will be        |
|  deleted, including:                     |
|                                          |
|  - All contacts and client data          |
|  - All deals and transaction history     |
|  - All property listings                 |
|  - All AI conversation history           |
|  - All uploaded documents and images     |
|                                          |
|  +------------------------------------+  |
|  | Active Subscriptions Warning       |  |
|  |                                    |  |
|  | You have an active subscription.   |  |
|  | Deleting your account will NOT     |  |
|  | cancel your subscription billing.  |  |
|  | Please cancel your subscription    |  |
|  | first.                             |  |
|  |                                    |  |
|  | [Manage Subscription]              |  |
|  +------------------------------------+  |
|                                          |
|  To confirm, type "DELETE" below:        |
|                                          |
|  +------------------------------------+  |
|  |                                    |  |
|  +------------------------------------+  |
|                                          |
|  [Cancel]          [Delete My Account]   |
|                    (red, destructive)     |
+------------------------------------------+
       |
       | User types "DELETE" and taps button
       v
+------------------------------------------+
|  IDENTITY VERIFICATION                   |
|                                          |
|  A verification code has been sent to    |
|  a***@example.com                        |
|                                          |
|  Enter the 6-digit code:                 |
|  [_] [_] [_] [_] [_] [_]               |
|                                          |
|  [Resend Code]         [Verify & Delete] |
+------------------------------------------+
       |
       | Code verified
       v
+------------------------------------------+
|  Backend processing:                     |
|                                          |
|  1. Mark account as "pending_deletion"   |
|  2. Revoke ALL sessions (web + native)   |
|  3. Schedule data deletion job (24hr     |
|     grace period for accidental delete)  |
|  4. Send confirmation email              |
|  5. After 24hr: cascade delete all data  |
|     from all tables                      |
|  6. Revoke Google Sign-In token          |
|  7. Remove from Sentry user context      |
|  8. Purge from any email marketing lists |
+------------------------------------------+
       |
       v
+------------------------------------------+
|  ACCOUNT DELETION SCHEDULED              |
|                                          |
|  Your account will be permanently        |
|  deleted within 24 hours. You will       |
|  receive a confirmation email.           |
|                                          |
|  If you change your mind, contact        |
|  support@skyecrm.com within 24 hours.    |
|                                          |
|  [OK]                                    |
+------------------------------------------+
       |
       | User taps OK
       v
  Clear all local data + navigate to Auth screen
```

**Server endpoint:**

```
POST /api/user/delete
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
X-Request-Signature: <HMAC signature>
{
  "verificationCode": "123456",
  "confirmationText": "DELETE"
}

Response:
200 OK
{
  "status": "pending_deletion",
  "deletionScheduledAt": "2026-03-03T12:00:00Z",
  "gracePeriodEndsAt": "2026-03-03T12:00:00Z"
}
```

---

### 6. Security Threat Model

This section maps threats from the OWASP Mobile Top 10 (2024) to Skye-specific mitigations.

#### 6.1 Jailbreak Detection

**OWASP M7: Insufficient Binary Protections**

**Decision: Detect and warn. Do NOT block.**

Apple may reject apps that refuse to run on jailbroken devices, as it could be seen as limiting device functionality. Instead, detect jailbreak status and:

1. Show a non-blocking warning dialog on first launch on a jailbroken device.
2. Log the event to the security audit log.
3. Optionally disable certain high-sensitivity features (e.g., biometric auth, since it may be compromised on jailbroken devices).

**Implementation using native heuristic checks:**

```swift
import Foundation
import UIKit

/// Performs jailbreak and environment integrity checks using native iOS APIs.
/// No third-party dependency required.
struct SecurityChecker: Sendable {

    struct SecurityStatus: Sendable {
        let isJailbroken: Bool
        let isDebuggerAttached: Bool
        let suspiciousFilesFound: Bool
    }

    static func performChecks() -> SecurityStatus {
        let jailbroken = checkJailbreak()
        let debugger = checkDebugger()
        return SecurityStatus(
            isJailbroken: jailbroken,
            isDebuggerAttached: debugger,
            suspiciousFilesFound: jailbroken
        )
    }

    // MARK: - Jailbreak Detection Heuristics

    private static func checkJailbreak() -> Bool {
        #if targetEnvironment(simulator)
        return false
        #else
        // Check for common jailbreak paths
        let suspiciousPaths = [
            "/Applications/Cydia.app",
            "/Applications/Sileo.app",
            "/Library/MobileSubstrate/MobileSubstrate.dylib",
            "/bin/bash",
            "/usr/sbin/sshd",
            "/etc/apt",
            "/private/var/lib/apt/",
            "/usr/bin/ssh",
            "/var/checkra1n.dmg",
            "/var/binpack"
        ]

        for path in suspiciousPaths {
            if FileManager.default.fileExists(atPath: path) {
                return true
            }
        }

        // Check if app can write outside sandbox
        let testPath = "/private/jailbreaktest_\(UUID().uuidString)"
        do {
```
