| `/properties/{id}` | `PropertyDetailView` | `propertyId: String` | Loads property listing detail |
| `/chat` | `ChatView` | (none) | Opens main chat interface |
| `/chat?briefing=today` | `ChatView` | `briefingDate: "today"` | Opens chat and auto-loads today's morning briefing |
| `/chat/{id}` | `ChatView` | `chatId: String` | Opens specific chat conversation |
| `/` | `HomeView` | (none) | Fallback: opens app to default tab |

#### 4.3 SwiftUI Deep Link Router

```swift
// DeepLink/DeepLinkRouter.swift

import SwiftUI

/// Represents a parsed deep link destination
enum DeepLinkDestination: Hashable {
    case contact(id: String, tab: ContactTab? = nil)
    case property(id: String)
    case chat(id: String? = nil, briefingDate: String? = nil)
    case home

    enum ContactTab: String, Hashable {
        case tasks
        case matches
    }
}

@Observable
final class DeepLinkRouter {
    var pendingDestination: DeepLinkDestination?
    var navigationPath = NavigationPath()

    /// Parse a URL (from push notification `deep_link` field, custom scheme, or universal link)
    /// into a structured destination.
    func parse(url: URL) -> DeepLinkDestination? {
        // Normalize: strip scheme prefix, get path components
        let path: String
        if url.scheme == "skyeapp" {
            // skyeapp:///contacts/abc  -> /contacts/abc
            path = url.path
        } else {
            // https://app.skye.com/contacts/abc -> /contacts/abc
            path = url.path
        }

        let components = URLComponents(url: url, resolvingAgainstBaseURL: false)
        let queryItems = components?.queryItems ?? []
        let pathSegments = path.split(separator: "/").map(String.init)

        switch pathSegments.first {
        case "contacts" where pathSegments.count >= 2:
            let contactId = pathSegments[1]
            let tab = queryItems.first(where: { $0.name == "tab" })?.value
            return .contact(
                id: contactId,
                tab: tab.flatMap { DeepLinkDestination.ContactTab(rawValue: $0) }
            )

        case "properties" where pathSegments.count >= 2:
            return .property(id: pathSegments[1])

        case "chat":
            if pathSegments.count >= 2 {
                return .chat(id: pathSegments[1])
            }
            let briefing = queryItems.first(where: { $0.name == "briefing" })?.value
            return .chat(briefingDate: briefing)

        default:
            return .home
        }
    }

    /// Parse a deep link path string (from notification `userInfo["deep_link"]`)
    func parse(path: String) -> DeepLinkDestination? {
        guard let url = URL(string: "skyeapp://\(path)") else { return nil }
        return parse(url: url)
    }

    /// Navigate to the parsed destination
    func navigate(to destination: DeepLinkDestination) {
        pendingDestination = destination
    }
}
```

**Integration with the SwiftUI App:**

```swift
@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    @State private var deepLinkRouter = DeepLinkRouter()
    @State private var authManager = AuthManager()

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            if authManager.isAuthenticated {
                MainTabView()
                    .environment(deepLinkRouter)
                    .onOpenURL { url in
                        // Handles both custom scheme (skyeapp://) and universal links
                        if let destination = deepLinkRouter.parse(url: url) {
                            deepLinkRouter.navigate(to: destination)
                        }
                    }
            } else {
                AuthView()
                    .environment(authManager)
                    .onOpenURL { url in
                        // Preserve deep link intent through auth flow
                        if let destination = deepLinkRouter.parse(url: url) {
                            DeepLinkIntentStore.preserve(destination)
                        }
                    }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 4.4 Auth Gate for Deep Links

Deep links may arrive when the user is not authenticated (e.g., token expired, app not opened in weeks). The app must preserve the deep link intent through the authentication flow.

**Implementation:**

```swift
// DeepLink/DeepLinkIntentStore.swift

enum DeepLinkIntentStore {
    private static let key = "pending_deep_link"
    private static let timestampKey = "pending_deep_link_timestamp"
    private static let ttl: TimeInterval = 3600  // 1 hour

    /// Preserve a deep link intent for post-auth resolution.
    static func preserve(_ destination: DeepLinkDestination) {
        if let encoded = try? JSONEncoder().encode(CodableDestination(destination)) {
            UserDefaults.standard.set(encoded, forKey: key)
            UserDefaults.standard.set(Date().timeIntervalSince1970, forKey: timestampKey)
        }
    }

    /// Resolve preserved intent after successful authentication.
    /// Returns the destination if still valid, then clears it.
    static func resolve() -> DeepLinkDestination? {
        let savedTimestamp = UserDefaults.standard.double(forKey: timestampKey)
        let age = Date().timeIntervalSince1970 - savedTimestamp

        guard age < ttl,
              let data = UserDefaults.standard.data(forKey: key),
              let codable = try? JSONDecoder().decode(CodableDestination.self, from: data) else {
            clear()
            return nil
        }

        clear()
        return codable.toDestination()
    }

    static func clear() {
        UserDefaults.standard.removeObject(forKey: key)
        UserDefaults.standard.removeObject(forKey: timestampKey)
    }
}
```

**Flow:**

```
[Deep link arrives (push tap, universal link, etc.)]
    |
    v
[Check auth state]
    |
    +---> Authenticated
    |         |
    |         v
    |    [Resolve deep link normally]
    |    [Navigate to target screen via DeepLinkRouter]
    |
    +---> Not Authenticated
              |
              v
         [Preserve deep link intent via DeepLinkIntentStore]
         [Show login/auth screen]
              |
              v
         [User authenticates successfully]
              |
              v
         [Check for preserved intent via DeepLinkIntentStore.resolve()]
              |
              +---> Intent exists
              |         |
              |         v
              |    [Navigate to deep link target]
              |
              +---> No intent
                        |
                        v
                   [Navigate to default home screen]
```

**TTL for preserved intents:** Deep link intents expire after 1 hour. If the user does not authenticate within that window, the intent is discarded to avoid navigating to stale content.

#### 4.5 Entity Not Found Handling

When a deep link points to a deleted or inaccessible entity:

```swift
// In the target view's data fetching logic

func loadContact(contactId: String) async {
    do {
        let contact = try await APIClient.shared.get("/api/contacts/\(contactId)", as: Contact.self)
        self.contact = contact
    } catch let error as APIError where error.statusCode == 404 {
        // Entity not found -- navigate to parent tab and show toast
        deepLinkRouter.navigate(to: .home)
        ToastManager.shared.show(
            message: "This contact is no longer available",
            type: .info,
            duration: 3.0
        )
    } catch {
        // Re-throw other errors for error handling
        self.error = error
    }
}
```

**Fallback routing table:**

| Target Screen | Fallback on 404 | Toast Message |
|---|---|---|
| `ContactDetailView` | Navigate to People tab (contact list) | "This contact is no longer available" |
| `PropertyDetailView` | Navigate to Properties tab | "This property listing is no longer available" |
| `ChatView` (conversation) | Navigate to Chat tab (conversation list) | "This conversation is no longer available" |

#### 4.6 Cold Start vs. Warm Start Deep Link Handling

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Cold start** (app killed, user taps notification) | App launches from scratch. Deep link is captured via the `UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate.userNotificationCenter(_:didReceive:withCompletionHandler:)` delegate method (which fires even on cold start). Navigation must wait for: (1) app initialization complete, (2) auth token validation complete, (3) SwiftUI view hierarchy mounted. Only then navigate to deep link target. |
| **Warm start** (app in background, user taps notification) | App is already initialized and authenticated. Deep link is received via the same delegate method. Navigation happens immediately. |
| **Foreground** (app is active, notification arrives) | Notification is displayed as a banner (via `userNotificationCenter(_:willPresent:withCompletionHandler:)` returning `[.banner, .sound, .badge]`). User taps it. `userNotificationCenter(_:didReceive:withCompletionHandler:)` fires. Navigation happens immediately. |

**Cold start initialization sequence:**

```
[App process started by iOS (notification tap)]
    |
    v
[1. SwiftUI App struct initializes]
    |
    v
[2. App body builds view hierarchy]
    |
    v
[3. Auth token loaded from Keychain]
    |
    v
[4. Auth token validated (silent refresh if expired)]
    |
    +---> Token valid
    |         |
    |         v
    |    [5. Main tab view renders]
    |    [6. UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate fires didReceive:]
    |    [7. DeepLinkRouter.parse() extracts destination from userInfo]
    |    [8. DeepLinkRouter.navigate() pushes target onto NavigationPath]
    |    [9. User sees target screen]
    |
    +---> Token invalid / expired
              |
              v
         [5. Show auth screen]
         [6. Deep link preserved via DeepLinkIntentStore]
         [7. After auth, resolve preserved intent]
```

**Notification delegate integration for cold and warm start:**

```swift
// AppDelegate or dedicated NotificationDelegate

final class NotificationDelegate: NSObject, UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate {
    var deepLinkRouter: DeepLinkRouter?

    /// Notification tapped (cold start, warm start, or foreground tap)
    func userNotificationCenter(
        _ center: UNUserNotificationCenter,
        didReceive response: UNNotificationResponse,
        withCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping () -> Void
    ) {
        let userInfo = response.notification.request.content.userInfo
        let actionIdentifier = response.actionIdentifier

        // Handle action button taps (background actions)
        if actionIdentifier != UNNotificationDefaultActionIdentifier {
            Task {
                await handleNotificationAction(
                    actionIdentifier: actionIdentifier,
                    userInfo: userInfo
                )
            }
            completionHandler()
            return
        }

        // Default tap -- navigate via deep link
        if let deepLink = userInfo["deep_link"] as? String,
           let destination = deepLinkRouter?.parse(path: deepLink) {
            deepLinkRouter?.navigate(to: destination)
        }

        // Track notification open for analytics
        if let notificationId = userInfo["notification_id"] as? String {
            Task {
                try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                    "/api/notifications/\(notificationId)/opened"
                )
            }
        }

        completionHandler()
    }

    /// Notification arrived while app is in foreground
    func userNotificationCenter(
        _ center: UNUserNotificationCenter,
        willPresent notification: UNNotification,
        withCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping (UNNotificationPresentationOptions) -> Void
    ) {
        // Show banner + sound + badge even when app is in foreground
        completionHandler([.banner, .sound, .badge])
    }
}
```

---

### 5. Badge Management

#### 5.1 Badge Count Strategy

The badge count on Skye's app icon represents the total number of actionable, unread notifications across all notification types.

**Badge count composition:**

| Notification Type | Contributes to Badge? | Cleared When |
|---|---|---|
| Engagement Alert | Yes | User views the contact OR dismisses the notification |
| Morning Briefing | Yes (1 per day max) | User opens the briefing in chat |
| Overdue Task | Yes | User marks task complete, snoozes, or views task |
| Property Match | Yes | User views the property |
| AI Completion | Yes | User views the AI result |

#### 5.2 Server-Driven Badge Count

**Every push notification includes the current badge count** in the `aps.badge` field. The server is the source of truth for badge count.

**Server-side badge calculation:**

```sql
SELECT
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM engagement_alerts
   WHERE user_id = $1 AND status = 'pending' AND dismissed_at IS NULL)
  +
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM morning_briefings
   WHERE user_id = $1 AND date = CURRENT_DATE AND read_at IS NULL)
  +
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM task_notifications
   WHERE user_id = $1 AND status = 'pending' AND dismissed_at IS NULL)
  +
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM property_match_notifications
   WHERE user_id = $1 AND status = 'pending' AND viewed_at IS NULL)
  +
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ai_completion_notifications
   WHERE user_id = $1 AND status = 'pending' AND viewed_at IS NULL)
AS total_badge_count;
```

**This query runs before every push send** to include the accurate badge count. For efficiency, maintain a materialized `notification_badge_count` column on the user table, updated via database triggers or application-level increment/decrement operations.

#### 5.3 Client-Side Badge Updates

```swift
@Observable
final class BadgeManager {
    static let shared = BadgeManager()

    /// Handle background notification action that decrements the badge
    func handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: String) async {
        switch actionIdentifier {
        case "dismiss", "mark_complete", "dismiss_briefing", "dismiss_ai", "snooze_1d", "snooze_task":
            // Decrement badge locally as immediate feedback
            let settings = await UNUserNotificationCenter.current().notificationSettings()
            guard settings.badgeSetting == .enabled else { return }

            do {
                // Use server as source of truth -- fetch updated count
                let response: BadgeCountResponse = try await APIClient.shared
                    .get("/api/users/me/badge-count")
                try await UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setBadgeCount(response.badgeCount)
            } catch {
                // Fallback: decrement locally
                // iOS 16.0+ provides setBadgeCount but no getBadgeCount.
                // Store locally in UserDefaults as cache.
                let current = UserDefaults.standard.integer(forKey: "cached_badge_count")
                let newCount = max(0, current - 1)
                UserDefaults.standard.set(newCount, forKey: "cached_badge_count")
                try? await UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setBadgeCount(newCount)
            }

        default:
            break
        }
    }

    /// Called when user navigates to a screen that clears relevant badges
    func onScreenViewed(_ screen: ScreenType) async {
        switch screen {
        case .contactDetail(let contactId):
            try? await APIClient.shared.post("/api/notifications/mark-read", body: [
                "type": "engagement",
                "contact_id": contactId
            ])

        case .chat(let chatId, let briefingDate):
            if let briefingDate {
                try? await APIClient.shared.post("/api/notifications/mark-read", body: [
                    "type": "briefing",
                    "date": briefingDate
                ])
            }
            if let chatId {
                try? await APIClient.shared.post("/api/notifications/mark-read", body: [
                    "type": "ai_complete",
                    "chat_id": chatId
                ])
            }

        case .propertyDetail(let propertyId):
            try? await APIClient.shared.post("/api/notifications/mark-read", body: [
                "type": "property",
                "property_id": propertyId
            ])
        }

        // Sync badge from server
        await syncBadge()
    }

    func syncBadge() async {
        do {
            let response: BadgeCountResponse = try await APIClient.shared
                .get("/api/users/me/badge-count")
            try await UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setBadgeCount(response.badgeCount)
            UserDefaults.standard.set(response.badgeCount, forKey: "cached_badge_count")
        } catch {
            Logger.push.warning("Badge sync failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
        }
    }

    enum ScreenType {
        case contactDetail(contactId: String)
        case chat(chatId: String?, briefingDate: String?)
        case propertyDetail(propertyId: String)
    }
}

private struct BadgeCountResponse: Codable {
    let badgeCount: Int
    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case badgeCount = "badge_count"
    }
}
```

#### 5.4 Badge Sync on App Foreground

When the app returns to the foreground, the badge count may be stale (e.g., the user dismissed a notification from the lock screen but the server-side count was not synced). Sync on every foreground event.

```swift
// In the main App struct or root view

struct MainTabView: View {
    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase

    var body: some View {
        TabView {
            // ... tabs
        }
        .onChange(of: scenePhase) { oldPhase, newPhase in
            if oldPhase != .active && newPhase == .active {
                // App returned to foreground -- sync badge
                Task {
                    await BadgeManager.shared.syncBadge()
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 5.5 Badge Clear on Full App Open

**Policy decision:** Do NOT auto-clear the entire badge on app open. The badge represents actionable items. Only clear badges for items the user actually views or dismisses. This ensures the badge remains a meaningful signal.

**Exception:** If the user explicitly taps "Mark All as Read" in a future in-app notification center, clear the entire badge:

```swift
try await UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setBadgeCount(0)
UserDefaults.standard.set(0, forKey: "cached_badge_count")
```

---

### 6. Notification Center Behavior

#### 6.1 Notification Grouping

iOS groups notifications using the `thread-id` field. Skye uses the following grouping strategy:

| Thread ID | Groups | Summary Text |
|---|---|---|
| `engagement-alerts` | All engagement/follow-up reminders | "%u+ contacts need follow-up" |
| `morning-briefing` | Morning briefings (typically only 1/day) | (No grouping needed) |
| `overdue-tasks` | All overdue task notifications | "%u+ overdue tasks" |
| `property-matches` | All property match notifications | "%u+ new property matches" |
| `ai-completions` | All AI task completion notifications | "%u AI results ready" |

**Summary text configuration:** The `categorySummaryFormat` parameter in `UNNotificationCategory` initialization controls the grouped summary. See Appendix A for the complete registration.

When 4 or more notifications stack in a group, iOS displays the summary text with the count.

#### 6.2 Notification Actions (Background Processing)

Actions that execute without opening the app are handled in the `UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate.userNotificationCenter(_:didReceive:withCompletionHandler:)` method. Both foreground and background actions route through this single delegate method.

```swift
// Notifications/NotificationActionHandler.swift

actor NotificationActionHandler {
    static let shared = NotificationActionHandler()

    func handle(actionIdentifier: String, userInfo: [AnyHashable: Any]) async {
        guard let notificationType = userInfo["type"] as? String else { return }

        switch actionIdentifier {
        // --- Engagement Alert Actions ---
        case "call_now":
            // Foreground action -- handled by navigation layer (opens app)
            break

        case "snooze_1d":
            guard let contactId = userInfo["contact_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                "/api/contacts/\(contactId)/snooze",
                body: ["duration": "1d"]
            )
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "snooze_1d")

        case "dismiss":
            guard let notificationId = userInfo["notification_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                "/api/notifications/\(notificationId)/dismiss"
            )
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "dismiss")

        // --- Overdue Task Actions ---
        case "mark_complete":
            guard let taskId = userInfo["task_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post("/api/tasks/\(taskId)/complete")
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "mark_complete")

        case "snooze_task":
            guard let taskId = userInfo["task_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                "/api/tasks/\(taskId)/snooze",
                body: ["duration": "1d"]
            )
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "snooze_task")

        // --- Morning Briefing ---
        case "dismiss_briefing":
            guard let notificationId = userInfo["notification_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                "/api/notifications/\(notificationId)/dismiss"
            )
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "dismiss_briefing")

        // --- AI Completion ---
        case "dismiss_ai":
            guard let notificationId = userInfo["notification_id"] as? String else { return }
            try? await APIClient.shared.post(
                "/api/notifications/\(notificationId)/dismiss"
            )
            await BadgeManager.shared.handleBackgroundAction(actionIdentifier: "dismiss_ai")

        default:
            Logger.push.warning("Unhandled notification action: \(actionIdentifier)")
        }
    }
}
```

**Integration in the notification delegate:**

```swift
// In NotificationDelegate.userNotificationCenter(_:didReceive:withCompletionHandler:)

func userNotificationCenter(
    _ center: UNUserNotificationCenter,
    didReceive response: UNNotificationResponse,
    withCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping () -> Void
) {
    let userInfo = response.notification.request.content.userInfo
    let actionIdentifier = response.actionIdentifier

    if actionIdentifier == UNNotificationDefaultActionIdentifier {
        // Default tap -- navigate via deep link
        if let deepLink = userInfo["deep_link"] as? String,
           let destination = deepLinkRouter?.parse(path: deepLink) {
            deepLinkRouter?.navigate(to: destination)
        }
    } else if actionIdentifier == UNNotificationDismissActionIdentifier {
        // User swiped away the notification (not via an action button)
        // Do NOT decrement badge -- item is still actionable
        // Optionally log this event for analytics
    } else {
        // Custom action button pressed
        Task {
            await NotificationActionHandler.shared.handle(
                actionIdentifier: actionIdentifier,
                userInfo: userInfo
            )
        }
    }

    completionHandler()
}
```

**Key advantage over React Native:** In native Swift, there is no "headless JS context" limitation. The notification delegate runs in the same process with full access to networking, Keychain (for auth tokens), Core Data, and all system APIs. Background action handling is straightforward `async/await` code -- no bridge, no separate JavaScript runtime.

#### 6.3 Rich Notifications (Notification Content Extension)

For property match notifications, a Notification Content Extension provides a rich, expanded view with the property image, address, price, and key details.

**Implementation requires a separate Xcode target:**

1. **Create target:** In Xcode, File > New > Target > Notification Content Extension. Name: `SkyeNotificationContent`.
2. **Configure Info.plist** of the extension:

```xml
<key>NSExtension</key>
<dict>
  <key>NSExtensionAttributes</key>
  <dict>
    <key>UNNotificationExtensionCategory</key>
    <string>PROPERTY_MATCH</string>
    <key>UNNotificationExtensionInitialContentSizeRatio</key>
    <real>0.5</real>
    <key>UNNotificationExtensionDefaultContentHidden</key>
    <true/>
  </dict>
  <key>NSExtensionPointIdentifier</key>
  <string>com.apple.usernotifications.content-extension</string>
  <key>NSExtensionPrincipalClass</key>
  <string>NotificationViewController</string>
</dict>
```

3. **NotificationViewController** layout:
   - Property hero image (full width, 16:9 aspect ratio)
   - Address label (bold, 17pt)
   - Price label (bold, 20pt, Skye brand color)
   - Bed/bath/sqft details in a horizontal stack
   - "Matched for: {contact_name}" subtitle

```swift
// SkyeNotificationContent/NotificationViewController.swift

import UIKit
import UserNotifications
import UserNotificationsUI

class NotificationViewController: UIViewController, UNNotificationContentExtension {

    @IBOutlet weak var propertyImageView: UIImageView!
    @IBOutlet weak var addressLabel: UILabel!
    @IBOutlet weak var priceLabel: UILabel!
    @IBOutlet weak var detailsLabel: UILabel!
    @IBOutlet weak var matchedForLabel: UILabel!

    func didReceive(_ notification: UNNotification) {
        let userInfo = notification.request.content.userInfo

        addressLabel.text = userInfo["property_address"] as? String
        addressLabel.font = .boldSystemFont(ofSize: 17)

        if let price = userInfo["property_price"] as? Int {
            let formatter = NumberFormatter()
            formatter.numberStyle = .currency
            formatter.maximumFractionDigits = 0
            priceLabel.text = formatter.string(from: NSNumber(value: price))
        }
        priceLabel.font = .boldSystemFont(ofSize: 20)

        let beds = userInfo["property_beds"] as? Int ?? 0
        let baths = userInfo["property_baths"] as? Int ?? 0
        detailsLabel.text = "\(beds) bd / \(baths) ba"

        if let contactName = userInfo["contact_name"] as? String {
            matchedForLabel.text = "Matched for: \(contactName)"
        }

        // Load the attached image (downloaded by the Service Extension)
        if let attachment = notification.request.content.attachments.first,
           attachment.url.startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() {
            defer { attachment.url.stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() }
            if let data = try? Data(contentsOf: attachment.url) {
                propertyImageView.image = UIImage(data: data)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

4. **Data flow:** The notification's `userInfo` contains `property_image_url`, `property_address`, `property_price`, `property_beds`, `property_baths`, `contact_name`. The Notification Service Extension (Section 2.4) downloads the image and attaches it. The Content Extension renders the custom UI.

**Note:** The Notification Content Extension uses UIKit (not SwiftUI) because it runs in a separate process with a storyboard-based layout. This is a system requirement for content extensions as of iOS 18.

#### 6.4 Notification Sounds

| Notification Type | Sound | Specification |
|---|---|---|
| Engagement Alert | System default | `"sound": "default"` |
| Morning Briefing | Custom gentle chime | `"sound": { "name": "skye_morning_chime.caf", "volume": 0.7 }` |
| Overdue Task | System default | `"sound": "default"` |
| Property Match | System default | `"sound": "default"` |
| AI Completion | System default | `"sound": "default"` |

**Custom sound file (`skye_morning_chime.caf`) specification:**

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Format | Core Audio Format (CAF) |
| Encoding | Linear PCM or IMA4 |
| Sample rate | 44,100 Hz |
| Bit depth | 16-bit |
| Channels | Mono (1 channel) |
| Duration | 3 seconds |
| Character | Soft ascending chime -- three gentle bell tones ascending in pitch. Non-jarring, professional, warm. |
| File size | Target under 100KB |
| Bundle location | Main app bundle, added to "Copy Bundle Resources" build phase in Xcode |

**Conversion command (for audio team):**

```bash
afconvert input.wav skye_morning_chime.caf -d LEI16 -f caff -c 1
```

#### 6.5 Do Not Disturb and Scheduled Delivery

iOS handles DND automatically -- notifications are silenced during DND/Focus modes. Skye should design around this:

**Morning briefing scheduling:**
- The morning briefing push is scheduled based on the user's preferred delivery time (stored server-side).
- Default: 7:30 AM local time.
- The backend scheduler fans out briefing pushes per-user based on their timezone and preference.
- If the user's preferred time falls during typical sleeping hours (11 PM - 5 AM) due to timezone misconfiguration, cap at 7:00 AM local time.
- The briefing is generated and cached before the push is sent, so when the user taps "View Briefing," the content loads instantly.

**Interruption levels (iOS 15+):**
- `time-sensitive`: Used for engagement alerts and overdue tasks. These can break through Focus modes if the user has allowed Time Sensitive notifications for Skye. Users must explicitly enable this in iOS Settings > Focus > [Focus Name] > Apps > Skye > Time Sensitive Notifications.
- `active`: Used for morning briefings, property matches, AI completions. Standard delivery, respects DND/Focus.
- `passive`: Not used by Skye (these are delivered silently without any alert).

**Skye should NOT use `critical` interruption level.** Critical alerts require a special entitlement from Apple (granted only for health/safety/security apps) and always play sound even at full volume during DND. Real estate notifications do not qualify.

---

### 7. Silent Push and Background Refresh

#### 7.1 Silent Push for Background Data Sync

Silent push notifications (`content-available: 1`) wake the app in the background to perform data sync operations without displaying a visible notification to the user.

**Silent push payload format:**

```json
{
  "aps": {
    "content-available": 1
  },
  "sync_type": "contacts_updated",
  "sync_data": {
    "updated_contact_ids": ["uuid1", "uuid2"],
    "timestamp": "2026-03-02T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Required APNs headers for silent push:**

| Header | Value | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apns-push-type` | `background` | Required for silent push on iOS 13+ |
| `apns-priority` | `5` | Must be `5` (not `10`) for background push. Priority `10` will be rejected for background push type. |
| `apns-topic` | `com.skye.app` | Bundle ID |

**Important constraint:** A silent push payload must NOT include `alert`, `badge`, or `sound` in the `aps` dictionary. Including these alongside `content-available: 1` creates a "hybrid" push that may not reliably wake the app in the background.

#### 7.2 Silent Push Use Cases

| Use Case | Trigger | Sync Action |
|---|---|---|
| Contact data sync | Server-side contact update (e.g., CRM import, lead assignment) | Fetch updated contact records, update local cache |
| Badge count correction | Periodic (every 6 hours) or after batch server operations | Fetch current badge count, update app badge |
| Briefing pre-fetch | 30 minutes before scheduled briefing delivery | Download and cache briefing data so it loads instantly when the visible notification arrives |
| Property data refresh | New MLS feed ingestion complete | Fetch updated property listings for cached searches |
| Chat message sync | New message in an existing conversation (when real-time WebSocket is disconnected) | Fetch recent messages, update conversation list |

#### 7.3 Background Fetch Handler

In native Swift, silent push handling is done directly in the `AppDelegate` via `application(_:didReceiveRemoteNotification:fetchCompletionHandler:)`. No headless JavaScript runtime, no bridge -- just native async/await code.

```swift
// AppDelegate.swift

func application(
    _ application: UIApplication,
    didReceiveRemoteNotification userInfo: [AnyHashable: Any],
    fetchCompletionHandler completionHandler: @escaping (UIBackgroundFetchResult) -> Void
) {
    guard let syncType = userInfo["sync_type"] as? String else {
        completionHandler(.noData)
        return
    }

    let syncData = userInfo["sync_data"] as? [String: Any]

    Task {
        do {
            switch syncType {
            case "contacts_updated":
                let contactIds = (syncData?["updated_contact_ids"] as? [String]) ?? []
                try await SyncManager.shared.syncContacts(ids: contactIds)
                completionHandler(.newData)

            case "badge_sync":
                await BadgeManager.shared.syncBadge()
                completionHandler(.newData)

            case "briefing_prefetch":
                let briefingDate = syncData?["briefing_date"] as? String ?? ""
                try await SyncManager.shared.prefetchBriefing(date: briefingDate)
                completionHandler(.newData)

            case "property_refresh":
                try await SyncManager.shared.refreshPropertyCache()
                completionHandler(.newData)

            case "chat_sync":
                let conversationIds = (syncData?["conversation_ids"] as? [String]) ?? []
                try await SyncManager.shared.syncRecentMessages(conversationIds: conversationIds)
                completionHandler(.newData)

            default:
                Logger.push.warning("Unknown sync type: \(syncType)")
                completionHandler(.noData)
            }
        } catch {
            Logger.push.error("Background sync failed: \(error.localizedDescription)")
            completionHandler(.failed)
        }

        // Must complete within ~30 seconds
        // iOS will terminate the process if exceeded
    }
}
```

#### 7.4 iOS Background Tasks Framework Integration

For more reliable background processing beyond silent push, register `BGTaskScheduler` tasks.

**Task registration (in App initialization):**

```swift
import BackgroundTasks

// In AppDelegate or @main App struct init

func registerBackgroundTasks() {
    BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(
        forTaskWithIdentifier: "com.skye.app.refresh",
        using: nil
    ) { task in
        guard let refreshTask = task as? BGAppRefreshTask else { return }
        self.handleAppRefresh(task: refreshTask)
    }

    BGTaskScheduler.shared.register(
        forTaskWithIdentifier: "com.skye.app.dataSync",
        using: nil
    ) { task in
        guard let processingTask = task as? BGProcessingTask else { return }
        self.handleDataSync(task: processingTask)
    }
}

func handleAppRefresh(task: BGAppRefreshTask) {
    // Schedule the next refresh
    scheduleAppRefresh()

    let syncTask = Task {
        do {
            // Lightweight sync: badge count, recent contacts, chat messages
            await BadgeManager.shared.syncBadge()
            try await SyncManager.shared.syncRecentData()
            task.setTaskCompleted(success: true)
        } catch {
            task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
        }
    }

    task.expirationHandler = {
        syncTask.cancel()
        task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
    }
}

func handleDataSync(task: BGProcessingTask) {
    let syncTask = Task {
        do {
            // Heavy sync: full property cache refresh, contact data
            try await SyncManager.shared.fullSync()
            task.setTaskCompleted(success: true)
        } catch {
            task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
        }
    }

    task.expirationHandler = {
        syncTask.cancel()
        task.setTaskCompleted(success: false)
    }
}

func scheduleAppRefresh() {
    let request = BGAppRefreshTaskRequest(identifier: "com.skye.app.refresh")
    request.earliestBeginDate = Date(timeIntervalSinceNow: 15 * 60)  // 15 minutes
    try? BGTaskScheduler.shared.submit(request)
}

func scheduleDataSync() {
    let request = BGProcessingTaskRequest(identifier: "com.skye.app.dataSync")
    request.earliestBeginDate = Date(timeIntervalSinceNow: 60 * 60)  // 1 hour
    request.requiresNetworkConnectivity = true
    request.requiresExternalPower = false
    try? BGTaskScheduler.shared.submit(request)
}
```

**Info.plist entries:**

```xml
<key>BGTaskSchedulerPermittedIdentifiers</key>
<array>
  <string>com.skye.app.refresh</string>
  <string>com.skye.app.dataSync</string>
</array>
```

**Xcode capabilities:** Ensure "Background Modes" includes both "Background fetch" and "Remote notifications."

#### 7.5 Battery Impact Mitigation

| Strategy | Implementation |
|---|---|
| Rate-limit silent pushes | Server sends a maximum of 2-3 silent pushes per hour per device. Batch updates instead of sending per-change. |
| Minimize network requests | Background sync fetches only delta/changed data, not full datasets. Use `If-Modified-Since` headers. |
| Cache aggressively | Store fetched data in local SwiftData / Core Data / SQLite. Only fetch what changed since `last_sync_timestamp`. |
| Respect Low Power Mode | Check `ProcessInfo.processInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled` before heavy sync operations. If enabled, skip non-critical syncs. |
| Use BGProcessingTask for heavy work | Large data syncs (full MLS refresh) use `BGProcessingTask` which runs during charging/idle, not `BGAppRefreshTask`. |
| Complete handlers promptly | Always call `completionHandler(.newData)`, `.noData`, or `.failed` within 25 seconds. iOS tracks completion time and penalizes slow apps by reducing future background execution opportunities. |

```swift
// Example: Respecting Low Power Mode in background sync

func syncRecentData() async throws {
    let isLowPower = ProcessInfo.processInfo.isLowPowerModeEnabled

    // Always sync badge (lightweight)
    await BadgeManager.shared.syncBadge()

    // Skip non-critical syncs in Low Power Mode
    if !isLowPower {
        try await syncContacts(ids: [])      // Delta sync
        try await syncRecentMessages(conversationIds: [])
    }
}
```

#### 7.6 Testing Strategy for Background Push Delivery

| Method | How | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| **Xcode Debug** | In Xcode, while debugging: Debug > Simulate Background Fetch. Alternatively, edit scheme > Run > Options > Background Fetch checkbox. | Quick iteration during development. |
| **Push notification tester** | Use a tool like Pusher or a custom script with `@parse/node-apn` to send test silent pushes to a development device. | Testing actual APNs delivery path. |
| **Console.app** | Connect device to Mac, open Console.app, filter by `dasd` (Duet Activity Scheduler Daemon) and your app's bundle ID. Shows when iOS grants/denies background execution. | Diagnosing why background tasks are not running. |
| **Xcode device logs** | Filter device logs for `BackgroundTask` and `pushRegistry`. Shows silent push receipt and processing. | Verifying silent push delivery on real devices. |
| **TestFlight** | Distribute TestFlight builds to QA devices. Use sandbox APNs environment. Send test pushes from staging backend. | Production-like testing. Critical since simulators do not support push notifications. |
| **Simulating BGTaskScheduler** | Use `e -l objc -- (void)[[BGTaskScheduler sharedScheduler] _simulateLaunchForTaskWithIdentifier:@"com.skye.app.refresh"]` in Xcode LLDB debugger. | Testing BGTaskScheduler tasks without waiting for iOS to schedule them. |

**Testing checklist:**

- Silent push received while app is in foreground (should trigger handler immediately)
- Silent push received while app is in background (should wake app)
- Silent push received while app is terminated/killed by user (will NOT be delivered -- by Apple design)
- Silent push with `apns-priority: 5` delivers within 5-15 minutes (not guaranteed to be instant)
- Background task completes within 30 seconds
- Badge count updated correctly after background sync
- Completion handler called with correct result (`.newData`, `.noData`, `.failed`)
- Low Power Mode: background sync degrades gracefully (skips non-critical operations)
- No crashes in background execution (all code is native -- no bridge or JS runtime constraints)

---

### Appendix A: Notification Category Registration (Complete)

All notification categories must be registered at app launch, before any notifications arrive. Place this in the app initialization sequence, after the `UNUserNotificationCenter` delegate is set.

```swift
// Notifications/NotificationCategoryRegistrar.swift

import UserNotifications

enum NotificationCategoryRegistrar {

    static func registerAll() {
        let center = UNUserNotificationCenter.current()

        // --- Engagement Alert ---
        let engagementCategory = UNNotificationCategory(
            identifier: "ENGAGEMENT_ALERT",
            actions: [
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "call_now", title: "Call Now",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "snooze_1d", title: "Snooze 1 Day",
                                     options: []),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "dismiss", title: "Dismiss",
                                     options: [.destructive]),
            ],
            intentIdentifiers: [],
            hiddenPreviewsBodyPlaceholder: "Follow-up reminder",
            categorySummaryFormat: "%u+ contacts need follow-up",
            options: []
        )

        // --- Morning Briefing ---
        let briefingCategory = UNNotificationCategory(
            identifier: "MORNING_BRIEFING",
            actions: [
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "view_briefing", title: "View Briefing",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "dismiss_briefing", title: "Dismiss",
                                     options: []),
            ],
            intentIdentifiers: [],
            options: []
        )

        // --- Overdue Task ---
        let overdueTaskCategory = UNNotificationCategory(
            identifier: "OVERDUE_TASK",
            actions: [
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "mark_complete", title: "Mark Complete",
                                     options: []),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "snooze_task", title: "Snooze",
                                     options: []),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "view_task", title: "View",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
            ],
            intentIdentifiers: [],
            categorySummaryFormat: "%u+ overdue tasks",
            options: []
        )

        // --- Property Match ---
        let propertyMatchCategory = UNNotificationCategory(
            identifier: "PROPERTY_MATCH",
            actions: [
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "view_property", title: "View Property",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "share_with_client", title: "Share with Client",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
            ],
            intentIdentifiers: [],
            categorySummaryFormat: "%u+ new property matches",
            options: []
        )

        // --- AI Completion ---
        let aiCompletionCategory = UNNotificationCategory(
            identifier: "AI_COMPLETION",
            actions: [
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "view_result", title: "View Result",
                                     options: [.foreground]),
                UNNotificationAction(identifier: "dismiss_ai", title: "Dismiss",
                                     options: []),
            ],
            intentIdentifiers: [],
            options: []
        )

        center.setNotificationCategories([
            engagementCategory,
            briefingCategory,
            overdueTaskCategory,
            propertyMatchCategory,
            aiCompletionCategory,
        ])
    }
}
```

**Registration call site:**

```swift
// In App init or AppDelegate didFinishLaunchingWithOptions

@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    @UIApplicationDelegateAdaptor(AppDelegate.self) var appDelegate

    init() {
        // Set notification delegate before app finishes launching
        let notificationDelegate = NotificationDelegate()
        UNUserNotificationCenter.current().delegate = notificationDelegate

        // Register all notification categories
        NotificationCategoryRegistrar.registerAll()
    }

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ContentView()
        }
    }
}
```

---

### Appendix B: Server-Side Push Service (Complete)

```javascript
// src/services/pushService.js (backend -- unchanged, server is platform-agnostic)

const apn = require('@parse/node-apn');

class PushService {
  constructor() {
    this.apnsProvider = new apn.Provider({
      token: {
        key: process.env.APNS_AUTH_KEY_PATH,
        keyId: process.env.APNS_KEY_ID,
        teamId: process.env.APNS_TEAM_ID,
      },
      production: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
    });
  }

  /**
   * Send a push notification to a specific user
   * across all their registered devices.
   */
  async sendToUser(userId, notificationPayload) {
    const subscriptions = await db.query(
      `SELECT * FROM push_subscriptions
       WHERE user_id = $1 AND is_active = true`,
      [userId]
    );

    const badgeCount = await this.getBadgeCount(userId);

    const results = await Promise.allSettled(
      subscriptions.map(async (sub) => {
        if (sub.platform === 'ios') {
          return this.sendAPNs(sub, notificationPayload, badgeCount);
        } else if (sub.platform === 'web') {
          return this.sendWebPush(sub, notificationPayload, badgeCount);
        }
      })
    );

    // Process results -- deactivate failed subscriptions
    for (let i = 0; i < results.length; i++) {
      const result = results[i];
      const sub = subscriptions[i];

      if (result.status === 'rejected' || result.value?.failed) {
        const failureReason = result.value?.reason || result.reason;
        if (this.isTokenInvalid(failureReason)) {
          await db.query(
            `UPDATE push_subscriptions
             SET is_active = false, updated_at = NOW()
             WHERE id = $1`,
            [sub.id]
          );
        }
      } else {
        await db.query(
          `UPDATE push_subscriptions SET last_used_at = NOW()
           WHERE id = $1`,
          [sub.id]
        );
      }
    }
  }

  /**
   * Send via APNs.
   */
  async sendAPNs(subscription, payload, badgeCount) {
    const notification = new apn.Notification();

    notification.alert = {
      title: payload.title,
      subtitle: payload.subtitle || undefined,
      body: payload.body,
    };
    notification.badge = badgeCount;
    notification.sound = payload.sound || 'default';
    notification.threadId = payload.threadId;
    notification.category = payload.category;
    notification.mutableContent = payload.mutableContent ? 1 : 0;
    notification.contentAvailable = payload.contentAvailable ? 1 : 0;

    if (payload.interruptionLevel) {
      notification.interruptionLevel = payload.interruptionLevel;
    }
    if (payload.relevanceScore !== undefined) {
      notification.relevanceScore = payload.relevanceScore;
    }

    notification.payload = {
      type: payload.type,
      deep_link: payload.deepLink,
      notification_id: payload.notificationId,
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      ...payload.customData,
    };

    notification.topic = process.env.APNS_BUNDLE_ID;
    notification.pushType =
      payload.contentAvailable ? 'background' : 'alert';
    notification.priority = payload.contentAvailable ? 5 : 10;

    if (payload.collapseId) {
      notification.collapseId = payload.collapseId;
    }
    if (payload.expiration) {
      notification.expiry =
        Math.floor(payload.expiration.getTime() / 1000);
    }

    const result = await this.apnsProvider.send(
      notification,
      subscription.token
    );

    if (result.failed.length > 0) {
      const failure = result.failed[0];
      return {
        failed: true,
        reason: failure.response?.reason || 'unknown',
        statusCode: failure.status,
      };
    }

    return { failed: false };
  }

  /**
   * Check if a failure reason indicates an invalid token.
   */
  isTokenInvalid(reason) {
    const invalidReasons = [
      'BadDeviceToken',
      'Unregistered',
      'ExpiredProviderToken',
      'TopicDisallowed',
      410,
      404,
    ];
    return invalidReasons.includes(reason);
  }

  /**
   * Get current badge count for a user.
   */
  async getBadgeCount(userId) {
    const result = await db.query(
      `SELECT badge_count FROM users WHERE id = $1`,
      [userId]
    );
    return result.rows[0]?.badge_count || 0;
  }

  /**
   * Graceful shutdown.
   */
  async shutdown() {
    await this.apnsProvider.shutdown();
  }
}

module.exports = new PushService();
```

---

---

## V2: Performance, Infrastructure & Build Pipeline

---

### 1. App Startup Performance

#### 1.1 Cold Launch Budget Breakdown

**Target: <1,500ms from tap to interactive on iPhone 13 (A15 Bionic, iOS 16+).** Native Swift compilation eliminates the JS engine overhead entirely, enabling a tighter budget than React Native.

| Phase | Budget | What Happens | Failure Action |
|-------|--------|-------------|----------------|
| **Native Init** | <300ms | iOS launches process, loads dylibs (UIKit, SwiftUI, Foundation, etc.), executes `main()`, displays `LaunchScreen.storyboard` | Profile with Instruments > App Launch. Reduce embedded frameworks, strip unused architectures. Enable `DEAD_CODE_STRIPPING`. |
| **Dylib Loading + Swift Runtime Init** | <200ms | Dynamic linker resolves symbols, Swift runtime metadata initializes, `@main` App struct `init()` runs | Reduce number of dynamic frameworks. Prefer static linking. Use `mergeable libraries` in Xcode 15+. |
| **SwiftUI View Body Evaluation** | <400ms | Root `App` body evaluates, `WindowGroup` scene activates, `NavigationStack` initializes, auth state resolves, active tab view renders | Defer non-critical `EnvironmentObject` setup. Keep root view body lightweight. Use `.task {}` for async work. |
| **First Meaningful Paint** | <600ms | Launch screen dismissed via `UIView.transition()`. User sees real UI with either skeleton placeholders or cached data. App accepts touch input. | Audit paint waterfall with Xcode Instruments > SwiftUI template. Reduce view hierarchy depth. Use cached data for instant paint. |
| **Total** | **<1,500ms** | | CI gate: fail build if P95 cold launch exceeds 2,000ms on iPhone 13 test device. |

#### 1.2 Native Swift Compilation (Mandatory)

Swift compiles to native ARM64 binaries via LLVM. There is no JavaScript engine, no bytecode interpreter, and no JIT warmup. This is non-negotiable and is inherent to the platform.

```swift
// Build Settings (Xcode project or Package.swift)
// These are configured in the Xcode project, not in code.
//
// Release build settings:
//   SWIFT_OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL = -O          // Full optimization
//   SWIFT_COMPILATION_MODE = wholemodule   // Whole-module optimization
//   GCC_OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL = -Os           // Optimize for size
//   DEAD_CODE_STRIPPING = YES
//   STRIP_INSTALLED_PRODUCT = YES
//   ENABLE_BITCODE = NO                    // Deprecated in Xcode 14+
```

**Why native Swift compilation is superior:**
- **No runtime bytecode parsing**: Swift compiles to native ARM64 machine code at build time via LLVM. There is no bytecode loading phase at runtime, eliminating ~600ms of JS bundle load time entirely.
- **Lower memory footprint**: No JS engine heap allocation (Hermes/JSC typically consumes 30-60MB). Swift uses ARC (Automatic Reference Counting) with deterministic deallocation, not garbage collection.
- **Instant code execution**: All code paths are compiled ahead of time. No JIT compilation warmup, no interpreter overhead.

**Build verification requirements:**
- Verify Release builds use `-O` (optimized) via Xcode Build Settings > Swift Compiler - Code Generation > Optimization Level.
- Confirm `wholemodule` compilation mode for Release (enables cross-file optimization).
- Profile with Instruments > App Launch template to verify dylib load count is under 200.
- Use `DYLD_PRINT_STATISTICS=1` environment variable in scheme to log pre-main() timing during development.

#### 1.3 App Binary Size Budget

**Target: <30MB compressed App Store download. Hard ceiling: 40MB (triggers immediate audit).** Note: Swift binaries are larger than JS bundles but the total app download is comparable since there is no bundled JS engine.

| Budget Category | Allocation | Notes |
|----------------|-----------|-------|
| Swift runtime + system frameworks | ~5MB | Embedded in iOS, not counted toward download |
| SwiftUI + Combine framework overhead | ~0MB | System frameworks, not bundled |
| Navigation (NavigationStack) | ~0MB | Built into SwiftUI |
| Networking layer (URLSession) | ~0MB | System framework |
| App business logic + screens (compiled) | ~8MB | Our Swift code, compiled ARM64 |
| Asset catalog (images, colors, icons) | ~5MB | Compressed in asset catalog |
| SPM dependencies (Nuke, KeychainAccess, etc.) | ~4MB | Statically linked, dead-code-stripped |
| Sentry SDK | ~3MB | Crash reporting framework |
| Remaining headroom | ~5MB | Safety margin |
| **Total** | **<30MB** | Compressed App Store download size |

**Binary size audit strategy:**

```bash
# Generate App Thinning Size Report after archive
xcodebuild -exportArchive \
  -archivePath build/Skye.xcarchive \
  -exportPath build/export \
  -exportOptionsPlist ExportOptions.plist

# The thinning report is at:
# build/export/App Thinning Size Report.txt
# Shows per-device variant sizes (iPhone 13, iPhone 15 Pro, etc.)

# Alternatively, use Xcode Organizer > App Size Report
# after uploading to App Store Connect
```

**Enforcement rules:**
1. CI step archives the app with `xcodebuild archive` and generates a thinning size report.
2. If compressed size exceeds 30MB for any device variant: warning annotation on PR.
3. If compressed size exceeds 40MB: CI fails with error `BINARY_SIZE_EXCEEDED`.
4. Every new SPM dependency addition must include a binary size impact assessment in the PR description (before/after size).
5. Weekly automated report: framework contribution breakdown via `bloaty` or Xcode Organizer size analysis, posted to team Slack channel.

**Anti-bloat practices:**
- Dead code stripping: ensure `DEAD_CODE_STRIPPING = YES` and `STRIP_INSTALLED_PRODUCT = YES` in Release builds.
- No heavy date libraries (use Foundation `DateFormatter` and `RelativeDateTimeFormatter`).
- Audit for unused SPM dependencies: review Package.resolved regularly.
- Images and assets: never bundle large images in the asset catalog. Use CDN for dynamic images, asset catalog only for icons, placeholders, and launch screen assets.
- Prefer system SF Symbols over custom icon assets where possible (zero binary cost).

#### 1.4 Lazy Loading Strategy

In a compiled Swift app, there is no code splitting or JS chunk loading. All code is compiled into the binary. However, **view instantiation** is deferred using lazy `NavigationLink` destinations and `@ViewBuilder` closures, meaning heavy views are not created until navigation occurs.

**Eagerly instantiated views** (created at app launch, part of the initial tab bar):
| Screen | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| `AuthView` (Login/SignUp) | Must be immediately available if user is unauthenticated |
| `ChatView` (Home tab) | Primary landing screen -- user sees this first on every launch |

**Lazily instantiated views** (created on first navigation via `NavigationStack` destination closures):
| Screen | Trigger | Estimated Instantiation Time |
|--------|---------|-------------------|
| `PeopleView` (Contacts list) | User taps People tab | <50ms (view body evaluation only) |
| `PeopleDetailView` | User taps a contact | <30ms |
| `PropertiesView` | User taps Properties tab | <50ms |
| `PropertyDetailView` | User taps a property | <30ms |
| `StudioView` | User taps Studio tab | <80ms (heaviest -- includes rich media components) |
| `SettingsView` | User taps Settings tab | <20ms |
| `ProfileView` | User taps profile | <20ms |
| All sheet/modal views | Triggered by user action | <30ms |

**Implementation:**

```swift
// Navigation/AppTabView.swift

struct AppTabView: View {
    @State private var selectedTab: AppTab = .chat

    var body: some View {
        TabView(selection: $selectedTab) {
            // Eagerly loaded -- part of initial tab bar
            ChatView()
                .tabItem { Label("Chat", systemImage: "message.fill") }
                .tag(AppTab.chat)

            // Lazily instantiated -- SwiftUI only evaluates the body
            // of the selected tab. Non-selected tabs are NOT instantiated
            // until the user taps them.
            PeopleView()
                .tabItem { Label("People", systemImage: "person.2.fill") }
                .tag(AppTab.people)

            PropertiesView()
                .tabItem { Label("Properties", systemImage: "building.2.fill") }
                .tag(AppTab.properties)

            StudioView()
                .tabItem { Label("Studio", systemImage: "paintbrush.fill") }
                .tag(AppTab.studio)

            SettingsView()
                .tabItem { Label("Settings", systemImage: "gear") }
                .tag(AppTab.settings)
        }
    }
}
```

```swift
// Detail views are lazily loaded via NavigationStack destinations
struct PeopleView: View {
    @StateObject private var viewModel = PeopleViewModel()

    var body: some View {
        NavigationStack {
            List(viewModel.filteredContacts) { contact in
                NavigationLink(value: contact) {
                    ContactListRow(contact: contact)
                }
            }
            .navigationDestination(for: Contact.self) { contact in
                // PeopleDetailView is only instantiated when navigated to
                PeopleDetailView(contact: contact)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

**Background prefetching of data:** After the app becomes interactive and the Chat view is rendered, idle-time prefetch the People and Properties data (the next most likely navigations) using background tasks:

```swift
// After launch screen hides and Chat renders:
Task(priority: .utility) {
    try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(1))
    await ContactsRepository.shared.prefetch()
    await PropertiesRepository.shared.prefetch()
}
```

#### 1.5 Splash Screen Strategy

**Requirement: Zero white flash. Zero layout jump. The native launch screen persists until the SwiftUI app has rendered its first meaningful frame.**

**Implementation: `LaunchScreen.storyboard` with manual dismissal via overlay transition.**

```swift
// LaunchScreen.storyboard configuration (set in Xcode):
// - Background color: #0A0A0F (matches app dark theme default)
// - Centered Skye logo image
// - Set in Info.plist: UILaunchStoryboardName = "LaunchScreen"
```

**Manual hide control:**

```swift
// SkyeApp.swift
import SwiftUI

@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    @StateObject private var appState = AppBootstrapState()

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            ZStack {
                if appState.isReady {
                    RootView()
                        .environmentObject(appState.authManager)
                        .environmentObject(appState.themeManager)
                        .transition(.opacity)
                }

                // Launch screen overlay -- covers the app until ready
                if appState.showLaunchScreen {
                    LaunchScreenView()
                        .ignoresSafeArea()
                        .transition(.opacity)
                }
            }
            .animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3), value: appState.showLaunchScreen)
            .task {
                await appState.prepare()
            }
        }
    }
}

@MainActor
final class AppBootstrapState: ObservableObject {
    @Published var isReady = false
    @Published var showLaunchScreen = true

    let authManager = AuthManager()
    let themeManager = ThemeManager()

    func prepare() async {
        do {
            // 1. Read auth token from Keychain
            let token = try? KeychainManager.shared.read(key: .authToken)

            // 2. Initialize auth state (optimistic -- details in 1.6)
            await authManager.initialize(token: token)

            // 3. Prefetch active tab data (Chat history or contacts)
            await prefetchActiveTabData()

            // 4. Mark ready
            isReady = true

            // 5. Dismiss launch screen after a brief layout pass
            try? await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(50))
            showLaunchScreen = false
        } catch {
            // Even on error, show the app (will show auth screen)
            isReady = true
            showLaunchScreen = false
            SentrySDK.capture(error: error)
        }
    }
}
```

**Critical rules:**
- The `LaunchScreen.storyboard` background color (`#0A0A0F`) MUST exactly match the app's root background color. Any mismatch creates a visible flash.
- The launch screen is rendered by iOS natively from a storyboard. It is NOT a SwiftUI view. This means it is visible instantly, before any Swift code executes.
- The `LaunchScreenView` overlay is dismissed ONLY after `isReady` is true, guaranteeing the SwiftUI view tree has painted pixels to the screen.
- Maximum splash duration hard cap: 5,000ms. If `prepare()` has not completed by then, force-hide and show whatever state is available. This prevents the app from appearing frozen.

#### 1.6 App Initialization Sequence

Exact order of operations from the moment the user taps the app icon:

```
T+0ms     -- USER TAPS APP ICON ──────────────────────────────────────
           |
T+0-50ms  -- iOS PROCESS LAUNCH ──────────────────────────────────────
           |  iOS kernel creates process, loads Mach-O binary
           |  Dynamic linker (dyld) loads dylibs (UIKit, SwiftUI, Foundation, etc.)
           |  Swift runtime metadata initializes
           |  main() is called -> @main App struct init()
           |
T+50ms    -- STEP 1: NATIVE LAUNCH SCREEN DISPLAYED ─────────────────
           |  iOS displays LaunchScreen.storyboard
           |  User sees Skye logo on dark background immediately
           |  SwiftUI WindowGroup scene activates
           |
T+50-250ms-- STEP 2: SWIFT APP INITIALIZATION ───────────────────────
           |  @StateObject properties initialize
           |  App body is first evaluated
           |  LaunchScreenView overlay is displayed (seamless from storyboard)
           |  .task {} modifier fires, calling prepare()
           |
T+250ms   -- STEP 3: SwiftUI VIEW TREE EVALUATES ────────────────────
           |  Root view body evaluated
           |  All @StateObject and @EnvironmentObject instances created
           |  NavigationStack, TabView structure established
           |  Target: complete by T+500ms
           |
T+500ms   -- STEP 4: AUTH TOKEN READ FROM KEYCHAIN ──────────────────
           |  KeychainManager.shared.read(key: .authToken)
           |  KeychainManager.shared.read(key: .refreshToken)
           |  Read is fast (~5-20ms) -- Keychain access on device
           |
T+520ms   -- STEP 5: TOKEN VALIDATION (OPTIMISTIC) ──────────────────
           |  IF token exists:
           |    |-- Decode JWT locally (check exp claim)
           |    |-- IF not expired: assume valid, proceed authenticated
           |    |-- IF expired but refresh token exists: proceed authenticated
           |    |   (refresh will happen async in background)
           |    |-- IF no tokens: proceed to auth screen
           |  IF no token:
           |    |-- Proceed to auth screen (login/signup)
           |
           |  CRITICAL: Do NOT make a network call to validate the token
           |  during startup. This would add 200-800ms to launch time.
           |  Validate asynchronously after the app is interactive.
           |
T+550ms   -- STEP 6: VIEW HIERARCHY ACTIVATES ───────────────────────
           |  Root view resolves which content to show:
           |    |-- Authenticated -> ChatView (home tab)
           |    |-- Unauthenticated -> AuthView
           |  TabView mounts (tabs registered but
           |  only active tab view body evaluates)
           |
T+600ms   -- STEP 7: ACTIVE TAB DATA PREFETCH BEGINS ────────────────
           |  IF authenticated:
           |    |-- ContactsRepository.shared.prefetch()
           |    |-- Read from local SwiftData/UserDefaults cache first
           |    |-- If cache hit: use immediately. If miss: fetch from API.
           |  This runs BEFORE launch screen hides. User sees splash during fetch.
           |  Timeout: 2000ms. If not complete, show skeleton instead.
           |
T+1000ms  -- STEP 8: LAUNCH SCREEN HIDES, APP IS INTERACTIVE ────────
(target)   |  showLaunchScreen set to false -> overlay fades out
           |  User sees either:
           |    |-- Full Chat screen with data (cache hit or fast fetch)
           |    |-- Chat screen with skeleton loading state (fetch pending)
           |    |-- Auth screen (unauthenticated)
           |  App accepts touch input. User can navigate.
           |
T+1000ms+ -- STEP 9: BACKGROUND TASKS ───────────────────────────────
           |  All of these run after launch screen hides, non-blocking:
           |
           |  |-- Token validation: POST /api/auth/validate
           |  |   IF invalid -> force logout, redirect to AuthView
           |  |   IF valid -> no-op (we were right to be optimistic)
           |  |
           |  |-- Token refresh (if token was expired):
           |  |   POST /api/auth/refresh with refresh_token
           |  |   Store new tokens in Keychain
           |  |
           |  |-- Push notification registration:
           |  |   Request permission (if not already granted)
           |  |   Get APNs device token
           |  |   POST /api/users/push-token
           |  |
           |  |-- Prefetch adjacent tab data:
           |  |   ContactsRepository.shared.prefetch()  // People tab
           |  |   PropertiesRepository.shared.prefetch() // Properties tab
           |  |
           |  |-- Sync offline queue (if pending mutations exist):
           |  |   NetworkQueue.shared.flush()
           |  |
           |  |-- Analytics: app_launch event with timing data
           |
           |-- APP IS FULLY LOADED ───────────────────────────────────
```

**Initialization timeout safety:** If any step in the critical path (Steps 4-7) takes longer than 3,000ms total, force-proceed to Step 8. Show whatever is available. Log the timeout to Sentry as a performance warning. The user must never stare at a splash screen for more than 3 seconds.

---

### 2. Runtime Performance

#### 2.1 Frame Rate Targets

| Context | Target FPS | Measurement Method |
|---------|-----------|-------------------|
| Scrolling (List, ScrollView) | 60fps (16.67ms/frame) | Xcode Instruments > Animation Hitches template, Core Animation FPS debug gauge |
| Scrolling on ProMotion devices (iPhone 13 Pro+) | 120fps (8.33ms/frame) | Must not lock to 60fps -- SwiftUI respects `CADisableMinimumFrameDurationOnPhone` by default |
| Animations (`withAnimation`, `.animation()`) | 60fps | All SwiftUI animations are GPU-accelerated via Core Animation -- runs on render server process |
| Screen transitions (NavigationStack) | 60fps | Native UIKit transitions under the hood -- no JS bridge overhead |
| Keyboard open/close | 60fps | Native keyboard animation via UIKit -- SwiftUI `.keyboardAdaptive()` modifier |
| Chat message streaming (typing indicator, token rendering) | 60fps | Batch state updates, buffer token appends every 50ms to avoid per-token view re-evaluation |

**Note on native performance:** SwiftUI views are backed by Core Animation layers rendered by a separate render server process. Achieving 60fps for standard scrolling and animations is trivial in native Swift -- the framework handles this automatically. The primary risk is performing expensive work in view `body` evaluations, which blocks the main thread.

**ProMotion support:**

```swift
// SwiftUI respects ProMotion (120Hz) automatically.
// Ensure Info.plist includes:
//   CADisableMinimumFrameDurationOnPhone = true
// This is the default in iOS 15+. No additional configuration needed.
// All SwiftUI animations and scrolling will render at 120fps on ProMotion devices.
```

**FPS monitoring in development:**

```swift
// Enable in debug builds only
#if DEBUG
// Use Xcode debug gauges (CPU, Memory, FPS) visible in the debug navigator
// Use Instruments > Core Animation template for detailed frame analysis
// Use os_signpost for custom frame timing:
import os

let perfLog = OSLog(subsystem: "com.skyecrm.app", category: .pointsOfInterest)

func measureFrameTime(_ label: String, block: () -> Void) {
    let signpostID = OSSignpostID(log: perfLog)
    os_signpost(.begin, log: perfLog, name: "Frame", signpostID: signpostID, "%{public}s", label)
    block()
    os_signpost(.end, log: perfLog, name: "Frame", signpostID: signpostID)
}
#endif
```

**CI enforcement:** Maestro E2E tests include automated scroll tests. If any test records a frame drop below 55fps for more than 500ms continuously, the test fails.

#### 2.2 Main Thread Budget

**Hard limit: <16ms per frame on main thread.** Any operation exceeding this will cause frame drops. In native Swift, there is a single main thread (no JS thread / UI thread split). All UI work happens on the main thread; heavy work must be dispatched to background queues.

**Operations that MUST run off the main thread:**

| Operation | Solution | Thread/Queue |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| Network requests | `URLSession` (async/await) | URLSession's delegate queue (background) |
| Image decoding | `AsyncImage` or Nuke (native decoding off-main-thread) | Background thread |
| Heavy data transforms (500+ contacts sort/filter) | `Task(priority: .utility)` with `@MainActor` publish | Background concurrent queue |
| JSON parsing of large responses | `JSONDecoder` in `Task(priority: .userInitiated)` | Background thread |
| Core Data / SwiftData batch operations | Background `ModelContext` | Background thread |
| File I/O (cache reads/writes) | `Task(priority: .background)` | Background thread |

**Operations acceptable on main thread (if <16ms):**

| Operation | Typical Duration | Acceptable? |
|-----------|-----------------|-------------|
| SwiftUI state update (single `@State` property) | 1-3ms | Yes |
| In-memory cache read | <1ms | Yes |
| Navigation push/pop | 2-5ms | Yes |
| Small array filter (<100 items) | 1-5ms | Yes |
| Date formatting (single value) | <1ms | Yes |

**Operations that MUST be deferred or chunked:**

| Operation | Strategy |
|-----------|----------|
| Sorting 500+ contacts | Compute in `Task(priority: .utility)`, publish result to `@Published` property on `@MainActor` |
| Full-text search across contacts | Debounce input by 300ms, run search in `Task(priority: .userInitiated)` |
| Chat history parsing (1000+ messages) | Paginate: load 50 messages initially, load more on scroll |
| JSON parsing of large API responses (>50KB) | Decode in background task, publish result to main actor |

#### 2.3 SwiftUI Rendering Optimization

##### 2.3.1 View Identity and Equatable Strategy

**Policy: SwiftUI's structural identity system automatically avoids unnecessary view updates. Use `Equatable` conformance on view models and data types to help SwiftUI's diffing algorithm. Do NOT over-optimize -- only where re-evaluation cost is measurable (>2ms).**

SwiftUI does not have React's "re-render everything" model. Views are value types; SwiftUI compares the view struct's properties and only re-evaluates body when inputs change. This is fundamentally more efficient than React's reconciliation.

| Component | Optimization | Reason |
|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `ContactListRow` | `Equatable` conformance on `Contact` model | Rendered inside List with 500+ items. SwiftUI diffs by identity -- `Equatable` helps skip body re-evaluation when contact data hasn't changed. |
| `PropertyListRow` | `Equatable` on `Property` model | Same reasoning as ContactListRow. |
| `ChatMessageBubble` | `Equatable` on `ChatMessage` model | Rendered in List. New messages should not re-evaluate all existing message views. |
| `ChatInputView` | Extract as separate view with own `@State` | Isolate input state changes from parent ChatView re-evaluation. |
| `AppTabBar` | Minimal -- TabView handles this | SwiftUI TabView only evaluates the selected tab's body. |
| `AvatarView` | Nuke `LazyImage` with pipeline caching | Rendered in many lists. Image loading is off-main-thread. |
| `ScreenHeader` | No special optimization | Renders once per screen. No perf benefit from additional optimization. |
| `SettingsView` | No special optimization | Rarely visited, simple view. |

```swift
// Example: Contact model with Equatable (synthesized automatically for structs)
struct Contact: Identifiable, Equatable, Hashable {
    let id: String
    var fullName: String
    var company: String?
    var avatarURL: URL?
    var phone: String?
    var email: String?
}

// Example: ContactListRow -- SwiftUI only re-evaluates body when `contact` changes
struct ContactListRow: View, Equatable {
    let contact: Contact
    let onTap: () -> Void

    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 12) {
            AvatarView(url: contact.avatarURL, name: contact.fullName, size: 48)
            VStack(alignment: .leading, spacing: 2) {
                Text(contact.fullName)
                    .font(.body.weight(.medium))
                if let company = contact.company {
                    Text(company)
                        .font(.subheadline)
                        .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
                }
            }
        }
        .contentShape(Rectangle())
        .onTapGesture(perform: onTap)
    }

    // Custom Equatable: only compare contact data, not closure
    static func == (lhs: ContactListRow, rhs: ContactListRow) -> Bool {
        lhs.contact == rhs.contact
    }
}
```

##### 2.3.2 Computed Properties and State Caching Policy

**Policy: Use computed properties for lightweight derivations. Use `@State` or view model `@Published` properties with explicit caching for computationally expensive derivations (>2ms). SwiftUI has no equivalent to `useMemo`/`useCallback` -- there is no "re-render" concept to guard against.**

| Pattern | Where | What | Why |
|---------|-------|------|-----|
| Cached `@Published` | `PeopleViewModel` | Filtered + sorted contacts list | Filtering 500+ contacts on every state change would take 5-15ms. Cache in `@Published var filteredContacts` and recompute only when `contacts`, `searchQuery`, or `sortKey` change via `Combine` pipeline. |
| Cached `@Published` | `ChatViewModel` | Grouped messages by date | Grouping 200+ messages is ~3ms. Cache and update only when `messages` array changes. |
| Computed property | `PropertyDetailViewModel` | Formatted price string | Simple `NumberFormatter` call on a single value -- trivial cost. |
| Separate child view | `PeopleView` | `ContactListRow` as distinct view | SwiftUI only re-evaluates child view when its inputs change. Extracting list items as separate views is the SwiftUI equivalent of `React.memo`. |
| **DO NOT** | `SettingsView` | Simple boolean toggles | Trivial computation. No caching needed. |
| **DO NOT** | Any | Static constants | Define outside view struct as `static let` or file-level constants. |

##### 2.3.3 List Optimization

SwiftUI `List` uses `UICollectionView` (iOS 16+) under the hood, which provides full cell recycling and virtualization automatically. Achieving 60fps scrolling in native List is trivial for most data sets.

Every List in the app MUST follow these patterns:

```swift
List {
    ForEach(viewModel.filteredContacts) { contact in
        NavigationLink(value: contact) {
            ContactListRow(contact: contact)
        }
    }
}
.listStyle(.plain)
// SwiftUI List is fully virtualized by default.
// Only visible rows + a small buffer are in memory.
// Cell recycling is handled automatically by UICollectionView.
// No equivalent of removeClippedSubviews needed -- this is native behavior.
```

**List-specific rules:**
- Contact list (500+ items): SwiftUI `List` handles this natively with cell recycling. Use `Identifiable` conformance on `Contact` for stable identity. If profiling shows issues, consider `LazyVStack` inside `ScrollView` for custom layouts.
- Chat messages (variable height): `List` handles variable height rows natively. For inverted scroll (newest at bottom), use `.defaultScrollAnchor(.bottom)` (iOS 17+) or a `ScrollViewReader` with `scrollTo`.
- **Performance note:** Native SwiftUI `List` on iOS 16+ uses `UICollectionView` with compositional layout. It achieves equivalent or better performance than FlashList/FlatList in React Native. Cell recycling, prefetching, and off-screen view management are all handled by the system. **60fps scrolling with 500+ items is expected behavior, not an optimization target.**

##### 2.3.4 State Isolation (Prevent Cascade Re-evaluations)

**Rule: Keep observable state scoped to the views that need it. A `@Published` property change only re-evaluates views that read that specific property (via `@ObservedObject` or `@EnvironmentObject`).**

SwiftUI's observation system (especially with `@Observable` macro in iOS 17+) provides fine-grained dependency tracking. Only views that read a specific property are re-evaluated when that property changes. This is fundamentally superior to React Context's all-or-nothing re-render model.

```
WRONG:
  One massive @Observable class with user, theme, contacts, chatMessages, notifications
  // Problem: SwiftUI tracks per-property, but a God Object is hard to reason about

CORRECT -- Split into focused observable objects:
```

| Observable | State | Update Frequency | Consumers |
|---------|-------|-----------------|-----------|
| `AuthManager` | `user`, `token`, `isAuthenticated` | Rare (login/logout only) | Navigation guard, profile displays |
| `ThemeManager` | `colorScheme`, `theme` object | Rare (user changes theme) | All UI components via `.environment()` |
| `NetworkMonitor` | `isOnline`, `connectionType` | Occasional (connectivity changes) | Offline banner, NetworkQueue |
| `NotificationManager` | `unreadCount`, `permissions` | Occasional | Tab badge, notification bell |

**Data fetching does NOT use EnvironmentObject.** All server data (contacts, properties, chat messages, etc.) flows through dedicated repository/view model patterns. Each view model publishes only the data its view needs. Views only re-evaluate when THEIR specific data changes.

```swift
// CORRECT: Each screen has its own view model with isolated state
@MainActor
final class PeopleViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published var contacts: [Contact] = []
    @Published var filteredContacts: [Contact] = []
    // Only PeopleView re-evaluates when contacts change
}

@MainActor
final class ChatViewModel: ObservableObject {
```
