```swift
    @Published var messages: [ChatMessage] = []
    // Only ChatView re-evaluates when messages change
    // Does NOT cause PeopleView to re-evaluate
}
```

#### 2.4 Image Performance

**Library: Nuke** (preferred for advanced caching, progressive loading, and memory management in native Swift apps. `AsyncImage` is acceptable for simple cases but lacks disk caching and advanced features).

```swift
import NukeUI

// Contact avatar -- 48x48pt display, load at 2x max (96x96px)
LazyImage(url: contact.avatarURL) { state in
    if let image = state.image {
        image
            .resizable()
            .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fill)
            .transition(.opacity.animation(.easeIn(duration: 0.2)))
    } else if state.isLoading {
        // BlurHash placeholder for instant display
        BlurHashView(hash: contact.avatarBlurhash)
    } else {
        Image(systemName: "person.circle.fill")
            .foregroundStyle(.secondary)
    }
}
.frame(width: 48, height: 48)
.clipShape(Circle())
.processors([.resize(size: CGSize(width: 96, height: 96))])
.priority(.normal)

// Property photo -- full width display
LazyImage(url: property.photoURL) { state in
    if let image = state.image {
        image
            .resizable()
            .aspectRatio(contentMode: .fill)
            .transition(.opacity.animation(.easeIn(duration: 0.3)))
    } else if state.isLoading {
        BlurHashView(hash: property.photoBlurhash)
    }
}
.frame(height: 240)
.clipped()
.priority(.high)
```

**Image rules:**
1. **Never load full-resolution images into thumbnails.** If displaying a 48x48pt avatar, request `?width=96&height=96` from the CDN/image service (2x for Retina). If the backend serves images via Supabase Storage, use Supabase image transforms: `supabaseUrl/storage/v1/object/public/avatars/img.jpg?width=96&height=96`.
2. **BlurHash for all user-generated images.** When images are uploaded, generate a BlurHash server-side (4x3 components, ~20-30 bytes). Store in the database alongside the image URL. Display BlurHash instantly while full image loads.
3. **Memory-disk cache hierarchy.** Nuke's `ImagePipeline` manages this natively. Memory cache for recently viewed (default ~100MB). Disk cache for all downloaded images (cap at 200MB, LRU eviction). Configure via `ImagePipeline.Configuration`.
4. **Prefetch images for visible list items.** Use Nuke's `LazyImageView` which handles prefetching automatically, or use `ImagePrefetcher` for manual control.
5. **Cancel image loads on disappear.** Nuke's `LazyImage` cancels requests automatically when the view disappears from the hierarchy.

#### 2.5 Memory Management

##### 2.5.1 Memory Budgets

| Screen | Memory Budget | Rationale |
|--------|--------------|-----------|
| ChatView | <140MB | Chat history with message bubbles, typing indicators, streamed content. May include rendered tool results (contact cards, property cards). |
| PeopleView | <90MB | List with 500+ contacts. Avatars in memory for visible + buffered rows (~40 items at any time). |
| PeopleDetailView | <110MB | Single contact with full details, activity timeline, notes, linked properties. May include multiple images. |
| PropertiesView | <110MB | Property cards with photos. Heavier images than People. |
| StudioView | <120MB | Rich media content, AI-generated marketing materials. |
| SettingsView | <40MB | Lightweight. Simple form fields. |
| **Total app at any time** | **<280MB** | iOS will terminate apps exceeding ~1.5GB on iPhone 13 (6GB RAM). We stay well under, leaving room for system + background apps. Native Swift apps have lower baseline memory than React Native (no JS engine heap). |

##### 2.5.2 Memory Warning Handling

```swift
// Register for iOS memory warnings
// AppDelegate.swift or via NotificationCenter

import UIKit
import Nuke

final class AppLifecycleObserver {
    static let shared = AppLifecycleObserver()

    func startObserving() {
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            self,
            selector: #selector(handleMemoryWarning),
            name: UIApplication.didReceiveMemoryWarningNotification,
            object: nil
        )
    }

    @objc private func handleMemoryWarning() {
        // 1. Clear image memory cache (disk cache persists)
        ImagePipeline.shared.cache.removeAll()

        // 2. Clear URLSession memory cache
        URLCache.shared.removeAllCachedResponses()

        // 3. Clear any in-memory data caches
        ContactsRepository.shared.trimCache()
        PropertiesRepository.shared.trimCache()

        // 4. Log to Sentry
        SentrySDK.capture(message: "Memory warning received") { scope in
            scope.setLevel(.warning)
            scope.setExtra(value: ProcessInfo.processInfo.physicalMemory, key: "physicalMemory")
            // Add current screen info if available
        }
    }
}
```

##### 2.5.3 Leak Detection

**Development-time leak detection:**

1. **Task cancellation audit:** Every view that creates `Task` instances or `URLSession` data tasks MUST cancel them on disappear:

```swift
// REQUIRED pattern for all views with async work
struct ContactDetailView: View {
    @State private var loadTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    var body: some View {
        VStack { /* ... */ }
            .task {
                // .task automatically cancels when view disappears
                await loadContactDetails()
            }
            .onDisappear {
                // Cancel any additional tasks not managed by .task
                loadTask?.cancel()
            }
    }
}
```

2. **Xcode Instruments -- Leaks template** (development builds): Profile for retain cycles, especially in closures capturing `self`. Use `[weak self]` in escaping closures.

3. **CI memory regression test:** Maestro E2E navigates through all tabs 3 times, then checks memory. If memory exceeds 380MB after returning to ChatView (which would indicate leaked views/view models), the test fails.

4. **Combine subscription audit:** In development, ensure all `AnyCancellable` subscriptions are stored in a `Set<AnyCancellable>` that is deallocated with the view model:

```swift
#if DEBUG
deinit {
    print("\(type(of: self)) deallocated -- \(cancellables.count) subscriptions cleaned up")
}
#endif
```

---

### 3. Network Performance

#### 3.1 API Client Architecture

```swift
// API/APIClient.swift -- Singleton API client

import Foundation

actor APIClient {
    static let shared = APIClient()

    private let session: URLSession
    private var pendingRequests: [String: Task<Any, Error>] = [:]
    private let baseURL: URL

    private init() {
        let config = URLSessionConfiguration.default
        config.httpMaximumConnectionsPerHost = 6
        config.timeoutIntervalForRequest = 15
        config.waitsForConnectivity = true
        // URLSession uses HTTP/2 multiplexing by default on iOS
        // Connection pooling is handled automatically by the system
        self.session = URLSession(configuration: config)
        self.baseURL = URL(string: AppConfig.apiURL)!
    }

    // --- REQUEST DEDUPLICATION ---
    func request<T: Decodable>(
        method: HTTPMethod,
        path: String,
        params: [String: String]? = nil,
        body: Encodable? = nil,
        priority: RequestPriority = .normal,
        timeout: TimeInterval? = nil
    ) async throws -> T {
        let dedupeKey = "\(method.rawValue):\(path):\(params?.description ?? "")"

        // If identical GET request is already in flight, await same task
        if method == .GET, let existing = pendingRequests[dedupeKey] {
            return try await existing.value as! T
        }

        let task = Task<Any, Error> {
            try await executeRequest(
                method: method,
                path: path,
                params: params,
                body: body,
                priority: priority,
                timeout: timeout
            ) as T
        }

        if method == .GET {
            pendingRequests[dedupeKey] = task
        }

        defer {
            if method == .GET {
                pendingRequests.removeValue(forKey: dedupeKey)
            }
        }

        return try await task.value as! T
    }

    // --- REQUEST EXECUTION ---
    private func executeRequest<T: Decodable>(
        method: HTTPMethod,
        path: String,
        params: [String: String]?,
        body: Encodable?,
        priority: RequestPriority,
        timeout: TimeInterval?
    ) async throws -> T {
        var components = URLComponents(url: baseURL.appendingPathComponent(path), resolvingAgainstBaseURL: false)!
        if let params {
            components.queryItems = params.map { URLQueryItem(name: $0.key, value: $0.value) }
        }

        var request = URLRequest(url: components.url!)
        request.httpMethod = method.rawValue
        request.timeoutInterval = timeout ?? 15

        // Headers
        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")
        request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept")
        request.setValue("gzip, deflate, br", forHTTPHeaderField: "Accept-Encoding")
        request.setValue(AppConfig.appVersion, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-Client-Version")
        request.setValue(priority.rawValue, forHTTPHeaderField: "X-Request-Priority")

        // Auth header
        if let token = await TokenManager.shared.getAccessToken() {
            request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
        }

        // Body
        if let body {
            request.httpBody = try JSONEncoder().encode(body)
        }

        let (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)

        guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
            throw APIError.invalidResponse
        }

        if httpResponse.statusCode == 401 {
            // Token expired -- attempt refresh
            let refreshed = await TokenManager.shared.refresh()
            if refreshed {
                // Retry with new token
                return try await executeRequest(
                    method: method, path: path, params: params,
                    body: body, priority: priority, timeout: timeout
                )
            } else {
                // Refresh failed -- logout
                await AuthManager.shared.logout()
                throw AuthError.sessionExpired
            }
        }

        guard (200...299).contains(httpResponse.statusCode) else {
            let errorBody = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) ?? ""
            throw APIError.httpError(status: httpResponse.statusCode, body: errorBody)
        }

        return try JSONDecoder().decode(T.self, from: data)
    }
}
```

##### 3.1.1 Request Prioritization

| Priority | Examples | Behavior |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `critical` | Chat message send, auth token refresh | Immediate execution, no queuing, 30s timeout |
| `high` | Current screen data fetch, contact detail load | Immediate execution, 15s timeout |
| `normal` | Background prefetch, adjacent tab data | Queued if >4 concurrent requests, 15s timeout |
| `low` | Analytics events, push token registration | Queued if >2 concurrent requests, 30s timeout, retryable |

```swift
// Priority-aware request queue
actor RequestQueue {
    private let concurrentLimit = 6  // iOS URLSession default
    private var activeCount = 0
    private var queue: [PrioritizedRequest] = []

    func enqueue(_ request: PrioritizedRequest) async {
        if request.priority == .critical || request.priority == .high {
            // Execute immediately, even if at concurrent limit
            // (iOS handles >6 connections with HTTP/2 multiplexing)
            await execute(request)
        } else {
            // Queue and execute when slot available
            queue.append(request)
            queue.sort { $0.priority.order < $1.priority.order }
            await drain()
        }
    }
}
```

##### 3.1.2 Compression

- **Response compression:** Server MUST send `Content-Encoding: gzip` or `br` (Brotli). All Next.js API routes on Vercel/AWS automatically gzip responses. Verify by checking response headers. `URLSession` decompresses gzip/br responses transparently.
- **Request compression:** For large request bodies (e.g., bulk contact import >10KB), compress with gzip before sending. For typical requests (chat messages, contact edits), compression overhead exceeds savings -- send uncompressed.
- **Expected savings:** Typical JSON API response compresses 70-80% with gzip. A 50KB contact list response becomes ~12KB on the wire.

#### 3.2 Caching Strategy (Repository Pattern + URLCache + SwiftData)

```swift
// Repositories/ContactsRepository.swift

import Foundation
import SwiftData

@MainActor
final class ContactsRepository: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = ContactsRepository()

    // --- IN-MEMORY CACHE ---
    @Published var contacts: [Contact] = []
    @Published var isLoading = false

    // --- CACHE CONFIGURATION ---
    private let staleTime: TimeInterval = 5 * 60       // 5 minutes
    private let gcTime: TimeInterval = 30 * 60          // 30 minutes
    private var lastFetchTime: Date?
    private var retryCount = 0
    private let maxRetries = 3

    var isStale: Bool {
        guard let lastFetch = lastFetchTime else { return true }
        return Date().timeIntervalSince(lastFetch) > staleTime
    }

    // --- FETCH WITH CACHE-FIRST STRATEGY ---
    func fetch(forceRefresh: Bool = false) async {
        // Return cached data immediately if not stale
        if !forceRefresh && !isStale && !contacts.isEmpty {
            return
        }

        // Load from persistent cache first (instant)
        if contacts.isEmpty {
            contacts = await PersistentCache.shared.loadContacts()
        }

        // Then fetch fresh data from network
        isLoading = true
        defer { isLoading = false }

        do {
            let response: ContactsResponse = try await APIClient.shared.request(
                method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
            )
            contacts = response.contacts
            lastFetchTime = Date()
            retryCount = 0

            // Persist to disk cache
            Task(priority: .background) {
                await PersistentCache.shared.saveContacts(response.contacts)
            }
        } catch {
            retryCount += 1
            if retryCount < maxRetries {
                // Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s
                let delay = min(TimeInterval(pow(2.0, Double(retryCount))), 8.0)
                try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(delay))
                await fetch(forceRefresh: forceRefresh)
            }
        }
    }

    // --- REFETCH TRIGGERS ---
    func onAppForeground() async {
        // Refetch when app comes to foreground if data is stale
        if isStale { await fetch() }
    }

    func onNetworkReconnect() async {
        // Refetch when network reconnects if data is stale
        if isStale { await fetch() }
    }

    func prefetch() async {
        await fetch()
    }

    func trimCache() {
        // Called on memory warning -- keep only visible data
    }
}
```

##### 3.2.1 Per-Resource Cache Configuration

| Resource | Cache Key | `staleTime` | `gcTime` | Refetch Strategy | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|-------------|----------|-----------------|-----------|
| Contacts list | `contacts` | 5min | 30min | On foreground, on reconnect | Contacts change occasionally; 5min staleness is acceptable. |
| Contact detail | `contacts/{id}` | 5min | 30min | On foreground | Same as list. Detail may be edited by user (optimistic update handles this). |
| Properties list | `properties` | 15min | 30min | On foreground | Properties change less frequently than contacts. |
| Property detail | `properties/{id}` | 15min | 30min | On foreground | Same as list. |
| Chat history | `chat-messages` | 1min | 30min | On foreground, polling every 30s when screen active | Chat is near-realtime. 1min staleness ensures quick updates. |
| Chat conversation | `chat/{conversationId}` | 30s | 15min | Polling every 15s when active | Active conversation needs freshest data. |
| User profile | `user-profile` | 30min | 60min | On foreground | Profile rarely changes. |
| Tasks list | `tasks` | 5min | 30min | On foreground | Tasks may be created by AI or user. |
| Notifications | `notifications` | 1min | 15min | Polling every 60s | Users expect near-realtime notification counts. |
| Search results | `search/{query}` | 0 (always stale) | 5min | Never auto-refetch | Search results are ephemeral -- always fetch fresh on new search. |

##### 3.2.2 Data Persistence (Offline-First)

```swift
// Cache/PersistentCache.swift
// Persist critical data to disk for instant startup

import Foundation

actor PersistentCache {
    static let shared = PersistentCache()

    private let cacheDirectory: URL
    private let maxAge: TimeInterval = 24 * 60 * 60  // 24 hours

    private let persistableResources: Set<String> = [
        "contacts", "properties", "user-profile", "tasks"
    ]

    private init() {
        cacheDirectory = FileManager.default.urls(
            for: .cachesDirectory, in: .userDomainMask
        ).first!.appendingPathComponent("SkyeQueryCache")

        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(
            at: cacheDirectory, withIntermediateDirectories: true
        )
    }

    func saveContacts(_ contacts: [Contact]) {
        save(contacts, forKey: "contacts")
    }

    func loadContacts() -> [Contact] {
        load(forKey: "contacts") ?? []
    }

    func saveProperties(_ properties: [Property]) {
        save(properties, forKey: "properties")
    }

    func loadProperties() -> [Property] {
        load(forKey: "properties") ?? []
    }

    private func save<T: Encodable>(_ value: T, forKey key: String) {
        guard persistableResources.contains(key) else { return }
        let url = cacheDirectory.appendingPathComponent("\(key).json")
        let wrapper = CacheEntry(data: value, timestamp: Date())
        try? JSONEncoder().encode(wrapper).write(to: url)
    }

    private func load<T: Decodable>(forKey key: String) -> T? {
        let url = cacheDirectory.appendingPathComponent("\(key).json")
        guard let data = try? Data(contentsOf: url),
              let entry = try? JSONDecoder().decode(CacheEntry<T>.self, from: data),
              Date().timeIntervalSince(entry.timestamp) < maxAge else {
            return nil
        }
        return entry.data
    }
}

struct CacheEntry<T: Codable>: Codable {
    let data: T
    let timestamp: Date
}
```

**Result:** On subsequent app launches, the contacts list, properties, and user profile are available INSTANTLY from persisted cache (before any network request). The user sees real data, not a loading spinner, within milliseconds. Fresh data is fetched in the background and seamlessly replaces cached data when available.

##### 3.2.3 Optimistic Updates

**Policy: All user-initiated mutations that modify a single resource use optimistic updates. The UI updates immediately; the server request happens in the background. If the server rejects the mutation, the UI rolls back.**

| Mutation | Optimistic? | Rollback Strategy |
|----------|-------------|------------------|
| Edit contact fields | Yes | Snapshot previous contact, restore on error, show toast "Save failed, changes reverted" |
| Create new note | Yes | Add note to list immediately with temporary ID, replace with server ID on success, remove on error |
| Complete/uncomplete task | Yes | Toggle immediately, revert on error |
| Send chat message | Yes | Add message to list immediately with "sending" status, update to "sent" on success, show "failed to send" on error with retry button |
| Delete contact | **No** | Destructive action -- confirm with user, wait for server confirmation, then remove from UI |
| Bulk operations | **No** | Too complex for optimistic updates -- show loading state |

```swift
// Example: Optimistic contact edit
@MainActor
final class ContactDetailViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published var contact: Contact

    func updateContact(_ updates: ContactUpdate) async {
        // Snapshot previous value
        let previousContact = contact

        // Optimistically update UI
        contact = contact.applying(updates)

        // Also update in the list cache
        ContactsRepository.shared.updateContactInList(contact)

        do {
            // Send to server
            let serverContact: Contact = try await APIClient.shared.request(
                method: .PATCH,
                path: "/api/contacts/\(contact.id)",
                body: updates
            )
            // Update with server response (may include server-generated fields)
            contact = serverContact
            ContactsRepository.shared.updateContactInList(serverContact)
        } catch {
            // Rollback
            contact = previousContact
            ContactsRepository.shared.updateContactInList(previousContact)

            // Refetch to ensure consistency
            await ContactsRepository.shared.fetch(forceRefresh: true)

            ToastManager.shared.show("Save failed. Changes reverted.", style: .error)
        }
    }
}
```

#### 3.3 Prefetching Strategy

| User Location | Prefetch Target | Trigger | Data Size |
|---------------|----------------|---------|-----------|
| People tab (list) | First 5 contact details | After list renders, idle time | ~5KB each = 25KB |
| People tab (list) | Contact avatars for visible rows | Nuke `ImagePrefetcher` on visible items | Images (cached by Nuke) |
| Chat tab | Recent chat history (last 50 messages) | On app launch (Step 7 in init sequence) | ~20KB |
| Chat tab | User's task list | After chat renders | ~5KB |
| Properties tab | First 3 property details | After list renders | ~10KB each = 30KB |
| Any tab | Adjacent tab data | After current tab renders + 1s delay | Variable |
| Contact detail | Linked properties for that contact | On detail view appear | ~10KB |

```swift
// Prefetch logic used in PeopleViewModel
@MainActor
final class PeopleViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published var contacts: [Contact] = []

    private var prefetchTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    func prefetchContactDetails() {
        prefetchTask?.cancel()
        prefetchTask = Task(priority: .utility) {
            guard !contacts.isEmpty else { return }

            // Wait 1s after render to avoid competing with initial display
            try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(1))

            // Prefetch first 5 contact details
            for contact in contacts.prefix(5) {
                guard !Task.isCancelled else { return }
                let _: Contact? = try? await APIClient.shared.request(
                    method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts/\(contact.id)"
                )
                // Result is cached by URLSession / PersistentCache
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 3.4 Offline Queue (NetworkQueue)

```swift
// Network/NetworkQueue.swift

import Foundation
import Network

struct QueuedMutation: Codable, Identifiable {
    let id: UUID
    let timestamp: Date
    let method: String          // "POST", "PATCH", "PUT", "DELETE"
    let path: String
    let body: Data?
    var retryCount: Int
    let maxRetries: Int         // Default: 5
    let priority: String        // "critical", "high", "normal"
}

actor NetworkQueue {
    static let shared = NetworkQueue()

    private var queue: [QueuedMutation] = []
    private var isFlushing = false
    private let storageKey = "SKYE_OFFLINE_QUEUE"
    private let maxQueueSize = 100
    private let monitor = NWPathMonitor()

    init() {
        // Restore queue from persistent storage on init
        Task { await restore() }

        // Listen for connectivity changes
        monitor.pathUpdateHandler = { [weak self] path in
            if path.status == .satisfied {
                Task { await self?.flush() }
            }
        }
        monitor.start(queue: DispatchQueue(label: "com.skyecrm.networkmonitor"))
    }

    // --- ENQUEUE ---
    func enqueue(
        method: String,
        path: String,
        body: Encodable?,
        priority: String = "normal",
        maxRetries: Int = 5
    ) async throws {
        guard queue.count < maxQueueSize else {
            throw NetworkQueueError.queueFull
        }

        let bodyData = if let body { try? JSONEncoder().encode(body) } else { nil as Data? }

        let entry = QueuedMutation(
            id: UUID(),
            timestamp: Date(),
            method: method,
            path: path,
            body: bodyData,
            retryCount: 0,
            maxRetries: maxRetries,
            priority: priority
        )

        queue.append(entry)
        queue.sort { lhs, rhs in
            let priorityOrder = ["critical": 0, "high": 1, "normal": 2]
            let lhsOrder = priorityOrder[lhs.priority] ?? 2
            let rhsOrder = priorityOrder[rhs.priority] ?? 2
            if lhsOrder != rhsOrder { return lhsOrder < rhsOrder }
            return lhs.timestamp < rhs.timestamp
        }

        await persist()

        // Attempt flush immediately (will no-op if offline)
        await flush()
    }

    // --- FLUSH ---
    func flush() async {
        guard !isFlushing, !queue.isEmpty else { return }

        let path = NWPathMonitor()
        // Quick connectivity check
        guard monitor.currentPath.status == .satisfied else { return }

        isFlushing = true
        defer { isFlushing = false }

        // Process FIFO (queue is already sorted by priority + timestamp)
        while !queue.isEmpty {
            var mutation = queue[0]

            do {
                var request = URLRequest(
                    url: URL(string: AppConfig.apiURL)!.appendingPathComponent(mutation.path)
                )
                request.httpMethod = mutation.method
                request.httpBody = mutation.body
                request.timeoutInterval = 30
                request.setValue("application/json", forHTTPHeaderField: "Content-Type")

                if let token = await TokenManager.shared.getAccessToken() {
                    request.setValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
                }

                let (_, response) = try await URLSession.shared.data(for: request)
                guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
                    throw APIError.invalidResponse
                }

                if (200...299).contains(httpResponse.statusCode) {
                    // Success -- remove from queue
                    queue.removeFirst()
                    await persist()
                } else if httpResponse.statusCode == 409 {
                    // Conflict -- server has newer data
                    queue.removeFirst()
                    await persist()
                    await MainActor.run {
                        ToastManager.shared.show("A sync conflict occurred. Refreshing data.", style: .warning)
                    }
                    // Force-refresh all caches
                    await ContactsRepository.shared.fetch(forceRefresh: true)
                } else {
                    throw APIError.httpError(status: httpResponse.statusCode, body: "")
                }
            } catch {
                mutation.retryCount += 1
                queue[0] = mutation

                if mutation.retryCount >= mutation.maxRetries {
                    // Max retries exceeded -- move to dead letter queue
                    queue.removeFirst()
                    await persist()
                    SentrySDK.capture(error: error) { scope in
                        scope.setExtra(value: mutation.path, key: "mutation_path")
                        scope.setExtra(value: "max_retries_exceeded", key: "reason")
                    }
                    await MainActor.run {
                        ToastManager.shared.show(
                            "Failed to sync: \(mutation.path). Change may be lost.",
                            style: .error
                        )
                    }
                } else {
                    // Network error or server error -- stop flushing, retry later
                    await persist()
                    break
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // --- PERSISTENCE ---
    private func persist() async {
        let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(queue)
        UserDefaults.standard.set(data, forKey: storageKey)
    }

    private func restore() async {
        guard let data = UserDefaults.standard.data(forKey: storageKey),
              let stored = try? JSONDecoder().decode([QueuedMutation].self, from: data) else {
            return
        }
        queue = stored
    }

    // --- STATUS ---
    var pendingCount: Int { queue.count }
    var hasPending: Bool { !queue.isEmpty }
}
```

**Conflict resolution strategy:**
1. **Last-write-wins** for simple field edits (contact name, phone number). The most recent mutation overwrites.
2. **Server-wins** on 409 Conflict. Discard local mutation, refetch server state, show user a toast explaining the conflict.
3. **Merge** for note creation / task creation. These are additive operations -- no conflict possible. If the server returns 409, retry with a new ID.

---

### 4. Build and CI/CD Pipeline

#### 4.1 Build System

| Criteria | Xcode Cloud | GitHub Actions + fastlane |
|----------|------------|--------------------------|
| Setup complexity | Minimal -- configured in Xcode | Moderate -- Fastfile, Matchfile, YAML workflows |
| Code signing | Managed by Xcode Cloud (auto-provisions) | `fastlane match` (Git-encrypted certs) |
| Build environment | Apple cloud (dedicated Mac hardware) | GitHub-hosted macOS runners or self-hosted |
| Build time | ~10-20min (Apple silicon, optimized) | ~10-20min (depends on runner) |
| Cost | 25 compute hours/month free. Additional at $0.08/min | GitHub Actions macOS runners: $0.08/min |
| TestFlight integration | First-class (auto-distribute) | Via `fastlane pilot` or `xcrun altool` |
| SwiftUI previews in CI | Not applicable | Not applicable |

**Recommendation: GitHub Actions with fastlane.** For a team-size project, GitHub Actions provides more flexibility for custom CI steps (linting, testing, size checks) while fastlane handles the Xcode-specific build and signing complexity. Xcode Cloud is viable if the team prefers Apple's integrated experience.

**Exception:** If the team prefers minimal configuration and Apple ecosystem integration, Xcode Cloud is a strong alternative with zero runner management.

#### 4.2 Build Configurations

```swift
// Xcode project build configurations:
// Debug, Staging, Release
//
// Managed via .xcconfig files:

// Config/Debug.xcconfig
// SWIFT_ACTIVE_COMPILATION_CONDITIONS = DEBUG
// API_URL = http:/$()/localhost:3000
// APP_ENV = development
// ENABLE_INSTRUMENTS = YES

// Config/Staging.xcconfig
// SWIFT_ACTIVE_COMPILATION_CONDITIONS = STAGING
// API_URL = https:/$()/staging-api.skyecrm.com
// APP_ENV = staging

// Config/Release.xcconfig
// SWIFT_ACTIVE_COMPILATION_CONDITIONS = RELEASE
// SWIFT_OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL = -O
// SWIFT_COMPILATION_MODE = wholemodule
// API_URL = https:/$()/api.skyecrm.com
// APP_ENV = production
```

| Configuration | Optimization | Signing | API Target | Distribution | Debug Tools |
|---------|-----------|---------|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| `Debug` | None (`-Onone`), debug symbols | Development | `localhost:3000` | Device install via Xcode | Xcode Instruments, debug gauges, `os_signpost` |
| `Staging` | Optimized (`-O`), dSYMs | Ad Hoc | `staging-api.skyecrm.com` | TestFlight internal testing | Sentry only |
| `Release` | Whole-module optimized (`-O`), dSYMs, stripped | App Store | `api.skyecrm.com` | App Store (via App Store Connect) | Sentry only |

#### 4.3 CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*']

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STAGE 1: Quality Gates (runs on every PR)
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  lint:
    name: Lint & Format
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 10
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: SwiftLint
        run: |
          brew install swiftlint
          swiftlint lint --strict --reporter github-actions-logging
      - name: SwiftFormat check
        run: |
          brew install swiftformat
          swiftformat . --lint

  typecheck-and-build:
    name: Build & Type Check
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_16.app
      - name: Resolve SPM dependencies
        run: xcodebuild -resolvePackageDependencies -project Skye.xcodeproj -scheme Skye
      - name: Build for testing
        run: |
          xcodebuild build-for-testing \
            -project Skye.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Skye \
            -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15,OS=18.0' \
            -configuration Debug \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO

  unit-tests:
    name: Unit & View Tests
    needs: [typecheck-and-build]
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_16.app
      - name: Run tests
        run: |
          xcodebuild test \
            -project Skye.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Skye \
            -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15,OS=18.0' \
            -resultBundlePath TestResults.xcresult \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
      - name: Check coverage thresholds
        run: |
          xcrun xccov view --report TestResults.xcresult --json > coverage.json
          swift scripts/CheckCoverage.swift coverage.json
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: TestResults.xcresult

  binary-size:
    name: Binary Size Check
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 20
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_16.app
      - name: Archive
        run: |
          xcodebuild archive \
            -project Skye.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Skye \
            -archivePath build/Skye.xcarchive \
            -configuration Release \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            DEVELOPMENT_TEAM=""
      - name: Check binary size
        run: |
          APP_SIZE=$(du -sk build/Skye.xcarchive/Products/Applications/Skye.app | awk '{print $1}')
          echo "Uncompressed app size: ${APP_SIZE}KB"
          # Estimate compressed size (~40% of uncompressed for typical Swift apps)
          ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED=$((APP_SIZE * 40 / 100))
          echo "Estimated compressed size: ${ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED}KB"
          if [ "$ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED" -gt 40960 ]; then  # 40MB in KB
            echo "::error::Estimated compressed size ${ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED}KB exceeds 40MB hard limit"
            exit 1
          elif [ "$ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED" -gt 30720 ]; then  # 30MB in KB
            echo "::warning::Estimated compressed size ${ESTIMATED_COMPRESSED}KB exceeds 30MB soft limit"
          fi

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STAGE 2: Build Preview (runs on merge to main)
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  build-preview:
    name: Build Staging & Deploy to TestFlight
    needs: [lint, typecheck-and-build, unit-tests, binary-size]
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 45
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_16.app
      - name: Install fastlane
        run: gem install fastlane
      - name: Match signing
        run: fastlane match adhoc --readonly
        env:
          MATCH_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.MATCH_PASSWORD }}
          MATCH_GIT_URL: ${{ secrets.MATCH_GIT_URL }}
      - name: Build & upload to TestFlight
        run: fastlane beta
        env:
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.ASC_KEY_ID }}
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_ISSUER_ID: ${{ secrets.ASC_ISSUER_ID }}
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ASC_API_KEY }}
      - name: Upload dSYMs to Sentry
        run: |
          brew install getsentry/tools/sentry-cli
          sentry-cli upload-dif build/Skye.xcarchive/dSYMs/ \
            --org skye-crm \
            --project skye-ios
        env:
          SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STAGE 3: E2E Tests (runs after preview build)
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  e2e-tests:
    name: E2E Tests (Maestro)
    needs: [build-preview]
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 30
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Install Maestro
        run: |
          curl -Ls "https://get.maestro.mobile.dev" | bash
          export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.maestro/bin"
      - name: Boot iOS Simulator
        run: xcrun simctl boot "iPhone 15"
      - name: Install app on Simulator
        run: xcrun simctl install booted build/Skye.app
      - name: Run Maestro tests
        run: maestro test .maestro/ --format junit --output maestro-results.xml
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: maestro-results
          path: maestro-results.xml

  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  # STAGE 4: Production Release (runs on version tag)
  # ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
  build-production:
    name: Build Production & Submit to App Store
    needs: [lint, typecheck-and-build, unit-tests, binary-size]
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
    runs-on: macos-15
    timeout-minutes: 60
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Select Xcode
        run: sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode_16.app
      - name: Install fastlane
        run: gem install fastlane
      - name: Match signing
        run: fastlane match appstore --readonly
        env:
          MATCH_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.MATCH_PASSWORD }}
          MATCH_GIT_URL: ${{ secrets.MATCH_GIT_URL }}
      - name: Build & submit to App Store
        run: fastlane release
        env:
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.ASC_KEY_ID }}
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_ISSUER_ID: ${{ secrets.ASC_ISSUER_ID }}
          APP_STORE_CONNECT_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ASC_API_KEY }}
      - name: Upload dSYMs to Sentry (production)
        run: |
          VERSION=$(agvtool what-marketing-version -terse1)
          sentry-cli upload-dif build/Skye.xcarchive/dSYMs/ \
            --org skye-crm \
            --project skye-ios \
            --release "com.skyecrm.app@${VERSION}"
        env:
          SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN }}
```

**Pipeline summary:**

| Trigger | Steps | Duration Target |
|---------|-------|----------------|
| PR opened/updated | Lint + Build + Unit Tests + Binary Size | <10 minutes |
| Merge to `main` | Above + fastlane beta (archive + TestFlight) + Sentry dSYMs | <30 minutes |
| Post-preview build | E2E tests (Maestro on simulator) | <15 minutes |
| Tag `v*.*.*` | Quality gates + fastlane release (archive + App Store submit) + Sentry | <45 minutes |

#### 4.4 Code Signing

**Recommended: fastlane match.**

fastlane match manages all iOS code signing via a private Git repository:
- Generates and stores distribution certificates and provisioning profiles encrypted in a Git repo.
- All team members and CI share the same certificates (no manual provisioning).
- Handles certificate rotation and expiry with `fastlane match nuke` + regenerate.

```ruby
# Matchfile
git_url("https://github.com/skyecrm/certificates.git")
storage_mode("git")
type("appstore") # or "adhoc", "development"
app_identifier("com.skyecrm.app")
team_id("XXXXXXXXXX")
```

```bash
# First-time setup (one-time)
fastlane match development   # Generate development cert + profile
fastlane match adhoc          # Generate ad hoc cert + profile (TestFlight)
fastlane match appstore       # Generate App Store cert + profile
```

**If Xcode Cloud is used instead:** Xcode Cloud manages signing automatically via Apple's servers. No additional configuration needed beyond connecting the Apple Developer account.

#### 4.5 Environment Variables

**Rule: NEVER commit secrets to the repository. NEVER commit `.env` files.**

| Variable | Source | Used In |
|----------|--------|---------|
| `API_URL` | `.xcconfig` file per build configuration | API client base URL |
| `APP_ENV` | `.xcconfig` file per build configuration | Feature flags, logging level |
| `SENTRY_DSN` | GitHub Secrets -> Xcode build phase | Sentry initialization |
| `MATCH_PASSWORD` | GitHub Secrets | CI code signing via fastlane match |
| `MATCH_GIT_URL` | GitHub Secrets | CI code signing certificate repo |
| `ASC_KEY_ID` | GitHub Secrets | App Store Connect API authentication |
| `ASC_ISSUER_ID` | GitHub Secrets | App Store Connect API authentication |
| `ASC_API_KEY` | GitHub Secrets | App Store Connect API authentication |
| `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN` | GitHub Secrets | dSYM upload to Sentry |

**Access in app code:**

```swift
// Config/AppConfig.swift
// Values injected via Info.plist from .xcconfig files

enum AppConfig {
    static let apiURL: String = {
        guard let url = Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["API_URL"] as? String else {
            fatalError("API_URL not configured in Info.plist")
        }
        return url
    }()

    static let appEnv: String = {
        Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["APP_ENV"] as? String ?? "production"
    }()

    static let sentryDSN: String = {
        Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["SENTRY_DSN"] as? String ?? ""
    }()

    static let appVersion: String = {
        Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["CFBundleShortVersionString"] as? String ?? "0.0.0"
    }()

    static let buildNumber: String = {
        Bundle.main.infoDictionary?["CFBundleVersion"] as? String ?? "0"
    }()
}
```

**Info.plist entries (referencing xcconfig variables):**
```xml
<key>API_URL</key>
<string>$(API_URL)</string>
<key>APP_ENV</key>
<string>$(APP_ENV)</string>
<key>SENTRY_DSN</key>
<string>$(SENTRY_DSN)</string>
```

**`.gitignore` must include:**
```
.env
.env.*
*.p12
*.mobileprovision
*.keystore
secrets/
*.xcconfig.local
```

#### 4.6 OTA Updates

**Native Swift apps cannot perform OTA (over-the-air) code updates.** Unlike React Native with EAS Update/CodePush, compiled Swift binaries must go through App Store review for every update. This is a platform constraint.

**Mitigation strategies:**
1. **TestFlight for rapid iteration.** TestFlight builds are reviewed in <24 hours (usually <1 hour). Use TestFlight internal testing (up to 100 testers) for rapid beta feedback.
2. **Feature flags for runtime behavior changes.** Use a remote configuration service (Firebase Remote Config, LaunchDarkly, or custom API endpoint) to toggle features, adjust thresholds, or enable/disable functionality without an app update.
3. **App Store expedited review.** For critical bug fixes, Apple offers expedited review (typically <6 hours). Request via App Store Connect.
4. **Server-driven UI for dynamic content.** Marketing materials, onboarding flows, and announcement banners can be server-driven, allowing content updates without app releases.

```swift
// FeatureFlags/FeatureFlagManager.swift
// Remote feature flags for runtime behavior changes

@MainActor
final class FeatureFlagManager: ObservableObject {
    static let shared = FeatureFlagManager()

    @Published var flags: [String: Bool] = [:]

    func refresh() async {
        do {
            let response: FeatureFlagResponse = try await APIClient.shared.request(
                method: .GET, path: "/api/config/feature-flags"
            )
            flags = response.flags
        } catch {
            // Use cached flags -- never crash on flag fetch failure
            SentrySDK.capture(error: error)
        }
    }

    func isEnabled(_ flag: String) -> Bool {
        flags[flag] ?? false
    }
}
```

#### 4.7 Build Versioning

**Scheme: Semantic Versioning (SemVer) `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`**

| Component | When Incremented | Example |
|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| MAJOR | Breaking changes, major redesign, incompatible API changes | 1.0.0 -> 2.0.0 |
| MINOR | New features, non-breaking enhancements | 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 |
| PATCH | Bug fixes, performance improvements, minor tweaks | 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1 |

**Build number:** Auto-incremented by fastlane or Xcode Cloud on every build. Independent of version number. Monotonically increasing integer. Managed via `agvtool`.

```ruby
# Fastfile -- auto-increment build number
lane :beta do
  increment_build_number(
    build_number: latest_testflight_build_number + 1
  )
  build_app(
    scheme: "Skye",
    configuration: "Staging",
    export_method: "ad-hoc"
  )
  upload_to_testflight(skip_waiting_for_build_processing: true)
end

lane :release do
  increment_build_number(
    build_number: latest_testflight_build_number + 1
  )
  build_app(
    scheme: "Skye",
    configuration: "Release",
    export_method: "app-store"
  )
  upload_to_app_store(
    submit_for_review: true,
    automatic_release: false
  )
end
```

**Version management:**

```swift
// Managed via Xcode project settings:
// Marketing Version (CFBundleShortVersionString): "1.0.0" -- user-facing
// Build Number (CFBundleVersion): auto-incremented by fastlane
```

**Release tagging:**
```bash
# When ready for App Store release:
# 1. Update marketing version in Xcode project
agvtool new-marketing-version 1.1.0
git add .
git commit -m "Bump version to 1.1.0"
git tag v1.1.0
git push origin v1.1.0   # Triggers production build + submit pipeline
```

---

### 5. Testing Infrastructure

#### 5.1 Unit Tests (XCTest)

**Framework: XCTest (built into Xcode). For async testing, use `XCTestExpectation` and Swift concurrency `async/await` support in XCTest.**

```swift
// SkyeTests/TestPlan.xctestplan or Xcode scheme settings:
//
// Code Coverage enabled for:
//   - Skye target (main app)
//   - Excluding: Generated files, Preview content
//
// Coverage thresholds enforced by scripts/CheckCoverage.swift:
//   Global: branches >= 60%, lines >= 60%
//   Utils/: branches >= 80%, lines >= 80%
//   Hooks/ (ViewModels): branches >= 60%, lines >= 60%
//   API/: branches >= 80%, lines >= 80%
```

**Unit test targets:**

| Module | Coverage Target | Key Test Cases |
|--------|----------------|---------------|
| `API/APIClient.swift` | 80% | Request deduplication, auth header injection, 401 retry, timeout handling, error parsing |
| `API/TokenManager.swift` | 90% | Token read/write Keychain, JWT expiry check, refresh flow, concurrent refresh prevention |
| `Utils/Formatters.swift` | 95% | Phone number formatting, currency, dates, relative time, truncation |
| `Utils/Validators.swift` | 95% | Email, phone, required fields, URL, numeric ranges |
| `ViewModels/PeopleViewModel.swift` | 60% | Filter/sort logic, pagination, search debounce |
| `ViewModels/ChatViewModel.swift` | 60% | Message sending, streaming response handling, error states |
| `Network/NetworkQueue.swift` | 80% | Enqueue, flush order, retry logic, conflict handling, persistence, max queue size |
| `Auth/AuthManager.swift` | 80% | Login, logout, token refresh, optimistic auth state |

**Example unit test:**

```swift
// SkyeTests/API/APIClientTests.swift
import XCTest
@testable import Skye

final class APIClientTests: XCTestCase {

    var mockSession: URLProtocolMock.Type!

    override func setUp() {
        super.setUp()
        URLProtocolMock.reset()
    }

    func testDeduplicatesConcurrentGETRequests() async throws {
        // Configure mock to track call count
        var callCount = 0
        URLProtocolMock.requestHandler = { request in
            callCount += 1
            let data = try! JSONEncoder().encode(ContactsResponse(contacts: []))
            let response = HTTPURLResponse(
                url: request.url!, statusCode: 200,
                httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
            )!
            return (response, data)
        }

        // Fire two identical requests concurrently
        async let result1: ContactsResponse = APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
        )
        async let result2: ContactsResponse = APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
        )

        let _ = try await (result1, result2)

        // Only one actual network call should have been made
        XCTAssertEqual(callCount, 1, "Concurrent identical GETs should be deduplicated")
    }

    func testDoesNotDeduplicatePOSTRequests() async throws {
        var callCount = 0
        URLProtocolMock.requestHandler = { request in
            callCount += 1
            let data = try! JSONEncoder().encode(["id": "1"])
            let response = HTTPURLResponse(
                url: request.url!, statusCode: 201,
                httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
            )!
            return (response, data)
        }

        async let _: [String: String] = APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .POST, path: "/api/contacts",
            body: ContactCreate(fullName: "A")
        )
        async let _: [String: String] = APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .POST, path: "/api/contacts",
            body: ContactCreate(fullName: "B")
        )

        XCTAssertEqual(callCount, 2, "POST requests should NOT be deduplicated")
    }

    func testRetriesRequestAfter401WithRefreshedToken() async throws {
        var callCount = 0
        URLProtocolMock.requestHandler = { request in
            callCount += 1
            if callCount == 1 {
                // First call: 401
                let response = HTTPURLResponse(
                    url: request.url!, statusCode: 401,
                    httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
                )!
                return (response, Data())
            } else {
                // Second call: success with new token
                let data = try! JSONEncoder().encode(
                    ContactsResponse(contacts: [Contact.mock])
                )
                let response = HTTPURLResponse(
                    url: request.url!, statusCode: 200,
                    httpVersion: nil, headerFields: nil
                )!
                return (response, data)
            }
        }

        // Mock token refresh success
        TokenManager.shared.mockRefreshSuccess = true

        let result: ContactsResponse = try await APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
        )

        XCTAssertEqual(callCount, 2, "Should retry after 401")
        XCTAssertFalse(result.contacts.isEmpty)
    }
}
```

#### 5.2 View Tests (XCTest + ViewInspector)

**Framework: XCTest for snapshot/behavior tests. For SwiftUI view testing, use `ViewInspector` library or Xcode's built-in UI testing. Every screen view MUST be tested in all 4 states.**

| State | What to Verify |
|-------|---------------|
| **Loading** | Skeleton/`ProgressView` is displayed. No data content visible. |
| **Success** | Correct data rendered. All interactive elements present. Accessibility labels on all touchable elements. |
| **Empty** | Empty state illustration and message displayed. CTA button present (e.g., "Add your first contact"). |
| **Error** | Error message displayed. Retry button present and functional. Error is not a raw technical message. |

```swift
// SkyeTests/Views/PeopleViewTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class PeopleViewTests: XCTestCase {

    func testShowsLoadingSkeletonOnInitialRender() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel()
        viewModel.isLoading = true
        viewModel.contacts = []

        let view = PeopleView(viewModel: viewModel)
        let body = try view.inspect()

        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(viewWithAccessibilityLabel: "Loading contacts"))
        XCTAssertThrowsError(try body.find(text: "John Doe"))
    }

    func testRendersContactListOnSuccess() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel()
        viewModel.isLoading = false
        viewModel.contacts = [
            Contact(id: "1", fullName: "John Doe", company: "Acme Realty"),
            Contact(id: "2", fullName: "Jane Smith", company: "Prime Properties"),
        ]
        viewModel.filteredContacts = viewModel.contacts

        let view = PeopleView(viewModel: viewModel)
        let body = try view.inspect()

        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(text: "John Doe"))
        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(text: "Jane Smith"))
    }

    func testShowsEmptyStateWhenNoContacts() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel()
        viewModel.isLoading = false
        viewModel.contacts = []
        viewModel.filteredContacts = []

        let view = PeopleView(viewModel: viewModel)
        let body = try view.inspect()

        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(text: "No contacts yet"))
        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(button: "Add your first contact"))
    }

    func testShowsErrorStateWithRetryButton() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel()
        viewModel.isLoading = false
        viewModel.error = .networkError("Something went wrong")

        let view = PeopleView(viewModel: viewModel)
        let body = try view.inspect()

        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(text: "Something went wrong"))
        XCTAssertNoThrow(try body.find(button: "Try again"))
    }

    func testFiltersContactsBySearchQuery() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel()
        viewModel.contacts = [
            Contact(id: "1", fullName: "John Doe"),
            Contact(id: "2", fullName: "Jane Smith"),
        ]
        viewModel.searchQuery = ""
        viewModel.applyFilters()

        XCTAssertEqual(viewModel.filteredContacts.count, 2)

        viewModel.searchQuery = "Jane"
        viewModel.applyFilters()

        XCTAssertEqual(viewModel.filteredContacts.count, 1)
        XCTAssertEqual(viewModel.filteredContacts.first?.fullName, "Jane Smith")
    }
}
```

**Accessibility testing (embedded in view tests):**

Every interactive element MUST have:
- `.accessibilityLabel()` -- describes the element (e.g., "View John Doe")
- Appropriate trait (`.isButton`, `.isHeader`, `.isImage`, etc.)
- `.accessibilityHint()` -- describes what happens on interaction (for non-obvious actions)

```swift
// Verify in tests or Xcode Accessibility Inspector:
// - All buttons have descriptive labels
// - All headers use .isHeader trait
// - All images have alt text via .accessibilityLabel
// - VoiceOver can navigate through all interactive elements

// Automated accessibility audit in CI:
func testAccessibilityAudit() throws {
    let app = XCUIApplication()
    app.launch()
    try app.performAccessibilityAudit()
}
```

#### 5.3 Integration Tests

**Framework: XCTest with mock URL protocols for API mocking, or real staging server calls.**

| Integration Test Suite | What It Verifies |
|----------------------|-----------------|
| **Auth Flow** | OAuth redirect -> callback -> token storage in Keychain -> authenticated API call succeeds -> token expires -> refresh token used -> new access token stored -> API call retried and succeeds |
| **Chat Flow** | Send message via API -> receive streaming response (SSE) -> tokens rendered incrementally -> tool call result (contact card) rendered -> message persisted in history |
| **Contact CRUD** | Fetch contacts list -> create new contact -> verify appears in list -> edit contact -> verify changes persist -> delete contact -> verify removed from list |
| **Offline Queue** | Go offline -> create contact -> edit contact -> go online -> verify both mutations synced in order -> verify server state matches |
| **Deep Linking** | App receives `skyecrm://contacts/123` -> navigates to contact detail -> correct data loaded |

```swift
// SkyeTests/Integration/AuthFlowTests.swift
import XCTest
@testable import Skye

final class AuthFlowTests: XCTestCase {

    func testCompletesFullAuthLifecycle() async throws {
        let keychain = MockKeychainManager()
        let authManager = AuthManager(keychain: keychain)

        // 1. Start unauthenticated
        XCTAssertNil(try keychain.read(key: .authToken))

        // 2. Simulate OAuth callback with auth code
        let tokens = try await authManager.handleOAuthCallback(code: "auth-code-123")

        // 3. Verify tokens stored
        XCTAssertEqual(try keychain.read(key: .authToken), tokens.accessToken)
        XCTAssertEqual(try keychain.read(key: .refreshToken), tokens.refreshToken)

        // 4. Verify authenticated API call works
        let contacts: ContactsResponse = try await APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
        )
        XCTAssertNotNil(contacts)

        // 5. Simulate token expiry
        try keychain.write(key: .authToken, value: "expired-token")

        // 6. Make API call -- should trigger refresh
        let refreshedContacts: ContactsResponse = try await APIClient.shared.request(
            method: .GET, path: "/api/contacts"
        )
        XCTAssertNotNil(refreshedContacts)

        // 7. Verify new token stored
        let newToken = try keychain.read(key: .authToken)
        XCTAssertNotEqual(newToken, "expired-token")
    }
}
```

#### 5.4 E2E Tests (Maestro)

**Framework: Maestro** (recommended for native iOS apps in 2026 -- YAML-based, no flaky waits, built-in mobile-specific commands, supports iOS simulator and real device).

**Why Maestro over XCUITest:**
| Criteria | Maestro | XCUITest |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Setup complexity | `brew install maestro` + YAML files | Xcode UI Test target, complex test bundle config |
| Test authoring | YAML (readable by QA, PM, anyone) | Swift (requires dev to write/maintain) |
| Flakiness | Low (built-in smart waits, retry logic) | Medium (requires manual waits, animation synchronization) |
| SwiftUI support | Works out of the box via accessibility tree | Full native support but verbose |
| CI integration | Simple -- single binary | Requires `xcodebuild test` with UI test scheme |
| Maintenance burden | Low | High |

**Maestro test suites:**

```yaml
# .maestro/login-and-chat.yaml
appId: com.skyecrm.app
---
# Critical Path: Login -> Chat -> Send Message -> Receive Response
- launchApp
- assertVisible: "Sign in with Google"
- tapOn: "Sign in with Google"
# OAuth flow completes (configured with test account in staging)
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "Chat"                    # Chat tab is active
- tapOn: "Message Skye..."                 # Chat input
- inputText: "Show me my top 5 contacts"
- tapOn: "Send"                            # Send button
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible:
    text: "Here are your top"              # AI response starts
    timeout: 15000                         # 15s for AI response
- assertVisible: "Contact"                 # Tool result card rendered
- scroll:
    direction: DOWN
    distance: 200
```

```yaml
# .maestro/crm-flow.yaml
appId: com.skyecrm.app
---
# CRM Flow: Search -> View -> Edit -> Save -> Verify
- launchApp
- tapOn: "People"                          # Navigate to People tab
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- tapOn: "Search contacts..."
- inputText: "John"
- assertVisible: "John Doe"
- tapOn: "John Doe"                        # Open contact detail
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "John Doe"               # Detail screen
- assertVisible: "Phone"
- assertVisible: "Email"
- tapOn: "Edit"
- clearText: "Company"
- inputText: "New Company LLC"
- tapOn: "Save"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "New Company LLC"         # Verify edit persisted
- back                                     # Return to list
- assertVisible: "New Company LLC"         # Verify in list too
```

```yaml
# .maestro/push-notification.yaml
appId: com.skyecrm.app
---
# Push Notification: Receive -> Tap -> Deep Link
- launchApp
- waitForAnimationToEnd
# Simulate push notification via maestro
- sendNotification:
    title: "New message from Jane"
    body: "I'm interested in the property on Oak Street"
    data:
      type: "chat_message"
      conversationId: "conv-123"
- tapOnNotification: "New message from Jane"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "Jane"                    # Chat screen with Jane's conversation
- assertVisible: "Oak Street"             # Message content visible
```

#### 5.5 Performance Tests

**Automated performance regression gates (run in CI after E2E build):**

| Metric | Target | Failure Threshold | Measurement |
|--------|--------|------------------|-------------|
| Cold launch time | <1,500ms | >2,000ms fails CI | Maestro `measurePerformance` + Xcode Instruments trace |
| FPS during contact list scroll (500 items) | 60fps | <55fps average fails CI | Maestro scroll + FPS recording |
| FPS during chat scroll (200 messages) | 60fps | <55fps average fails CI | Maestro scroll + FPS recording |
| Memory on ChatView (after 5min interaction) | <140MB | >170MB fails CI | Xcode Memory Gauge via `xcrun simctl` |
| Memory on PeopleView (500 contacts loaded) | <90MB | >110MB fails CI | Xcode Memory Gauge |
| Time to interactive after tab switch | <200ms | >400ms fails CI | Maestro timing |

**Note:** 60fps scrolling is trivially achieved in native SwiftUI `List` for standard data sets. The CI gates exist to catch regressions from expensive custom views or view body computations, not as aspirational targets.

```yaml
# .maestro/perf-scroll-contacts.yaml
appId: com.skyecrm.app
---
- launchApp
- tapOn: "People"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- repeat:
    times: 10
    commands:
      - scroll:
          direction: DOWN
          distance: 500
          duration: 300
- assertMinFps: 55      # Fail if average drops below 55fps
```

```bash
# scripts/perf-startup-test.sh -- Run on CI
#!/bin/bash
# Measure cold launch time on iOS Simulator
RESULTS=()
for i in {1..5}; do
  xcrun simctl terminate booted com.skyecrm.app 2>/dev/null
  sleep 2
  START=$(date +%s%N)
  xcrun simctl launch booted com.skyecrm.app
  # Wait for "app ready" log marker via os_signpost
  xcrun simctl spawn booted log stream \
    --predicate 'subsystem == "com.skyecrm.app" AND category == "performance"' | \
    head -1 | while read line; do
      END=$(date +%s%N)
      DURATION=$(( ($END - $START) / 1000000 ))
      RESULTS+=($DURATION)
      echo "Launch $i: ${DURATION}ms"
    done
done

# Calculate P95
P95=$(echo "${RESULTS[@]}" | tr ' ' '\n' | sort -n | tail -1)
echo "P95 cold launch: ${P95}ms"
if [ "$P95" -gt 2000 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: P95 cold launch ${P95}ms exceeds 2000ms threshold"
  exit 1
fi
```

#### 5.6 Test Environments

| Test Type | API Server | Data | Authentication |
|-----------|-----------|------|---------------|
| Unit tests | None (pure logic) | Mocked inline | Mocked |
| View tests | None (mock view models) | Fixture data per test | Mocked |
| Integration tests | URLProtocol mock or staging server | Seed data + test accounts | Test OAuth tokens |
| E2E tests (Maestro) | Staging server | Seeded staging DB (reset before each run) | Real test account on staging |
| Performance tests | Staging server | Pre-populated with 500+ contacts, 200+ messages | Real test account |

**Staging server requirements:**
- Mirrors production API exactly (same 129 routes).
- Seeded with realistic data: 500 contacts, 50 properties, 1000 chat messages.
- Test account: `test@skyecrm.com` with OAuth bypass for CI (API key auth fallback).
- Database reset script: runs before E2E suite, takes <60s.

---

### 6. Monitoring and Observability

#### 6.1 Sentry Configuration

```swift
// SentrySetup.swift
import Sentry

func initializeSentry() {
    SentrySDK.start { options in
        options.dsn = AppConfig.sentryDSN

        // --- RELEASE TRACKING ---
        options.releaseName = "com.skyecrm.app@\(AppConfig.appVersion)"
        options.dist = AppConfig.buildNumber

        // --- ENVIRONMENT ---
        options.environment = AppConfig.appEnv  // "development", "staging", "production"

        // --- PERFORMANCE MONITORING ---
        options.tracesSampleRate = AppConfig.appEnv == "production" ? 0.2 : 1.0
        // 20% of transactions in production (cost control)
        // 100% in staging (full visibility during testing)

        options.profilesSampleRate = AppConfig.appEnv == "production" ? 0.1 : 0.5
        // Profile 10% of transactions in production

        // --- SESSION TRACKING ---
        options.enableAutoSessionTracking = true
        options.sessionTrackingIntervalMillis = 30000

        // Automatic breadcrumbs for:
        // - Navigation events (view controller transitions)
        // - Network requests (URL, method, status code -- NOT request/response bodies)
        // - User interactions (button taps with accessibility label)

        options.enableCrashHandler = true
        options.enableAutoPerformanceTracing = true
        options.enableUIViewControllerTracing = true
        options.enableSwizzling = true
        options.enableNetworkTracking = true

        // --- APP LAUNCH PROFILING ---
        options.enableAppLaunchProfiling = true
        options.enablePreWarmedAppStartTracing = true

        // --- DATA SCRUBBING ---
        options.beforeSend = { event in
            // Remove any PII from breadcrumbs
            event.breadcrumbs = event.breadcrumbs?.map { breadcrumb in
                if breadcrumb.category == "http",
                   let urlString = breadcrumb.data?["url"] as? String,
                   var components = URLComponents(string: urlString) {
                    let sensitiveKeys = ["email", "phone", "name", "token"]
                    components.queryItems = components.queryItems?.map { item in
                        if sensitiveKeys.contains(item.name.lowercased()) {
                            return URLQueryItem(name: item.name, value: "[REDACTED]")
                        }
                        return item
                    }
                    var modified = breadcrumb
                    modified.data?["url"] = components.string
                    return modified
                }
                return breadcrumb
            }
            return event
        }

        // --- BREADCRUMB FILTERING ---
        options.beforeBreadcrumb = { breadcrumb in
            // Don't track debug/info console logs in production
            if breadcrumb.level == .debug || breadcrumb.level == .info {
                return AppConfig.appEnv == "production" ? nil : breadcrumb
            }
            return breadcrumb
        }

        // --- METRIC KIT INTEGRATION ---
        options.enableMetricKit = true
        // Automatically captures MetricKit diagnostic reports
        // including hang diagnostics, crash diagnostics, and disk write diagnostics
    }
}
```

**dSYM upload (in CI):**

```bash
# Uploaded automatically in CI pipeline (see Section 4.3)
# Ensures stack traces in Sentry show original Swift source with file/line info
sentry-cli upload-dif build/Skye.xcarchive/dSYMs/ \
  --org skye-crm \
  --project skye-ios \
  --release "com.skyecrm.app@${VERSION}"
```

**Sentry alert rules:**

| Alert | Condition | Action |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| New crash type | First occurrence of a new unhandled exception | Slack notification to #skye-crashes + PagerDuty if P0 |
| Crash-free rate drop | Crash-free sessions drops below 99% in any 1-hour window | Slack alert + PagerDuty page |
| Performance regression | P95 screen load time increases by >50% compared to 7-day average | Slack notification to #skye-performance |
| High error rate | >100 errors of same type in 1 hour | Slack notification + auto-create GitHub issue |
| Memory warning spike | >10 memory warnings in 1 hour | Slack notification to #skye-performance |

#### 6.2 Custom Analytics Events

**If an analytics SDK is chosen (Mixpanel, Amplitude, PostHog, or Segment), track these events:**

| Event | Properties | Purpose |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| `app_launched` | `launch_type: cold/warm`, `duration_ms`, `cached_data: boolean` | Measure startup performance |
| `screen_viewed` | `screen_name`, `previous_screen`, `load_time_ms` | Navigation patterns |
| `chat_message_sent` | `message_length`, `has_attachment: boolean` | Chat engagement |
| `chat_response_received` | `response_time_ms`, `token_count`, `had_tool_call: boolean` | AI performance |
| `contact_viewed` | `contact_id` (hashed), `source: search/list/chat` | CRM engagement |
| `contact_action_taken` | `action: call/text/email/note`, `contact_id` (hashed) | Agent productivity |
| `property_viewed` | `property_id` (hashed), `source: search/list/chat` | Property engagement |
| `search_performed` | `query_length`, `result_count`, `time_to_first_result_ms` | Search quality |
| `error_displayed` | `error_type`, `screen`, `retry_tapped: boolean` | Error UX quality |
| `offline_queue_synced` | `mutation_count`, `conflict_count`, `total_sync_time_ms` | Offline reliability |
| `push_notification_received` | `notification_type`, `app_state: foreground/background` | Push delivery |
| `push_notification_tapped` | `notification_type`, `time_to_tap_ms` | Push engagement |

**Funnel tracking:**

```
Funnel: Agent Productivity
  1. app_launched
  2. screen_viewed (screen: People)
  3. contact_viewed
  4. contact_action_taken (action: call | text | email)

Funnel: AI Assistant Usage
  1. app_launched
  2. screen_viewed (screen: Chat)
  3. chat_message_sent
  4. chat_response_received
  5. contact_action_taken (from chat tool result)
```

**Analytics rules:**
- All PII is hashed before sending (contact IDs, property IDs). Never send names, emails, or phone numbers to analytics.
- Analytics events are batched and sent every 30s or when batch reaches 20 events (whichever comes first).
- Analytics is `priority: low` in the request queue -- never competes with user-facing requests.
- Users can opt out of analytics in Settings (CCPA/GDPR compliance).

#### 6.3 Health Metrics Dashboard

**Dashboard (DataDog, Grafana, or Sentry Dashboard):**

| Metric | Data Source | Target | Warning | Critical |
|--------|-----------|--------|---------|----------|
| **Crash-free rate** | Sentry | >99.5% | <99.5% | <99.0% |
| **App launch time (P50)** | Sentry Performance / MetricKit | <1,000ms | >1,300ms | >2,000ms |
| **App launch time (P95)** | Sentry Performance / MetricKit | <1,500ms | >1,800ms | >2,500ms |
| **API response time (P50)** | Sentry Performance | <300ms | >500ms | >1,000ms |
| **API response time (P95)** | Sentry Performance | <800ms | >1,200ms | >2,000ms |
| **Chat response time (P50)** | Custom analytics | <2,000ms | >3,000ms | >5,000ms |
| **Push notification delivery** | APNs dashboard | >95% | <95% | <90% |
| **App binary size** | CI artifact | <30MB | >30MB | >40MB |
| **Active crash issues** | Sentry | <5 open | >5 open | >10 open |
| **Offline queue failure rate** | Custom analytics | <2% | >2% | >5% |
| **Memory warning rate** | Sentry / MetricKit | <0.1% of sessions | >0.1% | >0.5% |
| **Scroll hitch rate** | MetricKit / Instruments | <5% of frames hitched | >5% | >10% |

**Dashboard refresh interval:** Real-time for crash-free rate and error spikes. 5-minute aggregation for performance metrics. Daily roll-up for trends.

---

### 7. Dependency Management

#### 7.1 Version Locking

**Policy: Exact versions only via Swift Package Manager (SPM). Package.resolved MUST be committed.**

```swift
// Package.swift dependencies (exact versions)
let package = Package(
    name: "Skye",
    platforms: [.iOS(.v16)],
    dependencies: [
        .package(url: "https://github.com/kean/Nuke.git", exact: "12.8.0"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/kishikawakatsumi/KeychainAccess.git", exact: "4.2.2"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-cocoa.git", exact: "8.40.1"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/apple/swift-collections.git", exact: "1.1.4"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(name: "Skye", dependencies: [
            .product(name: "Nuke", package: "Nuke"),
            .product(name: "NukeUI", package: "Nuke"),
            .product(name: "KeychainAccess", package: "KeychainAccess"),
            .product(name: "Sentry", package: "sentry-cocoa"),
            .product(name: "Collections", package: "swift-collections"),
        ]),
    ]
)
```

**Rules:**
- `Package.resolved` is committed to the repository. Always.
- CI resolves packages with `xcodebuild -resolvePackageDependencies` -- uses the lock file exactly.
- Dependabot or Renovate is configured to create PRs for SPM dependency updates weekly. Each update is tested through the full CI pipeline before merge.
- Major version upgrades require a dedicated PR with manual testing.

#### 7.2 Dependency Audit

**Automated vulnerability scanning:**

```yaml
# .github/workflows/security.yml
name: Security Audit
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 6 * * 1'   # Every Monday at 6 AM UTC
  push:
    paths:
      - 'Package.swift'
      - 'Package.resolved'

jobs:
  audit:
    runs-on: macos-15
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Check for known vulnerabilities
        run: |
          # Check GitHub Advisory Database for SPM dependencies
          swift package audit
      - name: Snyk scan (optional, more comprehensive)
        uses: snyk/actions/swift@master
        with:
          args: --severity-threshold=high
        env:
          SNYK_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SNYK_TOKEN }}
```

**Policy:**
- CRITICAL vulnerabilities: fix within 24 hours (patch dependency or replace).
- HIGH vulnerabilities: fix within 1 week.
- MEDIUM vulnerabilities: fix within 1 month or document accepted risk.
- LOW vulnerabilities: address during regular maintenance.

#### 7.3 Framework Compatibility

**Every third-party framework in the project must pass this checklist before inclusion:**

| Check | Requirement | How to Verify |
|-------|------------|---------------|
| iOS version support | iOS 16.0+ | Check Package.swift for `platforms: [.iOS(.v16)]` or equivalent |
| Architecture | arm64 (Apple Silicon + all iPhones) | Verify no architecture exclusions in build settings |
| No private APIs | Does not use Apple private APIs | Check for App Store rejection history in framework's GitHub issues |
| SPM support | Available as Swift Package | Check for Package.swift in repo root |
| Swift version | Swift 5.9+ / Swift 6 compatible | Check Package.swift `swiftLanguageVersions` or CI matrix |
| Strict concurrency | No data race warnings with `SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY = complete` | Build with strict concurrency and check for warnings |

**Currently approved frameworks:**

| Framework | Version | Purpose | Last Verified |
|--------|---------|---------|---------------|
| `Nuke` + `NukeUI` | 12.x | High-performance image loading + SwiftUI integration | Verified with iOS 18 |
| `KeychainAccess` | 4.x | Keychain wrapper for secure token storage | Verified with iOS 18 |
| `Sentry` (sentry-cocoa) | 8.x | Crash reporting + performance monitoring | Verified with iOS 18 |
| `swift-collections` | 1.x | OrderedDictionary, Deque for efficient data structures | Apple-maintained, always compatible |

**System frameworks used (no version concerns -- bundled with iOS):**

| Framework | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| `SwiftUI` | UI framework |
| `Combine` | Reactive data flow |
| `Foundation` / `URLSession` | Networking, JSON, dates, formatters |
| `SwiftData` | Local persistence (iOS 17+) / Core Data fallback for iOS 16 |
| `UserNotifications` | Push notifications |
| `AuthenticationServices` | Sign in with Apple, OAuth |
| `Network` (NWPathMonitor) | Network reachability |
| `os` (OSLog, os_signpost) | Logging and performance instrumentation |
| `MetricKit` | System performance metrics |
| `StoreKit` | In-app purchases (if needed) |

#### 7.4 Dependency Review Checklist

**Before adding ANY new SPM package to the project, the developer must answer ALL of the following. This checklist is included in the PR template.**

```markdown
## New Dependency Checklist

Package: `<package-name>@<version>`

- [ ] **Actively maintained?** Last commit within 6 months. Link to repo: ___
- [ ] **Swift 6 / strict concurrency compatible?** No data race warnings: Yes/No
- [ ] **Privacy Manifest?** If the framework uses Required Reason APIs (UserDefaults, disk space,
      file timestamps, system boot time, active keyboard list), it must include a
      `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` manifest.
      Does it use Required Reason APIs? Yes/No. If Yes, does it include a Privacy Manifest? Yes/No.
- [ ] **Binary size impact?** Before: ___MB, After: ___MB, Delta: ___MB
      (measured via Xcode archive thinning report)
- [ ] **Duplicates existing functionality?** Does this package overlap with something
      we already have or a system framework? If so, why not extend existing? Explanation: ___
- [ ] **iOS 16+ compatible?** Checked Package.swift platforms: ___
- [ ] **arm64 support?** Verified no architecture exclusions: Yes/No
- [ ] **SPM support?** Has Package.swift: Yes/No
- [ ] **License compatible?** License type: ___. Compatible with commercial use: Yes/No
- [ ] **GitHub stars?** ___ (as a proxy for community adoption)
- [ ] **Open issues count?** ___ open issues (red flag if >100 with many unanswered)
```

**Approval requirement:** Any new third-party framework requires approval from the tech lead. Prefer system frameworks (Foundation, SwiftUI, Combine, etc.) over third-party alternatives whenever possible.

---

### Appendix A: Performance Monitoring Cheat Sheet

Quick reference for the development team:

| What to Measure | Tool | How |
|----------------|------|-----|
| Cold launch time | Xcode Instruments > App Launch template | Profile on physical iPhone 13 |
| Main thread hangs | Xcode Instruments > Time Profiler + Hang Detection | Profile during typical usage |
| Scroll FPS | Xcode Instruments > Animation Hitches template | Record while scrolling long lists |
| View body evaluation | Xcode Instruments > SwiftUI template | Enable "SwiftUI View Body" instrument |
| Binary size | Xcode Organizer > App Size Report | After upload to App Store Connect |
| Memory usage | Xcode > Debug Navigator > Memory gauge | Profile on device during typical usage |
| Memory leaks | Xcode Instruments > Leaks template | Profile during navigation cycles |
| Network waterfall | Xcode Instruments > Network template | Inspect request timing, caching |
| Disk I/O | Xcode Instruments > File Activity template | Check for excessive cache writes |
| Custom metrics | `os_signpost` API | Wrap critical code paths with signposts, view in Instruments |
| MetricKit diagnostics | MetricKit framework | Review daily aggregated reports in Xcode Organizer |

### Appendix B: Performance Budget Summary

Single-page reference of all numeric targets:

| Metric | Target | Hard Limit |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Cold launch (iPhone 13) | <1,500ms | 2,000ms |
| App binary size (compressed) | <30MB | 40MB |
| FPS (all interactions) | 60fps | 55fps minimum |
| Main thread frame budget | <16ms | 16.67ms |
| Memory -- ChatView | <140MB | 170MB |
| Memory -- PeopleView | <90MB | 110MB |
| Memory -- PropertiesView | <110MB | 132MB |
| Memory -- Total app | <280MB | 340MB |
