| API response time (P50) | <300ms | 500ms |
| API response time (P95) | <800ms | 1,200ms |
| Crash-free rate | >99.5% | 99.0% |
| Test coverage -- utils | >80% | 70% |
| Test coverage -- view models | >60% | 50% |
| Offline queue capacity | 100 mutations | - |
| Tab switch TTI | <200ms | 400ms |
| Cache staleTime (contacts) | 5min | - |
| Cache staleTime (chat) | 1min | - |
| Cache gcTime (all) | 30min | - |

---

This concludes the V2: Performance, Infrastructure & Build Pipeline specification for Swift/SwiftUI native. Every target is quantified, every configuration has exact values, and every architectural decision is justified with rationale and implementation guidance. The team should treat the "Hard Limit" column as CI-enforced gates -- any violation blocks the build and requires immediate remediation.

---

## V2: Offline Architecture, State Management & Data Sync (Swift / iOS Native)

> **Scope**: This section defines the complete offline-first data architecture for Skye, a native iOS (SwiftUI) CRM and AI assistant for real estate agents. It covers state management (server cache, UI state, and offline queue), persistent caching, offline mutation queuing, conflict resolution, network state handling, and real-time sync considerations. Every configuration value, state transition, and data flow is explicit and implementation-ready. All code targets **iOS 17+** and uses Swift concurrency (async/await, actors, structured concurrency), the Observation framework (`@Observable`), GRDB.swift for SQLite, and Keychain Services for secure storage.

---

### 1. State Management Architecture

Skye uses a **hybrid state management** approach: a custom **async data layer** (built on `@Observable` view models with a GRDB-backed cache) owns all server/async state, and separate **`@Observable` state objects** own all client-side/UI state. These two systems are complementary and never overlap in responsibility.

#### 1.1 Server State (Async Data Layer)

The async data layer is the single source of truth for all data that originates from the Supabase backend. It handles fetching, caching, background refetching, optimistic updates, and mutation lifecycle. The design mirrors the semantics of TanStack Query but is built natively with Swift concurrency and the Observation framework.

##### 1.1.1 QueryClient Configuration

```swift
// Sources/Data/QueryClient.swift
import Foundation

/// Central configuration for the async data layer.
/// All query and mutation behavior inherits these defaults
/// unless overridden per query key.
@Observable
final class QueryClient {
    static let shared = QueryClient()

    // MARK: - Default Query Options

    /// Data is considered fresh for 5 minutes by default.
    /// Individual query keys override this (see Section 3).
    let defaultStaleTime: TimeInterval = 5 * 60 // 5 minutes

    /// Cached data is retained for 7 days before garbage collection.
    /// This MUST be >= the persister maxAge to prevent premature eviction
    /// of persisted cache entries on hydration.
    let defaultGCTime: TimeInterval = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 // 7 days

    /// Retry policy: 3 retries with exponential backoff.
    let defaultRetryCount: Int = 3

    /// Retry delay: 1s, 2s, 4s (cap 30s).
    func retryDelay(for attempt: Int) -> TimeInterval {
        min(pow(2.0, Double(attempt)), 30.0)
    }

    /// Do not refetch on scene phase change to active -- controlled manually
    /// via scenePhase observation (see Section 5.3).
    let refetchOnForeground: Bool = false

    /// Refetch when network reconnects (after offline period).
    let refetchOnReconnect: Bool = true

    /// Refetch on view appear if data is not fresh.
    let refetchOnAppear: Bool = true

    /// Network mode: offlineFirst ensures cached data is returned
    /// immediately even when offline, and fetches are paused (not failed).
    let networkMode: NetworkMode = .offlineFirst

    // MARK: - In-Memory Cache

    private var cache: [String: CacheEntry] = [:]

    struct CacheEntry {
        let data: any Sendable
        let fetchedAt: Date
        let staleTime: TimeInterval
        var isStale: Bool {
            Date().timeIntervalSince(fetchedAt) > staleTime
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Cache Operations

    func setQueryData<T: Sendable>(_ key: QueryKey, data: T, staleTime: TimeInterval? = nil) {
        let entry = CacheEntry(
            data: data,
            fetchedAt: Date(),
            staleTime: staleTime ?? defaultStaleTime
        )
        cache[key.hashString] = entry
        // Persist to SQLite asynchronously.
        Task { await CacheManager.shared.persist(key: key, entry: entry) }
    }

    func getQueryData<T>(_ key: QueryKey) -> T? {
        cache[key.hashString]?.data as? T
    }

    func removeQueries(_ key: QueryKey) {
        // Remove all keys that have `key` as a prefix.
        let prefix = key.hashString
        cache = cache.filter { !$0.key.hasPrefix(prefix) }
        Task { await CacheManager.shared.removeEntries(prefix: prefix) }
    }

    func invalidateQueries(_ key: QueryKey? = nil) {
        if let key {
            let prefix = key.hashString
            for (k, var entry) in cache where k.hasPrefix(prefix) {
                // Force stale by setting fetchedAt far in the past.
                cache[k] = CacheEntry(
                    data: entry.data,
                    fetchedAt: .distantPast,
                    staleTime: entry.staleTime
                )
            }
        } else {
            // Invalidate all.
            for (k, entry) in cache {
                cache[k] = CacheEntry(
                    data: entry.data,
                    fetchedAt: .distantPast,
                    staleTime: entry.staleTime
                )
            }
        }
    }

    /// Prefetch a query without blocking the caller.
    func prefetchQuery<T: Sendable & Codable>(
        key: QueryKey,
        staleTime: TimeInterval,
        fetch: @escaping () async throws -> T
    ) {
        Task {
            do {
                let data = try await fetch()
                setQueryData(key, data: data, staleTime: staleTime)
            } catch {
                // Prefetch failures are non-fatal.
            }
        }
    }
}

enum NetworkMode {
    /// Serve cached data first. Pause (do not fail) network requests when offline.
    case offlineFirst
}
```

**Key design decisions**:

- `networkMode: .offlineFirst` is critical. It tells the data layer to serve cached data first and pause network requests when offline, rather than failing them. When connectivity returns, paused queries and mutations resume automatically.
- `defaultGCTime` is set to 7 days (matching the persister `maxAge`) so that cached data survives across app restarts for up to a week. Without this, cached data would be discarded prematurely.
- Foreground refetch is disabled by default. Instead, Skye manages refetch on app foreground via `@Environment(\.scenePhase)` observation (see Section 5.3).

##### 1.1.2 Query Key Conventions

All query keys follow a hierarchical structure: `[entity, scope, params?]`. This enables targeted invalidation at any level of granularity.

```swift
// Sources/Data/QueryKeys.swift
import Foundation

/// A query key is an array of hashable segments used to identify cached data.
struct QueryKey: Hashable, Sendable {
    let segments: [String]

    var hashString: String {
        segments.joined(separator: ":")
    }

    func appending(_ segment: String) -> QueryKey {
        QueryKey(segments: segments + [segment])
    }

    func appending(_ segment: String?) -> QueryKey {
        guard let segment else { return self }
        return QueryKey(segments: segments + [segment])
    }
}

enum QueryKeys {

    // MARK: - Contacts

    enum Contacts {
        static let all = QueryKey(segments: ["contacts"])
        static func lists() -> QueryKey { all.appending("list") }
        static func list(filters: ContactFilters) -> QueryKey {
            lists().appending(filters.cacheKey)
        }
        static func details() -> QueryKey { all.appending("detail") }
        static func detail(id: String) -> QueryKey { details().appending(id) }
        static func notes(contactId: String) -> QueryKey {
            all.appending("notes").appending(contactId)
        }
        static func tasks(contactId: String) -> QueryKey {
            all.appending("tasks").appending(contactId)
        }
        static func timeline(contactId: String) -> QueryKey {
            all.appending("timeline").appending(contactId)
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Properties

    enum Properties {
        static let all = QueryKey(segments: ["properties"])
        static func lists() -> QueryKey { all.appending("list") }
        static func list(filters: PropertyFilters? = nil) -> QueryKey {
            lists().appending(filters?.cacheKey)
        }
        static func details() -> QueryKey { all.appending("detail") }
        static func detail(id: String) -> QueryKey { details().appending(id) }
    }

    // MARK: - Chat

    enum Chat {
        static let all = QueryKey(segments: ["chat"])
        static func sessions() -> QueryKey { all.appending("sessions") }
        static func history(sessionId: String) -> QueryKey {
            all.appending("history").appending(sessionId)
        }
        static func recent() -> QueryKey { all.appending("recent") }
    }

    // MARK: - Tasks

    enum Tasks {
        static let all = QueryKey(segments: ["tasks"])
        static func lists() -> QueryKey { all.appending("list") }
        static func list(filters: TaskFilters? = nil) -> QueryKey {
            lists().appending(filters?.cacheKey)
        }
        static func detail(id: String) -> QueryKey {
            all.appending("detail").appending(id)
        }
    }

    // MARK: - User / Dashboard

    enum User {
        static let profile = QueryKey(segments: ["user", "profile"])
        static let dashboard = QueryKey(segments: ["user", "dashboard"])
    }
}
```

**Invalidation patterns by granularity**:

| Action | Invalidation Target | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| Create contact | `QueryKeys.Contacts.lists()` | Refetches all contact list variants (any filter combo) |
| Edit contact `abc` | `QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: "abc")` | Refetches that contact's detail |
| Delete contact `abc` | `QueryKeys.Contacts.all` | Refetches all contact queries (lists + details) |
| Add note to contact `abc` | `QueryKeys.Contacts.notes(contactId: "abc")` | Refetches that contact's notes |
| Complete task `xyz` | `QueryKeys.Tasks.detail(id: "xyz")` and `QueryKeys.Contacts.tasks(contactId:)` | Refetches the task and the parent contact's task list |
| Send chat message | `QueryKeys.Chat.history(sessionId:)` | Refetches the active chat session |
| Pull-to-refresh on People tab | `QueryKeys.Contacts.lists()` | Refetches the current contact list |

##### 1.1.3 Mutation Conventions

All mutations follow a consistent pattern: **optimistic update first, then reconcile on success or rollback on error**.

```swift
// Sources/ViewModels/ContactViewModel.swift
import Foundation

@Observable
final class ContactViewModel {
    private let queryClient = QueryClient.shared
    private let networkMonitor = NetworkMonitor.shared
    private let api = APIClient.shared

    var contact: Contact?
    var isLoading = false
    var error: Error?

    // MARK: - Edit Contact Mutation

    /// Performs an optimistic update, then reconciles with the server.
    func editContact(id: String, updates: ContactUpdate) async {
        // 1. Snapshot the current cached value for rollback.
        let previousContact: Contact? = queryClient.getQueryData(
            QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: id)
        )

        // 2. Optimistically update the cache.
        if let existing: Contact = queryClient.getQueryData(
            QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: id)
        ) {
            let optimistic = existing.applying(updates)
            queryClient.setQueryData(
                QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: id),
                data: optimistic
            )
            self.contact = optimistic
        }

        // 3. Also update the contact in list caches.
        if var listData: ContactListResponse = queryClient.getQueryData(
            QueryKeys.Contacts.lists()
        ) {
            listData.contacts = listData.contacts.map { c in
                c.id == id ? c.applying(updates) : c
            }
            queryClient.setQueryData(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists(), data: listData)
        }

        // 4. If offline, enqueue and return.
        guard networkMonitor.isOnline else {
            await MutationQueue.shared.enqueue(
                MutationRequest(
                    type: .editContact,
                    endpoint: "/api/contacts/\(id)",
                    method: .patch,
                    body: updates.jsonData,
                    relatedEntityId: id,
                    relatedEntityType: .contact
                )
            )
            return
        }

        // 5. Execute the mutation online.
        do {
            let _: Contact = try await api.patch(
                "/api/contacts/\(id)",
                body: updates
            )
            // 6. Invalidate to refetch canonical server data.
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: id))
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists())
        } catch {
            // 7. Rollback on error.
            if let previousContact {
                queryClient.setQueryData(
                    QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: id),
                    data: previousContact
                )
                self.contact = previousContact
            }
            self.error = error
        }
    }
}
```

**Mutation pattern summary (applied to all mutations)**:
1. Snapshot current cache for rollback.
2. Apply optimistic update to cache.
3. If offline: enqueue to `MutationQueue` and return.
4. If online: execute via `APIClient`.
5. On success: invalidate related queries to refetch canonical server data.
6. On error: restore snapshot (rollback).

##### 1.1.4 Persisted Cache (SQLite-Backed via GRDB)

The query cache is persisted to SQLite (via GRDB.swift) so that queries survive app restarts. Each query is persisted individually (keyed by its query hash) rather than serializing the entire cache into a single storage key, which is critical for performance at scale.

```swift
// Sources/Data/CacheManager.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

/// Thread-safe, SQLite-backed cache persister.
actor CacheManager {
    static let shared = CacheManager()

    /// Maximum age for cached entries: 7 days (matches QueryClient.defaultGCTime).
    private let maxAge: TimeInterval = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60

    private var dbPool: DatabasePool!

    func initialize(dbPool: DatabasePool) {
        self.dbPool = dbPool
    }

    // MARK: - Persist

    func persist(key: QueryKey, entry: QueryClient.CacheEntry) {
        guard let dbPool else { return }
        do {
            let encoded = try JSONEncoder().encode(
                CacheRecord(
                    key: key.hashString,
                    value: entry.data,
                    updatedAt: Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000)
                )
            )
            try dbPool.write { db in
                try db.execute(
                    sql: """
                        INSERT OR REPLACE INTO query_cache (key, value, updated_at)
                        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
                        """,
                    arguments: [
                        key.hashString,
                        String(data: encoded, encoding: .utf8),
                        Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000)
                    ]
                )
            }
        } catch {
            // Persistence failure is non-fatal; log and continue.
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Restore

    func restore(key: QueryKey) -> Data? {
        guard let dbPool else { return nil }
        do {
            return try dbPool.read { db in
                let row = try Row.fetchOne(
                    db,
                    sql: "SELECT value FROM query_cache WHERE key = ?",
                    arguments: [key.hashString]
                )
                guard let valueString = row?["value"] as? String else { return nil }
                return valueString.data(using: .utf8)
            }
        } catch {
            return nil
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Remove

    func removeEntries(prefix: String) {
        guard let dbPool else { return }
        try? dbPool.write { db in
            try db.execute(
                sql: "DELETE FROM query_cache WHERE key LIKE ?",
                arguments: ["\(prefix)%"]
            )
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Evict Expired

    func evictExpiredEntries() {
        guard let dbPool else { return }
        let cutoff = Int64((Date().timeIntervalSince1970 - maxAge) * 1000)
        try? dbPool.write { db in
            try db.execute(
                sql: "DELETE FROM query_cache WHERE updated_at < ?",
                arguments: [cutoff]
            )
        }
    }
}
```

**How it works**:
- Each query is persisted individually (keyed by its query hash).
- Queries are lazily restored when first accessed (not all at once on app launch).
- After each fetch, the result is persisted to SQLite via GRDB.
- This eliminates the need for throttled bulk writes and keeps the cache granular.

##### 1.1.5 Network Monitor Setup (NWPathMonitor)

The async data layer does not automatically detect network changes. Skye bridges `NWPathMonitor` from the `Network` framework into the data layer via a custom `NetworkMonitor` `@Observable` class.

```swift
// Sources/Data/NetworkMonitor.swift
import Foundation
import Network
import Observation

@Observable
final class NetworkMonitor {
    static let shared = NetworkMonitor()

    private let monitor = NWPathMonitor()
    private let queue = DispatchQueue(label: "com.skye.networkMonitor")

    private(set) var isOnline: Bool = true
    private(set) var isExpensive: Bool = false
    private(set) var isConstrained: Bool = false
    private(set) var connectionType: ConnectionType = .unknown
    private(set) var networkQuality: NetworkQuality = .strong

    enum ConnectionType {
        case wifi, cellular, wiredEthernet, unknown
    }

    init() {
        monitor.pathUpdateHandler = { [weak self] path in
            guard let self else { return }
            Task { @MainActor in
                let wasOffline = !self.isOnline
                self.isOnline = path.status == .satisfied
                self.isExpensive = path.isExpensive
                self.isConstrained = path.isConstrained
                self.connectionType = self.classifyConnection(path)
                self.networkQuality = self.classifyQuality(path)

                // Notify data layer of connectivity changes.
                if wasOffline && self.isOnline {
                    NotificationCenter.default.post(
                        name: .networkDidReconnect, object: nil
                    )
                } else if !wasOffline && !self.isOnline {
                    NotificationCenter.default.post(
                        name: .networkDidDisconnect, object: nil
                    )
                }
            }
        }
        monitor.start(queue: queue)
    }

    private func classifyConnection(_ path: NWPath) -> ConnectionType {
        if path.usesInterfaceType(.wifi) { return .wifi }
        if path.usesInterfaceType(.cellular) { return .cellular }
        if path.usesInterfaceType(.wiredEthernet) { return .wiredEthernet }
        return .unknown
    }

    private func classifyQuality(_ path: NWPath) -> NetworkQuality {
        guard path.status == .satisfied else { return .offline }
        if path.usesInterfaceType(.wifi) || path.usesInterfaceType(.wiredEthernet) {
            return .strong
        }
        if path.usesInterfaceType(.cellular) {
            // On iOS, NWPath does not directly expose cellular generation.
            // Use isExpensive/isConstrained as proxy for quality.
            return path.isConstrained ? .weak : .strong
        }
        return .weak
    }
}

extension Notification.Name {
    static let networkDidReconnect = Notification.Name("networkDidReconnect")
    static let networkDidDisconnect = Notification.Name("networkDidDisconnect")
}
```

**Important**: `NWPath.status == .satisfied` alone can be insufficient. A device can be "connected" to Wi-Fi but have no internet access. Skye also performs a periodic health-check ping (see Section 5.4) to confirm API reachability.

---

#### 1.2 Client/UI State (`@Observable` Classes)

`@Observable` classes manage all state that does NOT originate from the server: authentication state, UI preferences, chat drafts, offline queue metadata, and notification state. Each concern gets its own state object (no mega-object).

##### 1.2.1 State Definitions

**Auth State** -- Persisted to Keychain Services (encrypted, OS keychain-backed):

```swift
// Sources/State/AuthState.swift
import Foundation
import Observation

@Observable
final class AuthState {
    static let shared = AuthState()

    var accessToken: String? {
        didSet { persistToKeychain() }
    }
    var refreshToken: String? {
        didSet { persistToKeychain() }
    }
    var user: UserProfile?
    var authStatus: AuthStatus = .idle

    enum AuthStatus: String, Codable {
        case idle, loading, authenticated, unauthenticated
    }

    func setTokens(access: String, refresh: String) {
        accessToken = access
        refreshToken = refresh
    }

    func setUser(_ user: UserProfile) {
        self.user = user
    }

    func clearAuth() {
        accessToken = nil
        refreshToken = nil
        user = nil
        authStatus = .unauthenticated
        KeychainService.delete(key: "skye-auth-access")
        KeychainService.delete(key: "skye-auth-refresh")
        KeychainService.delete(key: "skye-auth-user")
    }

    // MARK: - Keychain Persistence

    private func persistToKeychain() {
        if let accessToken {
            KeychainService.save(key: "skye-auth-access", value: accessToken)
        }
        if let refreshToken {
            KeychainService.save(key: "skye-auth-refresh", value: refreshToken)
        }
    }

    func restoreFromKeychain() {
        accessToken = KeychainService.load(key: "skye-auth-access")
        refreshToken = KeychainService.load(key: "skye-auth-refresh")
        if let userData = KeychainService.loadData(key: "skye-auth-user") {
            user = try? JSONDecoder().decode(UserProfile.self, from: userData)
        }
    }
}
```

**UI State** -- Persisted to `UserDefaults`:

```swift
// Sources/State/UIState.swift
import Foundation
import Observation

@Observable
final class UIState {
    static let shared = UIState()

    var activeTab: Tab = .people {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var contactListFilters: ContactFilters = .init() {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var contactListSort: SortConfig = .init(field: .name, direction: .ascending) {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var propertyListFilters: PropertyFilters = .init() {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var themeOverride: ThemeOverride = .system {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var hasCompletedOnboarding: Bool = false {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var lastViewedContactId: String? {
        didSet { persist() }
    }

    enum Tab: String, Codable {
        case people, properties, chat, tasks, settings
    }

    enum ThemeOverride: String, Codable {
        case light, dark, system
    }

    // MARK: - UserDefaults Persistence

    private let defaults = UserDefaults.standard
    private let storageKey = "skye-ui"

    private func persist() {
        guard let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(PersistedUIState(from: self)) else { return }
        defaults.set(data, forKey: storageKey)
    }

    func restore() {
        guard let data = defaults.data(forKey: storageKey),
              let restored = try? JSONDecoder().decode(PersistedUIState.self, from: data)
        else { return }
        activeTab = restored.activeTab
        contactListFilters = restored.contactListFilters
        contactListSort = restored.contactListSort
        propertyListFilters = restored.propertyListFilters
        themeOverride = restored.themeOverride
        hasCompletedOnboarding = restored.hasCompletedOnboarding
        lastViewedContactId = restored.lastViewedContactId
    }
}

private struct PersistedUIState: Codable {
    let activeTab: UIState.Tab
    let contactListFilters: ContactFilters
    let contactListSort: SortConfig
    let propertyListFilters: PropertyFilters
    let themeOverride: UIState.ThemeOverride
    let hasCompletedOnboarding: Bool
    let lastViewedContactId: String?

    init(from state: UIState) {
        activeTab = state.activeTab
        contactListFilters = state.contactListFilters
        contactListSort = state.contactListSort
        propertyListFilters = state.propertyListFilters
        themeOverride = state.themeOverride
        hasCompletedOnboarding = state.hasCompletedOnboarding
        lastViewedContactId = state.lastViewedContactId
    }
}
```

**Chat State** -- Persisted to `UserDefaults` (draft message survives app kill):

```swift
// Sources/State/ChatState.swift
import Foundation
import Observation

@Observable
final class ChatState {
    static let shared = ChatState()

    var activeSessionId: String? {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var inputDraft: String = "" {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var isStreaming: Bool = false
    var streamingMessageId: String?
    var streamedContent: String = ""

    func setStreaming(_ streaming: Bool, messageId: String? = nil) {
        isStreaming = streaming
        streamingMessageId = messageId
    }

    func appendStreamedContent(_ chunk: String) {
        streamedContent += chunk
    }

    func clearStreamedContent() {
        streamedContent = ""
        streamingMessageId = nil
    }

    // MARK: - Persistence (only draft and active session)

    private let defaults = UserDefaults.standard
    private let storageKey = "skye-chat"

    private func persist() {
        let persisted = PersistedChatState(
            activeSessionId: activeSessionId,
            inputDraft: inputDraft
        )
        if let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(persisted) {
            defaults.set(data, forKey: storageKey)
        }
    }

    func restore() {
        guard let data = defaults.data(forKey: storageKey),
              let restored = try? JSONDecoder().decode(
                  PersistedChatState.self, from: data
              )
        else { return }
        activeSessionId = restored.activeSessionId
        inputDraft = restored.inputDraft
    }
}

private struct PersistedChatState: Codable {
    let activeSessionId: String?
    let inputDraft: String
}
```

**Offline State** -- Persisted to `UserDefaults` (metadata only; actual queue is in SQLite):

```swift
// Sources/State/OfflineState.swift
import Foundation
import Observation

enum NetworkQuality: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case strong, weak, offline
}

enum SyncStatus: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case idle, syncing, error
}

@Observable
final class OfflineState {
    static let shared = OfflineState()

    var networkQuality: NetworkQuality = .strong
    var syncStatus: SyncStatus = .idle
    var pendingMutationCount: Int = 0
    var lastSyncTimestamp: Date? {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var failedMutationCount: Int = 0

    // MARK: - Persistence (only lastSyncTimestamp)

    private let defaults = UserDefaults.standard
    private let storageKey = "skye-offline"

    private func persist() {
        if let ts = lastSyncTimestamp {
            defaults.set(ts.timeIntervalSince1970, forKey: storageKey)
        }
    }

    func restore() {
        let ts = defaults.double(forKey: storageKey)
        if ts > 0 {
            lastSyncTimestamp = Date(timeIntervalSince1970: ts)
        }
    }
}
```

**Notification State** -- Persisted to `UserDefaults`:

```swift
// Sources/State/NotificationState.swift
import Foundation
import Observation

@Observable
final class NotificationState {
    static let shared = NotificationState()

    var badgeCounts: BadgeCounts = .init() {
        didSet { persist() }
    }
    var pendingDeepLink: String?
    var pushPermissionStatus: PushPermissionStatus = .undetermined {
        didSet { persist() }
    }

    struct BadgeCounts: Codable {
        var people: Int = 0
        var tasks: Int = 0
        var chat: Int = 0
    }

    enum PushPermissionStatus: String, Codable {
        case granted, denied, undetermined
    }

    func setBadgeCount(tab: WritableKeyPath<BadgeCounts, Int>, count: Int) {
        badgeCounts[keyPath: tab] = count
    }

    func clearBadgeCount(tab: WritableKeyPath<BadgeCounts, Int>) {
        badgeCounts[keyPath: tab] = 0
    }

    // MARK: - Persistence

    private let defaults = UserDefaults.standard
    private let storageKey = "skye-notifications"

    private func persist() {
        let persisted = PersistedNotificationState(
            badgeCounts: badgeCounts,
            pushPermissionStatus: pushPermissionStatus
        )
        if let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(persisted) {
            defaults.set(data, forKey: storageKey)
        }
    }

    func restore() {
        guard let data = defaults.data(forKey: storageKey),
              let restored = try? JSONDecoder().decode(
                  PersistedNotificationState.self, from: data
              )
        else { return }
        badgeCounts = restored.badgeCounts
        pushPermissionStatus = restored.pushPermissionStatus
    }
}

private struct PersistedNotificationState: Codable {
    let badgeCounts: NotificationState.BadgeCounts
    let pushPermissionStatus: NotificationState.PushPermissionStatus
}
```

##### 1.2.2 Storage Adapters

```swift
// Sources/Data/KeychainService.swift
import Foundation
import Security

/// Wraps iOS Keychain Services for secure, encrypted storage.
enum KeychainService {
    static func save(key: String, value: String) {
        guard let data = value.data(using: .utf8) else { return }
        save(key: key, data: data)
    }

    static func save(key: String, data: Data) {
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key,
            kSecAttrService as String: "com.skye.app",
            kSecValueData as String: data,
            kSecAttrAccessible as String: kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly,
        ]
        SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary) // Remove existing.
        SecItemAdd(query as CFDictionary, nil)
    }

    static func load(key: String) -> String? {
        guard let data = loadData(key: key) else { return nil }
        return String(data: data, encoding: .utf8)
    }

    static func loadData(key: String) -> Data? {
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key,
            kSecAttrService as String: "com.skye.app",
            kSecReturnData as String: true,
            kSecMatchLimit as String: kSecMatchLimitOne,
        ]
        var result: AnyObject?
        let status = SecItemCopyMatching(query as CFDictionary, &result)
        guard status == errSecSuccess else { return nil }
        return result as? Data
    }

    static func delete(key: String) {
        let query: [String: Any] = [
            kSecClass as String: kSecClassGenericPassword,
            kSecAttrAccount as String: key,
            kSecAttrService as String: "com.skye.app",
        ]
        SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary)
    }
}
```

**Storage selection rationale**:

| State Object | Storage Backend | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `AuthState` | Keychain Services (`Security` framework) | Tokens are sensitive. iOS Keychain provides hardware-backed encryption. |
| `UIState` | `UserDefaults` | Fast synchronous reads. UI state is not sensitive. Accessed frequently. |
| `ChatState` | `UserDefaults` | Draft persistence needs to be fast and survive app kill. Not sensitive. |
| `OfflineState` | `UserDefaults` (metadata only) | Only `lastSyncTimestamp` is persisted. Actual mutation queue lives in SQLite (GRDB). |
| `NotificationState` | `UserDefaults` | Badge counts and permission status. Not sensitive. Fast reads for tab bar. |

##### 1.2.3 State Initialization Sequence

On every app launch, the following sequence executes in order. The app shows a splash screen until step 5 completes.

```
App Launch
  |
  v
Step 1: Restore persisted client state (synchronous for UserDefaults stores)
  |   - UIState, ChatState, OfflineState, NotificationState
  |     hydrate from UserDefaults synchronously (< 1ms each).
  |   - AuthState hydrates from Keychain (sync call, ~10-50ms).
  |
  v
Step 2: Validate authentication
  |   - Read accessToken from AuthState.
  |   - If no token: authStatus = .unauthenticated, navigate to login.
  |   - If token exists: decode JWT, check expiry.
  |     - If expired: attempt silent refresh via refreshToken.
  |       - Refresh success: update tokens, authStatus = .authenticated.
  |       - Refresh failure: authStatus = .unauthenticated, navigate to login.
  |     - If valid: authStatus = .authenticated.
  |
  v
Step 3: Initialize NetworkMonitor
  |   - Start NWPathMonitor to bridge network state into @Observable.
  |   - Determine initial network quality (strong / weak / offline).
  |
  v
Step 4: Hydrate query cache from SQLite (GRDB)
  |   - CacheManager handles this lazily
  |     (queries are restored on first access, not all at once).
  |   - No blocking wait needed.
  |
  v
Step 5: App Ready
  |   - Hide splash screen.
  |   - If authenticated: show main app (People tab by default).
  |   - If unauthenticated: show login screen.
  |   - If pending deep link exists: navigate to target.
  |
  v
Step 6: Background cache warming (non-blocking, after UI is visible)
      - If online: prefetch contact list, first page of properties, recent chat.
      - If offline: skip -- cached data from persister already available.
      - Process any pending offline mutations.
```

**Timing budget**: Target < 500ms total. `UserDefaults` hydration is < 5ms. Keychain hydration is < 50ms. JWT decode is < 5ms.

---

### 2. Offline Storage Schema (SQLite via GRDB)

#### 2.1 Database Setup

```swift
// Sources/Data/Database.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

/// Singleton database manager using GRDB.swift with WAL mode.
actor DatabaseManager {
    static let shared = DatabaseManager()

    private var dbPool: DatabasePool?

    func initialize() throws -> DatabasePool {
        if let dbPool { return dbPool }

        let databaseURL = try FileManager.default
            .url(for: .applicationSupportDirectory, in: .userDomainMask,
                 appropriateFor: nil, create: true)
            .appendingPathComponent("skye.db")

        var config = Configuration()
        config.foreignKeysEnabled = true

        let pool = try DatabasePool(path: databaseURL.path, configuration: config)

        // Enable WAL mode for concurrent read/write performance.
        try pool.write { db in
            try db.execute(sql: "PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
        }

        try runMigrations(pool)

        self.dbPool = pool
        await CacheManager.shared.initialize(dbPool: pool)
        await MutationQueue.shared.initialize(dbPool: pool)

        return pool
    }

    func getPool() -> DatabasePool? { dbPool }
}
```

#### 2.2 Schema Definition (Migration v1)

```sql
-- Schema Version Tracking
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
  version     INTEGER NOT NULL,
  applied_at  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now') * 1000)
);

-- Query Cache (per-query persistence)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS query_cache (
  key         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  value       TEXT    NOT NULL,
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_query_cache_updated ON query_cache(updated_at);

-- Contacts Cache
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contacts (
  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  data        TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- JSON: full contact object
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,          -- server-side updated_at (ms)
  accessed_at INTEGER NOT NULL,          -- last time this row was read (ms)
  is_stale    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contacts_updated ON contacts(updated_at);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contacts_accessed ON contacts(accessed_at);

-- Contact Details Cache (lazy-loaded, separate from list data)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS contact_details (
  contact_id  TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  notes       TEXT,                      -- JSON array
  deals       TEXT,                      -- JSON array
  tasks       TEXT,                      -- JSON array
  timeline    TEXT,                      -- JSON array
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (contact_id) REFERENCES contacts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_contact_details_updated ON contact_details(updated_at);

-- Properties Cache
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS properties (
  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  data        TEXT    NOT NULL,
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,
  accessed_at INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_properties_updated ON properties(updated_at);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_properties_accessed ON properties(accessed_at);

-- Chat Sessions Cache
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_sessions (
  id          TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  messages    TEXT    NOT NULL,          -- JSON array
  updated_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chat_sessions_updated ON chat_sessions(updated_at);

-- Offline Mutation Queue
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mutation_queue (
  id              TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  type            TEXT    NOT NULL,
  endpoint        TEXT    NOT NULL,
  method          TEXT    NOT NULL,
  body            TEXT    NOT NULL,
  headers         TEXT,
  created_at      INTEGER NOT NULL,
  status          TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  retry_count     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  max_retries     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 3,
  next_retry_at   INTEGER,
  error           TEXT,
  completed_at    INTEGER,
  idempotency_key TEXT    UNIQUE,
  related_entity_id   TEXT,
  related_entity_type TEXT
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mutation_queue_status ON mutation_queue(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mutation_queue_created ON mutation_queue(created_at);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mutation_queue_related
  ON mutation_queue(related_entity_type, related_entity_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mutation_queue_next_retry
  ON mutation_queue(next_retry_at) WHERE status = 'pending' OR status = 'failed';

-- Dead Letter Queue
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dead_letter_queue (
  id                TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  original_mutation TEXT    NOT NULL,
  failure_reason    TEXT    NOT NULL,
  moved_at          INTEGER NOT NULL,
  user_action       TEXT,
  resolved_at       INTEGER
);

-- Image Cache Metadata
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS image_cache (
  url         TEXT    PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  local_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,
  size_bytes  INTEGER NOT NULL,
  mime_type   TEXT,
  accessed_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
  created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_image_cache_accessed ON image_cache(accessed_at);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_image_cache_size ON image_cache(size_bytes);

-- App Metadata
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS metadata (
  key   TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  value TEXT NOT NULL
);

INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (1);
```

#### 2.3 Migration System

```swift
// Sources/Data/Migrations.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

struct Migration {
    let version: Int
    let description: String
    let up: (Database) throws -> Void
}

/// v1 is the initial schema (above). Future migrations are appended here.
private let migrations: [Migration] = [
    // Future migrations:
    // Migration(version: 2, description: "Add full-text search") { db in ... },
]

func runMigrations(_ pool: DatabasePool) throws {
    try pool.write { db in
        // Ensure schema_version table exists for bootstrapping.
        try db.execute(sql: initialSchemaSQL)

        let currentVersion: Int = try Int.fetchOne(
            db,
            sql: "SELECT MAX(version) FROM schema_version"
        ) ?? 0

        for migration in migrations where migration.version > currentVersion {
            try db.inSavepoint {
                try migration.up(db)
                try db.execute(
                    sql: "INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)",
                    arguments: [migration.version]
                )
                return .commit
            }
        }
    }
}

/// The full initial schema SQL (version 1).
private let initialSchemaSQL: String = """
    -- (the full SQL from Section 2.2 above)
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_version (
      version     INTEGER NOT NULL,
      applied_at  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%s', 'now') * 1000)
    );
    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (1);
    -- ... (remainder of schema tables, see Section 2.2)
"""
```

---

### 3. Cache Strategy

#### 3.1 Cache-First Pattern

Skye uses a **cache-first** pattern for all data screens. The user always sees data immediately (from cache), and a background refresh updates the display when available. This eliminates loading spinners for repeat visits.

```
User taps tab
  |
  v
QueryClient checks in-memory cache
  |
  +--> Cache HIT
  |      |
  |      v
  |    Render cached data immediately (0ms perceived latency)
  |      |
  |      v
  |    Stale? --> YES: background refetch + subtle indicator
  |           --> NO: done, no network request
  |
  +--> Cache MISS
         |
         v
       Show skeleton loading --> fetch --> populate cache --> render
```

#### 3.2 Stale Times by Entity

| Entity | `staleTime` | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Contact list | 5 minutes | Contacts change infrequently |
| Contact detail | 5 minutes | Same; invalidated immediately on local edit |
| Property list | 15 minutes | Property data changes less frequently (MLS sync cadence) |
| Property detail | 15 minutes | Same as list |
| Chat history | 1 minute | Messages can arrive from AI or other sessions |
| Chat sessions list | 2 minutes | Changes when new chats are started |
| Task list | 3 minutes | Tasks are time-sensitive |
| User profile | 30 minutes | Rarely changes |
| Dashboard stats | 5 minutes | Should feel reasonably current |

#### 3.3 Cache Invalidation Triggers

**Time-based**: Handled automatically by the data layer's `staleTime` check.

**Event-based**:

```swift
// Sources/Data/CacheInvalidation.swift
import Foundation

enum CacheInvalidation {
    private static var queryClient: QueryClient { .shared }

    static func afterContactMutation(contactId: String? = nil) {
        queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists())
        if let contactId {
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: contactId))
        }
    }

    static func afterPushNotification(_ payload: PushPayload) {
        switch payload.type {
        case .contactUpdated:
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(
                QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: payload.entityId)
            )
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists())
        case .newMessage:
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(
                QueryKeys.Chat.history(sessionId: payload.sessionId)
            )
        case .taskAssigned:
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Tasks.all)
        default:
            queryClient.invalidateQueries()
        }
    }

    static func afterExtendedBackground() {
        queryClient.invalidateQueries() // Invalidate all.
    }

    static func afterQueueDrain() {
        queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.all)
        queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Tasks.all)
        queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Properties.all)
    }
}
```

**App foreground invalidation** (after >5 minutes backgrounded):

```swift
// Sources/Views/AppForegroundRefreshModifier.swift
import SwiftUI

struct AppForegroundRefreshModifier: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase

    private static let backgroundThreshold: TimeInterval = 5 * 60 // 5 minutes
    @State private var backgroundTimestamp: Date?

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            .onChange(of: scenePhase) { oldPhase, newPhase in
                switch newPhase {
                case .background, .inactive:
                    backgroundTimestamp = Date()
                case .active:
                    if let ts = backgroundTimestamp {
                        let elapsed = Date().timeIntervalSince(ts)
                        if elapsed > Self.backgroundThreshold {
                            CacheInvalidation.afterExtendedBackground()
                        }
                    }
                    backgroundTimestamp = nil
                @unknown default:
                    break
                }
            }
    }
}

extension View {
    func appForegroundRefresh() -> some View {
        modifier(AppForegroundRefreshModifier())
    }
}
```

**Manual**: Pull-to-refresh calls the view model's `refresh()` method, which re-fetches the active query.

#### 3.4 Cache Eviction

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| Data cache limit | 50 MB total across contacts, properties, chat sessions, query cache |
| Image cache limit | 100 MB (separate from data cache) |
| Eviction strategy | LRU based on `accessed_at` column |
| Eviction target | When limit exceeded, free down to 80% of limit |
| Protected from eviction | Contacts with pending offline mutations; currently viewed items |
| Eviction schedule | After every successful sync; on app foreground; on storage pressure |

```swift
// Sources/Data/CacheEviction.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

actor CacheEvictor {
    static let shared = CacheEvictor()

    private let maxDataCacheBytes: Int = 50 * 1024 * 1024  // 50 MB
    private let maxImageCacheBytes: Int = 100 * 1024 * 1024 // 100 MB

    func evictDataCacheIfNeeded(dbPool: DatabasePool) async throws {
        try await dbPool.read { db in
            // Calculate total cache size across all tables.
            let totalBytes = try Int.fetchOne(db, sql: """
                SELECT
                  COALESCE(SUM(LENGTH(data)), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(notes)+LENGTH(deals)
                            +LENGTH(tasks)+LENGTH(timeline))
                            FROM contact_details), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(data)) FROM properties), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(messages)) FROM chat_sessions), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(value)) FROM query_cache), 0)
                AS total FROM contacts
                """) ?? 0

            guard totalBytes > self.maxDataCacheBytes else { return }

            let bytesToFree = totalBytes - Int(Double(self.maxDataCacheBytes) * 0.8)

            // Get protected IDs (contacts with pending mutations).
            let protectedIds = try String.fetchAll(db, sql: """
                SELECT DISTINCT related_entity_id FROM mutation_queue
                WHERE related_entity_type = 'contact'
                  AND status IN ('pending', 'processing', 'failed')
                  AND related_entity_id IS NOT NULL
                """)

            // Build exclusion clause.
            let placeholders = protectedIds.isEmpty
                ? "''"
                : protectedIds.map { _ in "?" }.joined(separator: ",")

            // LRU eviction: oldest accessed contacts first, skipping protected.
            struct Candidate: FetchableRecord, Decodable {
                let id: String
                let size: Int
            }

            let candidates = try Candidate.fetchAll(db, sql: """
                SELECT id, LENGTH(data) as size FROM contacts
                WHERE id NOT IN (\(placeholders))
                ORDER BY accessed_at ASC
                """, arguments: StatementArguments(protectedIds))

            var freed = 0
            var toEvict: [String] = []
            for c in candidates {
                if freed >= bytesToFree { break }
                toEvict.append(c.id)
                freed += c.size
            }

            guard !toEvict.isEmpty else { return }

            let evictPlaceholders = toEvict.map { _ in "?" }.joined(separator: ",")
            // Must switch to write context for deletions.
            // (In practice, call this from a write block.)
        }

        // Perform deletions in a write transaction.
        // Re-compute toEvict or pass from above using a different pattern.
        try await dbPool.write { db in
            // Repeat candidate selection and deletion in write context.
            let protectedIds = try String.fetchAll(db, sql: """
                SELECT DISTINCT related_entity_id FROM mutation_queue
                WHERE related_entity_type = 'contact'
                  AND status IN ('pending', 'processing', 'failed')
                  AND related_entity_id IS NOT NULL
                """)

            let totalBytes = try Int.fetchOne(db, sql: """
                SELECT
                  COALESCE(SUM(LENGTH(data)), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(data)) FROM properties), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(messages)) FROM chat_sessions), 0) +
                  COALESCE((SELECT SUM(LENGTH(value)) FROM query_cache), 0)
                AS total FROM contacts
                """) ?? 0

            guard totalBytes > self.maxDataCacheBytes else { return }
            let bytesToFree = totalBytes - Int(Double(self.maxDataCacheBytes) * 0.8)

            let placeholders = protectedIds.isEmpty
                ? "''"
                : protectedIds.map { _ in "?" }.joined(separator: ",")

            struct Candidate: FetchableRecord, Decodable {
                let id: String
                let size: Int
            }

            let candidates = try Candidate.fetchAll(db, sql: """
                SELECT id, LENGTH(data) as size FROM contacts
                WHERE id NOT IN (\(placeholders))
                ORDER BY accessed_at ASC
                """, arguments: StatementArguments(protectedIds))

            var freed = 0
            var toEvict: [String] = []
            for c in candidates {
                if freed >= bytesToFree { break }
                toEvict.append(c.id)
                freed += c.size
            }

            if !toEvict.isEmpty {
                let ph = toEvict.map { _ in "?" }.joined(separator: ",")
                try db.execute(
                    sql: "DELETE FROM contacts WHERE id IN (\(ph))",
                    arguments: StatementArguments(toEvict)
                )
                try db.execute(
                    sql: "DELETE FROM contact_details WHERE contact_id IN (\(ph))",
                    arguments: StatementArguments(toEvict)
                )
            }

            // Same pattern for properties, chat_sessions, and old query_cache entries.
        }
    }
}
```

Image cache eviction follows the same LRU pattern, also deleting the actual files from disk via `FileManager`.

#### 3.5 Cache Warming

On login (or first launch after authentication), Skye proactively prefetches the most-needed data:

```swift
// Sources/Data/CacheWarming.swift
import Foundation

enum CacheWarming {
    static func warmCache() async {
        let queryClient = QueryClient.shared
        let api = APIClient.shared

        await withTaskGroup(of: Void.self) { group in
            group.addTask {
                queryClient.prefetchQuery(
                    key: QueryKeys.Contacts.list(
                        filters: ContactFilters(status: .all, search: "")
                    ),
                    staleTime: 5 * 60
                ) {
                    try await api.getContacts(
                        filters: ContactFilters(status: .all, search: "")
                    )
                }
            }

            group.addTask {
                queryClient.prefetchQuery(
                    key: QueryKeys.Properties.list(),
                    staleTime: 15 * 60
                ) {
                    try await api.getProperties()
                }
            }

            group.addTask {
                queryClient.prefetchQuery(
                    key: QueryKeys.Chat.recent(),
                    staleTime: 1 * 60
                ) {
                    try await api.getRecentChats()
                }
            }

            group.addTask {
                queryClient.prefetchQuery(
                    key: QueryKeys.Tasks.list(),
                    staleTime: 3 * 60
                ) {
                    try await api.getTasks()
                }
            }

            group.addTask {
                queryClient.prefetchQuery(
                    key: QueryKeys.User.profile,
                    staleTime: 30 * 60
                ) {
                    try await api.getUserProfile()
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

---

### 4. Offline Mutation Queue

#### 4.1 Queue Architecture

The offline mutation queue is a durable FIFO queue backed by the `mutation_queue` SQLite table (via GRDB). It guarantees that every user action taken while offline is persisted, survives app kills, and is replayed in correct order when connectivity returns.

```
User performs mutation
  |
  v
Is device online?
  +--> YES: Execute directly via APIClient async method.
  |         On network error: fall through to offline path.
  |
  +--> NO (or network error):
         1. Generate UUID + idempotency key.
         2. Apply optimistic update to QueryClient cache.
         3. INSERT into mutation_queue (status: 'pending') via GRDB.
         4. Update OfflineState.pendingMutationCount.
         5. Show "Pending sync" badge on affected entity.
```

#### 4.2 Supported Offline Mutations

| Mutation | Method | Endpoint | Offline Behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| Create contact | `POST` | `/api/crm/contacts` | Queued. Temp client UUID assigned. Mapped to server ID on sync. |
| Edit contact | `PATCH` | `/api/contacts/[id]` | Queued. Optimistic update shown. |
| Delete contact | `DELETE` | `/api/contacts/[id]` | Queued. Item hidden from list. Restored on sync failure. |
| Add note | `POST` | `/api/contacts/[id]/notes` | Queued. Note appears with "pending" badge. |
| Edit note | `PATCH` | `/api/contacts/[id]/notes/[noteId]` | Queued. Optimistic update. |
| Create task | `POST` | `/api/tasks` | Queued. Task appears with "pending" badge. |
| Complete task | `PATCH` | `/api/tasks/[id]` | Queued. Marked complete immediately. |
| Send chat message | `POST` | `/api/chat` | Queued with warning: "AI responses require connectivity." |

**Not supported offline** (disabled in UI):
- Starting new AI chat sessions (requires server-side session creation).
- Uploading images/documents (too large for reliable queue).
- Bulk import/export operations.
- Account settings changes.

#### 4.3 Enqueue Implementation

```swift
// Sources/Data/MutationQueue.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

/// Thread-safe, GRDB-backed offline mutation queue.
actor MutationQueue {
    static let shared = MutationQueue()

    private var dbPool: DatabasePool?

    func initialize(dbPool: DatabasePool) {
        self.dbPool = dbPool
    }

    // MARK: - Enqueue

    func enqueue(_ request: MutationRequest) async -> String {
        let id = UUID().uuidString
        let now = Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000)
        let idempotencyKey = "\(request.type.rawValue):\(request.relatedEntityId ?? "new"):\(now)"

        guard let dbPool else { return id }

        do {
            try dbPool.write { db in
                try db.execute(
                    sql: """
                        INSERT INTO mutation_queue
                          (id, type, endpoint, method, body, created_at, status,
                           retry_count, max_retries, idempotency_key,
                           related_entity_id, related_entity_type)
                        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending', 0, 3, ?, ?, ?)
                        """,
                    arguments: [
                        id,
                        request.type.rawValue,
                        request.endpoint,
                        request.method.rawValue,
                        String(data: request.body, encoding: .utf8) ?? "{}",
                        now,
                        idempotencyKey,
                        request.relatedEntityId,
                        request.relatedEntityType?.rawValue,
                    ]
                )
            }
        } catch {
            // Log enqueue failure. This should not happen under normal conditions.
        }

        await refreshPendingCount()
        return id
    }

    // MARK: - Pending Count

    @MainActor
    private func refreshPendingCount() async {
        guard let dbPool else { return }
        let count = try? dbPool.read { db in
            try Int.fetchOne(
                db,
                sql: """
                    SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mutation_queue
                    WHERE status IN ('pending', 'failed')
                    """
            )
        }
        OfflineState.shared.pendingMutationCount = count ?? 0
    }
}

// MARK: - Supporting Types

struct MutationRequest: Sendable {
    let type: MutationType
    let endpoint: String
    let method: HTTPMethod
    let body: Data
    let relatedEntityId: String?
    let relatedEntityType: EntityType?

    init(
        type: MutationType,
        endpoint: String,
        method: HTTPMethod,
        body: Data,
        relatedEntityId: String? = nil,
        relatedEntityType: EntityType? = nil
    ) {
        self.type = type
        self.endpoint = endpoint
        self.method = method
        self.body = body
        self.relatedEntityId = relatedEntityId
        self.relatedEntityType = relatedEntityType
    }
}

enum MutationType: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case createContact = "create_contact"
    case editContact = "edit_contact"
    case deleteContact = "delete_contact"
    case addNote = "add_note"
    case editNote = "edit_note"
    case createTask = "create_task"
    case completeTask = "complete_task"
    case sendChatMessage = "send_chat_message"
}

enum HTTPMethod: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case post = "POST"
    case patch = "PATCH"
    case put = "PUT"
    case delete = "DELETE"
}

enum EntityType: String, Codable, Sendable {
    case contact, property, task, chatSession, note
}
```

#### 4.4 Queue Processor (SyncCoordinator)

```swift
// Sources/Data/SyncCoordinator.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

/// Orchestrates draining the offline mutation queue when connectivity is restored.
actor SyncCoordinator {
    static let shared = SyncCoordinator()

    /// Map of client IDs to server IDs for entities created offline.
    private var idRemapping: [String: String] = [:]

    private let api = APIClient.shared

    // MARK: - Process Queue

    func processQueue() async {
        let offlineState = OfflineState.shared

        guard await MainActor.run(body: { offlineState.syncStatus }) != .syncing else { return }
        guard await MainActor.run(body: { offlineState.networkQuality }) != .offline else { return }

        guard let dbPool = await DatabaseManager.shared.getPool() else { return }

        await MainActor.run { offlineState.syncStatus = .syncing }

        do {
            // FIFO: process sequentially (order matters).
            let pendingMutations = try dbPool.read { db -> [QueuedMutation] in
                try QueuedMutation.fetchAll(db, sql: """
                    SELECT * FROM mutation_queue
                    WHERE status IN ('pending', 'failed')
                      AND (next_retry_at IS NULL OR next_retry_at <= ?)
                    ORDER BY created_at ASC
                    """, arguments: [Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000)])
            }

            for mutation in pendingMutations {
                await processSingleMutation(dbPool: dbPool, mutation: mutation)
            }

            CacheInvalidation.afterQueueDrain()
            await MainActor.run {
                offlineState.syncStatus = .idle
                offlineState.lastSyncTimestamp = Date()
            }
        } catch {
            await MainActor.run { offlineState.syncStatus = .error }
        }

        await MutationQueue.shared.refreshPendingCount()
    }

    // MARK: - Process Single Mutation

    private func processSingleMutation(
        dbPool: DatabasePool,
        mutation: QueuedMutation
    ) async {
        try? dbPool.write { db in
            try db.execute(
                sql: "UPDATE mutation_queue SET status = 'processing' WHERE id = ?",
                arguments: [mutation.id]
            )
        }

        do {
            let endpoint = remapIds(in: mutation.endpoint)
            var bodyData = mutation.body.data(using: .utf8) ?? Data()
            bodyData = remapBodyIds(in: bodyData)

            let response: APIResponse = try await api.request(
                method: HTTPMethod(rawValue: mutation.method) ?? .post,
                url: endpoint,
                body: bodyData,
                headers: [
                    "Idempotency-Key": mutation.idempotencyKey ?? ""
                ],
                timeout: 30
            )

            // Record ID mapping for "create" mutations.
            if mutation.type.hasPrefix("create_"),
               let serverId = response.id {
                if let clientId = mutation.relatedEntityId,
                   clientId != serverId {
                    idRemapping[clientId] = serverId
                }
            }

            try? dbPool.write { db in
                try db.execute(
                    sql: """
                        UPDATE mutation_queue
                        SET status = 'completed', completed_at = ?, error = NULL
                        WHERE id = ?
                        """,
                    arguments: [
                        Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000),
                        mutation.id,
                    ]
                )
            }
        } catch {
            await handleMutationError(dbPool: dbPool, mutation: mutation, error: error)
        }
    }

    // MARK: - ID Remapping

    private func remapIds(in endpoint: String) -> String {
        var result = endpoint
        for (clientId, serverId) in idRemapping {
            result = result.replacingOccurrences(of: clientId, with: serverId)
        }
        return result
    }

    private func remapBodyIds(in bodyData: Data) -> Data {
        guard var bodyString = String(data: bodyData, encoding: .utf8) else {
            return bodyData
        }
        for (clientId, serverId) in idRemapping {
            bodyString = bodyString.replacingOccurrences(of: clientId, with: serverId)
        }
        return bodyString.data(using: .utf8) ?? bodyData
    }
}

/// GRDB-fetchable representation of a queued mutation row.
struct QueuedMutation: FetchableRecord, Decodable {
    let id: String
    let type: String
    let endpoint: String
    let method: String
    let body: String
    let headers: String?
    let createdAt: Int64
    let status: String
    let retryCount: Int
    let maxRetries: Int
    let nextRetryAt: Int64?
    let error: String?
    let completedAt: Int64?
    let idempotencyKey: String?
    let relatedEntityId: String?
    let relatedEntityType: String?

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case id, type, endpoint, method, body, headers, status, error
        case createdAt = "created_at"
        case retryCount = "retry_count"
        case maxRetries = "max_retries"
        case nextRetryAt = "next_retry_at"
        case completedAt = "completed_at"
        case idempotencyKey = "idempotency_key"
        case relatedEntityId = "related_entity_id"
        case relatedEntityType = "related_entity_type"
    }
}
```

**Error handling logic**:

```swift
// Sources/Data/SyncCoordinator+ErrorHandling.swift
import Foundation
import GRDB

extension SyncCoordinator {

    func handleMutationError(
        dbPool: DatabasePool,
        mutation: QueuedMutation,
        error: Error
    ) async {
        let statusCode = (error as? APIError)?.statusCode
        let newRetryCount = mutation.retryCount + 1
        let permanentFailureCodes = [400, 403, 404, 422]

        // 409 Conflict: special handling (see Section 4.5).
        if statusCode == 409 {
            await handleConflict(
                dbPool: dbPool,
                mutation: mutation,
                serverData: (error as? APIError)?.responseData
            )
            return
        }

        // Other permanent failures: dead letter.
        if let code = statusCode, permanentFailureCodes.contains(code) {
            await moveToDeadLetter(
                dbPool: dbPool,
                mutation: mutation,
                reason: "HTTP \(code): \(error.localizedDescription)"
            )
            return
        }

        // Transient failures: retry with exponential backoff.
        if newRetryCount >= mutation.maxRetries {
            await moveToDeadLetter(
                dbPool: dbPool,
                mutation: mutation,
                reason: "Max retries (\(mutation.maxRetries)) exceeded. "
                    + "Last error: \(error.localizedDescription)"
            )
            return
        }

        let backoffSeconds = min(pow(2.0, Double(newRetryCount)), 60.0)
        let nextRetryAt = Int64(
            (Date().timeIntervalSince1970 + backoffSeconds) * 1000
        )

        let errorJSON: String
        if let data = try? JSONEncoder().encode(
            ["statusCode": statusCode ?? 0, "message": error.localizedDescription]
        ) {
            errorJSON = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8) ?? "{}"
        } else {
            errorJSON = "{}"
        }

        try? dbPool.write { db in
            try db.execute(
                sql: """
                    UPDATE mutation_queue
                    SET status = 'failed', retry_count = ?,
                        next_retry_at = ?, error = ?
                    WHERE id = ?
                    """,
                arguments: [newRetryCount, nextRetryAt, errorJSON, mutation.id]
            )
        }
    }

    private func moveToDeadLetter(
        dbPool: DatabasePool,
        mutation: QueuedMutation,
        reason: String
    ) async {
        let now = Int64(Date().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000)
        try? dbPool.write { db in
            // Encode the full mutation as JSON for the dead letter record.
            let mutationJSON = try JSONEncoder().encode(mutation)
            try db.execute(
                sql: """
                    INSERT INTO dead_letter_queue
                      (id, original_mutation, failure_reason, moved_at)
                    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
                    """,
                arguments: [
                    mutation.id,
                    String(data: mutationJSON, encoding: .utf8) ?? "{}",
                    reason,
                    now,
                ]
            )
            try db.execute(
                sql: "DELETE FROM mutation_queue WHERE id = ?",
                arguments: [mutation.id]
            )
        }

        await MainActor.run {
            OfflineState.shared.failedMutationCount += 1
        }
    }
}
```

#### 4.5 Conflict Resolution

```swift
// Sources/Data/ConflictResolution.swift
import Foundation

/// Field-level conflict resolution strategy.
enum ConflictStrategy: String {
    case lastWriteWins = "last_write_wins"
    case append = "append"
    case userMerge = "user_merge"
}

/// Strategy mapping per contact field.
let fieldStrategies: [String: ConflictStrategy] = [
    "name":    .lastWriteWins,
    "email":   .lastWriteWins,
    "phone":   .lastWriteWins,
    "address": .lastWriteWins,
    "status":  .lastWriteWins,
    "company": .lastWriteWins,
    "title":   .lastWriteWins,
    "notes":   .append,     // Both offline note and remote notes are kept.
    "tags":    .append,
    "deals":   .userMerge,  // Complex -- needs user decision.
]
```

When the server returns 409:
1. For each changed field, apply the field-level strategy.
2. `lastWriteWins`: client value wins (the agent made the most recent intentional change).
3. `append`: merge both arrays (deduplicate by ID if applicable).
4. `userMerge`: mark the mutation as needing user resolution. The `SyncStatusBadge` shows "Conflict -- Tap to resolve" and opens a merge UI showing both versions side by side.

If all fields are auto-resolved, the mutation is retried with the merged data. If any field requires `userMerge`, the mutation is paused until the user resolves it.

#### 4.6 Queue Processing Triggers

```swift
// Sources/Data/QueueTriggers.swift
import SwiftUI
import Network

/// A SwiftUI view modifier that sets up queue processing triggers.
struct QueueTriggersModifier: ViewModifier {
    @Environment(\.scenePhase) private var scenePhase

    let offlineState = OfflineState.shared
    let networkMonitor = NetworkMonitor.shared

    func body(content: Content) -> some View {
        content
            // Trigger 1: Network restoration.
            .onReceive(
                NotificationCenter.default.publisher(for: .networkDidReconnect)
            ) { _ in
                // Stabilize connection first (2 second delay).
                Task {
                    try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(2))
                    await SyncCoordinator.shared.processQueue()
                }
            }
            // Trigger 2: App foreground.
            .onChange(of: scenePhase) { _, newPhase in
                if newPhase == .active {
                    if offlineState.networkQuality != .offline
                        && offlineState.pendingMutationCount > 0 {
                        Task {
                            await SyncCoordinator.shared.processQueue()
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
    }
}

extension View {
    func queueTriggers() -> some View {
        modifier(QueueTriggersModifier())
    }
}

// Trigger 3: Manual sync (exposed for UI).
func manualSync() {
    Task {
        await SyncCoordinator.shared.processQueue()
    }
}
```

#### 4.7 UI Indicators

```swift
// Sources/Views/Components/SyncStatusBadge.swift
import SwiftUI

/// Sync status for a specific entity.
enum EntitySyncStatus: String, Sendable {
    case synced, pending, syncing, failed, conflict
}

/// Polls the mutation_queue every 2 seconds for this entity
/// and returns the appropriate sync status.
@Observable
final class EntitySyncStatusProvider {
    var status: EntitySyncStatus = .synced

    private let entityType: String
    private let entityId: String
    private var pollingTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    init(entityType: String, entityId: String) {
        self.entityType = entityType
        self.entityId = entityId
        startPolling()
    }

    deinit {
        pollingTask?.cancel()
    }

    private func startPolling() {
        pollingTask = Task { [weak self] in
            while !Task.isCancelled {
                self?.refreshStatus()
                try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(2))
            }
        }
    }

    private func refreshStatus() {
        // Query mutation_queue for this entity.
        // Returns .synced if no pending mutations.
        // Returns .pending | .syncing | .failed | .conflict based on queue status.
    }
}
```

| Status | Visual Treatment |
|---|---|
| `pending` | Amber badge with clock icon: "Pending sync" |
| `syncing` | Blue badge with spinning icon: "Syncing..." |
| `synced` | Brief green flash, then badge removed |
| `failed` | Red badge with exclamation: "Sync failed -- Tap to retry" |
| `conflict` | Orange badge with merge icon: "Conflict -- Tap to resolve" |

---

### 5. Network State Management

#### 5.1 Network Quality Detection

```swift
// Sources/Data/NetworkQualityClassifier.swift
import Foundation
import Network

/// Classifies the current NWPath into a NetworkQuality level.
func classifyNetworkQuality(_ path: NWPath) -> NetworkQuality {
    guard path.status == .satisfied else { return .offline }

    if path.usesInterfaceType(.wifi) || path.usesInterfaceType(.wiredEthernet) {
        return .strong
    }

    if path.usesInterfaceType(.cellular) {
        // NWPath does not expose cellular generation directly on iOS.
        // Use isExpensive/isConstrained as a proxy for quality.
        // isConstrained indicates Low Data Mode; treat as weak.
        return path.isConstrained ? .weak : .strong
    }

    return .weak // Unknown type
}
```

#### 5.2 Network State Machine

```
  +-----------+     signal degrades     +-----------+
  |  STRONG   | ----------------------> |   WEAK    |
  |  (online) | <---------------------- |  (online) |
  +-----+-----+    signal improves      +-----+-----+
        |                                      |
        | connection lost         connection lost
        v                                      v
  +----------------------------------------------------+
  |                    OFFLINE                           |
  |  - Show offline banner                              |
  |  - Mutations route to offline queue                 |
  |  - Serve all data from cache                        |
  |  - Disable auto-prefetch                            |
  |  - Periodic health check continues (30s interval)   |
  +----------------------------------------------------+
        |                                      |
        | connection restored    connection restored
        v                                      v
  +-----------+                          +-----------+
  |  STRONG   |                          |   WEAK    |
  +-----------+                          +-----------+
```

##### 5.2.1 Transition: Online -> Offline

1. Show non-blocking banner: "You're offline -- showing cached data"
2. Cancel all in-flight `URLSession` tasks (they will fail anyway).
3. The data layer automatically pauses pending mutations (`networkMode: .offlineFirst`).
4. Disable background prefetch timers.
5. Start health check polling (every 30 seconds).

##### 5.2.2 Transition: Offline -> Online

1. Health check confirms API reachability (do not trust `NWPathMonitor` alone).
2. Hide offline banner.
3. Process offline mutation queue via `SyncCoordinator`.
4. Data layer's `refetchOnReconnect` automatically refetches all active queries.
5. Re-enable auto-prefetch.

##### 5.2.3 Transition: Strong -> Weak

1. Reduce image quality in API requests (`image_quality: "low"`).
2. Increase request timeouts from 15s to 30s.
3. Disable auto-prefetch (save bandwidth).
4. Show subtle network quality indicator (icon change, not a banner).

##### 5.2.4 Transition: Weak -> Strong

1. Restore normal image quality (`image_quality: "high"`).
2. Restore normal timeouts (15s).
3. Re-enable auto-prefetch.

#### 5.3 Network-Aware UI Component

```swift
// Sources/Views/Components/NetworkBanner.swift
import SwiftUI

struct NetworkBanner: View {
    let offlineState = OfflineState.shared

    var body: some View {
        if offlineState.networkQuality == .strong
            && offlineState.syncStatus == .idle {
            EmptyView()
        } else if offlineState.networkQuality == .offline {
            BannerView(
                variant: .warning,
                icon: "wifi.slash",
                message: offlineBannerMessage
            )
        } else if offlineState.syncStatus == .syncing {
            BannerView(
                variant: .info,
                icon: "arrow.triangle.2.circlepath",
                spinning: true,
                message: "Syncing \(offlineState.pendingMutationCount) pending changes..."
            )
        } else if offlineState.syncStatus == .error {
            BannerView(
                variant: .error,
                icon: "exclamationmark.circle",
                message: "Sync failed -- tap to retry",
                onTap: { manualSync() }
            )
        }
    }

    private var offlineBannerMessage: String {
        var msg = "You're offline -- showing cached data"
        if offlineState.pendingMutationCount > 0 {
            msg += " (\(offlineState.pendingMutationCount) changes pending sync)"
        }
        return msg
    }
}

struct BannerView: View {
    enum Variant { case warning, info, error }

    let variant: Variant
    let icon: String
    var spinning: Bool = false
    let message: String
    var onTap: (() -> Void)?

    var body: some View {
        HStack(spacing: 8) {
            Image(systemName: icon)
                .rotationEffect(spinning ? .degrees(360) : .zero)
                .animation(
                    spinning
                        ? .linear(duration: 1).repeatForever(autoreverses: false)
                        : .default,
                    value: spinning
                )
            Text(message)
                .font(.footnote)
                .lineLimit(2)
        }
        .padding(.horizontal, 16)
        .padding(.vertical, 10)
        .frame(maxWidth: .infinity)
        .background(backgroundColor)
        .foregroundStyle(foregroundColor)
        .onTapGesture { onTap?() }
    }

    private var backgroundColor: Color {
        switch variant {
        case .warning: .yellow.opacity(0.15)
        case .info: .blue.opacity(0.15)
        case .error: .red.opacity(0.15)
        }
    }

    private var foregroundColor: Color {
        switch variant {
        case .warning: .orange
        case .info: .blue
        case .error: .red
        }
    }
}
```

#### 5.4 Health Check Ping

```swift
// Sources/Data/HealthCheck.swift
import Foundation

actor HealthChecker {
    static let shared = HealthChecker()

    private let healthEndpoint = "/api/health"
    private let healthCheckInterval: TimeInterval = 30 // 30 seconds when offline
    private let healthCheckTimeout: TimeInterval = 5

    private var pollingTask: Task<Void, Never>?

    func checkAPIHealth() async -> Bool {
        guard let url = URL(string: "\(APIConfig.baseURL)\(healthEndpoint)") else {
            return false
        }
        var request = URLRequest(url: url)
        request.httpMethod = "HEAD"
        request.timeoutInterval = healthCheckTimeout

        do {
            let (_, response) = try await URLSession.shared.data(for: request)
            guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
                return false
            }
            return (200..<300).contains(httpResponse.statusCode)
        } catch {
            return false
        }
    }

    /// Starts polling when offline. Stops when connectivity confirmed.
    func startPolling() {
        guard pollingTask == nil else { return }
        pollingTask = Task {
            while !Task.isCancelled {
                try? await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(healthCheckInterval))
                let isHealthy = await checkAPIHealth()
                if isHealthy {
                    await MainActor.run {
```
