```swift
                        OfflineState.shared.networkQuality = .strong
                    }
                    NotificationCenter.default.post(
                        name: .networkDidReconnect, object: nil
                    )
                    stopPolling()
                    return
                }
            }
        }
    }

    func stopPolling() {
        pollingTask?.cancel()
        pollingTask = nil
    }
}
```

#### 5.5 Special Cases

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Airplane mode** | Treated as `offline`. `NWPathMonitor` detects immediately. |
| **VPN changes** | Re-validate via health check. VPN can alter API reachability. |
| **Captive portal** | `NWPathMonitor` may report satisfied but API is unreachable. Health check catches this. |
| **iOS background fetch** | Check connectivity, process mutation queue if online. |

---

### 6. Data Flow Diagrams

#### 6.1 Scenario A: User Opens People Tab (Online)

```
User taps "People" tab
  |
  v
[1] View appears, ContactListViewModel.loadContacts(filters:) called
  |
  v
[2] QueryClient checks in-memory cache for contacts:list:{filters}
  |
  +--> Cache HIT
  |     [3] Return cached data immediately (0ms latency)
  |     [4] SwiftUI renders contact list
  |     [5] Is data stale? (staleTime 5min exceeded?)
  |          +--> YES:
  |          |     [6] Background GET /api/crm/contacts?{filters}
  |          |     [7] Subtle refresh indicator (thin progress bar, NOT skeleton)
  |          |     [8] Response received
  |          |     [9] Cache updated with fresh data
  |          |     [10] GRDB persister writes to query_cache
  |          |     [11] @Observable triggers SwiftUI re-render (imperceptible)
  |          |     [12] Refresh indicator hidden
  |          +--> NO: Done, no network request
  |
  +--> Cache MISS
        [3] Skeleton loading state
        [4] GET /api/crm/contacts?{filters}
        [5] Response received
        [6] Cache populated
        [7] GRDB persister writes data
        [8] SwiftUI renders (skeleton replaced)
```

#### 6.2 Scenario B: User Opens People Tab (Offline)

```
User taps "People" tab
  |
  v
[1] View appears, ContactListViewModel.loadContacts(filters:) called
  |
  v
[2] QueryClient checks in-memory cache
  |
  +--> Cache HIT (from previous session, restored by CacheManager)
  |     [3] Return cached data immediately
  |     [4] SwiftUI renders contact list
  |     [5] Background refetch attempted
  |     [6] NetworkMonitor reports offline -> fetch PAUSED (not failed)
  |     [7] NetworkBanner: "You're offline -- showing cached data"
  |     [8] User browses cached data normally
  |     [9] When connectivity returns:
  |         - Paused fetch resumes automatically
  |         - Banner hides
  |         - Fresh data replaces cached seamlessly
  |
  +--> Cache MISS
        [3] Empty state: "No cached data. Connect to load contacts."
        [4] NetworkBanner: "You're offline"
        [5] When online: fetch fires automatically
```

#### 6.3 Scenario C: User Edits Contact (Online)

```
User changes phone number, taps "Save"
  |
  v
[1] ContactViewModel.editContact(id:updates:) called
  |
  v
[2] Optimistic update:
  |   - Snapshot current cache value (previousContact)
  |   - Update detail cache: existing.applying(updates)
  |   - Update list caches: find contact in all list variants, apply update
  |
  v
[3] SwiftUI re-renders with new phone (0ms perceived latency)
  |
  v
[4] APIClient.patch("/api/contacts/abc", body: updates)
  |
  +--> SUCCESS (200)
  |     [5] Invalidate detail + list queries
  |     [6] Background refetch gets canonical server data
  |     [7] GRDB persister updated
  |     Done.
  |
  +--> FAILURE (network/5xx)
        [5] Restore previousContact (rollback)
        [6] SwiftUI reverts to original phone
        [7] Retry (up to 3x, exponential backoff via Task.sleep)
        [8] All retries fail: error toast "Failed to save. Try again."
```

#### 6.4 Scenario D: User Edits Contact (Offline)

```
User changes phone number, taps "Save" (offline)
  |
  v
[1] ContactViewModel.editContact(id:updates:) called
  |
  v
[2] Optimistic update (same as online)
  |
  v
[3] SwiftUI shows new phone number immediately
  |
  v
[4] NetworkMonitor reports offline -> MutationQueue path activated
  |
  v
[5] Offline queue system activates:
  |   - MutationQueue.shared.enqueue(MutationRequest(...))
  |   - INSERT into mutation_queue (status: 'pending') via GRDB
  |   - OfflineState.pendingMutationCount++
  |
  v
[6] SyncStatusBadge shows "Pending sync" on this contact
  |
  v
[7] User continues offline. Contact shows updated phone everywhere.
  |
  --- CONNECTIVITY RETURNS ---
  |
  v
[8] NWPathMonitor fires, health check confirms API reachable
  |
  v
[9] SyncCoordinator.processQueue() starts
  |
  v
[10] PATCH /api/contacts/abc with Idempotency-Key header
  |
  +--> SUCCESS (200)
  |     [11] mutation_queue status = 'completed'
  |     [12] CacheInvalidation.afterQueueDrain()
  |     [13] Refetch gets canonical server data
  |     [14] SyncStatusBadge -> 'synced' -> green flash -> badge removed
  |
  +--> CONFLICT (409)
  |     [11] Server returns its version (someone else edited this contact)
  |     [12] For 'phone': lastWriteWins -> auto-resolve (client wins)
  |     [13] For 'deals' (if changed): userMerge -> show merge UI
  |     [14] Auto-resolved: retry with merged data
  |          User merge needed: badge shows "Conflict -- Tap to resolve"
  |
  +--> PERMANENT FAILURE (404: contact deleted elsewhere)
        [11] moveToDeadLetter()
        [12] Rollback optimistic cache update
        [13] Toast: "Contact deleted by another user. Changes not applied."
        [14] SyncStatusBadge: "Sync failed" with dismiss option
```

#### 6.5 Scenario E: User Sends Chat Message (Offline)

```
User types message, taps Send (offline)
  |
  v
[1] ChatState.inputDraft consumed and cleared
  |
  v
[2] Message object created locally:
  |   ChatMessage(id: UUID().uuidString, role: .user, content: "...",
  |               status: .queued, createdAt: Date())
  |
  v
[3] Optimistic update: message appended to chat history cache
  |
  v
[4] SwiftUI renders message with muted styling + clock icon
  |   Label: "Queued -- will send when online"
  |
  v
[5] Warning banner: "AI responses require connectivity"
  |
  v
[6] MutationQueue.shared.enqueue(MutationRequest(type: .sendChatMessage, ...))
  |   Persisted to mutation_queue via GRDB.
  |
  --- CONNECTIVITY RETURNS ---
  |
  v
[7] SyncCoordinator.processQueue() picks up chat mutation
  |
  v
[8] POST /api/chat { sessionId, content }
  |
  +--> SUCCESS
  |     [9] Message status: .queued -> .sent (styling normalizes)
  |     [10] Invalidate chat history query -> refetch
  |     [11] Refetch returns full history including AI response
  |     [12] AI response appears in chat thread
  |     [13] If user is elsewhere: chat tab badge increments
  |
  +--> FAILURE (session expired, etc.)
        [9] Move to dead letter queue
        [10] Message shows red indicator: "Failed to send -- tap to retry"
```

---

### 7. Real-Time Sync Considerations

#### 7.1 The Two-Frontend Problem

Skye agents may use the web dashboard (Next.js) and the native iOS app simultaneously. Both frontends read from and write to the same Supabase database. Without real-time push synchronization, the two clients can diverge.

**Current state (V2)**: Poll-based synchronization via the data layer's `staleTime` and refetch mechanisms. No WebSocket or SSE channel exists.

**Acceptable for V2**: Real estate CRM data is not latency-sensitive at the sub-minute level. A 5-minute delay before seeing another device's edits is acceptable.

#### 7.2 Current Sync Model

```
Native App ----GET /api/contacts (every 5min)----> Supabase
                                                       ^
Web App -------GET /api/contacts (every 5min)----> ---|

Native App ----PATCH /api/contacts/abc-----------> Supabase
  (Web does not see this until its next poll)
```

#### 7.3 Conflict Detection

When both frontends edit the same entity within a short window:

```
t=0s    Contact 'abc' has updated_at = 1000
t=10s   Native PATCH with updated_at = 1000 (matches) -> SUCCESS
        Server sets updated_at = 1010
t=15s   Web PATCH with updated_at = 1000 (stale!) -> 409 CONFLICT
        Server returns { serverVersion, serverUpdatedAt: 1010 }
```

Client-side resolution uses the field-level strategies from Section 4.5.

#### 7.4 Future-Proofing for Real-Time

The state layer is designed to accept real-time updates without architectural changes:

1. **QueryClient cache as single source of truth**: Any real-time update calls `queryClient.setQueryData()` -- identical to optimistic updates. No view changes needed.

2. **Query key hierarchy supports targeted updates**: A WebSocket event like `{ type: "contact_updated", id: "abc", data: {...} }` maps directly to `queryClient.setQueryData(QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: "abc"), data: data)`.

3. **Invalidation over replacement**: For complex updates, real-time events can invalidate the relevant key, triggering a refetch. Simpler and safer than merging partial updates.

```swift
// Sources/Data/RealtimeHandler.swift (future -- not implemented in V2)
import Foundation

enum RealtimeHandler {
    static func handle(_ event: RealtimeEvent) {
        let queryClient = QueryClient.shared

        switch event.type {
        case .contactUpdated:
            queryClient.setQueryData(
                QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: event.entityId),
                data: event.data
            )
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists())

        case .contactDeleted:
            queryClient.removeQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.detail(id: event.entityId))
            queryClient.invalidateQueries(QueryKeys.Contacts.lists())

        case .chatMessage:
            // Append the new message to the cached chat history.
            if var history: ChatHistory = queryClient.getQueryData(
                QueryKeys.Chat.history(sessionId: event.sessionId)
            ) {
                history.messages.append(event.message)
                queryClient.setQueryData(
                    QueryKeys.Chat.history(sessionId: event.sessionId),
                    data: history
                )
            }
        }
    }
}
```

4. **`@Observable` state objects are event-driven**: Property assignment works identically whether called from user action, fetch callback, or WebSocket handler. SwiftUI automatically re-renders on changes.

5. **Offline queue coexists**: When real-time is added, the `MutationQueue` remains the fallback on disconnect.

#### 7.5 Sync Latency Budget (V2)

| Data Type | Max Staleness (Same Device) | Max Staleness (Cross-Frontend) |
|---|---|---|
| Contacts | 5 min (staleTime) | 5 min (next poll by other client) |
| Properties | 15 min | 15 min |
| Chat messages | 1 min | 1 min |
| Tasks | 3 min | 3 min |
| After push notification | 0 (immediate invalidation) | N/A (push to native only) |
| After offline queue drain | 0 (immediate on native) | Up to staleTime on web |

#### 7.6 Dependency Summary

| Package / Framework | Source | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Observation` framework | iOS 17+ SDK | `@Observable` state management for all UI and server state |
| `GRDB.swift` (groue/GRDB.swift) | SPM | SQLite database for offline cache, mutation queue, and query persistence (WAL mode) |
| `Network` framework (`NWPathMonitor`) | iOS SDK | Network connectivity detection and quality classification |
| `Security` framework (Keychain Services) | iOS SDK | Hardware-backed encrypted storage for auth tokens |
| `Foundation.UUID` | iOS SDK | UUID generation for mutation queue IDs and offline entity creation |
| `Foundation.JSONEncoder` / `JSONDecoder` | iOS SDK | `Codable`-based serialization for all cache persistence and API communication |
| `Foundation.UserDefaults` | iOS SDK | Fast synchronous key-value storage for UI state, chat drafts, notification state |
| `Foundation.FileManager` | iOS SDK | Local file operations for image cache management |
| `URLSession` | iOS SDK | HTTP networking for API requests and health checks |
| SwiftUI `@Environment(\.scenePhase)` | iOS SDK | App lifecycle detection (foreground/background transitions) |

---

### Appendix A: Complete App Entry Point & Environment Setup

```swift
// Sources/SkyeApp.swift
import SwiftUI

@main
struct SkyeApp: App {
    @State private var isReady = false

    init() {
        // Step 1: Restore persisted client state.
        UIState.shared.restore()
        ChatState.shared.restore()
        OfflineState.shared.restore()
        NotificationState.shared.restore()
        AuthState.shared.restoreFromKeychain()
    }

    var body: some Scene {
        WindowGroup {
            Group {
                if isReady {
                    RootView()
                        .environment(NetworkMonitor.shared)
                        .environment(OfflineState.shared)
                        .environment(AuthState.shared)
                        .environment(UIState.shared)
                        .environment(ChatState.shared)
                        .environment(NotificationState.shared)
                        .appForegroundRefresh()
                        .queueTriggers()
                } else {
                    SplashScreen()
                }
            }
            .task {
                await initializeApp()
            }
        }
    }

    private func initializeApp() async {
        // Step 2: Validate authentication.
        let auth = AuthState.shared
        if let token = auth.accessToken {
            // Decode JWT, check expiry, refresh if needed.
            let isValid = await AuthService.validateToken(token)
            if isValid {
                auth.authStatus = .authenticated
            } else if let refreshToken = auth.refreshToken {
                let refreshed = await AuthService.refreshTokens(refreshToken)
                auth.authStatus = refreshed ? .authenticated : .unauthenticated
            } else {
                auth.authStatus = .unauthenticated
            }
        } else {
            auth.authStatus = .unauthenticated
        }

        // Step 3: Initialize NetworkMonitor (already started in init).
        // NWPathMonitor is running; initial quality is set.

        // Step 4: Initialize database (GRDB) -- lazy cache hydration.
        do {
            _ = try await DatabaseManager.shared.initialize()
        } catch {
            // Database init failure is critical; log and handle.
        }

        // Step 5: App Ready.
        isReady = true

        // Step 6: Background cache warming (non-blocking).
        if auth.authStatus == .authenticated && NetworkMonitor.shared.isOnline {
            Task {
                await CacheWarming.warmCache()
            }
            Task {
                await SyncCoordinator.shared.processQueue()
            }
        }
    }
}

struct RootView: View {
    @Environment(AuthState.self) private var authState

    var body: some View {
        ZStack {
            if authState.authStatus == .authenticated {
                MainTabView()
            } else {
                LoginView()
            }

            // Overlay the network banner at the top.
            VStack {
                NetworkBanner()
                Spacer()
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Appendix B: Testing Strategy for Offline Flows

| Test Scenario | Method | Validation |
|---|---|---|
| Cache-first rendering | Unit: pre-populate QueryClient cache, render view | SwiftUI view renders without network fetch |
| Stale data refetch | Unit: set staleTime=0, observe fetch | Background fetch fires, `@Observable` updates trigger re-render |
| Offline mutation queue persistence | Integration: enqueue via GRDB, terminate app, restart, query SQLite | Mutation exists in `mutation_queue` after restart |
| Queue processing order | Integration: enqueue create + edit for same entity | Edit uses server ID from create (via `idRemapping`) |
| Conflict resolution (auto) | Integration: simulate 409 for scalar field | Mutation retried with client value (lastWriteWins) |
| Conflict resolution (manual) | E2E: simulate 409 for complex field | SwiftUI merge view appears with `@Binding` for user resolution |
| Dead letter handling | Integration: simulate 404 | Mutation in `dead_letter_queue` table, error badge shown |
| Online -> offline transition | E2E: disable network in Simulator | Banner appears, queue mode active, cache serves data |
| Offline -> online transition | E2E: re-enable network | Banner hides, `SyncCoordinator.processQueue()` runs, refresh |
| Cache eviction | Integration: fill >50MB via GRDB | LRU eviction runs, protected entities preserved |
| Cold start with persisted cache | E2E: kill app, relaunch offline | Cached data visible immediately from GRDB-persisted cache |

---

This completes the V2 Offline Architecture, State Management, and Data Sync specification for Skye (Swift / iOS Native). Every configuration value, data flow, state transition, and architectural decision has been made explicit with implementation-ready Swift code.

**Architecture summary**:
- **`NetworkMonitor`** (`@Observable`, `NWPathMonitor`) -- connectivity detection and quality classification
- **`CacheManager`** (actor, GRDB-backed) -- per-query SQLite cache persistence with 7-day TTL
- **`MutationQueue`** (actor, GRDB-backed) -- durable FIFO offline mutation queue
- **`SyncCoordinator`** (actor) -- orchestrates queue drain on reconnect with retry, conflict resolution, and dead-letter handling
- **`QueryClient`** (`@Observable`) -- in-memory cache with stale-time checking, invalidation, and prefetch
- **`@Observable` state objects** (`AuthState`, `UIState`, `ChatState`, `OfflineState`, `NotificationState`) -- client/UI state with `Codable` persistence to Keychain / `UserDefaults`

**Key Swift/iOS native references**:
- [GRDB.swift documentation](https://github.com/groue/GRDB.swift)
- [Observation framework (iOS 17+)](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/observation)
- [NWPathMonitor documentation](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/network/nwpathmonitor)
- [Keychain Services](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/security/keychain_services)
- [Swift Concurrency (async/await, actors)](https://docs.swift.org/swift-book/documentation/the-swift-programming-language/concurrency/)
- [SwiftUI @Environment and scenePhase](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swiftui/scenephase)

---

## V2: Camera, OCR, Media & Studio — Swift / iOS Native

---

### 1. Camera System

#### 1.1 Implementation: `AVFoundation` (Native)

**Decision: Use `AVFoundation` directly (`AVCaptureSession`, `AVCapturePhotoOutput`, `AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer`).**

| Criterion | AVFoundation (Native) |
|---|---|
| Real-time frame analysis (Vision framework) | Yes — direct GPU/CPU pipeline, zero bridge overhead |
| Manual controls (ISO, shutter, WB, focus) | Full (`AVCaptureDevice` API) |
| Real-time card-edge detection potential | Yes (via `VNDetectRectanglesRequest` on live `CMSampleBuffer`) |
| Capture latency | Lowest possible — native GPU-backed pipeline with no interprocess bridge |
| Vision framework integration | Direct — `VNRecognizeTextRequest` on `CIImage` or `CVPixelBuffer` |
| Setup complexity | Moderate (AVFoundation boilerplate) |
| Maintenance | Apple first-party framework, stable across iOS releases |

**Justification:** Skye's business-card-scanning feature is a core differentiator. The camera is not a casual selfie feature — it must feel instantaneous and professional. Native `AVFoundation` provides:
- **Direct Vision framework access** for future auto-edge-detection of business cards (detect card boundaries in real time via `VNDetectRectanglesRequest`, trigger auto-capture).
- **Lowest possible capture latency** because the entire camera pipeline is native — no bridge layer, no serialization, no thread hops.
- **Full sensor control** via `AVCaptureDevice` for challenging lighting conditions (dim conference halls, fluorescent-lit offices).
- Native Swift has direct access to Vision framework — no bridge layer, better performance, lower latency for real-time text detection compared to any cross-platform solution.

#### 1.2 Permission Flow

All permission logic is encapsulated in a `CameraAccessManager` observable class that manages the full flow using `AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .video)`.

##### 1.2.1 Permission States

| State | Detection | UX |
|---|---|---|
| `.notDetermined` | First launch, permission never requested | Show Pre-Permission Screen |
| `.authorized` | User previously approved | Open camera immediately |
| `.denied` | User previously tapped "Don't Allow" | Show Denied Explanation Screen |
| `.restricted` | MDM / parental controls / enterprise policy | Show Restricted Screen |

##### 1.2.2 Pre-Permission Screen (state: `.notDetermined`)

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Business Card Illustration]             |
|        (Lottie animation: card scanning)        |
|                                                 |
|     "Scan Business Cards Instantly"             |
|                                                 |
|  Skye uses your camera to read business         |
|  cards and create contacts in seconds.          |
|  No photos are saved to your device.            |
|                                                 |
|     [ Enable Camera Access ]  (primary btn)     |
|     [ Not Now ]               (text link)       |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **Illustration:** Lottie animation showing a business card being scanned with a subtle sweep effect, 3 seconds looped, 120x120pt. Rendered via `LottieView` from `lottie-ios` SPM package.
- **Title:** "Scan Business Cards Instantly" — SF Pro Display Semibold, 22pt, `Color.textPrimary`.
- **Body:** SF Pro Text Regular, 15pt, `Color.textSecondary`. Two sentences maximum. The privacy line ("No photos are saved") is intentional — agents handle sensitive contact info.
- **"Enable Camera Access" button:** Full-width, 50pt height, `Color.brandPrimary` background, white text, 12pt corner radius. On tap: calls `AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .video)` which triggers the iOS system alert.
- **"Not Now" link:** Below the button, `Color.textTertiary`, 14pt. Dismisses the screen and returns to the previous context. Does NOT trigger the system prompt.
- **Transition:** If permission granted after system prompt, immediately animate-transition to camera viewfinder (matched geometry effect on the card illustration if feasible, otherwise a 250ms crossfade via `.transition(.opacity.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.25)))`).

##### 1.2.3 Permission Granted Flow

```
User taps "Scan Card" anywhere in app
  -> Check permission status
  -> Status is .authorized
  -> Present camera viewfinder (full-screen cover, 300ms slide-up)
```

No intermediate screen. Camera opens immediately. Time from tap to live viewfinder must be under 500ms (measured).

##### 1.2.4 Permission Denied Screen (state: `.denied`)

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Camera icon, crossed out]               |
|        (SF Symbol: camera.slash)                |
|                                                 |
|     "Camera Access Required"                    |
|                                                 |
|  To scan business cards, Skye needs access      |
|  to your camera. You can enable this in         |
|  your device settings.                          |
|                                                 |
|     [ Open Settings ]         (primary btn)     |
|     [ Enter Manually ]        (secondary btn)   |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **"Open Settings" button:** Calls `UIApplication.shared.open(URL(string: UIApplication.openSettingsURLString)!)` which deep-links directly to Skye's entry in iOS Settings. Full-width primary button.
- **"Enter Manually" button:** Secondary style (outlined), navigates to blank contact creation form so the user is never blocked from adding a contact.
- **Return handling:** When the user returns from Settings, the app re-checks permission status via `NotificationCenter.default` observation of `UIApplication.willEnterForegroundNotification`. If now authorized, automatically transition to camera viewfinder. If still denied, remain on this screen.

##### 1.2.5 Permission Restricted Screen (state: `.restricted`)

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Lock icon]                              |
|        (SF Symbol: lock.shield)                 |
|                                                 |
|     "Camera Access Restricted"                  |
|                                                 |
|  Camera access is restricted on this device.    |
|  This is usually due to parental controls or    |
|  a device management profile.                   |
|                                                 |
|     [ Enter Contact Manually ]  (primary btn)   |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **No "Open Settings" button.** Restricted state cannot be changed by the user in Settings; showing the button would be misleading.
- **Single action:** "Enter Contact Manually" — navigates to blank contact creation form.

##### 1.2.6 Permission Manager API

```swift
// CameraAccessManager.swift

import AVFoundation
import SwiftUI

enum CameraAccessState {
    case loading
    case notDetermined
    case authorized
    case denied
    case restricted
}

@Observable
final class CameraAccessManager {
    private(set) var status: CameraAccessState = .loading

    init() {
        checkStatus()
        observeForeground()
    }

    func checkStatus() {
        switch AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .video) {
        case .notDetermined: status = .notDetermined
        case .authorized:    status = .authorized
        case .denied:        status = .denied
        case .restricted:    status = .restricted
        @unknown default:    status = .denied
        }
    }

    func requestPermission() async -> Bool {
        let granted = await AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .video)
        await MainActor.run { checkStatus() }
        return granted
    }

    func openSettings() {
        guard let url = URL(string: UIApplication.openSettingsURLString) else { return }
        UIApplication.shared.open(url)
    }

    private func observeForeground() {
        NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
            forName: UIApplication.willEnterForegroundNotification,
            object: nil,
            queue: .main
        ) { [weak self] _ in
            self?.checkStatus()
        }
    }
}
```

- On init: reads `AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .video)` (synchronous).
- `requestPermission()`: calls `AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .video)`, updates state.
- `openSettings()`: calls `UIApplication.shared.open(URL(string: UIApplication.openSettingsURLString)!)`.
- Observes `UIApplication.willEnterForegroundNotification` to re-check permission when app returns from background.

#### 1.3 Camera UI

The camera screen is presented as a **full-screen cover** (no navigation bar, no status bar — `.statusBarHidden()` modifier while camera is active, or `prefersStatusBarHidden` override on the hosting `UIViewController`).

##### 1.3.1 Viewfinder Layout

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
| [X Close]                    [Flash: Auto]     |
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
|    +-----------------------------------+        |
|    |                                   |        |
|    |    (Live camera preview)          |        |
|    |    Business card frame overlay    |        |
|    |    Aspect ratio 3.5:2             |        |
|    |                                   |        |
|    +-----------------------------------+        |
|                                                 |
|    "Position business card within frame"        |
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
|              [ Capture ]                        |
|                                                 |
|         [ Choose from Library ]                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

##### 1.3.2 Component Specifications

**Camera Preview:**
- `AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer` hosted in a `UIViewRepresentable` wrapper, back camera device, session running.
- `AVCapturePhotoOutput` added to the session to enable photo capture.
- `videoGravity = .resizeAspectFill` for full-screen preview.
- `outputOrientation` configured via `AVCaptureConnection.videoRotationAngle` to ensure correct EXIF orientation regardless of device rotation.
- Zoom: default 1x, pinch-to-zoom via `UIPinchGestureRecognizer` controlling `AVCaptureDevice.videoZoomFactor` (min 1x, max 3x), smooth ramp using `ramp(toVideoZoomFactor:withRate:)`.

**Overlay Frame (Business Card Guide):**
- Semi-transparent black overlay (`Color.black.opacity(0.55)`) covering the entire screen EXCEPT the card cutout region, achieved via a `Path` with an `.eoFill` rule in a SwiftUI `Canvas` or `Shape`.
- Card cutout: centered horizontally, positioned at 35% from top of screen.
- Aspect ratio: **3.5:2** (standard US business card, 3.5 inches x 2 inches).
- Width: 85% of screen width. Height: calculated from aspect ratio.
- Corner brackets: white, 2pt stroke, 20pt length on each corner edge, 8pt corner radius. Animated subtle pulse (opacity 0.7 to 1.0, 2s cycle, `.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 2).repeatForever(autoreverses: true))`).
- When card is detected (future auto-detect): corner brackets turn `Color.brandPrimary` (green/blue) and guidance text changes.

**Guidance Text:**
- "Position business card within the frame"
- SF Pro Text Regular, 14pt, white, shadow via `.shadow(color: .black.opacity(0.5), radius: 4)`.
- Centered horizontally, positioned 16pt below the card frame overlay.
- **Auto-capture hint (future enhancement):** When Vision framework `VNDetectRectanglesRequest` detects a card within the guide region, text changes to "Hold steady..." with a subtle 1.5s progress ring around the capture button. If the card remains stable for 1.5 seconds, auto-capture fires. This feature is gated behind a feature flag (`FeatureFlag.autoCapture`) and is OFF in initial V2 release.

**Capture Button:**
- Position: center-bottom, 40pt from bottom safe area.
- Outer ring: 70pt diameter, white, 4pt stroke, no fill (transparent center).
- Inner circle: 58pt diameter, white fill.
- **Press state:** On touch-down, inner circle scales to 0.9x (100ms, `.easeOut`). On touch-up, scales back to 1.0x (100ms). Implemented via `.scaleEffect` with `@State` toggle and `.animation(.easeOut(duration: 0.1))`.
- **Haptic feedback:** `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator(style: .medium).impactOccurred()` on capture.
- **Capture animation:** On capture, the entire screen flashes white (opacity 0 to 0.4 to 0, 200ms total) to simulate a shutter effect. The overlay briefly freezes the last frame.
- **Disabled state:** While a capture is in progress (typically <100ms), button is disabled and inner circle shows a brief loading spinner (`ProgressView()`). Prevents double-tap captures.

**Flash Toggle:**
- Position: top-right, 16pt from top safe area, 16pt from right edge.
- States: `auto` (default), `on`, `off`.
- Icons: SF Symbols — `bolt.badge.a` (auto), `bolt.fill` (on), `bolt.slash` (off).
- Size: 28pt icon within 44pt tap target (minimum iOS tap target).
- Color: white with shadow.
- Tap cycles through: auto -> on -> off -> auto.
- Current state label: small text below icon ("Auto", "On", "Off"), 10pt, white, fades in/out on change via `.transition(.opacity)`.
- Persisted: flash preference saved in `UserDefaults` (`camera.flash_mode`) via `@AppStorage("camera.flash_mode")`, restored on next camera open.

**Close Button:**
- Position: top-left, 16pt from top safe area, 16pt from left edge.
- Icon: SF Symbol `xmark`, 18pt, white, within a 36pt circular background (`Color.black.opacity(0.3)`).
- Tap target: 44pt (via `.frame(width: 44, height: 44)` with `.contentShape(Rectangle())`).
- Action: dismisses camera full-screen cover with 300ms slide-down animation via `dismiss()` environment action.
- **Confirmation:** No confirmation dialog needed — no data is lost by closing the camera.

**"Choose from Library" Link:**
- Position: centered, 16pt below the capture button.
- Text: "Choose from Library", SF Pro Text Regular, 14pt, white, underlined.
- Tap target: full text width + 16pt horizontal padding, 44pt height.
- Action: dismisses camera, presents `PHPickerViewController` (see Section 3).

#### 1.4 Photo Capture Pipeline

```
User taps Capture
  -> Haptic fires (immediate, before capture completes)
  -> let settings = AVCapturePhotoSettings()
     settings.flashMode = currentFlashMode  // .auto, .on, .off
     photoOutput.capturePhoto(with: settings, delegate: self)
  -> Photo returned via AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate as AVCapturePhoto
  -> let imageData = photo.fileDataRepresentation()
  -> Shutter flash animation plays
  -> Resize image (see 1.4.1)
  -> Navigate to Preview screen (see 2.1)
  -> Camera session remains configured but stopRunning() (instant restart on "Retake")
```

##### 1.4.1 Image Processing (Pre-Upload)

All processing occurs in-memory. No files are written to the camera roll or persistent disk.

| Step | Detail |
|---|---|
| **1. Read captured data** | `AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate` delivers `AVCapturePhoto`. Call `photo.fileDataRepresentation()` to get raw JPEG/HEIF data. |
| **2. Correct orientation** | Create a `CIImage` from the data. Use `CIImage.oriented()` with the correct `CGImagePropertyOrientation` to physically rotate pixels to match EXIF orientation and strip the EXIF orientation tag. This guarantees the server receives a correctly-oriented image regardless of EXIF support. |
| **3. Resize** | Resize so the longest edge is **1920px**. If the captured image's longest edge is already <=1920px, skip resize. Use `CILanczosScaleTransform` filter via `CIFilter` for quality, or `UIGraphicsImageRenderer` with `.draw(in:)` for Lanczos-equivalent resampling. |
| **4. Compress** | Re-encode as **JPEG at 85% quality** via `UIImage.jpegData(compressionQuality: 0.85)`. This balances OCR accuracy (needs sharp text) against upload size (typically 300-600KB after resize). |
| **5. Validate** | Assert file size < 5MB. If somehow larger, re-compress at 75% quality. If still larger, re-compress at 65%. Log a warning if this fallback is needed. |
| **6. Generate metadata** | Create a struct: `ProcessedImage(data: Data, width: Int, height: Int, fileSize: Int, mimeType: "image/jpeg")`. |

```swift
// ImageProcessor.swift

import CoreImage
import UIKit

struct ProcessedImage {
    let data: Data
    let width: Int
    let height: Int
    let fileSize: Int
    let mimeType: String  // "image/jpeg"
    let tempFileURL: URL
}

final class ImageProcessor {
    private let context = CIContext(options: [.useSoftwareRenderer: false])
    private let maxLongEdge: CGFloat = 1920
    private let maxFileSize = 5 * 1024 * 1024  // 5MB

    func process(photo: AVCapturePhoto) throws -> ProcessedImage {
        guard let data = photo.fileDataRepresentation(),
              var ciImage = CIImage(data: data) else {
            throw ImageProcessingError.invalidData
        }

        // Step 2: Correct orientation
        let orientation = CGImagePropertyOrientation(
            rawValue: UInt32(photo.metadata[kCGImagePropertyOrientation as String] as? Int ?? 1)
        ) ?? .up
        ciImage = ciImage.oriented(orientation)

        // Step 3: Resize if needed
        let longestEdge = max(ciImage.extent.width, ciImage.extent.height)
        if longestEdge > maxLongEdge {
            let scale = maxLongEdge / longestEdge
            ciImage = ciImage.transformed(by: CGAffineTransform(scaleX: scale, y: scale))
        }

        // Step 4: Convert to UIImage and compress
        guard let cgImage = context.createCGImage(ciImage, from: ciImage.extent) else {
            throw ImageProcessingError.renderFailed
        }
        let uiImage = UIImage(cgImage: cgImage)

        // Try compression at decreasing quality levels
        var jpegData: Data?
        for quality in [0.85, 0.75, 0.65] as [CGFloat] {
            guard let compressed = uiImage.jpegData(compressionQuality: quality) else { continue }
            if compressed.count <= maxFileSize {
                jpegData = compressed
                break
            }
            if quality < 0.85 {
                // Log warning that fallback compression was needed
                print("[ImageProcessor] Warning: Fallback compression at \(quality) quality")
            }
        }

        guard let finalData = jpegData else {
            throw ImageProcessingError.compressionFailed
        }

        // Step 6: Write to temp file and generate metadata
        let tempURL = FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory
            .appendingPathComponent(UUID().uuidString)
            .appendingPathExtension("jpg")
        try finalData.write(to: tempURL)

        return ProcessedImage(
            data: finalData,
            width: Int(ciImage.extent.width),
            height: Int(ciImage.extent.height),
            fileSize: finalData.count,
            mimeType: "image/jpeg",
            tempFileURL: tempURL
        )
    }
}
```

**Temporary file lifecycle:**
- The processed JPEG exists only in the app's temporary directory (`FileManager.default.temporaryDirectory`, backed by `NSTemporaryDirectory()` on iOS).
- It is explicitly deleted (`try? FileManager.default.removeItem(at: tempFileURL)`) after OCR upload completes (success or final failure).
- It is also deleted if the user navigates away from the OCR flow (retake, close, back).
- **Never** call `PHPhotoLibrary.shared().performChanges { PHAssetChangeRequest.creationRequestForAsset(from:) }`. The image must never reach the user's photo library (privacy compliance for business contacts).

##### 1.4.2 Capture Error Handling

| Error | Detection | UX |
|---|---|---|
| Camera hardware failure | `AVCapturePhotoCaptureDelegate.didFinishProcessingPhoto` delivers error | Toast: "Couldn't take photo. Please try again." Camera remains open for retry. |
| Out of disk space | `Data.write(to:)` throws with `NSFileWriteOutOfSpaceError` | Alert: "Not enough storage space. Free up space and try again." Dismiss camera. |
| Processing failure (resize/compress) | `ImageProcessor.process()` throws | Toast: "Photo processing failed. Please try again." Return to viewfinder. |

---

### 2. OCR Pipeline

#### 2.1 Complete Flow Diagram

```
[Camera Viewfinder]
        |
        v (capture)
[Preview Screen] ---- "Retake" -----> [Camera Viewfinder]
        |
        v ("Use Photo")
[Upload + Processing] ---- timeout/error -----> [Error State]
        |                                            |
        v (success)                                  v
[Review Screen] ---- "Try Again" -----> [Camera Viewfinder]
        |           "Enter Manually" -> [Blank Contact Form]
        |
        v ("Looks Good")
[Creating Contact...] ---- error -----> [Creation Error]
        |
        v (success)
[Success Screen] ---- "View Contact" -----> [Contact Detail]
                 ---- "Scan Another" -----> [Camera Viewfinder]
                 ---- auto-dismiss (3s) --> [Previous Screen]
```

#### 2.2 Preview Screen

Presented immediately after photo capture, before any network request.

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
| [<- Back]              Preview                  |
|                                                 |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
| |                                           |  |
| |                                           |  |
| |      [Captured business card image]       |  |
| |      (full-width, aspect-fit)             |  |
| |                                           |  |
| |                                           |  |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
|   [ Retake ]              [ Use Photo ]         |
|   (secondary)             (primary)             |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

**Image display:**
- Full width of screen minus 24pt horizontal padding.
- Aspect-fit within a container with max-height of 60% of screen. Use SwiftUI `Image` with `.resizable()` and `.scaledToFit()` inside a frame with `.frame(maxHeight: UIScreen.main.bounds.height * 0.6)`.
- Subtle rounded corners (8pt) via `.clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: 8))` and 1pt border in `Color.borderSecondary`.
- **Perspective correction (enhancement):** If Vision framework `VNDetectRectanglesRequest` is available, apply lightweight perspective correction using corner detection and `CIPerspectiveCorrection` CIFilter. This is a V2.1 enhancement and is NOT required for V2 launch. For V2, the image is shown as-captured.

**"Retake" button:**
- Left-aligned in a bottom button bar.
- Secondary style: outlined, `Color.textPrimary` text, `Color.borderPrimary` border.
- Action: navigates back to camera viewfinder. Camera re-activates (`session.startRunning()`). The temp file from the previous capture is deleted.

**"Use Photo" button:**
- Right-aligned in the bottom button bar.
- Primary style: `Color.brandPrimary` background, white text.
- Action: initiates upload to OCR endpoint. Transitions to Upload/Processing state.

**"Back" button (top-left):**
- Navigates back to camera viewfinder (same as "Retake").
- If the user has not yet tapped "Use Photo", no confirmation needed.

#### 2.3 Upload + Processing State

This is a **single screen** with two visual phases: uploading and processing.

##### 2.3.1 Upload Phase

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
|        [Small card image thumbnail]             |
|        (80x50pt, rounded corners)               |
|                                                 |
|        "Uploading..."                           |
|                                                 |
|   [progress bar]  45%                           |
|                                                 |
|        [ Cancel ]                               |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

**Upload request:**
```swift
// Models/OCRModels.swift

struct OCRUploadResponse: Codable {
    let success: Bool
    let data: OCRData?
    let error: OCRError?
}

struct OCRData: Codable {
    var name: String?
    var firstName: String?
    var lastName: String?
    var title: String?
    var company: String?
    var phones: [OCRPhone]?
    var emails: [OCREmail]?
    var address: OCRAddress?
    var website: String?
    var rawText: String?           // Full OCR text for debugging
    var confidenceOverall: Double  // 0.0 to 1.0

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case name
        case firstName = "first_name"
        case lastName = "last_name"
        case title, company, phones, emails, address, website
        case rawText = "raw_text"
        case confidenceOverall = "confidence_overall"
    }
}

struct OCRPhone: Codable {
    let type: String
    let number: String
    let confidence: Double
}

struct OCREmail: Codable {
    let address: String
    let confidence: Double
}

struct OCRAddress: Codable {
    var street: String?
    var city: String?
    var state: String?
    var zip: String?
    let confidence: Double
}

struct OCRError: Codable {
    let code: String
    let message: String
}
```

**Upload mechanics:**
- Method: `POST` to `/api/contacts/ocr`.
- Content-Type: `multipart/form-data`.
- Body: single field `image` containing the JPEG file.
- Authorization: Bearer token in `Authorization` header (same as all API calls).
- **Progress tracking:** Use `URLSessionUploadTask` with a `URLSessionTaskDelegate` conforming object to receive `urlSession(_:task:didSendBodyData:totalBytesSent:totalBytesExpectedToSend:)` callbacks for real-time byte-level progress. Do NOT use a simple `URLSession.shared.data(for:)` call (no upload progress support).
- **Progress bar:** Horizontal bar, full width minus 48pt padding. Height 4pt, rounded ends. Background: `Color.bgTertiary`. Fill: `Color.brandPrimary`. Percentage text: right-aligned, 13pt, `Color.textSecondary`.
- **Cancel button:** Text button, `Color.textSecondary`, centered below progress bar. Calls `uploadTask.cancel()`. On cancel: delete temp file, return to Preview screen. No confirmation dialog.

##### 2.3.2 Processing Phase

Begins when upload completes (progress reaches 100%) and server is performing OCR.

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
|        [Skye logo animation]                    |
|        (Lottie, 80x80pt, pulsing/thinking)      |
|                                                 |
|        "Reading business card..."               |
|                                                 |
|        [Subtle shimmer animation]               |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **Skye logo animation:** Lottie animation of the Skye logo with a subtle thinking/processing motion (e.g., gentle orbit, pulse, or sparkle effect). 80x80pt, looping. Asset: `lottie/skye-thinking.json`. Rendered via `LottieView` from `lottie-ios` SPM package with `.loopMode(.loop)`.
- **Text:** "Reading business card..." — SF Pro Text Regular, 16pt, `Color.textSecondary`. Ellipsis animates (dots appear one at a time, 500ms interval, looping) via a `Timer.publish` or `TimelineView`.
- **No cancel button during processing** — the upload is already complete; we are waiting for the server response. The user can still swipe down to dismiss, which would abandon the result and return to camera.

##### 2.3.3 Timeout Handling

| Condition | Threshold | UX |
|---|---|---|
| Upload taking too long | 60 seconds total upload time | Show "Upload is taking longer than expected. Check your connection." with "Cancel" and "Keep Waiting" buttons. |
| Server processing timeout | 30 seconds after upload completes with no response | Show "This is taking longer than expected" screen (see below). |
| Overall timeout | 90 seconds from "Use Photo" tap to response | Force timeout, show error state. |

**Processing Timeout Screen:**

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Hourglass icon]                         |
|                                                 |
|     "This is taking too long"                   |
|                                                 |
|  The server is taking longer than expected       |
|  to read this card.                             |
|                                                 |
|     [ Try Again ]            (primary)          |
|     [ Enter Manually ]       (secondary)        |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- "Try Again": returns to camera viewfinder.
- "Enter Manually": navigates to blank contact creation form.

#### 2.4 Review Screen (Critical UX Moment)

This is the most important screen in the OCR flow. The user must be able to quickly verify and correct OCR results.

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
| [<- Back]          Review Contact        [card] |
|                                                 |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
| | [Thumbnail of business card]   40x25pt    |  |
| | Tap to enlarge                             |  |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                 |
| First Name *                                    |
| [ John                                    ] ok  |
|                                                 |
| Last Name *                                     |
| [ Smith                                   ] ok  |
|                                                 |
| Title                                           |
| [ Senior VP, Commercial Lending           ] ok  |
|                                                 |
| Company                                         |
| [ Westfield Realty Group                  ] ok  |
|                                                 |
| Phone                                           |
| [ (555) 123-4567                          ] !   |
| [ + Add phone number ]                          |
|                                                 |
| Email *                                         |
| [ john.smlth@westfield.com               ] !   |
| [ + Add email ]                                 |
|                                                 |
| Address                                         |
| [ 123 Main St, Suite 400                 ]      |
| [ Anytown, CA 90210                      ]      |
|                                                 |
| Website                                         |
| [ www.westfieldrealty.com                 ] ok  |
|                                                 |
|                                                 |
| [ Enter Manually ]  [ Try Again ]  [Looks Good] |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

##### 2.4.1 Field Layout and Behavior

**Field structure:**
Each field is an editable `TextField` with:
- Label above (SF Pro Text Medium, 13pt, `Color.textSecondary`, uppercase via `.textCase(.uppercase)`).
- Input field (SF Pro Text Regular, 16pt, `Color.textPrimary`, standard text input styling).
- Confidence indicator on the right side of the input via `.overlay(alignment: .trailing)`.

**Confidence indicators:**

| Confidence Level | Threshold | Visual | Behavior |
|---|---|---|---|
| High | >= 0.85 | Small green checkmark icon (SF Symbol: `checkmark.circle.fill`, 16pt, `Color.statusSuccess`) | Field appears normal. |
| Medium | 0.60 - 0.84 | Yellow warning icon (SF Symbol: `exclamationmark.triangle.fill`, 16pt, `Color.statusWarning`) + yellow left border (3pt) on the input | "Please verify" hint text below input in yellow, 12pt. Field is subtly highlighted to draw attention. |
| Low | < 0.60 | Red warning icon (SF Symbol: `exclamationmark.circle.fill`, 16pt, `Color.statusError`) + red left border (3pt) | "Low confidence — please check" hint text below input in red, 12pt. Field has red-tinted background (`Color.red.opacity(0.03)`). |

**Field ordering:** Fields are displayed in this fixed order: First Name, Last Name, Title, Company, Phone(s), Email(s), Address (street, city/state/zip on separate lines), Website. Empty fields that OCR did not detect are still shown (empty input) so the user can fill them in.

**Phone and Email arrays:**
- OCR may return multiple phones and emails.
- Each is displayed as a separate input row.
- "Remove" button (red minus icon, `SF Symbol: minus.circle.fill`) to the left of each row (except if only one remains).
- "+ Add phone number" / "+ Add email" link below the last entry. Tapping adds a blank input row with focus via `@FocusState`.
- Phone inputs use `.keyboardType(.phonePad)`.
- Email inputs use `.keyboardType(.emailAddress)` and `.textInputAutocapitalization(.never)`.

**Required fields:** First Name and Email are marked with `*` and must be non-empty to enable "Looks Good". If both are empty, "Looks Good" is disabled (grayed out, opacity 0.4) with a subtle hint: "At least a name or email is required."

##### 2.4.2 Business Card Thumbnail

- Position: top of the screen, below the navigation bar.
- Size: full width, max height 80pt, aspect-fit.
- Rounded corners: 6pt. Border: 1pt `Color.borderSecondary`.
- **Tap to enlarge:** On tap, opens a full-screen image viewer (`.fullScreenCover`) with `MagnifyGesture` for pinch-to-zoom and `DragGesture` for pan. Dismiss by tapping "X" or swiping down. This allows the user to zoom into the card to verify a hard-to-read phone number or email character.
- **Caption:** "Tap to view original" — 11pt, `Color.textTertiary`, centered below thumbnail.

##### 2.4.3 Action Buttons

Bottom action bar, pinned above keyboard (if keyboard is visible) or pinned to bottom safe area. Use a `VStack` with `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` or a toolbar with `.toolbar { ToolbarItemGroup(placement: .keyboard) { } }`.

**"Looks Good" button (primary):**
- Right-aligned. Primary style. SF Pro Text Semibold, 16pt.
- Action: Creates the contact via `POST /api/crm/contacts` with the reviewed/edited field data.
- Loading state: button text changes to `ProgressView()` + "Creating..." while API call is in flight.
- Disabled if: (a) no first name AND no email are filled, or (b) API call is in flight.

**"Try Again" button (secondary):**
- Center position. Outlined style.
- Action: discards OCR results, returns to camera viewfinder. Temp image is deleted.

**"Enter Manually" button (tertiary):**
- Left-aligned. Text-only style, `Color.textSecondary`.
- Action: clears all OCR-populated fields, transitions to the standard blank contact creation form (same form used from the Contacts tab "+ Add Contact" flow). The business card image is discarded.

##### 2.4.4 Keyboard Handling

- Screen content is inside a `ScrollViewReader` within a `ScrollView`. SwiftUI on iOS 15+ automatically adjusts scroll content for keyboard avoidance.
- When a field is focused, the scroll view auto-scrolls to keep the focused field visible above the keyboard via `ScrollViewReader.scrollTo(id:anchor:)`.
- "Looks Good" button row floats above the keyboard via `.safeAreaInset(edge: .bottom)` or keyboard-attached toolbar.
- Dismiss keyboard by tapping outside inputs (`.onTapGesture { UIApplication.shared.sendAction(#selector(UIResponder.resignFirstResponder), to: nil, from: nil, for: nil) }`) or scrolling.

#### 2.5 Contact Creation (from Review Screen)

```swift
// Models/CreateContactFromOCR.swift

struct CreateContactFromOCR: Codable {
    let firstName: String
    var lastName: String?
    var title: String?
    var company: String?
    var phones: [ContactPhone]
    var emails: [ContactEmail]
    var address: ContactAddress?
    var website: String?
    let source: String  // Always "business_card_scan" for OCR-created contacts
    var metadata: OCRMetadata?

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case firstName = "first_name"
        case lastName = "last_name"
        case title, company, phones, emails, address, website, source, metadata
    }
}

struct ContactPhone: Codable {
    let number: String
    let type: PhoneType  // .mobile, .work, .home, .other

    enum PhoneType: String, Codable {
        case mobile, work, home, other
    }
}

struct ContactEmail: Codable {
    let address: String
    let type: EmailType  // .work, .personal, .other

    enum EmailType: String, Codable {
        case work, personal, other
    }
}

struct ContactAddress: Codable {
    var street: String?
    var city: String?
    var state: String?
    var zip: String?
}

struct OCRMetadata: Codable {
    let ocrConfidence: Double
    let scanTimestamp: String  // ISO 8601

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case ocrConfidence = "ocr_confidence"
        case scanTimestamp = "scan_timestamp"
    }
}
```

- **Source tracking:** `source: "business_card_scan"` allows analytics to track how many contacts came from scanning vs. manual entry vs. import.
- **Duplicate detection:** The API should return a `409 Conflict` if a contact with the same email or phone already exists. The client handles this in the error states below.

#### 2.6 Success State

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Checkmark animation]                    |
|        (Lottie: success-check.json, 80x80pt)    |
|                                                 |
|     "Contact Created!"                          |
|                                                 |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
| | [JD]  John Smith                           |  |
| |       Senior VP, Westfield Realty Group    |  |
| |       john.smith@westfield.com             |  |
| |       (555) 123-4567                       |  |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                 |
|     [ View Contact ]          (primary)         |
|     [ Scan Another Card ]     (secondary)       |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **Checkmark animation:** Lottie animation via `lottie-ios` SPM, plays once (`.loopMode(.playOnce)`), 1 second duration. Combined with `UINotificationFeedbackGenerator().notificationOccurred(.success)`.
- **Contact card preview:** Compact card showing avatar (initials-based, see Section 4.2), name, title + company, primary email, primary phone. Same component as used in the contact list. Tappable (same as "View Contact").
- **"View Contact":** Navigates to the full Contact Detail screen for the newly created contact. Dismisses the entire OCR modal stack.
- **"Scan Another Card":** Returns to camera viewfinder. Resets entire OCR state.
- **Auto-dismiss:** If the user takes no action for 5 seconds, gently pulse the "View Contact" button to draw attention via `.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.8).repeatForever(autoreverses: true))` on opacity. No auto-navigation — the user must explicitly choose.

#### 2.7 Error States

##### 2.7.1 OCR Returns No Data

Condition: API returns `success: true` but all fields are empty or `confidenceOverall < 0.15`.

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Document icon with question mark]       |
|                                                 |
|     "Couldn't Read This Card"                   |
|                                                 |
|  We weren't able to extract contact details     |
|  from this image. Tips:                         |
|  - Make sure the card is well-lit               |
|  - Avoid glare and shadows                      |
|  - Hold the camera steady                       |
|  - Try a flat surface                           |
|                                                 |
|     [ Try Again ]             (primary)         |
|     [ Enter Manually ]        (secondary)       |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

##### 2.7.2 Network Error During Upload

Condition: `URLSessionUploadTask` completion handler delivers error with `URLError` code, or HTTP status 0 (no connection).

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Wi-Fi icon with exclamation]            |
|                                                 |
|     "Upload Failed"                             |
|                                                 |
|  Check your internet connection and try again.  |
|                                                 |
|     [ Retry Upload ]         (primary)          |
|     [ Enter Manually ]       (secondary)        |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **Retry Upload:** Re-attempts the upload with the same processed image (still in temp cache). Does NOT require re-capture.
- **Automatic retry:** Before showing this screen, the system automatically retries once with a 2-second delay. Only if the automatic retry also fails does the error screen appear.
- **Temporary image preservation:** If the user navigates away ("Enter Manually") and then returns, the image is gone. But while on this error screen, the image is preserved for retry.

##### 2.7.3 Server Error (HTTP 500)

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Server icon with exclamation]           |
|                                                 |
|     "Something Went Wrong"                      |
|                                                 |
|  Our servers are having trouble processing      |
|  this image. Please try again in a moment.      |
|                                                 |
|     [ Try Again ]             (primary)         |
|     [ Enter Manually ]        (secondary)       |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- "Try Again" returns to camera (re-capture, since server-side issues may be image-specific).

##### 2.7.4 Duplicate Contact Detected (HTTP 409)

```
+-----------------------------------------------+
|                                                 |
|        [Person icon with badge]                 |
|                                                 |
|     "Contact May Already Exist"                 |
|                                                 |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
| | [JS]  John Smith                           |  |
| |       Westfield Realty Group               |  |
| |       john.smith@westfield.com             |  |
| +-------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                 |
|  A contact with this email or phone already     |
|  exists. What would you like to do?             |
|                                                 |
|     [ View Existing Contact ] (primary)         |
|     [ Create Anyway ]         (secondary)       |
|     [ Merge Contacts ]        (tertiary)        |
|                                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------+
```

- **"View Existing Contact":** navigates to the existing contact's detail screen.
- **"Create Anyway":** sends `POST /api/crm/contacts` with `force: true` to bypass duplicate check.
- **"Merge Contacts":** sends `POST /api/crm/contacts/merge` with existing contact ID + new OCR data. Server merges fields intelligently (new data fills in blanks, does not overwrite existing).

---

### 3. Photo Library Access

#### 3.1 Entry Point

The "Choose from Library" link on the Camera Viewfinder screen (Section 1.3.1) triggers the photo library flow.

#### 3.2 Implementation: `PHPickerViewController` (PhotosUI Framework)

Use `PHPickerViewController` (iOS 14+) for photo library access. It wraps Apple's `PHPicker`, which:
- Runs in a separate process (the user sees all their photos regardless of "limited access" setting).
- Returns only the selected photo(s) to the app.
- Does NOT require `NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription` permission for picking (the system picker is privacy-preserving by design).
- Provides a native, familiar UI that users already know.

**Note on iOS 17+ Limited Photo Access:** Apple's `PHPicker` intentionally shows all photos to the user even when "limited access" is granted. This is by design: the picker runs in a separate process and only returns selected items. No custom picker is needed. The `NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription` key is still included in `Info.plist` as a fallback for edge cases, with the message: "Skye uses your photo library to select business card images for scanning."

#### 3.3 Configuration

```swift
// PhotoLibraryPicker.swift

import PhotosUI
import SwiftUI

struct PhotoLibraryPicker: UIViewControllerRepresentable {
    let onImageSelected: (UIImage, Data) -> Void
    let onCancelled: () -> Void

    func makeUIViewController(context: Context) -> PHPickerViewController {
        var config = PHPickerConfiguration()
        config.filter = .images           // Photos only, no video
        config.selectionLimit = 1         // Single image only
        config.preferredAssetRepresentationMode = .compatible  // Auto JPEG conversion

        let picker = PHPickerViewController(configuration: config)
        picker.delegate = context.coordinator
        return picker
    }

    func updateUIViewController(_ uiViewController: PHPickerViewController, context: Context) {}

    func makeCoordinator() -> Coordinator {
        Coordinator(onImageSelected: onImageSelected, onCancelled: onCancelled)
    }

    final class Coordinator: NSObject, PHPickerViewControllerDelegate {
        let onImageSelected: (UIImage, Data) -> Void
        let onCancelled: () -> Void

        init(onImageSelected: @escaping (UIImage, Data) -> Void,
             onCancelled: @escaping () -> Void) {
            self.onImageSelected = onImageSelected
            self.onCancelled = onCancelled
        }

        func picker(_ picker: PHPickerViewController,
                     didFinishPicking results: [PHPickerResult]) {
            guard let result = results.first else {
                onCancelled()
                return
            }

            result.itemProvider.loadObject(ofClass: UIImage.self) { [weak self] object, error in
                guard let image = object as? UIImage,
                      let data = image.jpegData(compressionQuality: 0.85) else {
                    DispatchQueue.main.async { self?.onCancelled() }
                    return
                }
                DispatchQueue.main.async {
                    self?.onImageSelected(image, data)
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

#### 3.4 Post-Selection Processing

The selected image enters the same pipeline as a camera-captured image:

```
User selects photo from library
  -> Check file size
  -> If > 10MB: resize longest edge to 1920px, compress to JPEG 85%
  -> If <= 10MB but longest edge > 1920px: resize to 1920px
  -> If <= 10MB and <= 1920px: compress to JPEG 85% only
  -> Navigate to Preview Screen (Section 2.2)
  -> Same flow: Preview -> Upload -> Processing -> Review -> Confirm
```

**File size gate:** Large files (>10MB) from modern iPhone cameras (ProRAW, HEIF at full resolution) must be resized before upload. The 10MB threshold is generous; most business card photos will be well under this.

#### 3.5 Edge Cases

| Scenario | Handling |
|---|---|
| User selects a screenshot | Proceed normally — OCR can handle screenshots of digital business cards. |
| User selects a non-card image (landscape, selfie) | Proceed normally — OCR will likely return low/no confidence. The "Couldn't Read This Card" error (2.7.1) handles this gracefully. |
| User selects a video (shouldn't be possible with `.images` filter) | Filter is enforced by the picker configuration. If somehow a video asset is returned, show toast: "Please select a photo, not a video." |
| User cancels picker | Return to camera viewfinder. No error, no toast. |
| HEIC/HEIF format | `PHPickerViewController` with `.preferredAssetRepresentationMode = .compatible` automatically converts to JPEG. No extra handling needed. Alternatively, `UIImage.jpegData()` handles the conversion natively. |

---

### 4. Image Handling Across the App

#### 4.1 Library Selection: Nuke SPM (Recommended)

**Decision: Use Nuke (kean/Nuke) via SPM.**

| Criterion | Nuke SPM |
|---|---|
| Active maintenance | Yes (Kean, frequent releases, Swift-first) |
| SwiftUI integration | `LazyImage` view, first-class SwiftUI support |
| BlurHash / ThumbHash placeholders | Supported via `NukeUI` and custom placeholder views |
| Transition animations (flicker-free) | Built-in via `LazyImage` transition parameter |
| Priority loading | Supported (`ImageRequest.Priority`) |
| Disk + memory caching | Yes (configurable `DataCache` + `ImageCache`) |
| Progressive decoding | Supported |
| Setup complexity | Zero-config with SPM, pure Swift |

**Justification:** Nuke is the modern Swift-native standard for image loading and caching. It provides first-class SwiftUI support via `LazyImage`, built-in disk and memory caching, priority loading, and supports blurhash placeholders (important for Street View placeholders). As a pure Swift package added via SPM, it integrates seamlessly with the native codebase with zero bridging overhead.

#### 4.2 Contact Avatars

##### 4.2.1 Avatar Generation

**Default: Initials-based avatar.**

```swift
// Utils/AvatarConfig.swift

struct AvatarConfig {
    let initials: String        // "JS" for John Smith
    let backgroundColor: Color  // Derived from name hash
    let textColor: Color        // Always white
}

func getAvatarConfig(for contact: Contact) -> AvatarConfig {
    let initials = getInitials(firstName: contact.firstName, lastName: contact.lastName) // Max 2 chars
    let backgroundColor = colorFromName("\(contact.firstName) \(contact.lastName)")
    return AvatarConfig(initials: initials, backgroundColor: backgroundColor, textColor: .white)
}

// Color derivation: deterministic hash of full name -> index into color palette
// Palette: 12 distinct, accessible colors that work with white text
private let avatarColors: [Color] = [
    Color(hex: "#E53935"), Color(hex: "#D81B60"), Color(hex: "#8E24AA"), Color(hex: "#5E35B1"),
    Color(hex: "#3949AB"), Color(hex: "#1E88E5"), Color(hex: "#00ACC1"), Color(hex: "#00897B"),
    Color(hex: "#43A047"), Color(hex: "#7CB342"), Color(hex: "#F4511E"), Color(hex: "#6D4C41"),
]

func colorFromName(_ name: String) -> Color {
    var hash: Int = 0
    for char in name.unicodeScalars {
        hash = Int(char.value) &+ ((hash &<< 5) &- hash)
    }
    return avatarColors[abs(hash) % avatarColors.count]
}

private func getInitials(firstName: String?, lastName: String?) -> String {
    let first = firstName?.first.map(String.init) ?? ""
    let last = lastName?.first.map(String.init) ?? ""
    let initials = "\(first)\(last)"
    return initials.isEmpty ? "?" : initials.uppercased()
}
```

**Why initials-based:** Real estate agents may have thousands of contacts. Generating or fetching real photos for all would be expensive and slow. Initials provide instant, distinctive, zero-network-cost avatars. The deterministic color hash means "John Smith" is always the same color — visually consistent across the app.

##### 4.2.2 Avatar Sizes

| Context | Size (pt) | Text Size | Font |
|---|---|---|---|
| Contact list row | 40 | 15pt | SF Pro Display Semibold |
| Chat/interaction cards | 60 | 22pt | SF Pro Display Semibold |
| Contact detail header | 80 | 30pt | SF Pro Display Semibold |
| Mini (tags, pills, mentions) | 24 | 10pt | SF Pro Display Semibold |

##### 4.2.3 Avatar Rendering

```swift
// Views/ContactAvatarView.swift

import SwiftUI
import NukeUI

enum AvatarSize: CGFloat {
    case mini = 24
    case list = 40
    case card = 60
    case detail = 80

    var fontSize: CGFloat {
        switch self {
        case .mini:   return 10
        case .list:   return 15
        case .card:   return 22
        case .detail: return 30
        }
    }
}

struct ContactAvatarView: View {
    let contact: Contact
    let size: AvatarSize
    var showBorder: Bool = true

    @Environment(\.colorScheme) private var colorScheme

    private var config: AvatarConfig {
        getAvatarConfig(for: contact)
    }

    private var shouldShowBorder: Bool {
        showBorder && colorScheme == .light
    }

    var body: some View {
        Group {
            if let profileImageURL = contact.profileImageURL {
                LazyImage(url: profileImageURL) { state in
                    if let image = state.image {
                        image
                            .resizable()
                            .scaledToFill()
                    } else {
                        initialsView
                    }
                }
                .transition(.opacity.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.2)))
            } else {
                initialsView
            }
        }
        .frame(width: size.rawValue, height: size.rawValue)
        .clipShape(Circle())
        .overlay(
            Circle()
                .stroke(Color.borderSecondary, lineWidth: shouldShowBorder ? 1 : 0)
        )
    }

    private var initialsView: some View {
        ZStack {
            Circle()
                .fill(config.backgroundColor)
            Text(config.initials)
                .font(.system(size: size.fontSize, weight: .semibold, design: .default))
                .foregroundColor(config.textColor)
        }
    }
}
```

- **Shape:** Circular (`.clipShape(Circle())`).
- **Border:** 1pt solid `Color.borderSecondary` in light mode. No border in dark mode (the dark background provides sufficient contrast).
- **Initials text:** Centered vertically and horizontally within the circle via `ZStack`.
- **Profile photo override:** If the contact has a `profileImageURL` (e.g., pulled from Google Profile via People API), display the photo instead of initials.
  - Use `LazyImage` from `NukeUI`.
  - Placeholder: the initials avatar (rendered as the fallback in `LazyImage`'s state handler).
  - Transition: 200ms crossfade from placeholder to loaded image.
  - Content mode: `.scaledToFill()`.
  - Cache policy: disk cache — profile photos rarely change.
- **Error fallback:** If profile photo URL fails to load (404, timeout), fall back to initials avatar via `LazyImage` error state. No broken image icon ever shown.

#### 4.3 Street View Images

##### 4.3.1 URL Construction

```swift
// Utils/StreetViewURL.swift

import UIKit

struct StreetViewParams {
    let latitude: Double
    let longitude: Double
    let width: Int      // in pixels (retina-adjusted)
    let height: Int     // in pixels (retina-adjusted)
    var heading: Int?   // 0-360, optional (API auto-selects if omitted)
    var pitch: Int = 0  // default 0 (horizontal)
    var fov: Int = 90   // default 90 (field of view)
}

func getStreetViewURL(params: StreetViewParams) -> URL? {
    var components = URLComponents(string: "https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/streetview")
    var queryItems: [URLQueryItem] = [
        URLQueryItem(name: "size", value: "\(params.width)x\(params.height)"),
        URLQueryItem(name: "location", value: "\(params.latitude),\(params.longitude)"),
        URLQueryItem(name: "fov", value: "\(params.fov)"),
        URLQueryItem(name: "pitch", value: "\(params.pitch)"),
        URLQueryItem(name: "key", value: AppConfig.googleMapsAPIKey),
    ]

    if let heading = params.heading {
        queryItems.append(URLQueryItem(name: "heading", value: "\(heading)"))
    }

    components?.queryItems = queryItems
    return components?.url
}

// Metadata check: determine if Street View is available at coordinates
func getStreetViewMetadataURL(lat: Double, lng: Double) -> URL? {
    var components = URLComponents(string: "https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/streetview/metadata")
    components?.queryItems = [
        URLQueryItem(name: "location", value: "\(lat),\(lng)"),
        URLQueryItem(name: "key", value: AppConfig.googleMapsAPIKey),
    ]
    return components?.url
}
```

**Retina sizing:** On a 3x retina device, a 120x80pt thumbnail needs a 360x240px image. Always multiply by `Int(UIScreen.main.scale)` for the API `size` parameter, up to a maximum of `640x640` (API limit).

##### 4.3.2 Display Sizes

| Context | Point Size | Pixel Size (@3x) | API Size |
|---|---|---|---|
| Property list thumbnail | 120 x 80pt | 360 x 240px | `360x240` |
| Property detail hero | Full width x 220pt | ~1170 x 660px | `640x400` (API max width 640, scale up client-side) |
| Map card popup | 160 x 100pt | 480 x 300px | `480x300` |

##### 4.3.3 Availability Check and Fallback

Before displaying a Street View image, check the metadata endpoint.

```swift
// Models/StreetViewMetadata.swift

struct StreetViewMetadata: Codable {
    let status: String  // "OK", "ZERO_RESULTS", "NOT_FOUND", "OVER_QUERY_LIMIT", "REQUEST_DENIED", "INVALID_REQUEST", "UNKNOWN_ERROR"
    let panoId: String?
    let date: String?          // "2023-05" format
    let location: Coordinate?

    enum CodingKeys: String, CodingKey {
        case status
        case panoId = "pano_id"
        case date, location
    }

    struct Coordinate: Codable {
        let lat: Double
        let lng: Double
    }
}
```

**Metadata caching:** Cache metadata responses in `UserDefaults` or a lightweight persistent key-value store keyed by `"sv_meta_\(lat)_\(lng)"` with a 30-day TTL. Metadata requests are free (no billing) so we can be aggressive with pre-checking.

**Fallback display when Street View is unavailable (`ZERO_RESULTS`):**

```
+-----------------------------------+
|                                    |
|   [House icon, 32pt, gray]        |
|   "No Street View Available"      |
|   (12pt, Color.textTertiary)      |
|                                    |
+-----------------------------------+
```

- Background: `Color.bgSecondary` (light gray).
- Rounded corners matching the image slot (8pt for thumbnails, 0 for full-width heroes).
- House icon: SF Symbol `house.fill`, 32pt, `Color.iconSecondary`.

##### 4.3.4 Street View Image Component

```swift
// Views/StreetViewImageView.swift

import SwiftUI
import NukeUI

enum StreetViewSize {
    case thumbnail  // 120x80pt
    case hero       // full width x 220pt
    case card       // 160x100pt
}

struct StreetViewImageView: View {
    let latitude: Double
    let longitude: Double
    let size: StreetViewSize

    @State private var metadataStatus: String?

    private var pointSize: CGSize {
        switch size {
        case .thumbnail: return CGSize(width: 120, height: 80)
        case .hero:      return CGSize(width: UIScreen.main.bounds.width, height: 220)
        case .card:      return CGSize(width: 160, height: 100)
        }
    }

    private var imageURL: URL? {
        let scale = Int(UIScreen.main.scale)
        let pixelWidth = min(Int(pointSize.width) * scale, 640)
        let pixelHeight = min(Int(pointSize.height) * scale, 640)
        return getStreetViewURL(params: StreetViewParams(
            latitude: latitude,
            longitude: longitude,
            width: pixelWidth,
            height: pixelHeight
        ))
    }

    var body: some View {
        Group {
            if metadataStatus == "ZERO_RESULTS" {
                fallbackView
            } else if let url = imageURL {
                LazyImage(url: url) { state in
                    if let image = state.image {
                        image
                            .resizable()
                            .scaledToFill()
                    } else if state.error != nil {
                        fallbackView
                    } else {
                        // Blurhash placeholder: neutral gray-blue
                        Color.gray.opacity(0.2)
                    }
                }
                .transition(.opacity.animation(.easeInOut(duration: 0.3)))
            } else {
                fallbackView
            }
        }
        .frame(width: size == .hero ? nil : pointSize.width, height: pointSize.height)
        .frame(maxWidth: size == .hero ? .infinity : nil)
        .clipShape(RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius: size == .hero ? 0 : 8))
    }

    private var fallbackView: some View {
        ZStack {
            Color.bgSecondary
            VStack(spacing: 4) {
                Image(systemName: "house.fill")
                    .font(.system(size: 32))
```
