##### 1.1.2 Offline Queue Unit Tests (`Tests/Lib/OfflineQueueTests.swift`)

```swift
import Testing
@testable import Skye

@Suite("OfflineQueue")
struct OfflineQueueTests {

    let mockStorage = MockPersistentStorage()
    var queue: OfflineQueue!

    init() {
        queue = OfflineQueue(
            config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
            storage: mockStorage
        )
    }

    // MARK: - Enqueue

    @Suite("enqueue")
    struct EnqueueTests {

        @Test("adds an operation to the queue with timestamp and incremented ID")
        func addsOperationWithTimestampAndID() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1", "name": "Updated"])
            )
            let items = await queue.getAll()
            #expect(items.count == 1)
            #expect(items[0].type == .updateContact)
            #expect(items[0].payload["id"] == "1")
            #expect(items[0].payload["name"] == "Updated")
            #expect(items[0].retryCount == 0)
            #expect(items[0].status == .pending)
            #expect(items[0].id.isEmpty == false)
            #expect(items[0].createdAt > 0)
        }

        @Test("persists queue to UserDefaults so it survives app restart")
        func persistsQueueToStorage() async throws {
            let storage = MockPersistentStorage()
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: storage
            )
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .createNote, payload: ["contactId": "1", "text": "Test"])
            )
            let storedData = storage.data(forKey: "offline_queue")
            #expect(storedData != nil)
            let stored = try JSONDecoder().decode([OfflineOperation].self, from: storedData!)
            #expect(stored.count == 1)
        }

        @Test("rejects enqueue when queue is at maxQueueSize (100) to prevent memory exhaustion")
        func rejectsEnqueueAtMaxSize() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            for i in 0..<100 {
                try await queue.enqueue(
                    OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "\(i)"])
                )
            }
            await #expect(throws: OfflineQueueError.queueFull) {
                try await queue.enqueue(
                    OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "101"])
                )
            }
        }

        @Test("maintains FIFO order for operations on the same entity")
        func maintainsFIFOOrder() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1", "name": "First"])
            )
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1", "name": "Second"])
            )
            let items = await queue.getAll()
            #expect(items[0].payload["name"] == "First")
            #expect(items[1].payload["name"] == "Second")
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Dequeue and Process

    @Suite("dequeue and process")
    struct DequeueTests {

        @Test("processes the oldest pending item first (FIFO)")
        func processesOldestFirst() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            try await queue.enqueue(OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["order": "A"]))
            try await queue.enqueue(OfflineOperation(type: .createNote, payload: ["order": "B"]))
            let next = await queue.dequeue()
            #expect(next?.payload["order"] == "A")
        }

        @Test("marks item as processing during execution to prevent duplicate processing")
        func marksItemAsProcessing() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.shouldDelay = true
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            )
            Task { try await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient) }
            try await Task.sleep(for: .milliseconds(50))
            let items = await queue.getAll()
            #expect(items[0].status == .processing)
            mockAPIClient.shouldDelay = false
        }

        @Test("removes item from queue on successful API call")
        func removesItemOnSuccess() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.result = .success(())
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            )
            try await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient)
            let items = await queue.getAll()
            #expect(items.isEmpty)
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Retry Logic

    @Suite("retry logic")
    struct RetryTests {

        @Test("increments retryCount on failure and returns item to pending status")
        func incrementsRetryCountOnFailure() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.result = .failure(AppError(type: .network))
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            )
            try? await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient)
            let items = await queue.getAll()
            #expect(items[0].retryCount == 1)
            #expect(items[0].status == .pending)
        }

        @Test("applies exponential backoff: delay = baseDelay * 2^retryCount")
        func appliesExponentialBackoff() {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            var item = OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            item.retryCount = 2
            let delay = queue.calculateRetryDelay(for: item)
            #expect(delay == 1000 * Int(pow(2.0, 2.0))) // 4000ms
        }

        @Test("caps retry delay at 30000ms regardless of retry count")
        func capsRetryDelay() {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            var item = OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            item.retryCount = 10
            let delay = queue.calculateRetryDelay(for: item)
            #expect(delay <= 30000)
        }
    }

    // MARK: - Dead Letter Handling

    @Suite("dead letter handling")
    struct DeadLetterTests {

        @Test("moves item to dead letter queue after maxRetries (3) exhausted")
        func movesToDeadLetterAfterMaxRetries() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.result = .failure(AppError(type: .server))
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1"])
            )
            // Process 3 times (all fail)
            for _ in 0..<3 {
                try? await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient)
            }
            let mainQueue = await queue.getAll()
            let deadLetters = await queue.getDeadLetters()
            #expect(mainQueue.isEmpty)
            #expect(deadLetters.count == 1)
            #expect(deadLetters[0].errorMessage == "Server error")
        }

        @Test("emits deadLetter event when item is moved so UI can show a warning")
        func emitsDeadLetterEvent() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.result = .failure(AppError(type: .server))

            var receivedDeadLetter: OfflineOperation?
            let cancellable = queue.deadLetterPublisher.sink { operation in
                receivedDeadLetter = operation
            }

            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .deleteNote, payload: ["id": "99"])
            )
            for _ in 0..<3 {
                try? await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient)
            }
            #expect(receivedDeadLetter != nil)
            #expect(receivedDeadLetter?.type == .deleteNote)
            #expect(receivedDeadLetter?.payload["id"] == "99")
            _ = cancellable // keep alive
        }

        @Test("does NOT retry 422 validation errors -- moves directly to dead letter")
        func doesNotRetryValidationErrors() async throws {
            let queue = OfflineQueue(
                config: .init(maxRetries: 3, retryDelayMs: 1000, maxQueueSize: 100),
                storage: MockPersistentStorage()
            )
            let mockAPIClient = MockAPIClient()
            mockAPIClient.result = .failure(AppError(type: .validation, statusCode: 422))
            try await queue.enqueue(
                OfflineOperation(type: .updateContact, payload: ["id": "1", "email": "invalid"])
            )
            try? await queue.processNext(apiClient: mockAPIClient)
            let mainQueue = await queue.getAll()
            let deadLetters = await queue.getDeadLetters()
            #expect(mainQueue.isEmpty)
            #expect(deadLetters.count == 1)
        }
    }
}
```

##### 1.1.3 Data Transformer Unit Tests (`Tests/Lib/TransformersTests.swift`)

```swift
import Testing
@testable import Skye

@Suite("transformApiContact to UIContact")
struct TransformApiContactTests {

    static let base = APIContact(
        id: "c_base",
        firstName: "Test",
        lastName: "User",
        emailAddresses: [],
        phoneNumbers: [],
        engagementScore: nil,
        lastActivityAt: nil,
        tags: [],
        customFields: [:]
    )

    @Test("maps flat API fields to nested UI model")
    func mapsFieldsToUIModel() {
        let apiContact = APIContact(
            id: "c_123",
            firstName: "Jane",
            lastName: "Doe",
            emailAddresses: [.init(value: "jane@example.com", label: "work")],
            phoneNumbers: [.init(value: "+14155551234", label: "mobile")],
            engagementScore: 85,
            lastActivityAt: "2025-12-01T10:00:00Z",
            tags: ["buyer", "hot-lead"],
            customFields: ["budget": "$500K-$1M"]
        )
        let result = transformApiContact(apiContact)
        #expect(result.id == "c_123")
        #expect(result.name.first == "Jane")
        #expect(result.name.last == "Doe")
        #expect(result.name.display == "Jane Doe")
        #expect(result.name.initials == "JD")
        #expect(result.emails.first?.value == "jane@example.com")
        #expect(result.emails.first?.label == "work")
        #expect(result.emails.first?.isPrimary == true)
        #expect(result.phones.first?.value == "+14155551234")
        #expect(result.phones.first?.formatted == "(415) 555-1234")
        #expect(result.phones.first?.label == "mobile")
        #expect(result.phones.first?.isPrimary == true)
        #expect(result.engagement.score == 85)
        #expect(result.engagement.level == .hot)
        #expect(result.engagement.dotColor == "#FF3B30")
        #expect(result.lastActivity?.timestamp != nil)
        #expect(result.tags == ["buyer", "hot-lead"])
        #expect(result.customFields["budget"] == "$500K-$1M")
    }

    @Test("handles missing optional fields without throwing")
    func handlesMissingOptionalFields() {
        let minimalContact = APIContact(
            id: "c_456", firstName: "Bob", lastName: nil,
            emailAddresses: [], phoneNumbers: [],
            engagementScore: nil, lastActivityAt: nil, tags: [], customFields: [:]
        )
        let result = transformApiContact(minimalContact)
        #expect(result.name.display == "Bob")
        #expect(result.name.initials == "B")
        #expect(result.emails.isEmpty)
        #expect(result.phones.isEmpty)
        #expect(result.engagement.level == .cold)
    }

    @Test("maps engagement score brackets correctly")
    func mapsEngagementScoreBrackets() {
        let base = Self.base
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: 95)).engagement.level == .hot)
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: 70)).engagement.level == .warm)
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: 40)).engagement.level == .cool)
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: 10)).engagement.level == .cold)
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: 0)).engagement.level == .cold)
        #expect(transformApiContact(base.with(engagementScore: nil)).engagement.level == .unknown)
    }

    @Test("formats US phone numbers with correct grouping")
    func formatsUSPhoneNumbers() {
        let contact = Self.base.with(
            phoneNumbers: [.init(value: "+12125551234", label: "mobile")]
        )
        #expect(transformApiContact(contact).phones[0].formatted == "(212) 555-1234")
    }

    @Test("preserves international phone numbers as-is when non-US")
    func preservesInternationalPhoneNumbers() {
        let contact = Self.base.with(
            phoneNumbers: [.init(value: "+442071234567", label: "mobile")]
        )
        #expect(transformApiContact(contact).phones[0].formatted == "+44 207 123 4567")
    }
}

@Suite("transformApiProperty to UIProperty")
struct TransformApiPropertyTests {

    static let base = APIProperty(
        id: "p_base", address: .init(street: "1 St", city: "SF", state: "CA", zip: "94105"),
        listPrice: 100000, bedrooms: 1, bathrooms: 1, squareFeet: 500,
        lotSizeSqft: nil, yearBuilt: nil, status: "active",
        daysOnMarket: 1, mlsId: "MLS1", photos: [], latitude: 37.78, longitude: -122.40
    )

    @Test("maps all property fields including computed metrics")
    func mapsAllPropertyFields() {
        let apiProperty = APIProperty(
            id: "p_789",
            address: .init(street: "123 Main St", city: "San Francisco", state: "CA", zip: "94105"),
            listPrice: 1250000,
            bedrooms: 3,
            bathrooms: 2,
            squareFeet: 1800,
            lotSizeSqft: 3200,
            yearBuilt: 1925,
            status: "active",
            daysOnMarket: 14,
            mlsId: "MLS12345",
            photos: ["https://cdn.example.com/p1.jpg"],
            latitude: 37.7849,
            longitude: -122.4094
        )
        let result = transformApiProperty(apiProperty)
        #expect(result.priceFormatted == "$1,250,000")
        #expect(result.pricePerSqFt == "$694")
        #expect(result.addressOneLine == "123 Main St, San Francisco, CA 94105")
        #expect(result.streetViewUrl.contains("maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/streetview"))
        #expect(result.streetViewUrl.contains("37.7849"))
    }

    @Test("handles missing lot_size and year_built gracefully")
    func handlesMissingOptionalFields() {
        let minimal = Self.base.with(lotSizeSqft: nil, yearBuilt: nil)
        let result = transformApiProperty(minimal)
        #expect(result.lotSize == nil)
        #expect(result.yearBuilt == nil)
    }
}

@Suite("transformApiDeal to UIDeal")
struct TransformApiDealTests {

    static let baseDeal = APIDeal(
        id: "d_1", contactId: "c_1", propertyId: "p_1",
        stage: "initial_contact", value: 100000
    )

    @Test("computes deal stage progress percentage")
    func computesDealStageProgress() {
        let deal = Self.baseDeal.with(stage: "under_contract")
        let result = transformApiDeal(deal)
        #expect(result.stageProgress == 0.75) // under_contract = 75%
    }

    @Test("formats deal value with comma separators")
    func formatsDealValue() {
        let deal = Self.baseDeal.with(value: 2500000)
        #expect(transformApiDeal(deal).valueFormatted == "$2,500,000")
    }
}
```

##### 1.1.4 Chat/SSE Parser Unit Tests (`Tests/Lib/ChatParserTests.swift`)

```swift
import Testing
@testable import Skye

@Suite("SSE Parser")
struct SSEParserTests {

    @Test("parses a single data: line into a text token")
    func parsesSingleDataLine() {
        let result = parseSSEChunk("data: {\"type\":\"text\",\"content\":\"Hello\"}\n\n")
        #expect(result == [SSEEvent(type: .text, content: "Hello")])
    }

    @Test("parses multiple events from a single chunk (buffered by network)")
    func parsesMultipleEvents() {
        let chunk = """
        data: {"type":"text","content":"Hello "}

        data: {"type":"text","content":"world"}

        """
        let result = parseSSEChunk(chunk)
        #expect(result.count == 2)
        #expect(result[0] == SSEEvent(type: .text, content: "Hello "))
        #expect(result[1] == SSEEvent(type: .text, content: "world"))
    }

    @Test("handles partial chunk at buffer boundary without data loss")
    func handlesPartialChunk() {
        let parser = IncrementalSSEParser()
        parser.feed("data: {\"type\":\"text\",\"con")
        #expect(parser.flush().isEmpty)
        parser.feed("tent\":\"Hello\"}\n\n")
        #expect(parser.flush() == [SSEEvent(type: .text, content: "Hello")])
    }

    @Test("parses [DONE] sentinel and emits stream-end event")
    func parsesDoneSentinel() {
        let result = parseSSEChunk("data: [DONE]\n\n")
        #expect(result == [SSEEvent(type: .done)])
    }

    @Test("ignores SSE comments (lines starting with :)")
    func ignoresSSEComments() {
        let chunk = ":keepalive\n\ndata: {\"type\":\"text\",\"content\":\"Hi\"}\n\n"
        let result = parseSSEChunk(chunk)
        #expect(result == [SSEEvent(type: .text, content: "Hi")])
    }

    @Test("parses tool_result event with structured card data")
    func parsesToolResultEvent() {
        let chunk = "data: {\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"tool\":\"property_search\",\"data\":{\"results\":[{\"id\":\"p_1\",\"address\":\"123 Main St\",\"price\":500000}]}}\n\n"
        let result = parseSSEChunk(chunk)
        #expect(result.count == 1)
        #expect(result[0].type == .toolResult)
        #expect(result[0].tool == "property_search")
        #expect(result[0].toolData != nil)
    }

    @Test("handles malformed JSON gracefully by emitting error event (no crash)")
    func handlesMalformedJSON() {
        let chunk = "data: {broken json\n\n"
        let result = parseSSEChunk(chunk)
        #expect(result.count == 1)
        #expect(result[0].type == .error)
        #expect(result[0].message?.contains("parse") == true)
    }
}

@Suite("Markdown Renderer Token Extraction")
struct MarkdownRendererTests {

    @Test("converts **bold** to styled text segment")
    func convertsBold() {
        let segments = parseMarkdownToSegments("This is **bold** text")
        #expect(segments.contains { $0.type == .bold && $0.text == "bold" })
    }

    @Test("converts inline `code` to styled text segment")
    func convertsInlineCode() {
        let segments = parseMarkdownToSegments("Use `swift build`")
        #expect(segments.contains { $0.type == .inlineCode && $0.text == "swift build" })
    }

    @Test("converts code blocks to code block segment with language")
    func convertsCodeBlock() {
        let md = "```javascript\nconsole.log(\"hi\");\n```"
        let segments = parseMarkdownToSegments(md)
        #expect(segments.contains {
            $0.type == .codeBlock && $0.language == "javascript" && $0.code == "console.log(\"hi\");"
        })
    }

    @Test("converts numbered lists to ordered list segments")
    func convertsNumberedLists() {
        let md = "1. First\n2. Second\n3. Third"
        let segments = parseMarkdownToSegments(md)
        #expect(segments[0].type == .orderedList)
        #expect(segments[0].items == ["First", "Second", "Third"])
    }

    @Test("handles mixed markdown elements in a single response")
    func handlesMixedMarkdown() {
        let md = "**Title**\n\nHere are properties:\n\n1. 123 Main St\n2. 456 Oak Ave\n\n> Note: prices are estimates"
        let segments = parseMarkdownToSegments(md)
        let types = segments.map(\.type)
        #expect(types == [.bold, .text, .orderedList, .blockquote])
    }
}
```

##### 1.1.5 Cache Manager Unit Tests (`Tests/Lib/CacheManagerTests.swift`)

```swift
import XCTest
@testable import Skye

final class CacheManagerTests: XCTestCase {

    var cache: CacheManager!

    override func setUp() {
        super.setUp()
        cache = CacheManager(maxEntries: 5, defaultTTLMs: 60000)
    }

    // MARK: - LRU Eviction

    func test_evictsLeastRecentlyUsedEntryWhenMaxEntriesExceeded() {
        cache.set("a", value: 1)
        cache.set("b", value: 2)
        cache.set("c", value: 3)
        cache.set("d", value: 4)
        cache.set("e", value: 5)
        // Access 'a' to make it recently used
        _ = cache.get("a") as Int?
        // Add one more, should evict 'b' (least recently used after 'a' was accessed)
        cache.set("f", value: 6)
        XCTAssertFalse(cache.has("b"))
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("a")) // 'a' was accessed, so not evicted
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("f"))
    }

    func test_updatesAccessOrderOnGetNotJustSet() {
        cache.set("a", value: 1)
        cache.set("b", value: 2)
        cache.set("c", value: 3)
        cache.set("d", value: 4)
        cache.set("e", value: 5)
        _ = cache.get("a") as Int? // 'a' is now most recently used
        _ = cache.get("b") as Int? // 'b' is now most recently used
        cache.set("f", value: 6) // Should evict 'c' (oldest untouched)
        XCTAssertFalse(cache.has("c"))
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("a"))
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("b"))
    }

    // MARK: - Staleness Checks

    func test_returnsNilForExpiredEntries() {
        cache.set("contacts", value: [["id": 1]], ttlMs: 5000)
        // Simulate time passing by using a cache with a custom clock
        let timedCache = CacheManager(maxEntries: 5, defaultTTLMs: 60000, clock: MockClock())
        timedCache.set("contacts", value: [["id": 1]], ttlMs: 5000)
        (timedCache.clock as! MockClock).advance(by: 5001)
        let result: [[String: Int]]? = timedCache.get("contacts")
        XCTAssertNil(result)
    }

    func test_returnsDataForNonExpiredEntries() {
        let timedCache = CacheManager(maxEntries: 5, defaultTTLMs: 60000, clock: MockClock())
        timedCache.set("contacts", value: [["id": 1]], ttlMs: 5000)
        (timedCache.clock as! MockClock).advance(by: 4999)
        let result: [[String: Int]]? = timedCache.get("contacts")
        XCTAssertEqual(result, [["id": 1]])
    }

    func test_isStaleReturnsTrueWhenEntryIsPastTTL() {
        let timedCache = CacheManager(maxEntries: 5, defaultTTLMs: 60000, clock: MockClock())
        timedCache.set("contacts", value: [["id": 1]], ttlMs: 5000)
        (timedCache.clock as! MockClock).advance(by: 5001)
        XCTAssertTrue(timedCache.isStale("contacts"))
    }

    func test_getStaleReturnsExpiredDataForStaleWhileRevalidatePattern() {
        let timedCache = CacheManager(maxEntries: 5, defaultTTLMs: 60000, clock: MockClock())
        timedCache.set("contacts", value: [["id": 1]], ttlMs: 5000)
        (timedCache.clock as! MockClock).advance(by: 5001)
        let freshResult: [[String: Int]]? = timedCache.get("contacts")
        XCTAssertNil(freshResult)
        let staleResult: [[String: Int]]? = timedCache.getStale("contacts")
        XCTAssertEqual(staleResult, [["id": 1]])
    }

    // MARK: - Cache Invalidation

    func test_invalidateKeyRemovesSpecificEntry() {
        cache.set("contacts", value: [Any]())
        cache.set("properties", value: [Any]())
        cache.invalidate("contacts")
        XCTAssertFalse(cache.has("contacts"))
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("properties"))
    }

    func test_invalidatePatternRemovesAllMatchingKeys() {
        cache.set("contacts:list", value: [Any]())
        cache.set("contacts:detail:1", value: [String: Any]())
        cache.set("properties:list", value: [Any]())
        cache.invalidatePattern(try! NSRegularExpression(pattern: "^contacts:"))
        XCTAssertFalse(cache.has("contacts:list"))
        XCTAssertFalse(cache.has("contacts:detail:1"))
        XCTAssertTrue(cache.has("properties:list"))
    }

    func test_invalidateAllClearsEntireCache() {
        cache.set("a", value: 1)
        cache.set("b", value: 2)
        cache.invalidateAll()
        XCTAssertEqual(cache.size(), 0)
    }
}
```

---

##### 1.1.6 Navigation Helper Unit Tests (`Tests/Lib/NavigationTests.swift`)

```swift
import Testing
@testable import Skye

@Suite("Deep Link Parser")
struct DeepLinkParserTests {

    @Test("parses skye://contact/c_123 to ContactDetail route with id param")
    func parsesContactDeepLink() {
        let route = parseDeepLink("skye://contact/c_123")
        #expect(route == Route(screen: .contactDetail, params: ["contactId": "c_123"]))
    }

    @Test("parses skye://property/p_456 to PropertyDetail route")
    func parsesPropertyDeepLink() {
        let route = parseDeepLink("skye://property/p_456")
        #expect(route == Route(screen: .propertyDetail, params: ["propertyId": "p_456"]))
    }

    @Test("parses skye://chat to Chat route with no params")
    func parsesChatDeepLink() {
        let route = parseDeepLink("skye://chat")
        #expect(route == Route(screen: .chat, params: [:]))
    }

    @Test("parses skye://chat?prefill=Show+listings to Chat with prefill text")
    func parsesChatWithPrefill() {
        let route = parseDeepLink("skye://chat?prefill=Show+listings")
        #expect(route == Route(screen: .chat, params: ["prefillText": "Show listings"]))
    }

    @Test("returns nil for unknown deep link scheme")
    func returnsNilForUnknownScheme() {
        #expect(parseDeepLink("https://example.com") == nil)
    }

    @Test("returns nil for malformed deep link (missing entity ID)")
    func returnsNilForMalformedLink() {
        #expect(parseDeepLink("skye://contact/") == nil)
    }

    @Test("handles deep link with extra path segments gracefully")
    func handlesExtraPathSegments() {
        let route = parseDeepLink("skye://contact/c_123/notes")
        #expect(route == Route(
            screen: .contactDetail,
            params: ["contactId": "c_123", "section": "notes"]
        ))
    }
}

@Suite("Route Resolution")
struct RouteResolutionTests {

    @Test("resolves push notification payload to correct navigation action")
    func resolvesNewMessageNotification() {
        let payload = NotificationPayload(
            type: "new_message",
            entityType: "contact",
            entityId: "c_123"
        )
        let action = resolveNotificationRoute(payload)
        #expect(action == NavigationAction(
            type: .navigate,
            screen: .contactDetail,
            params: ["contactId": "c_123", "initialTab": "timeline"]
        ))
    }

    @Test("resolves deal notification to deal detail within contact")
    func resolvesDealNotification() {
        let payload = NotificationPayload(
            type: "deal_update",
            entityType: "deal",
            entityId: "d_789",
            contactId: "c_123"
        )
        let action = resolveNotificationRoute(payload)
        #expect(action == NavigationAction(
            type: .navigate,
            screen: .contactDetail,
            params: ["contactId": "c_123", "initialTab": "deals", "highlightDealId": "d_789"]
        ))
    }

    @Test("returns fallback to Home for notification with unknown type")
    func returnsFallbackForUnknownType() {
        let payload = NotificationPayload(
            type: "unknown_type",
            entityType: nil,
            entityId: "x_1"
        )
        let action = resolveNotificationRoute(payload)
        #expect(action == NavigationAction(type: .navigate, screen: .home, params: [:]))
    }
}
```

---

#### 1.2 Component Tests (25% of Test Effort)

**Tooling:** `ViewInspector` SPM (nalexn/ViewInspector) for SwiftUI view testing, `swift-snapshot-testing` SPM (pointfreeco/swift-snapshot-testing) for snapshot tests, XCTest for assertions, protocol-based mocks for API layer.

**Rule: Every screen view must be tested in 4 states.** This is enforced by a custom SwiftLint rule (`skye_require_four_states`) that flags any `*ScreenTests.swift` file missing `loading`, `success`, `empty`, or `error` in its test method names or nested type names.

---

##### 1.2.1 ContactListItem View Tests

```swift
// Tests/Views/ContactListItemTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class ContactListItemTests: XCTestCase {

    var onPressCalled: UIContact?
    var onCallCalled: String?
    var onMessageCalled: String?

    let defaultContact = UIContact.mock(
        name: .init(first: "Jane", last: "Doe", display: "Jane Doe", initials: "JD"),
        engagement: .init(score: 85, level: .hot, dotColor: "#FF3B30"),
        lastActivity: .init(timestamp: Date(), relative: "2 hours ago")
    )

    override func setUp() {
        super.setUp()
        onPressCalled = nil
        onCallCalled = nil
        onMessageCalled = nil
    }

    // MARK: - Rendering

    func test_rendersContactDisplayName() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(
            contact: defaultContact,
            onPress: { _ in }
        )
        let text = try sut.inspect().find(text: "Jane Doe")
        XCTAssertNotNil(text)
    }

    func test_rendersInitialsAvatarWhenNoPhotoURLProvided() throws {
        let contact = defaultContact.with(photoUrl: nil)
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: contact, onPress: { _ in })
        let initials = try sut.inspect().find(text: "JD")
        XCTAssertNotNil(initials)
    }

    func test_rendersPhotoAvatarWhenPhotoURLIsProvided() throws {
        let contact = defaultContact.with(photoUrl: "https://cdn.example.com/photo.jpg")
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: contact, onPress: { _ in })
        let asyncImage = try sut.inspect().find(ViewType.AsyncImage.self)
        XCTAssertNotNil(asyncImage)
    }

    func test_rendersEngagementDotWithCorrectColorForHot() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: defaultContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let dot = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "engagement-dot")
        // ViewInspector foregroundColor check
        XCTAssertNotNil(dot)
    }

    func test_rendersEngagementDotGreenForWarm() throws {
        let warmContact = UIContact.mock(
            engagement: .init(score: 70, level: .warm, dotColor: "#34C759")
        )
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: warmContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let dot = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "engagement-dot")
        XCTAssertNotNil(dot)
    }

    func test_rendersEngagementDotGrayForCold() throws {
        let coldContact = UIContact.mock(
            engagement: .init(score: 10, level: .cold, dotColor: "#8E8E93")
        )
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: coldContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let dot = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "engagement-dot")
        XCTAssertNotNil(dot)
    }

    func test_rendersLastActivityTimeInSubtitle() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: defaultContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let text = try sut.inspect().find(text: "2 hours ago")
        XCTAssertNotNil(text)
    }

    // MARK: - Interactions

    func test_callsOnPressWithContactWhenTapped() throws {
        var received: UIContact?
        let sut = ContactListItemView(
            contact: defaultContact,
            onPress: { received = $0 }
        )
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contact-list-item").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(received?.id, defaultContact.id)
    }

    func test_swipeLeftRevealsCallAndMessageActions() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(
            contact: defaultContact,
            onPress: { _ in },
            onCall: { _ in },
            onMessage: { _ in }
        )
        // Swipe actions are declared via .swipeActions modifier in SwiftUI
        let swipeActions = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contact-list-item")
        XCTAssertNotNil(swipeActions)
    }

    func test_callActionButtonTriggersOnCallWithContactPhoneNumber() throws {
        var calledNumber: String?
        let sut = ContactListItemView(
            contact: defaultContact,
            onPress: { _ in },
            onCall: { calledNumber = $0 }
        )
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "action-call").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(calledNumber, defaultContact.phones.first?.value)
    }

    // MARK: - Accessibility

    func test_hasAccessibilityLabelWithFullContactNameAndEngagementStatus() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: defaultContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let item = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contact-list-item")
        let label = try item.accessibilityLabel().string()
        XCTAssertEqual(label, "Jane Doe, hot lead, last active 2 hours ago")
    }

    func test_hasAccessibilityTraitButton() throws {
        let sut = ContactListItemView(contact: defaultContact, onPress: { _ in })
        let item = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contact-list-item")
        let traits = try item.accessibilityTraits()
        XCTAssertTrue(traits.contains(.isButton))
    }
}
```

##### 1.2.2 ChatMessage View Tests

```swift
// Tests/Views/ChatMessageTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class ChatMessageTests: XCTestCase {

    // MARK: - Text Rendering

    func test_rendersPlainTextMessage() throws {
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: "Hello"))
        let text = try sut.inspect().find(text: "Hello")
        XCTAssertNotNil(text)
    }

    func test_rendersBoldMarkdownAsBoldStyledText() throws {
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: "This is **important**"))
        let boldText = try sut.inspect().find(text: "important")
        XCTAssertNotNil(boldText)
        // ViewInspector font weight check
        let font = try boldText.attributes().font()
        XCTAssertEqual(font.weight, .bold)
    }

    func test_rendersInlineCodeWithMonospaceFont() throws {
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: "Run `swift build`"))
        let codeText = try sut.inspect().find(text: "swift build")
        XCTAssertNotNil(codeText)
        let font = try codeText.attributes().font()
        XCTAssertTrue(font.isMonospace)
    }

    func test_rendersMultiLineCodeBlockWithSyntaxHighlightingContainer() throws {
        let text = "```javascript\nconst x = 1;\nconsole.log(x);\n```"
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: text))
        let codeBlock = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "code-block")
        XCTAssertNotNil(codeBlock)
        let langLabel = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "code-block-language")
        let langText = try langLabel.text().string()
        XCTAssertEqual(langText, "javascript")
    }

    func test_rendersNumberedListsWithCorrectNumbering() throws {
        let text = "1. First item\n2. Second item\n3. Third item"
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: text))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "First item"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "Second item"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "Third item"))
    }

    // MARK: - Tool Result Cards

    func test_rendersPropertySearchResultsAsPropertyCardViews() throws {
        let message = ChatMessage.mock(
            toolResults: [
                ToolResult(
                    tool: "property_search",
                    data: .propertySearch(results: [
                        .init(id: "p_1", address: "123 Main St", price: 500000, beds: 3, baths: 2),
                        .init(id: "p_2", address: "456 Oak Ave", price: 750000, beds: 4, baths: 3),
                    ])
                )
            ]
        )
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: message)
        let cards = try sut.inspect().findAll(PropertyCardView.self)
        XCTAssertEqual(cards.count, 2)
    }

    func test_rendersContactLookupResultAsContactCardView() throws {
        let message = ChatMessage.mock(
            toolResults: [
                ToolResult(
                    tool: "contact_lookup",
                    data: .contactLookup(contact: .init(id: "c_1", name: "Jane Doe", phone: "+14155551234"))
                )
            ]
        )
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: message)
        let card = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contact-card-c_1")
        XCTAssertNotNil(card)
    }

    func test_rendersViewDetailsActionButtonOnToolResultCards() throws {
        let message = ChatMessage.mock(
            toolResults: [
                ToolResult(
                    tool: "property_search",
                    data: .propertySearch(results: [
                        .init(id: "p_1", address: "123 Main St", price: 500000, beds: 3, baths: 2)
                    ])
                )
            ]
        )
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: message)
        let button = try sut.inspect().find(text: "View Details")
        XCTAssertNotNil(button)
    }

    func test_viewDetailsNavigatesToCorrectDetailScreen() throws {
        let mockRouter = MockRouter()
        let message = ChatMessage.mock(
            toolResults: [
                ToolResult(
                    tool: "property_search",
                    data: .propertySearch(results: [
                        .init(id: "p_1", address: "123 Main St", price: 500000, beds: 3, baths: 2)
                    ])
                )
            ]
        )
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: message)
            .environmentObject(mockRouter)
        try sut.inspect().find(text: "View Details").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(mockRouter.navigatedTo, .propertyDetail(id: "p_1"))
    }

    // MARK: - Message States

    func test_rendersSendingIndicatorWhenStatusIsSending() throws {
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(status: .sending))
        let indicator = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "sending-indicator")
        XCTAssertNotNil(indicator)
    }

    func test_rendersErrorStateWithRetryButtonWhenStatusIsError() throws {
        var retryCalled = false
        let sut = ChatMessageView(
            message: .mock(status: .error),
            onRetry: { retryCalled = true }
        )
        let failedText = try sut.inspect().find(text: "Failed to send")
        XCTAssertNotNil(failedText)
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "retry-button").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertTrue(retryCalled)
    }

    func test_rendersStreamingDotsWhenMessageIsStreamingAndTextIsEmpty() throws {
        let sut = ChatMessageView(message: .mock(text: "", isStreaming: true))
        let indicator = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "streaming-indicator")
        XCTAssertNotNil(indicator)
    }
}
```

```swift
// Tests/Views/PropertyCardTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class PropertyCardTests: XCTestCase {

    let mockProperty = UIProperty.mock(
        id: "p_1",
        addressOneLine: "123 Main St, San Francisco, CA 94105",
        priceFormatted: "$1,250,000",
        beds: 3,
        baths: 2,
        sqft: 1800,
        photos: ["https://cdn.example.com/photo1.jpg"],
        streetViewUrl: "https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/streetview?...",
        status: "active",
        daysOnMarket: 14
    )

    func test_rendersPropertyImageFromPhotosArray() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        let asyncImage = try sut.inspect().find(ViewType.AsyncImage.self)
        XCTAssertNotNil(asyncImage)
    }

    func test_fallsBackToStreetViewImageWhenPhotosArrayIsEmpty() throws {
        let noPhotos = mockProperty.with(photos: [])
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: noPhotos)
        let asyncImage = try sut.inspect().find(ViewType.AsyncImage.self)
        XCTAssertNotNil(asyncImage)
    }

    func test_rendersFormattedAddress() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        let text = try sut.inspect().find(text: "123 Main St, San Francisco, CA 94105")
        XCTAssertNotNil(text)
    }

    func test_rendersFormattedPrice() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        let text = try sut.inspect().find(text: "$1,250,000")
        XCTAssertNotNil(text)
    }

    func test_rendersBedBathSqftMetrics() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "3 bed"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "2 bath"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "1,800 sqft"))
    }

    func test_rendersDaysOnMarketBadge() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "14 days"))
    }

    func test_rendersStatusBadgeWithCorrectColorForActive() throws {
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty)
        let badge = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "status-badge")
        let text = try badge.text().string()
        XCTAssertEqual(text, "Active")
    }

    func test_callsOnPressWithPropertyIDWhenTapped() throws {
        var pressedId: String?
        let sut = PropertyCardView(property: mockProperty, onPress: { pressedId = $0 })
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "property-card").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(pressedId, "p_1")
    }
}
```
