##### 1.2.4 SearchBar View Tests

```swift
// Tests/Views/SearchBarTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
import Combine
@testable import Skye

final class SearchBarTests: XCTestCase {

    func test_callsOnSearchAfter300msDebounceWhenUserTypes() {
        let expectation = expectation(description: "debounced search")
        var searchedText: String?
        let sut = SearchBarViewModel(debounceMs: 300) { text in
            searchedText = text
            expectation.fulfill()
        }
        sut.searchText = "Jane"
        // Before debounce fires
        XCTAssertNil(searchedText)
        waitForExpectations(timeout: 1.0)
        XCTAssertEqual(searchedText, "Jane")
    }

    func test_resetsDebounceTimerOnEachKeystroke() {
        let expectation = expectation(description: "debounced search")
        var callCount = 0
        var lastText: String?
        let sut = SearchBarViewModel(debounceMs: 300) { text in
            callCount += 1
            lastText = text
            expectation.fulfill()
        }
        sut.searchText = "J"
        DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.2) { sut.searchText = "Ja" }
        DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.4) { sut.searchText = "Jan" }
        waitForExpectations(timeout: 2.0)
        XCTAssertEqual(callCount, 1)
        XCTAssertEqual(lastText, "Jan")
    }

    func test_showsClearButtonWhenInputHasText() throws {
        let sut = SearchBarView(onSearch: { _ in })
        // Empty state: no clear button
        let emptyView = sut.inspect(searchText: "")
        XCTAssertNil(try? emptyView.find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "search-clear-button"))
        // With text: clear button visible
        let filledView = sut.inspect(searchText: "test")
        XCTAssertNotNil(try filledView.find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "search-clear-button"))
    }

    func test_clearButtonResetsInputTextAndCallsOnSearchWithEmptyString() {
        var searchedText: String? = "initial"
        let viewModel = SearchBarViewModel(debounceMs: 0) { searchedText = $0 }
        viewModel.searchText = "test"
        viewModel.clearSearch()
        XCTAssertEqual(viewModel.searchText, "")
        XCTAssertEqual(searchedText, "")
    }

    func test_hasCorrectAccessibilityLabel() throws {
        let sut = SearchBarView(onSearch: { _ in }, placeholder: "Search contacts")
        let input = try sut.inspect().find(ViewType.TextField.self)
        let label = try input.accessibilityLabel().string()
        XCTAssertEqual(label, "Search contacts")
    }
}
```

---

##### 1.2.5 FilterChips View Tests

```swift
// Tests/Views/FilterChipsTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class FilterChipsTests: XCTestCase {

    let filters: [FilterOption] = [
        .init(key: "buyer", label: "Buyer"),
        .init(key: "seller", label: "Seller"),
        .init(key: "hot", label: "Hot Lead"),
        .init(key: "cold", label: "Cold Lead"),
    ]

    func test_rendersAllFilterChips() throws {
        let sut = FilterChipsView(filters: filters, selected: [], onSelectionChange: { _ in })
        for filter in filters {
            XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: filter.label))
        }
    }

    func test_appliesActiveVisualStyleToSelectedChips() throws {
        let sut = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters,
            selected: ["buyer"],
            onSelectionChange: { _ in }
        )
        let chip = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "filter-chip-buyer")
        XCTAssertNotNil(chip)
        // Selected chip should have a distinct background
    }

    func test_supportsMultiSelectTappingUnselectedChipAddsToSelection() throws {
        var received: [String]?
        let sut = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters,
            selected: ["buyer"],
            onSelectionChange: { received = $0 }
        )
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "filter-chip-seller").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(received, ["buyer", "seller"])
    }

    func test_tappingSelectedChipRemovesItFromSelection() throws {
        var received: [String]?
        let sut = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters,
            selected: ["buyer", "seller"],
            onSelectionChange: { received = $0 }
        )
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "filter-chip-buyer").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(received, ["seller"])
    }

    func test_rendersClearAllButtonWhenFiltersAreSelected() throws {
        let emptySelection = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters, selected: [], onSelectionChange: { _ in }
        )
        XCTAssertNil(try? emptySelection.inspect().find(text: "Clear All"))

        let withSelection = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters, selected: ["buyer"], onSelectionChange: { _ in }
        )
        XCTAssertNotNil(try withSelection.inspect().find(text: "Clear All"))
    }

    func test_clearAllResetsSelectionToEmptyArray() throws {
        var received: [String]?
        let sut = FilterChipsView(
            filters: filters,
            selected: ["buyer", "hot"],
            onSelectionChange: { received = $0 }
        )
        try sut.inspect().find(text: "Clear All").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertEqual(received, [])
    }
}
```

---

##### 1.2.6 InputBar View Tests

```swift
// Tests/Views/InputBarTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class InputBarTests: XCTestCase {

    func test_rendersInCollapsedSingleLineStateByDefault() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        XCTAssertTrue(viewModel.isSingleLine)
    }

    func test_expandsToMultiLineWhenTextExceedsOneLine() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        viewModel.text = String(repeating: "A very long text ", count: 20)
        viewModel.updateHeight(contentHeight: 88)
        XCTAssertFalse(viewModel.isSingleLine)
    }

    func test_collapsesBackToSingleLineWhenTextIsCleared() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        viewModel.text = "Some text"
        viewModel.updateHeight(contentHeight: 88)
        viewModel.text = ""
        viewModel.updateHeight(contentHeight: 22)
        XCTAssertTrue(viewModel.isSingleLine)
    }

    func test_sendButtonIsHiddenWhenInputIsEmpty() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        XCTAssertFalse(viewModel.canSend)
    }

    func test_sendButtonAppearsWhenInputHasNonWhitespaceText() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        viewModel.text = "   "
        XCTAssertFalse(viewModel.canSend)
        viewModel.text = "Hello"
        XCTAssertTrue(viewModel.canSend)
    }

    func test_sendButtonCallsOnSendWithTrimmedTextAndClearsInput() throws {
        var sentText: String?
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { sentText = $0 })
        viewModel.text = "  Hello world  "
        viewModel.send()
        XCTAssertEqual(sentText, "Hello world")
        XCTAssertEqual(viewModel.text, "")
    }

    func test_attachmentButtonTriggersOnAttachmentCallback() throws {
        var attachmentCalled = false
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(
            onSend: { _ in },
            onAttachment: { attachmentCalled = true }
        )
        viewModel.triggerAttachment()
        XCTAssertTrue(attachmentCalled)
    }

    func test_disablesSendButtonAndInputWhileSendingPropIsTrue() throws {
        let viewModel = InputBarViewModel(onSend: { _ in })
        viewModel.text = "Hello"
        viewModel.isSending = true
        XCTAssertFalse(viewModel.canSend) // Disabled while sending
        XCTAssertFalse(viewModel.isEditable) // Input not editable
    }
}
```

---

##### 1.2.7 Screen-Level View Tests (4-State Pattern)

Every screen follows this template. Here is the People screen as the canonical example:

```swift
// Tests/Screens/PeopleScreenTests.swift
import XCTest
import ViewInspector
@testable import Skye

final class PeopleScreenTests: XCTestCase {

    // MARK: - Loading State

    func test_loading_rendersSkeletonPlaceholdersWhileContactsAreLoading() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .loading, data: nil, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        let skeletons = try sut.inspect().findAll(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: /^skeleton-contact-item-/)
        XCTAssertEqual(skeletons.count, 10)
    }

    func test_loading_doesNotRenderSearchBarDuringInitialLoad() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .loading, data: nil, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNil(try? sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "search-bar"))
    }

    // MARK: - Success State

    func test_success_rendersListWithAllContacts() throws {
        let contacts = UIContact.mockArray(count: 50)
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: contacts, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        let list = try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contacts-list")
        XCTAssertNotNil(list)
    }

    func test_success_rendersSearchBarAboveTheList() throws {
        let contacts = UIContact.mockArray(count: 5)
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: contacts, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "search-bar"))
    }

    func test_success_rendersFilterChipsBelowSearchBar() throws {
        let contacts = UIContact.mockArray(count: 5)
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: contacts, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "filter-chips"))
    }

    func test_success_rendersSectionHeadersWithAlphabeticalGrouping() throws {
        let contacts = [
            UIContact.mock(name: .init(first: "Alice", last: "Adams", display: "Alice Adams", initials: "AA")),
            UIContact.mock(name: .init(first: "Bob", last: "Brown", display: "Bob Brown", initials: "BB")),
        ]
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: contacts, error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "A"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "B"))
    }

    // MARK: - Empty State

    func test_empty_rendersEmptyStateIllustrationAndMessage() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: [], error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "empty-state-illustration"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "No contacts yet"))
    }

    func test_empty_rendersAddContactCTAButton() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: .success, data: [], error: nil)
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "Add Contact"))
    }

    func test_empty_rendersEmptySearchResultsState() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(
            status: .success, data: [], error: nil, searchQuery: "xyznotfound"
        )
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "No contacts match \"xyznotfound\""))
    }

    // MARK: - Error State

    func test_error_rendersErrorMessageAndRetryButton() throws {
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(
            status: .error,
            data: nil,
            error: AppError(type: .server, userMessage: "Something went wrong")
        )
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "Something went wrong"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "retry-button"))
    }

    func test_error_retryButtonTriggersDataRefetch() throws {
        var refetchCalled = false
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(
            status: .error,
            data: nil,
            error: AppError(type: .network),
            refetch: { refetchCalled = true }
        )
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "retry-button").callOnTapGesture()
        XCTAssertTrue(refetchCalled)
    }

    func test_error_showsOfflineBannerWithCachedData() throws {
        let contacts = UIContact.mockArray(count: 10) // Stale cached data
        let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(
            status: .error,
            data: contacts,
            error: AppError(type: .network)
        )
        let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "offline-banner"))
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(text: "You're offline. Showing cached data."))
        // List should still render with stale data
        XCTAssertNotNil(try sut.inspect().find(viewWithAccessibilityIdentifier: "contacts-list"))
    }

    func test_error_neverRendersBlankScreen() throws {
        // Test with every possible state combination
        let states: [(DataStatus, [UIContact]?, AppError?)] = [
            (.loading, nil, nil),
            (.success, [], nil),
            (.success, UIContact.mockArray(count: 1), nil),
            (.error, nil, AppError(type: .server)),
            (.error, UIContact.mockArray(count: 5), AppError(type: .network)),
        ]
        for (status, data, error) in states {
            let viewModel = PeopleViewModel.mock(status: status, data: data, error: error)
            let sut = PeopleScreen(viewModel: viewModel)
            let body = try sut.inspect()
            // Body should have at least one visible child
            XCTAssertTrue(body.count > 0, "Blank screen detected for state: \(status)")
        }
    }
}
```

The same 4-state pattern applies to: `ChatScreenTests.swift`, `PropertiesScreenTests.swift`, `ContactDetailScreenTests.swift`, `StudioScreenTests.swift`, `HomeScreenTests.swift`.

---

#### 1.3 End-to-End Tests (15% of Test Effort)

**Tooling:** Maestro (version 1.36+). YAML-based, works natively with iOS apps, supports visual assertions. Alternatively, XCUITest for tighter Xcode integration.

**CI execution:** Run against staging backend (`staging.skye-app.com`) with seeded test data. Triggered on every PR merge to `main` and nightly at 02:00 UTC.

---

##### 1.3.1 E2E: Auth Flow

```yaml
# e2e/auth-flow.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "Auth Flow - Sign in with Google and verify home screen"
tags:
  - critical
  - auth
---
- launchApp:
    clearState: true
    clearKeychain: true
- assertVisible: "Sign in with Google"
- tapOn: "Sign in with Google"
# For CI: use test Google account via Maestro's pre-configured auth
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- runFlow:
    when:
      visible: "Choose an account"
    commands:
      - tapOn: "skyetest@gmail.com"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "Good morning"  # Home screen greeting
- assertVisible:
    id: "user-avatar"
- assertVisible:
    id: "bottom-tab-bar"
- assertVisible: "People"
- assertVisible: "Chat"
# Verify token persistence: kill and relaunch
- stopApp
- launchApp:
    clearState: false
- assertNotVisible: "Sign in with Google"
- assertVisible: "Good morning"  # Should auto-login

# Edge case: Cancel OAuth
- launchApp:
    clearState: true
    clearKeychain: true
- tapOn: "Sign in with Google"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- runFlow:
    when:
      visible: "Choose an account"
    commands:
      - pressKey: back
- assertVisible: "Sign in with Google"  # Returns to auth screen cleanly
```

---

##### 1.3.2 E2E: Chat Flow

```yaml
# e2e/chat-flow.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "Chat Flow - Send message, receive streaming response with tool result"
tags:
  - critical
  - chat
---
- launchApp
- tapOn:
    id: "bottom-tab-chat"
- assertVisible:
    id: "chat-screen"
- tapOn:
    id: "input-bar-text"
- inputText: "Show me properties in San Francisco under $1M"
- assertVisible:
    id: "send-button"
- tapOn:
    id: "send-button"
# Verify user message appears
- assertVisible: "Show me properties in San Francisco under $1M"
# Wait for streaming response (first token within 2s)
- waitForAnimationToEnd:
    timeout: 5000
- assertVisible:
    id: "assistant-message"
    timeout: 5000
# Verify tool result card appears
- assertVisible:
    id: "tool-result-property-search"
    timeout: 10000
# Verify property card data
- assertVisible:
    text: ".*San Francisco.*"
    regex: true
- assertVisible:
    text: ".*\\$.*"
    regex: true
# Tap "View Details" on first property result
- tapOn:
    text: "View Details"
    index: 0
- assertVisible:
    id: "property-detail-screen"
- pressKey: back
- assertVisible:
    id: "chat-screen"
```

---

##### 1.3.3 E2E: Contact CRUD

```yaml
# e2e/contact-crud.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "Contact CRUD - Search, view, edit phone, verify persistence"
tags:
  - critical
  - crm
---
- launchApp
- tapOn:
    id: "bottom-tab-people"
- assertVisible:
    id: "contacts-list"
# Search for test contact
- tapOn:
    id: "search-bar"
- inputText: "Jane Testuser"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible: "Jane Testuser"
- tapOn: "Jane Testuser"
# Verify contact detail loads
- assertVisible:
    id: "contact-detail-screen"
- assertVisible: "Jane Testuser"
# Edit phone number
- scrollUntilVisible:
    element:
      id: "edit-button"
    direction: DOWN
- tapOn:
    id: "edit-button"
- tapOn:
    id: "phone-field-0"
- clearText
- inputText: "+14155559999"
- tapOn: "Save"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
# Verify change persisted
- assertVisible: "(415) 555-9999"
# Navigate away and back to verify persistence
- pressKey: back
- tapOn:
    id: "search-bar"
- clearText
- inputText: "Jane Testuser"
- tapOn: "Jane Testuser"
- assertVisible: "(415) 555-9999"
# Reset test data (update phone back to original)
- tapOn:
    id: "edit-button"
- tapOn:
    id: "phone-field-0"
- clearText
- inputText: "+14155551234"
- tapOn: "Save"
```

---

##### 1.3.4 E2E: Camera OCR Flow

```yaml
# e2e/ocr-flow.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "OCR Flow - Scan business card, review, create contact"
tags:
  - critical
  - ocr
---
- launchApp
- tapOn:
    id: "bottom-tab-people"
- tapOn:
    id: "add-contact-button"
- tapOn: "Scan Business Card"
# On CI: use test image injection via Maestro's camera mocking
- runFlow:
    env:
      MAESTRO_MOCK_CAMERA: "fixtures/business-card-sample.jpg"
    commands:
      - assertVisible:
          id: "camera-viewfinder"
      - tapOn:
          id: "capture-button"
- waitForAnimationToEnd:
    timeout: 10000
# Review extracted data
- assertVisible:
    id: "ocr-review-screen"
- assertVisible: "John Smith"     # Extracted name
- assertVisible: "Acme Realty"    # Extracted company
- assertVisible:
    text: ".*555.*"               # Extracted phone (partial match)
    regex: true
# Confirm contact creation
- tapOn: "Confirm"
- waitForAnimationToEnd
- assertVisible:
    id: "contact-detail-screen"
- assertVisible: "John Smith"
# Clean up: delete test contact
- scrollUntilVisible:
    element:
      text: "Delete Contact"
    direction: DOWN
- tapOn: "Delete Contact"
- tapOn: "Delete"  # Confirm dialog
```

---

##### 1.3.5 E2E: Push Notification Deep Link

```yaml
# e2e/push-deep-link.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "Push Deep Link - Tap notification, navigate to correct screen"
tags:
  - critical
  - notifications
---
- launchApp
# Send test notification via staging API
- runScript:
    file: "scripts/send-test-notification.swift"
    env:
      NOTIFICATION_TYPE: "new_message"
      ENTITY_TYPE: "contact"
      ENTITY_ID: "c_test_123"
      TITLE: "New message from Jane Testuser"
      BODY: "Hey, I'm interested in the listing on Main St"
# Pull down notification shade
- swipe:
    direction: DOWN
    from:
      x: 50%
      y: 0%
    to:
      x: 50%
      y: 50%
- assertVisible: "New message from Jane Testuser"
- tapOn: "New message from Jane Testuser"
# Verify correct screen opens
- assertVisible:
    id: "contact-detail-screen"
- assertVisible: "Jane Testuser"
```

---

##### 1.3.6 E2E: Offline Flow

```yaml
# e2e/offline-flow.yaml
appId: com.skye.crm
name: "Offline Flow - Browse cached data offline, refresh on reconnect"
tags:
  - critical
  - offline
---
- launchApp
# Load contacts while online to populate cache
- tapOn:
    id: "bottom-tab-people"
- assertVisible:
    id: "contacts-list"
- scroll:
    direction: DOWN
    distance: 200
# Enable airplane mode
- toggleAirplaneMode
- wait: 2000
# Verify offline banner appears
- assertVisible:
    id: "offline-banner"
# Verify cached contacts are still visible
- assertVisible:
    id: "contacts-list"
- scroll:
    direction: UP
    distance: 200
# Search should work against cached data
- tapOn:
    id: "search-bar"
- inputText: "Jane"
- assertVisible: "Jane Testuser"
# Disable airplane mode
- toggleAirplaneMode
- wait: 3000
# Verify offline banner disappears
- assertNotVisible:
    id: "offline-banner"
    timeout: 10000
# Verify data refreshes (pull to refresh)
- scroll:
    direction: DOWN
    from:
      x: 50%
      y: 20%
    to:
      x: 50%
      y: 80%
- waitForAnimationToEnd
```

---

### 2. Acceptance Test Suite

Each acceptance criterion is mapped to concrete, executable test cases with pass/fail criteria, edge cases, and automation designation.

---

#### AC1: App Builds and Runs on Simulator

| Dimension | Specification |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Test ID** | AC1-BUILD |
| **Method** | CI automated |
| **Command** | `xcodebuild -scheme Skye -configuration Release -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 15 Pro' build` |
| **Pass Criteria** | Exit code 0, no Swift compiler errors, no build warnings treated as errors |
| **Timing** | Build completes in <5 minutes on CI (M1 Mac runner) |

| Dimension | Specification |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Test ID** | AC1-RENDER |
| **Method** | Maestro E2E |
| **Script** | `e2e/ac1-first-render.yaml` |
| **Pass Criteria** | App renders first interactive screen within 5000ms of launch (measured via `launchApp` + `assertVisible` with timeout) |

| Dimension | Specification |
|-----------|--------------|
| **Test ID** | AC1-NO-CRASH |
| **Method** | Maestro E2E |
| **Script** | `e2e/ac1-stability.yaml` -- Navigate through every tab (Home, People, Chat, Properties, Studio), no crash within 60 seconds of navigation |
| **Pass Criteria** | App remains responsive, no crash log generated |

---

#### AC2: Google OAuth Authentication

| Test ID | Scenario | Steps | Pass Criteria | Automation |
|---------|----------|-------|---------------|------------|
| AC2-HAPPY | Successful sign-in | Tap "Sign in with Google" > complete Google OAuth with test account > verify home screen | Home screen visible with user name, Keychain contains valid token, `/api/crm/contacts` returns 200 | E2E: `e2e/auth-flow.yaml` |
| AC2-CANCEL | User cancels OAuth | Tap "Sign in with Google" > tap back/cancel on Google screen | App returns to auth screen, no error shown, no token in Keychain | E2E: `e2e/auth-flow.yaml` (cancel branch) |
| AC2-NETWORK | Network error during OAuth | Disconnect network before completing OAuth | Error message: "Unable to connect. Check your network and try again." Retry button visible. | View test: `AuthScreenTests.swift` (error state) |
| AC2-TOKEN-PERSIST | Token survives app restart | Sign in > kill app > relaunch | App skips auth, shows home screen directly | E2E: `e2e/auth-flow.yaml` (persistence check) |
| AC2-TOKEN-REFRESH | Token refresh on 401 | Wait for token expiry (or mock) > make API call | Token is silently refreshed, API call succeeds, user sees no interruption | Unit test: `APIClientTests.swift` (token refresh) |
| AC2-SIGNOUT | Sign out clears all data | Tap profile > sign out > confirm | Keychain cleared, cache cleared, app returns to auth screen, back gesture does not return to authenticated screens | E2E: `e2e/signout-flow.yaml` |

---

#### AC3: Chat Streaming Quality

| Test ID | Scenario | Steps | Pass Criteria | Automation |
|---------|----------|-------|---------------|------------|
| AC3-FIRST-TOKEN | Response latency | Send "hello" | First token appears within 2000ms of send (measured from send button tap to first assistant text visible) | E2E + performance measurement |
| AC3-COMPLETE | Full response integrity | Send "List 5 things to do before a home inspection" | Response contains numbered list, no dropped characters, complete sentences, matches web app output for same prompt | Manual comparison + E2E |
| AC3-MARKDOWN | Markdown rendering | Send "Show me a code example for calculating mortgage payments" | Bold text renders bold, code blocks have syntax highlighting, lists are properly numbered, links are tappable | View test: `ChatMessageTests.swift` |
| AC3-TOOL-CALL | Tool result card | Send "Find properties in Austin under $500k" | Tool result card appears with property data, "View Details" button navigates to property detail | E2E: `e2e/chat-flow.yaml` |
| AC3-LONG | Long response (2000+ tokens) | Send "Write a comprehensive market analysis for the San Francisco Bay Area real estate market" | Full response renders, scrolling is smooth (60fps), no memory spike >50MB above baseline | E2E + Instruments profiling |
| AC3-FPS | Rendering performance | Stream 100+ tokens | FPS during streaming >=55fps average measured via Xcode Instruments Time Profiler or `XCTMetric` with `XCTClockMetric` | Manual: Instruments trace |
| AC3-ERROR | Stream interruption | Kill network mid-stream | Partial response preserved, error message appended: "Response interrupted. Tap to retry.", retry resends from original message | View test + E2E |
| AC3-MULTI | Conversation continuity | Send 5 messages in sequence | All messages visible in scroll, context maintained between messages, assistant references earlier messages correctly | E2E |

---

#### AC4: People Screen (CRM Contact List)

| Test ID | Scenario | Steps | Pass Criteria | Automation |
|---------|----------|-------|---------------|------------|
| AC4-LOAD-500 | Large dataset performance | Load account with 500+ contacts | List renders within 2s, scrolling at 60fps (`XCTest.measure {}` with `XCTClockMetric` and `XCTMemoryMetric`), memory under 100MB | XCUITest performance test + Instruments |

---

## V2: Build Roadmap, Dependencies & Execution Strategy (Swift/iOS Native)

**Document Version:** 2.0
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-02
**Status:** LOCKED — governs all development decisions
**Platform:** iOS (Swift / SwiftUI native)

---

### 1. Refined Phase Structure

---

#### Phase 0: Pre-Development Setup

**Purpose:** Eliminate every "we should have done this first" moment. Zero code is written until Phase 0 passes its exit criteria.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Project has been greenlit by stakeholders
- At least one developer is allocated full-time
- Access to all required accounts (Apple Developer, Google Cloud, Supabase, Sentry) is available or in progress

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Owner |
|---|------|-----------|-------|
| 0.1 | Activate Apple Developer Program membership (paid), create App ID with bundle identifier `com.skye.crm`, enable Push Notifications and Associated Domains capabilities | None | Lead |
| 0.2 | Google Cloud Console: create OAuth 2.0 iOS client ID, register redirect URI `com.googleusercontent.apps.{CLIENT_ID}:/oauthredirect`, create web client ID (for token exchange on backend) | None | Lead |
| 0.3 | Supabase: audit all RLS policies with a mobile-issued JWT (not a web session cookie); confirm `auth.uid()` resolves correctly from a bearer token; document any policies that assume web-only session format | None | Backend |
| 0.4 | Design system finalized: color palette (light + dark), typography scale (SF Pro or custom), icon set (SF Symbols or bundled), spacing scale (4px base), component inventory (buttons, cards, inputs, avatars, badges, chips, empty states, skeleton loaders) — delivered as a Figma library or equivalent, not loose mockups | None | Design |
| 0.5 | Lock all Architecture Decision Records (ADR-1 through ADR-5, see Section 3) — no revisiting during build | 0.4 | Lead + Team |
| 0.6 | Dev environment provisioned: Xcode 16+ installed, Swift 6.0+ toolchain active, Homebrew dependencies current, at least one physical iOS device registered for development in Apple Developer portal, command-line tools (`xcode-select --install`) verified | None | All Devs |
| 0.7 | Repository initialized: Xcode project with SwiftUI App lifecycle (`@main struct SkyeApp: App`), `.gitignore` (including Xcode-specific entries), `SwiftLint` config (`.swiftlint.yml`), `Package.swift` for SPM dependencies, `.xcconfig` files for Debug/Release/Staging configurations, CI pipeline stub (GitHub Actions with `xcodebuild` or Xcode Cloud), branch protection rules on `main` | 0.6 | Lead |
| 0.8 | Sentry project created, DSN obtained, invite team members | None | Lead |
| 0.9 | Create `DECISIONS.md` in repo root documenting every ADR with date, participants, rationale, and "reversibility cost" rating | 0.5 | Lead |
| 0.10 | Backend: implement `/api/health` endpoint (returns `{ "status": "ok", "timestamp": <ISO> }`, no auth required) | None | Backend |
| 0.11 | Backend: implement `/api/auth/native` endpoint (accepts Google auth code + code verifier from native PKCE flow, exchanges for Google tokens, creates/updates Supabase user, returns access + refresh tokens) | 0.2 | Backend |
| 0.12 | Backend: implement `/api/user/delete` endpoint (Apple requires in-app account deletion; cascades through all user data, returns confirmation) | None | Backend |
| 0.13 | Backend: audit all 129 API routes for JSON-only responses (no HTML error pages), correct `Content-Type` headers, and absence of `Origin` header requirements (native apps do not send Origin) | None | Backend |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Apple Developer portal shows App ID with Push Notifications enabled
- Google Cloud Console shows iOS OAuth client ID with correct redirect URI
- A test JWT issued by Supabase (simulating mobile auth) passes RLS checks against every table the app will query
- Design system Figma (or equivalent) is marked "ready for dev" with no open comments
- All 5 ADRs are documented in `DECISIONS.md` and signed off
- `xcodebuild -scheme Skye -configuration Debug -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` succeeds on every developer's machine
- A physical iOS device can build and run the Xcode project via Xcode IDE (Product > Run) or `xcodebuild -destination 'platform=iOS,id=<DEVICE_UDID>'`
- `/api/health`, `/api/auth/native`, and `/api/user/delete` endpoints are deployed and pass integration tests
- Backend audit report confirms all routes return JSON with correct headers

**Decision Gate:**
All ADR decisions are irrevocable after Phase 0 sign-off. Changing auth library, state management, or navigation approach after this point requires a formal change request with impact analysis.

**Deliverables:**
- Configured Apple Developer portal
- Configured Google Cloud Console
- Supabase RLS audit report
- Figma design system library
- `DECISIONS.md` with 5 locked ADRs
- Repository with Xcode project, CI pipeline, SwiftLint, `.xcconfig` files
- Three new backend endpoints deployed
- Backend JSON audit report

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] `curl https://api.skye.app/api/health` returns 200 with JSON body
- [ ] Google OAuth test flow completes on iOS device using registered redirect URI
- [ ] Supabase RLS test script passes with mobile-format JWT
- [ ] `xcodebuild -scheme Skye -showBuildSettings` succeeds without errors
- [ ] All team members can build to a physical device
- [ ] `DECISIONS.md` exists and contains 5 ADR entries

---

#### Phase 1: Foundation — Scaffold, Theming, Auth, API Client, Offline Storage, State Management

**Purpose:** Build the invisible infrastructure that every subsequent phase depends on. At the end of Phase 1, the app authenticates, fetches data, stores it offline, and renders a themed shell with tab navigation — but no real screens.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 0 exit criteria fully met
- Design system tokens (colors, typography, spacing) exported and available
- `/api/auth/native` endpoint deployed and tested

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 1.1 | Create Xcode project with SwiftUI App lifecycle (`@main struct SkyeApp: App`), configure `Info.plist` (bundle ID `com.skye.crm`, URL schemes, version, icon placeholder, launch screen storyboard or SwiftUI launch screen), set up `.xcconfig` files for Debug/Release/Staging with `$(VARIABLE)` substitution for environment-specific values (API URL, Google Client IDs, Sentry DSN) | None | 2 |
| 1.2 | Build navigation structure using SwiftUI: create `RootView` with conditional rendering, `AuthNavigationStack` (NavigationStack: WelcomeView, SignInView), `MainTabView` (TabView: Chat, People, Properties, Studio), and stub placeholder views for each tab | 1.1 | 4 |
| 1.3 | Build theming system: create `Theme.swift` with light and dark `ColorScheme` tokens, typography scale using SF Pro (or custom font), spacing scale, border radii; create `ThemeManager` as an `@Observable` class; use `@Environment(\.colorScheme)` to auto-detect system preference; store user override in `@AppStorage` (UserDefaults) | 1.1 | 6 |
| 1.4 | Create `AppStorageManager.swift` utility using `UserDefaults` / `@AppStorage` with typed get/set helpers for: theme preference, onboarding state, last sync timestamps. For non-trivial key-value data, use a lightweight wrapper around `UserDefaults` with Codable support | 1.1 | 2 |
| 1.5 | Create `KeychainManager.swift` utility wrapping Keychain Services (Security framework) with `SecItemAdd`, `SecItemCopyMatching`, `SecItemUpdate`, `SecItemDelete`; store refresh tokens here (NOT in UserDefaults). Provide async/await interface | 1.1 | 2 |
| 1.6 | Add `GRDB.swift` via SPM, create database initialization module: `AppDatabase.swift` opens database, runs migration scripts on app start; create migration system (version table, sequential migration files using GRDB's `DatabaseMigrator`); create initial schema: `contacts`, `properties`, `conversations`, `messages`, `sync_meta` tables with GRDB record types conforming to `Codable`, `FetchableRecord`, `PersistableRecord` | 1.1 | 8 |
| 1.7 | Build data layer using `@Observable` pattern: create `DataManager` class with configurable cache durations (staleTime: 5 min, automatic retry: 2 attempts, refresh on network reconnect: true); implement query caching with in-memory dictionary keyed by request identifier; implement cache persistence to UserDefaults for lightweight query metadata | 1.1, 1.4 | 4 |
| 1.8 | Create `@Observable` state stores: `AuthState` (user, tokens, isAuthenticated, login/logout actions), `UIState` (theme, sheetState, toastQueue), `NetworkState` (isConnected, lastConnectedAt) | 1.1 | 4 |
| 1.9 | Build API client: create `APIClient.swift` using `URLSession` (native, no third-party HTTP library needed); implement request interceptor that attaches bearer token from `AuthState`; implement response interceptor that catches 401, attempts token refresh via `/api/auth/refresh`, retries original request, or forces logout on failure; implement request queuing to prevent concurrent refresh races (actor-based isolation pattern) | 1.8, 1.5 | 8 |
| 1.10 | Build auth flow: implement Google Sign-In using `ASWebAuthenticationSession` with PKCE; on successful Google auth, send auth code to `/api/auth/native`; receive Supabase tokens; store access token in `AuthState` (memory), refresh token in Keychain via `KeychainManager`; navigate to `MainTabView`; implement logout (clear all state, clear Keychain, navigate to `AuthNavigationStack`) | 1.2, 1.8, 1.9, 1.5 | 10 |
| 1.11 | Build network connectivity monitor: use `NWPathMonitor` (Network framework) to detect connectivity changes; update `NetworkState`; show a non-intrusive banner when offline; queue mutations when offline | 1.8 | 3 |
| 1.12 | Build error boundary: create `ErrorBoundaryView` modifier that catches errors per-tab, logs to Sentry, shows "Something went wrong" view with retry button; wrap each tab independently (one tab crashing does not kill others). Use Swift's `Result` type and `do/catch` for structured error propagation | 1.1 | 3 |
| 1.13 | Add `sentry-cocoa` via SPM (getsentry/sentry-cocoa), configure in `SkyeApp.init()`: DSN, environment, release tracking; add Sentry breadcrumb integration for navigation tracking via a custom `NavigationObserver` | 1.1, 1.2 | 3 |
| 1.14 | Build splash/loading screen: animated Skye logo during app initialization (auth check, database migration, cache hydration) using SwiftUI animations; transition to auth view or main tabs based on stored auth state | 1.3, 1.10 | 4 |
| 1.15 | Create shared UI primitives: `SkyeText` (themed, variant-based using `ViewModifier`), `SkyeButton` (primary, secondary, ghost, destructive, loading state), `SkyeCard`, `SkyeAvatar`, `SkyeBadge`, `SkeletonLoader`, `EmptyStateView`, `ErrorStateView` — all with dark mode support, accessibility labels, and `.accessibilityIdentifier()` for testing | 1.3 | 10 |
| 1.16 | Create 4-state screen wrapper: `ScreenContainer<Content: View>` component that accepts loading, error, empty, and data states and renders the appropriate state (skeleton, error with retry, empty with CTA, or content); enforces the "every screen has 4 states" principle using a `LoadState` enum | 1.15, 1.12 | 4 |
| 1.17 | Configure build pipeline: create Xcode schemes for Debug, Staging, and Release; set up GitHub Actions workflow or Xcode Cloud with `xcodebuild` commands for CI builds; run first Debug build to physical device; verify Swift compilation and linking completes cleanly | 1.1 | 3 |
| 1.18 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: API client (token attachment, refresh flow, 401 handling), auth state (login, logout, token rotation), UserDefaults utilities, Keychain utilities, theme manager (light/dark switching) | 1.9, 1.10, 1.4, 1.5, 1.3 | 6 |
| 1.19 | Write first Maestro E2E flow (or XCUITest): app launches, splash screen appears, transitions to sign-in screen, Google auth button is visible and tappable | 1.14, 1.17 | 3 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- App launches on a physical iOS device within 2 seconds
- User can sign in with Google, see the tab bar, and sign out
- Token refresh works when access token expires (tested with a short-lived token)
- Offline banner appears when device enters airplane mode
- Theme switches between light and dark (follows system and manual toggle)
- GRDB database is created with correct schema on first launch
- Sentry receives a test error event
- All unit tests pass
- Maestro / XCUITest E2E flow passes

**Decision Gate:**
Confirm that the auth flow works end-to-end with the production Supabase instance (not just local). Confirm API client handles all error codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 500) gracefully.

**Deliverables:**
- Functioning app shell with tab navigation
- Complete auth flow (sign in, sign out, token refresh)
- Theming system (light + dark)
- API client with interceptors
- Offline storage layer (GRDB + UserDefaults + Keychain)
- State management (`@Observable` classes)
- Error boundary + Sentry integration
- Shared UI component library
- Unit + E2E test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Cold start to interactive < 2 seconds (measured with Xcode Instruments)
- [ ] Sign in with Google completes without errors
- [ ] Manually expire access token; next API call triggers refresh and succeeds
- [ ] Sign out clears all persisted state (verify Keychain, UserDefaults, GRDB)
- [ ] Toggle system appearance; app theme updates immediately
- [ ] Enable airplane mode; offline banner appears within 3 seconds
- [ ] Trigger a Swift error; verify it appears in Sentry dashboard
- [ ] `xcodebuild test -scheme Skye -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` passes with >80% coverage on foundation modules
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flow runs green on physical device

---

#### Phase 2: Chat Screen — SSE Streaming, Markdown, Tool Cards, Input Bar

**Purpose:** Build the most important and technically complex screen in the app. The chat screen is the core product experience — it must feel native, fast, and reliable.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 1 exit criteria fully met
- API client with auth interceptors is working
- Backend chat/streaming endpoint is documented and accessible

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 2.1 | Create `ChatView` with `ScreenContainer` wrapper; implement 4 states: loading (skeleton chat bubbles), empty (welcome message + suggested prompts), error (retry button), success (message list) | None | 4 |
| 2.2 | Build `MessageListView` using SwiftUI `List` with `ScrollViewReader` for virtualized performance; messages render from bottom using `.defaultScrollAnchor(.bottom)`; handle scroll-to-bottom on new messages; preserve scroll position when loading older messages | 2.1 | 8 |
| 2.3 | Build `UserBubbleView` component: right-aligned, themed background, text content, timestamp, sent/delivered/error status indicator | 2.1 | 3 |
| 2.4 | Build `AssistantBubbleView` component: left-aligned, themed background, supports streaming text (character-by-character append using `@Observable` model without re-render of entire list), typing indicator animation during initial response delay | 2.1 | 6 |
| 2.5 | Implement SSE streaming client: create `StreamingChatService` actor using `URLSession` bytes streaming API (`URLSession.AsyncBytes`); connect to backend streaming endpoint with auth header; handle events: `message_start`, `content_delta`, `content_stop`, `tool_use`, `error`; implement reconnection with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 30s); handle mid-stream disconnection (preserve partial response, resume or restart); track connection state in `@Observable` stream state | 1.9, 2.4 | 12 |
| 2.6 | Build markdown renderer for assistant messages: use `swift-markdown` (apple/swift-markdown) SPM package to parse markdown AST and render with `AttributedString`; support: bold, italic, code inline, code blocks (with monospaced font and background), bulleted lists, numbered lists, headers, links (open in `SFSafariViewController`), tables (basic); ensure all markdown elements are themed (light/dark) | 2.4 | 8 |
| 2.7 | Build `ToolCardView` component system: when the assistant invokes a tool (contact lookup, property search, schedule event), render a structured card inside the chat; card types: `ContactToolCard` (avatar, name, phone, tap to navigate), `PropertyToolCard` (thumbnail, address, price, tap to navigate), `ActionToolCard` (generic action confirmation); cards are rendered inline in the message flow, not as separate messages | 2.4 | 8 |
| 2.8 | Build `ChatInputBar`: multi-line `TextField` (or `TextEditor`) with auto-grow (max 4 lines then scroll), send button (disabled while streaming), attachment button (placeholder for Phase 6), haptic feedback on send using `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator`; keyboard-aware positioning using SwiftUI's built-in keyboard avoidance (`.scrollDismissesKeyboard(.interactively)`) | 2.1 | 6 |
| 2.9 | Implement conversation persistence: save messages to GRDB `messages` table as they arrive (both user and assistant); on app relaunch, load conversation from GRDB and hydrate the message list; implement pagination (load 50 messages at a time, load more on scroll-up) using GRDB's `ValueObservation` for reactive updates | 1.6, 2.2 | 6 |
| 2.10 | Implement conversation list/management: create `ConversationListView` (accessible from chat header); list all conversations with title, last message preview, timestamp; swipe to delete using `.swipeActions()`; tap to switch conversation; "New Chat" button | 2.9 | 6 |
| 2.11 | Implement suggested prompts: on empty chat, show 4-6 contextual prompt suggestions (e.g., "Draft a follow-up email", "Find contacts in 90210", "What's my schedule today?"); tapping a suggestion sends it as a user message | 2.1, 2.8 | 3 |
| 2.12 | Implement chat memory pressure management: for conversations exceeding 200 messages, only keep the most recent 200 in memory; older messages are in GRDB only, loaded on demand when scrolling up; implement estimated row heights for smooth scroll performance | 2.2, 2.9 | 4 |
| 2.13 | Implement streaming error recovery: if SSE connection drops mid-response, show "Connection lost" inline with retry button; if retry succeeds, append new content after the partial response; if retry fails 3 times, show persistent error with "Copy partial response" option | 2.5 | 4 |
| 2.14 | Add haptic feedback: light impact on message send, medium impact on tool card appearance, selection feedback on prompt tap using `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator` and `UISelectionFeedbackGenerator` | 2.8, 2.7, 2.11 | 2 |
| 2.15 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: SSE event parsing, markdown rendering edge cases, message persistence CRUD, conversation management, token attachment on SSE connection | 2.5, 2.6, 2.9, 2.10 | 6 |
| 2.16 | Write Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows: send a message and receive a streamed response; scroll through long conversation; switch conversations; test offline behavior (send while offline, message queues) | 2.15 | 4 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- User can send a message and see a streamed response appear token by token
- Markdown renders correctly (bold, italic, code, lists, links)
- Tool cards render inline and are tappable (navigation wired in Phase 4)
- Conversations persist across app restarts
- Long conversations (200+ messages) scroll smoothly at 60 FPS
- SSE reconnects automatically after network interruption
- Empty, loading, and error states all render correctly
- Dark mode renders all chat elements correctly

**Decision Gate:**
Performance benchmark: measure FPS during rapid streaming on oldest supported device (iPhone SE 3rd gen / iOS 16). If below 50 FPS, investigate and resolve before proceeding. Determine if `List` row recycling needs per-message-type height estimation or if `LazyVStack` with manual recycling provides better performance.

**Deliverables:**
- Complete chat screen with streaming
- Conversation persistence layer
- Conversation list/management
- Markdown renderer
- Tool card system
- Chat input bar
- Test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Send 10 messages in rapid succession; all responses stream correctly
- [ ] Paste a response with markdown (bold, code block, list, link) — all render correctly
- [ ] Tool card appears when assistant invokes a tool; card is tappable
- [ ] Kill app during active stream; relaunch; partial response is preserved
- [ ] Scroll through 500-message conversation at 60 FPS (measure with Instruments Core Animation)
- [ ] Enable airplane mode mid-stream; "Connection lost" appears; disable airplane mode; retry succeeds
- [ ] Switch to dark mode; all chat elements are legible and correctly themed
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flows pass on physical device

---

#### Phase 3: People Tab — Contact List, Search, Filters, Swipe Actions, Offline Cache

**Purpose:** Build the CRM backbone. Real estate agents live in their contacts — this screen must be fast, searchable, and work offline.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 2 exit criteria fully met (or Phase 1 exit criteria met — Phase 3 can begin after Phase 1 if resources allow parallel work, but the developer must not be the same person building Phase 2)
- Backend `/api/contacts` endpoint is documented and accessible

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 3.1 | Create `PeopleView` with `ScreenContainer` wrapper; implement 4 states | None | 3 |
| 3.2 | Build `ContactListView` using SwiftUI `List` with sections; render `ContactRowView` components: avatar (initials fallback), full name, company/role, last interaction date, lead status badge; section headers by alphabetical grouping (A, B, C...); fast scroll index strip using `ScrollViewReader` with `sectionIndexTitles` style sidebar | 3.1 | 8 |
| 3.3 | Implement contact data fetching with `@Observable` data layer: `ContactsRepository` class calls `/api/contacts` with pagination (cursor-based, 50 per page); infinite scroll using `onAppear` on sentinel rows; data is cached in memory and persisted to GRDB for offline access | 1.7, 1.6 | 6 |
| 3.4 | Implement offline-first sync: on app launch or pull-to-refresh, fetch contacts from API and upsert into GRDB; when offline, query GRDB directly; track `last_synced_at` per entity in `sync_meta` table; show "Last synced: X minutes ago" in header when offline | 3.3, 1.11 | 8 |
| 3.5 | Build search: SwiftUI `.searchable()` modifier with debounced input (300ms using `Combine` or structured concurrency `Task.sleep`); search executes against local GRDB (LIKE query on name, email, phone, company) for instant results; simultaneously hits `/api/contacts/search` for server results if online; merge and deduplicate results | 3.2, 3.3 | 6 |
| 3.6 | Build filter system: filter chips below search bar — by lead status (Lead, Client, Past Client, Vendor), by tag, by last interaction date range; filters apply to both local GRDB query and API query; multiple filters combine with AND logic; active filter count shown on chip | 3.5 | 6 |
| 3.7 | Implement swipe actions using SwiftUI `.swipeActions()` modifier: swipe trailing reveals "Call" (green) and "Message" (blue) actions; swipe leading reveals "Archive" (yellow) and "Delete" (red, `.destructive` role) actions; haptic feedback on action trigger using `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator` | 3.2 | 6 |
| 3.8 | Implement pull-to-refresh: SwiftUI `.refreshable {}` async modifier on the `List`; triggers full sync with server; shows success/failure toast on completion | 3.3 | 2 |
| 3.9 | Build Add Contact FAB: floating action button (bottom-right, above tab bar) using `ZStack` overlay; tapping opens `AddContactSheet` (`.sheet()` with `.presentationDetents([.medium, .large])`): name, email, phone, company, lead status; validates required fields; submits to `/api/contacts` and inserts into GRDB | 3.1 | 5 |
| 3.10 | Implement "quick actions" from contact row: long-press reveals context menu using SwiftUI `.contextMenu {}` modifier (call, text, email, copy phone, copy email) | 3.2 | 4 |
| 3.11 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: contact sync logic, search ranking, filter combination, swipe action handlers, add contact validation | 3.4, 3.5, 3.6, 3.7, 3.9 | 5 |
| 3.12 | Write Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows: scroll contact list, search for contact, apply filter, swipe to call, add new contact, pull to refresh, test offline mode (airplane mode shows cached contacts) | 3.11 | 4 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Contact list loads and displays 500+ contacts smoothly (60 FPS scroll)
- Search returns results within 200ms for local queries
- Filters narrow the list correctly (verified with known test data)
- Swipe actions work reliably on first attempt
- App shows cached contacts when offline
- Pull-to-refresh syncs new contacts from server
- Add contact form validates and submits correctly
- All 4 screen states render correctly

**Decision Gate:**
Evaluate whether GRDB FTS (Full-Text Search via FTS5) is needed for search performance at scale (1000+ contacts). If search latency exceeds 200ms with LIKE queries, implement FTS5 virtual table using GRDB's FTS support.

**Deliverables:**
- Complete People tab with list, search, filters
- Offline sync system
- Swipe actions
- Add contact flow
- Test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Load 500 contacts; scroll at 60 FPS (Instruments Core Animation)
- [ ] Search "john" returns results in <200ms
- [ ] Apply "Lead" filter; only leads are shown
- [ ] Apply "Lead" + tag "Buyer"; intersection is shown
- [ ] Swipe right on contact; "Call" action opens phone dialer with correct number
- [ ] Enable airplane mode; contacts still display with "offline" indicator
- [ ] Pull to refresh while online; new contacts appear
- [ ] Add a contact; it appears in the list immediately
- [ ] Long-press contact; context menu appears with correct actions
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flows pass on physical device

---

#### Phase 4: Detail Screens — Contact Detail, Properties List, Property Detail

**Purpose:** Build the detail/drill-down layer. Users tap into contacts and properties from lists and from chat tool cards.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 3 exit criteria met (contact list is operational)
- Backend `/api/contacts/:id`, `/api/properties`, `/api/properties/:id` endpoints are documented

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 4.1 | Build `ContactDetailView`: header with avatar (large), name, role, company; action row (call, text, email, share) using `HStack` of `Button` views; detail sections: contact info, address, tags, notes, lead status, source, created/updated dates; all data from GRDB (offline) or API (online); edit mode toggle using `@State var isEditing` | None | 8 |
| 4.2 | Build contact edit mode: inline editing of all fields using SwiftUI `TextField` / `Picker`; save button validates and PATCHes to `/api/contacts/:id`; optimistic update in GRDB and in-memory cache; revert on failure with toast notification | 4.1 | 6 |
| 4.3 | Build contact activity timeline: `ActivityTimelineView` component below contact details; shows chronological list of interactions (calls, emails, meetings, notes, AI conversations); each entry: icon (SF Symbol), title, timestamp, preview; data from `/api/contacts/:id/activity`; paginated (load 20 at a time) | 4.1 | 6 |
| 4.4 | Build contact notes section: list of notes with add/edit/delete; notes stored in GRDB and synced to API; markdown support in note body using `AttributedString`; timestamps and "by" attribution | 4.1 | 5 |
| 4.5 | Wire chat tool cards to contact detail: `ContactToolCard` in chat navigates to `ContactDetailView` with `contactId` param via `NavigationLink` or programmatic navigation; back button returns to chat | 2.7, 4.1 | 2 |
| 4.6 | Build `PropertiesView` (Properties tab): 4-state screen; SwiftUI `List` of property cards; each card: hero image (using `AsyncImage` or Nuke SPM for caching + progressive loading), address, price, beds/baths/sqft, status badge (Active, Pending, Sold, Off-Market); sort options (price, date listed, status) via `Menu` picker; pull-to-refresh with `.refreshable {}` | None | 8 |
| 4.7 | Implement property data fetching and offline cache: `PropertiesRepository` with `@Observable` pattern + GRDB sync (same pattern as contacts) | 1.7, 1.6 | 4 |
| 4.8 | Build property search and filters: search by address/MLS number using `.searchable()`; filters: status, price range (SwiftUI `Slider`), beds (min), baths (min), sqft range; same local + server search pattern as contacts | 4.6 | 6 |
| 4.9 | Build `PropertyDetailView`: image gallery (horizontal `TabView` with `.tabViewStyle(.page)` and page dots, `MagnifyGesture` for pinch-to-zoom), property info section (address, price, beds/baths/sqft, lot size, year built, MLS number), description (collapsible `DisclosureGroup` if long), listing agent info, status history, associated contacts (linked buyers/sellers with `NavigationLink` to navigate) | 4.6 | 10 |
| 4.10 | Wire chat tool cards to property detail: `PropertyToolCard` in chat navigates to `PropertyDetailView` with `propertyId` param | 2.7, 4.9 | 2 |
| 4.11 | Build share functionality: share contact (vCard format) or property (formatted text with link) via `ShareLink` (SwiftUI) or `UIActivityViewController` | 4.1, 4.9 | 3 |
| 4.12 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: contact detail rendering, edit/save flow, activity timeline pagination, property list rendering, property filters, image gallery | All above | 6 |
| 4.13 | Write Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows: navigate to contact detail from People list, edit a field and save, navigate to property detail from Properties tab, swipe through property images, navigate to contact detail from chat tool card | 4.12 | 4 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Contact detail screen loads in <500ms from tap
- Contact edit saves correctly (optimistic update + server sync)
- Activity timeline loads and paginates
- Property list renders with images loading progressively (blur-up placeholder)
- Property detail image gallery supports swipe and pinch-to-zoom
- All navigation paths work (list to detail, chat to detail, back)
- Offline: detail screens show cached data with "offline" indicator

**Decision Gate:**
Decide whether property images need aggressive pre-caching (disk cache budget) or lazy loading is sufficient. Measure image memory pressure on iPhone SE (2 GB RAM).

**Deliverables:**
- Contact detail screen with edit, activity timeline, notes
- Properties list screen with search and filters
- Property detail screen with image gallery
- Navigation wiring from chat tool cards
- Test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Tap contact in People list; detail screen appears in <500ms
- [ ] Edit contact phone number; save; verify change persists after app restart
- [ ] Activity timeline shows 20 items; scroll down triggers load of next 20
- [ ] Properties list loads with images; no blank white squares during load
- [ ] Property detail: swipe through 5 images; pinch-to-zoom on one
- [ ] Tap `ContactToolCard` in chat; navigates to contact detail; press back; returns to chat
- [ ] Tap `PropertyToolCard` in chat; navigates to property detail; press back; returns to chat
- [ ] Share contact; share sheet opens with formatted vCard
- [ ] Airplane mode: all detail screens show cached data
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flows pass on physical device

---

#### Phase 5: Push Notifications & Deep Links

**Purpose:** Make Skye proactive. Notifications bring users back into the app; deep links take them to the right screen.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 1 exit criteria met (auth and navigation working)
- Phase 2 and Phase 3 exit criteria met (screens exist to deep link into)
- Backend push subscribe and send endpoints are ready (see Section 5)
- APNs key (.p8 file) is uploaded to backend push service or configured in Xcode project

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 5.1 | Implement push notification registration using `UserNotifications` framework (`UNUserNotificationCenter`): request permission on first launch (with pre-permission prompt explaining value), register for remote notifications via `UIApplication.shared.registerForRemoteNotifications()`, obtain native APNs device token in `AppDelegate` or via `UIApplicationDelegateAdaptor` | None | 4 |
| 5.2 | Backend: modify `/api/push/subscribe` to accept `{ token, platform: "ios", tokenType: "apns" }` alongside existing web push subscriptions; store with platform discriminator; deduplicate tokens per user-device pair | None (backend) | 4 |
| 5.3 | Backend: modify `/api/push/send` to route to APNs (directly or via a push service) for iOS tokens, Web Push for browser tokens; implement provider abstraction | 5.2 | 4 |
| 5.4 | Implement token registration flow: after auth, call `/api/push/subscribe` with device token; re-register on token change (via `application(_:didRegisterForRemoteNotificationsWithDeviceToken:)`); unregister on logout | 5.1, 5.2 | 3 |
| 5.5 | Implement notification categories: define `UNNotificationCategory` instances for notification types (new_message, contact_update, property_alert, reminder, system); each type has: title template, body template, category identifier, action buttons (`UNNotificationAction`) if applicable; register categories with `UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setNotificationCategories()` | 5.1 | 4 |
| 5.6 | Implement foreground notification handling: conform to `UNUserNotificationCenterDelegate`, implement `userNotificationCenter(_:willPresent:)` to show/hide notifications based on current screen (don't show "new message" notification if user is already on chat screen); configure custom sound for high-priority notifications via `UNNotificationSound` | 5.1, 5.5 | 4 |
| 5.7 | Implement notification tap handling: implement `userNotificationCenter(_:didReceive:withCompletionHandler:)` to extract `userInfo` payload (type, entityId); route to correct screen (chat, contact detail, property detail) via navigation state; handle cold start (app was killed, user taps notification, app launches and navigates directly) by storing pending navigation in `@Observable` state | 5.6, 1.2 | 6 |
| 5.8 | Implement deep linking: configure URL scheme (`skye://`) in `Info.plist` under `CFBundleURLTypes` and universal links (`https://app.skye.com/...`) via Associated Domains entitlement and `apple-app-site-association` file; handle incoming URLs via `.onOpenURL {}` modifier in SwiftUI, parsing URL paths to navigate to screens (`/chat/:conversationId`, `/contacts/:contactId`, `/properties/:propertyId`); handle auth-gated deep links (if not authenticated, store deep link target in `@Observable` state, authenticate, then navigate) | 1.2, 5.7 | 8 |
| 5.9 | Implement notification preferences screen: allow users to toggle notification categories on/off; store preferences in API and locally in UserDefaults; respect preferences in foreground handler delegate method | 5.5 | 4 |
| 5.10 | Implement badge count management: update app badge count via `UNUserNotificationCenter.current().setBadgeCount()` on notification receipt; clear badge when user opens app; keep badge in sync with unread message count | 5.1 | 2 |
| 5.11 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: token registration, notification routing logic, deep link URL parsing, preference management | All above | 4 |
| 5.12 | Write Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows: receive notification (simulated), tap notification, verify correct screen opens; test deep link from Safari | 5.11 | 3 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Permission prompt shows clear value proposition, not raw system dialog
- Push notifications are received when app is in foreground, background, and killed states
- Tapping a notification navigates to the correct screen
- Deep links from Safari or other apps open the correct screen
- Auth-gated deep links work (unauthenticated user taps link, signs in, then sees target screen)
- Badge count reflects unread state
- Notification preferences are respected

**Decision Gate:**
Choose between direct APNs integration (full control, no third-party dependency) vs. a push service abstraction. Recommendation: direct APNs via backend for V1, since the native Swift app already has the device token natively — no intermediary like Expo Push API is needed.

**Deliverables:**
- Push notification system (registration, receipt, handling)
- Deep link system (URL scheme + universal links)
- Notification preferences screen
- Backend push infrastructure
- Test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Fresh install: permission prompt shows custom pre-permission screen, then system dialog
- [ ] Send test push via APNs test tool or backend admin; notification appears on device
- [ ] App in background: notification appears; tap opens correct screen
- [ ] App killed: notification appears; tap launches app and opens correct screen
- [ ] Open `skye://contacts/123` in Safari; app opens to contact detail for ID 123
- [ ] Open deep link while signed out; sign-in screen appears; after sign-in, navigates to target
- [ ] Toggle off "Contact Updates" in preferences; send a contact update push; notification does NOT appear
- [ ] Verify badge count clears when app is opened
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flows pass on physical device

#### Phase 6: Camera & Studio — OCR Flow, Content Generation

**Purpose:** Build the camera/OCR utility and the content generation studio. These are differentiating features that leverage AI.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phase 1 exit criteria met (foundation)
- Phase 2 exit criteria met (streaming/AI infrastructure can be reused)
- Backend OCR and studio endpoints are documented and accessible

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 6.1 | Build camera view using `AVFoundation` (`AVCaptureSession`, `AVCapturePhotoOutput`): request camera permission via `AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .video)` (with pre-permission prompt); create `CameraView` using `UIViewRepresentable` wrapping `AVCaptureVideoPreviewLayer` with viewfinder overlay, capture button, flash toggle (`AVCaptureDevice.torchMode`), front/back toggle (`AVCaptureDevice.Position`) | None | 6 |
| 6.2 | Implement photo capture: capture high-resolution image via `AVCapturePhotoOutput.capturePhoto()`, receive in delegate as `AVCapturePhoto`; show preview with "Retake" and "Use Photo" options; implement basic image cropping/adjustment using `Vision` framework `VNDetectRectanglesRequest` if needed | 6.1 | 4 |
| 6.3 | Implement OCR flow: after capture, send image to backend OCR endpoint (`/api/ocr/extract`) using multipart form upload via `URLSession`; show processing indicator with `ProgressView`; receive structured data (name, phone, email, company, address from business card; or property details from flyer/sign); display extracted fields in editable form using `Form` with `TextField`; user confirms or corrects; save as new contact or property | 6.2, 1.9 | 10 |
| 6.4 | Implement gallery picker: use `PhotosPicker` (PhotosUI framework, SwiftUI native) to select from camera roll; same OCR flow applies to gallery images | 6.3 | 3 |
| 6.5 | Build Studio tab screen with `ScreenContainer`: list of content generation templates (Property Listing Description, Social Media Post, Email Follow-Up, Open House Invite, Market Update, Neighborhood Guide); each template: SF Symbol icon, title, description; rendered as `NavigationLink` rows | None | 4 |
| 6.6 | Build template execution flow: user selects template; `Form` collects required inputs (property address, target audience, tone, key features, etc.); submit to backend `/api/studio/generate`; show streaming response (reuse SSE infrastructure from Phase 2 via `StreamingChatService`); result screen with formatted output using `AttributedString` | 6.5, 2.5 | 8 |
| 6.7 | Build result actions: copy to clipboard via `UIPasteboard.general.string`, share via `ShareLink` or `UIActivityViewController`, save as draft (stored in GRDB), regenerate (re-submit with same inputs), edit and refine (send edited version back for AI improvement) | 6.6 | 4 |
| 6.8 | Build Studio history: list of previously generated content; stored in GRDB `studio_drafts` table with template type, inputs, output, timestamp; search and filter by template type using `.searchable()` modifier; tap to view/re-use | 6.7 | 4 |
| 6.9 | Implement camera permission recovery: if user denied camera permission, show instructions to enable in Settings with a button that opens iOS Settings directly via `UIApplication.shared.open(URL(string: UIApplication.openSettingsURLString)!)` | 6.1 | 2 |
| 6.10 | Write unit tests using XCTest + Swift Testing: OCR data extraction parsing, template form validation, studio generation request formatting, result actions | 6.3, 6.6, 6.7 | 4 |
| 6.11 | Write Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows: open camera, capture image, see OCR results, save as contact; open Studio, select template, fill form, see generated content, copy to clipboard | 6.10 | 4 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Camera opens with viewfinder overlay within 1 second
- Photo capture produces a usable image
- OCR extracts text from a business card with >80% field accuracy
- Extracted data can be saved as a contact
- Studio templates produce streaming AI content
- Generated content can be copied, shared, and saved
- Studio history persists across sessions
- All permission flows handle denial gracefully

**Decision Gate:**
Evaluate OCR accuracy with real-world business cards and property flyers. If backend OCR accuracy is below 80% on a test set of 20 images, consider supplementing with on-device `VNRecognizeTextRequest` (Vision framework) pre-processing or a different OCR provider.

**Deliverables:**
- Camera capture screen
- OCR extraction and editing flow
- Studio template list screen
- Content generation with streaming
- Result actions (copy, share, save, regenerate)
- Studio history
- Test suites

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Camera permission prompt shows custom pre-permission screen
- [ ] Capture a business card photo; OCR extracts name and phone correctly
- [ ] Correct an extracted field; save as contact; contact appears in People tab
- [ ] Select "Property Listing Description" template; fill form; see streaming output
- [ ] Copy generated content; paste in Notes app; content matches
- [ ] Share generated content; share sheet opens with formatted text
- [ ] View Studio history; previously generated content is listed
- [ ] Deny camera permission; recovery screen appears with Settings button
- [ ] Maestro / XCUITest flows pass on physical device

#### Phase 7: Polish & Compliance — Dark Mode Audit, Accessibility, Privacy Manifest, Performance

**Purpose:** Transform the functional app into a submission-quality product. This phase is not "make it look nice" — it is a systematic audit with concrete pass/fail criteria.

**Entry Criteria:**
- Phases 1-6 exit criteria all met
- All features are functionally complete
- No known crash bugs in Sentry

**Task List:**

| # | Task | Depends On | Est. Hours |
|---|------|-----------|-----------|
| 7.1 | Dark mode audit: walk through every screen in dark mode; verify: no white flashes during transitions, no unreadable text on dark backgrounds, no invisible icons, no hardcoded colors bypassing theme, status bar style correct on all screens (`.preferredColorScheme()` and system automatic); fix all issues | None | 6 |
| 7.2 | Accessibility audit: every interactive element has an `.accessibilityLabel()`; every image has `.accessibilityLabel()` with descriptive text; all buttons have minimum 44x44pt touch target; VoiceOver can navigate every screen meaningfully; focus order is logical; no information conveyed only by color; Dynamic Type scaling works (up to accessibility large sizes) via SwiftUI's built-in font scaling | None | 8 |
| 7.3 | Performance audit: measure and optimize cold start time (target: <2s), screen transition time (target: <300ms), list scroll FPS (target: 60 FPS), memory usage (target: <150 MB at rest, <300 MB under heavy use), app binary size (target: <50 MB IPA); use Xcode Instruments (Time Profiler, Allocations, Core Animation, Leaks) | None | 8 |
| 7.4 | Privacy manifest: create/update `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` with all required reason APIs — `NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryFileTimestamp` (C617.1), `NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategorySystemBootTime` (35F9.1), `NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryDiskSpace` (E174.1), `NSPrivacyAccessedAPICategoryUserDefaults` (CA92.1); add any additional categories used by third-party SPM packages; add `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` to the Xcode project target's "Copy Bundle Resources" build phase | None | 3 |
| 7.5 | Privacy nutrition labels: prepare accurate data collection declarations for App Store Connect — what data is collected, linked to identity, used for tracking; this must match actual app behavior exactly | 7.4 | 3 |
| 7.6 | Third-party SDK audit: verify every SPM dependency's privacy manifest status; check for private API usage that could cause rejection; verify no SDK downloads executable code at runtime; document audit results | None | 4 |
| 7.7 | Animation polish: ensure all transitions use SwiftUI's native animation system (`.animation()`, `withAnimation {}`, `matchedGeometryEffect`, `.transition()`); add micro-interactions — tab switch animations, button press scale (`.scaleEffect()` on press), list item appearance (`.transition(.opacity)`), pull-to-refresh custom animation; verify no janky animations on oldest supported device | None | 6 |
| 7.8 | Error message review: audit every user-facing error message for clarity and actionability; replace technical messages ("Error 500") with human messages ("Something went wrong. Please try again."); ensure all error states have retry actions | None | 3 |
| 7.9 | Offline experience review: walk through every screen while offline; verify: appropriate offline indicators, cached data displays correctly, mutations queue and sync when back online, no blank screens, no spinners that never resolve | None | 4 |
| 7.10 | Memory leak audit: navigate through all screens repeatedly (50 times); monitor memory with Xcode Instruments (Leaks + Allocations); identify and fix any retain cycles (common with closures capturing `self` — ensure `[weak self]` where needed); particular focus on SSE `URLSession` connections, `NWPathMonitor`, `NotificationCenter` observers, and `Timer` instances | None | 6 |
| 7.11 | Haptic feedback audit: verify haptic feedback is consistent and appropriate across the app; not too frequent (annoying) or too sparse (missing affordance) | None | 2 |
| 7.12 | Splash screen and app icon: finalize app icon (1024x1024 single-size asset in asset catalog — Xcode auto-generates all sizes), launch screen configuration (SwiftUI launch screen or `LaunchScreen.storyboard`), loading states | None | 4 |
| 7.13 | Implement account deletion flow (Apple requirement): Settings > Delete Account > confirmation dialog (`.alert()`) > call `/api/user/delete` via `APIClient` > clear all local data (GRDB, UserDefaults, Keychain) > navigate to sign-in screen | 0.12 | 4 |
| 7.14 | Run full Maestro / XCUITest E2E test suite: all flows from all phases; fix any regressions | All above | 4 |
| 7.15 | Run `xcodebuild -scheme Skye -configuration Release` with all warnings enabled (`-Werror`); resolve all warnings. Run Xcode Analyze (Product > Analyze) and fix all static analysis issues | All above | 2 |

**Exit Criteria:**
- Zero dark mode visual bugs across all screens
- VoiceOver can navigate the entire app without getting stuck
- Cold start < 2 seconds on iPhone SE (3rd gen)
- 60 FPS scroll on all lists (measured, not estimated)
- Privacy manifest is complete and validated
- No third-party SDK uses private APIs
- No memory leaks detected after 50-screen navigation cycle
- Account deletion works end-to-end
- Full Maestro / XCUITest suite passes

**Decision Gate:**
If performance targets are missed on oldest supported device, decide whether to raise the minimum iOS version (dropping device support) or invest additional optimization time. This decision has App Store reach implications.

**Deliverables:**
- Polished, accessible, performant app
- Privacy manifest
- SDK audit report
- Performance benchmark report
- Accessibility audit report
- Account deletion flow
- Complete Maestro / XCUITest test suite (green)

**Verification Checklist:**
- [ ] Screenshot every screen in dark mode; no visual issues
- [ ] VoiceOver reads every screen correctly (manual walkthrough)
- [ ] Cold start measured at <2s on physical iPhone SE
- [ ] Scroll People list (500 contacts) at 60 FPS (Instruments)
- [ ] Scroll Chat (200 messages) at 60 FPS (Instruments)
- [ ] Memory after 50-screen cycle is within 10% of initial measurement
- [ ] Privacy manifest passes Xcode validation (Product > Generate Privacy Report)
- [ ] All Maestro / XCUITest E2E flows pass
- [ ] `xcodebuild` with Release config and Analyze produce zero warnings
- [ ] Delete account; relaunch; sign-in screen appears; no residual data
